title: Synopsys Launches Industry's First Broad-Scale Cloud SaaS Solution to Transform Chip Development Landscape
summary: Synopsys has launched Synopsys Cloud, the industry's first broad-scale cloud Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution for chip development. This new model, optimized for electronic design automation (EDA) and deployed on Microsoft Azure, offers a pay-per-use approach to provide unparalleled flexibility and efficiency for complex chip designs. The collaboration with Microsoft aims to streamline access to compute and EDA resources, addressing the escalating demands of chip design and verification.

title: Outbrain expands strategic partnership with Microsoft, leveraging Microsoft Azure to improve business outcomes for media owners and advertisers – Middle East & Africa News Center
summary: Outbrain Inc. and Microsoft have expanded their strategic partnership, with Outbrain leveraging Microsoft Azure for its media platform. This multi-year engagement aims to accelerate innovation, global expansion, and improve connectivity for advertisers and publishers on Outbrain's platform. The partnership reinforces both companies' commitment to supporting independent journalism and enhancing business outcomes through advanced technology.

title: Microsoft Dynamics Construction ISV Adds Civil Construction Tools
summary: SIS, LLC has released a civil-highway version of its Construct 365 product suite, which extends Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance. This new offering targets heavy and civil contractors and aims to capitalize on the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. The solution provides specialized functionality for project management, accounting, and labor cost management, integrating with existing estimating systems like HCSS HeavyBid and B2W.

title: Microsoft Phone Link upgrades the name, look, and feel of Your Phone companion
summary: Microsoft is rebranding its "Your Phone" companion app as "Phone Link," bringing a new interface and improved features. The update includes more prominent notifications, tabbed navigation, and a simpler setup process, particularly for Windows 11 users who will be able to connect via QR code. The app's design has also been updated to align with the visual style of Windows 11, featuring more rounded corners and simplified icons, aiming to enhance the seamless phone-to-PC experience.

title: 3 things learned from Microsoft’s carbon removal report
summary: Microsoft's latest carbon removal report reveals insights from its efforts to achieve net-zero by 2030. The company saw fewer, but higher quality, proposals for carbon removal projects after refining its RFP criteria. Microsoft is diversifying its high-durability solutions beyond direct air capture and continues to invest in biochar for medium-durability carbon storage.

title: Microsoft's cloud business targeted by EU antitrust regulators
summary: EU antitrust regulators are questioning Microsoft's rivals and customers regarding its cloud business and licensing deals, potentially leading to a formal investigation. European antitrust enforcers are scrutinizing whether Microsoft is leveraging its dominant position in some software markets to unfairly compete in cloud computing services, following complaints from several European tech companies. The inquiry focuses on licensing deal terms, technical limitations, and the differences in license fees between various programs.

title: Business On Air – MTR
summary: This article features an interview with Ted Suen, CIO of MTR Corporation, discussing their digital transformation strategy and its impact on their 40,000 global staff. MTR, as Hong Kong's key railway operator, has a long history of technological innovation, and its current focus is on empowering employees and improving customer experience through its Digital Workplace Initiative. Suen emphasizes that executive support and an engaging change program, including gamification and design thinking, were crucial for overcoming challenges and seizing opportunities during the implementation, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Internet of Things Market Size [2022-2029] Worth USD 2465.26 Billion | Exhibiting a CAGR of 26.4%
summary: The global Internet of Things (IoT) market is projected to reach USD 2,465.26 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 26.4% from USD 384.70 billion in 2021. This growth is driven by the increasing penetration of smart cities, deployment of vehicle solutions, and strong demand from the healthcare sector. Key players like Amazon Web Services, Intel, and Microsoft are focusing on product rollouts and technological advancements.

title: Clean Harbors’ CIO: Hybrid approach to the cloud is a win-win
summary: Clean Harbors, a hazardous waste cleanup giant, has adopted a hybrid cloud strategy, leveraging Microsoft Azure for advanced AI and RPA capabilities while retaining its core Waste Information Network (WIN) on-premises. This approach allows them to innovate with cloud services for customer-facing applications and data analytics, while ensuring the high availability and security of critical legacy systems and data, a model increasingly favored by enterprises. The company's CIO, Sharon Gabriel, highlights their cautious, methodical approach to cloud adoption, informed by past experiences and a need to manage 42 years of sensitive data.

title: Investing in esports in the context of recent events
summary: This article discusses the growth of esports, significant investment it has received, and current challenges due to global events like the COVID-19 pandemic and the Russia-Ukraine conflict. It identifies investment opportunities in publicly traded tech companies, specialized esports firms, and esports-focused ETFs, while also raising questions about future growth amidst potential new pandemics and the integration of NFTs.

title: Ball Aerospace Collaborates with Microsoft Azure on On-orbit Computing
summary: Ball Aerospace is partnering with Microsoft Azure to enhance on-orbit satellite data processing, combining Ball's space-based hardware expertise with Microsoft's cloud capabilities. This collaboration aims to create "smart" satellites that extend cloud data processing power to space, enabling faster, more flexible, and dynamic data handling for complex missions. Future demonstrations will test this software on-orbit, leveraging Microsoft Azure's integrated cloud services for advanced analytics and mission-critical government workloads.

title: Boeing and Microsoft deepen partnership in digital aviation
summary: Boeing and Microsoft are expanding their strategic partnership, with Boeing leveraging the Microsoft Cloud and its AI capabilities to modernize its critical infrastructure, streamline business processes, and accelerate digital innovation in aviation. This collaboration aims to unlock sustainable value from Boeing's data, building on a long history of the companies working together in aerospace technology.

title: Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Take-Two Interactive Software Inc. stock fell 3.09% on Wednesday amidst a broader market decline, closing at $147.20. Despite the loss, the stock demonstrated mixed performance compared to competitors like Microsoft, Sony, and Activision Blizzard, which also experienced declines. The trading volume for Take-Two exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Leveraging Intel Technology as a Force for Good
summary: Intel's RISE Technology Initiative supported 63 projects in the past year, investing $9.5 million to leverage Intel technology, employee talent, and partnerships for global good. These projects address accessibility, economic recovery, education, health and life sciences, social equity and human rights, and sustainability and climate. The initiative demonstrates how technology can be a force for positive change across diverse communities worldwide.

title: What’s Changed: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Transportation Management Systems
summary: This article highlights the changes in the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Transportation Management Systems (TMS), outlining the core and expanded capabilities of TMS software. It details the leaders, challengers, and niche players identified by Gartner, providing insights into their strengths, weaknesses, and target markets. Key updates include the addition of 3Gtms and the absence of BluJay Solutions due to its acquisition by E2open.

title: Shuffle Board: C-Suite Moves at Nordstrom, Burlington, JD, Zappos, American Textile
summary: This article details significant C-suite personnel changes across several major retail and textile companies, including Nordstrom, Burlington Stores, JD.com, Zappos.com, and American Textile Company. Key appointments and retirements are highlighted, along with new roles aimed at strategic growth, customer experience, and sustainability within these organizations. The changes reflect ongoing efforts by these companies to adapt to market demands and streamline operations.

title: Oracle Named a 2022 Gartner® Peer Insights™ ‘Voice of the Customer’: Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
summary: Oracle has been recognized as a Customers’ Choice in the 2022 Gartner® Peer Insights™ ‘Voice of the Customer’: Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms for Oracle APEX, based on customer reviews. Oracle APEX is a low-code development platform, offered as a no-cost feature of Oracle Database and available for free on Oracle Cloud's Always Free services, empowering developers to build scalable enterprise apps. The article includes testimonials from customers praising APEX for its speed, functionality, and ability to solve business problems.

title: DeFiChain Adds New dTokens Corresponding to Disney, Intel, MicroStrategy, and iShares MSCI China ETF
summary: DeFiChain has announced the addition of four new decentralized tokens, or dTokens, following a community vote. These new dTokens correspond to Walt Disney Co ($dDIS), iShares MSCI China ETF ($dMCHI), MicroStrategy Incorporated ($dMSTR), and Intel Corporation ($dINTC), allowing users to gain price exposure to these traditional assets without ownership. DeFiChain, a blockchain built on the Bitcoin network, aims to provide decentralized financial applications and services, expanding its dToken universe to offer an alternative to traditional financial brokers.

title: Native Advertising Vs Social Media: What Do Audiences Prefer? Outbrain-Savanta Report
summary: A report by Outbrain and Savanta indicates a shift in consumer behavior, with global audiences planning to spend less time on social media and showing a greater preference for editorial content environments. The study reveals that consumers trust editorial sites and the ads within them more than social media, and consider native advertising less intrusive. Marketers are advised to diversify their strategies to include advertising on open-web publishers to align with these changing preferences.

title: Data Analytics Market Size to grow by USD 196.47 Billion | 32% of the market growth will originate from APAC | 17,000+ Technavio Reports
summary: The global data analytics market is projected to grow by USD 196.47 billion from 2021 to 2026, accelerating at a CAGR of 13.54%. The services component segment dominated the market in 2021 and will continue to do so. APAC is expected to contribute 32% of the market's growth, with China and India being key markets.

title: Nikon Partners with Microsoft to Produce VR and 3D Video Content
summary: Nikon has established a new wholly-owned subsidiary, Nikon Creates Corporation, which will focus on planning, shooting, and producing volumetric, VR, and 3D content. This new venture makes Nikon the first licensed partner of Microsoft’s Mixed Reality Capture Studios, leveraging high-end production technology. Nikon Creates plans to open a state-of-the-art video production facility in Tokyo in Fall 2022, offering clients advanced video solutions for entertainment and advertising.

title: Sungard Availability Services Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Sungard Availability Services has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the U.S. and for protection under the CCAA in Canada, seeking to complete the process within four to six months. This follows a previous Chapter 11 filing in 2019 and insolvency proceedings for its UK subsidiary, highlighting ongoing financial distress due to declining revenue, fixed costs, and competition from cloud providers. The company's restructuring support agreement contemplates either a sale of assets or equitization of its prepetition funded debt.

title: BPCL collaborates with Microsoft to leverage cloud and AI to redefine customer experience
summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd. (BPCL) and Microsoft have announced a seven-year strategic cloud partnership to accelerate BPCL's digital transformation and enhance customer experience in the oil and gas industry. This collaboration, part of BPCL's 'Project Anubhav', will leverage Microsoft's cloud and AI capabilities, including Azure, for initiatives like UFill for an innovative fueling solution, IoT-powered supply chains, and a conversational AI chatbot named Urja for customer support. The partnership aims to build a resilient, cloud-led business operation, redefine customer experience through trust, personalization, and convenience, and strengthen BPCL's business operations with security at its core.

title: UK gamers prepare for ‘post-console’ future, finds Amdocs
summary: Amdocs research indicates that UK gamers are increasingly ready for a "post-console" future, driven by cloud gaming and 5G connectivity. The study found high enthusiasm for cloud gaming and the metaverse, with many gamers willing to pay more for these services and forgo new hardware purchases. Key priorities for gamers include content quality and the ability to stream from any device, while concerns about the metaverse mainly revolve around its early stage, hardware costs, and internet connection capabilities.

title: AI used for chip counting at City of Dreams Manila
summary: City of Dreams Manila is now using AI technology developed by Cogniac Corp to count and verify casino chips with 99.7% accuracy, significantly increasing speed and efficiency. This implementation for a loyalty program is noted as a unique AI application in the casino industry, allowing Melco Resorts to reallocate staff to more strategic areas and enhance operational accuracy and guest experience.

title: Microsoft India appoints Amrita Thapar as its CMO
summary: Microsoft India has appointed Amrita Thapar as its new Chief Marketing Officer. Thapar previously served as India communications lead at Amazon Web Services (AWS) and held a vice president role at Genpact for over 13 years. She brings more than 15 years of experience in marketing and communications to her new role, specializing in brand storytelling, digital operations, and corporate communications.

title: BPCL teams up with Microsoft to leverage cloud, AI technologies
summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd (BPCL) has formed a strategic cloud partnership with Microsoft to accelerate its digital transformation and foster innovation within the oil and gas industry. This collaboration, spanning seven years, will leverage Microsoft's secure cloud platform, including Azure native services for IoT and analytics, to enhance BPCL's operations, supply chains, and customer engagement. The initiative aligns with BPCL's "Project Anubhav" aimed at redefining customer experience through trust, personalization, and convenience.

title: Why the Immense Selling Pressure on the SPY ETF Must End
summary: The article discusses why the intense selling pressure on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) is likely to subside, presenting a buying opportunity. It attributes recent declines to concerns surrounding major holdings like Microsoft, Amazon, and Tesla, including antitrust reviews, Rivian losses, and Elon Musk's distraction with Twitter. Despite these issues, the author argues that the fundamental strength of the top companies in the S&P 500 remains, and investors should accumulate SPY shares on dips.

title: Putting zero trust into practice: An overview of Zscaler’s platform offerings, solution elements, and partner ecosystem
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of Zscaler's Zero Trust Exchange (ZTE) platform, detailing its components designed to secure users, workloads, and IoT/OT systems. It explains how integrated solutions like Zscaler Internet Access (ZIA) and Zscaler Private Access (ZPA) enable secure connectivity and data protection. The piece also highlights Zscaler's ecosystem integrations that consolidate vendor services and optimize cloud access.

title: Microsoft Launches Operator Connect Accelerator
summary: Microsoft has introduced the Operator Connect Accelerator program to help carriers achieve Operator Connect accreditation more quickly. This initiative involves certified partners assisting service providers with API integration, managed SBC as a service, and other necessary steps to streamline the process. The program aims to reduce time-to-market for telecom services integrating with Microsoft Teams, making it easier for businesses to adopt Teams as their primary phone system.

title: FIS’ Modern Banking Platform Now Available on the Microsoft Azure Cloud Platform
summary: FIS announced that its Modern Banking Platform is now available on the Microsoft Azure cloud, expanding digital online banking services to financial institutions in the UK, Thailand, and New Zealand. This platform integrates advanced cloud security, monitoring, resiliency, and operational analytics with a cloud-native core, utilizing AI and component-based architecture. This partnership enables banks to quickly develop and deliver new products and services, adapting to regulatory changes and escalating customer expectations.

title: Latest Oracle Exadata servers ascend to the cloud
summary: Oracle has announced the availability of its latest Exadata Cloud Infrastructure X9M appliance in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, marking the rollout of its newest generation of Exadata processors. This new service, based on AMD microprocessors, offers significant improvements in performance, throughput, and capacity without increased costs, allowing organizations to consolidate various database workloads. Oracle claims superior performance compared to AWS RDS and Azure SQL, with the service designed for scalable deployment both on-premises and across Oracle's expanding global cloud footprint.

title: JPMorgan Chase, Microsoft among growing number of companies turning to neurodiverse workers to help meet need for talent
summary: A growing number of companies, including JPMorgan Chase and Microsoft, are actively recruiting neurodiverse individuals to fill talent needs and leverage their unique skills. This trend is driven by an increasing recognition of the neurodiverse population, their problem-solving abilities, and the ongoing demand for talent, with companies implementing structured programs for interviewing, hiring, and onboarding. These initiatives not only provide career opportunities for neurodiverse workers but also positively impact company culture and team dynamics.

title: Apple Spends Record High on Lobbying Amid Pressure from Congress
summary: Apple Inc. spent a record $2.5 million on lobbying in the first quarter of 2022, marking a 34% increase from the previous quarter. This significant expenditure comes as the company faces growing pressure from the U.S. Congress regarding antitrust concerns. Other major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft are also heavily investing in lobbying efforts as Congress considers antitrust legislation that could impact the technology sector.

title: International Business Machines Corp. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) shares rose 1.11% to $139.85, outperforming a generally down market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both fell. This marks the third consecutive day of gains for IBM, which also outperformed competitors like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet. The company closed $6.14 below its 52-week high, and its trading volume was more than double its 50-day average.

title: Lapsus$ Cyberattack Victim List: Globant, Microsoft, Nvidia, Okta, Samsung, T-Mobile -
summary: The Lapsus$ hacker group continued to target high-profile companies even after some alleged members were arrested in March 2022. This article lists several major companies, including Globant, Microsoft, Nvidia, Okta, Samsung, and T-Mobile, that have been victims of Lapsus$ cyberattacks. It also provides guidance on how to protect against and respond to ransomware attacks, emphasizing data backups, multi-factor authentication, and prompt incident reporting.

title: Kraft Heinz and Microsoft join forces to accelerate supply chain transformation
summary: Kraft Heinz is partnering with Microsoft to digitally transform its supply chain using Microsoft Azure, marking its largest technology investment to date. This collaboration aims to create a more resilient supply chain by establishing a "supply chain control tower" and "digital twins" of manufacturing facilities, leveraging AI, IoT, and data analytics. The initiative seeks to enhance operational efficiency, anticipate consumer demand, and optimize product delivery for Kraft Heinz's extensive portfolio.

title: How LV Vaidyanathan, the Satya Nadella fan, is looking to rewrite P&G India's fortunes
summary: LV Vaidyanathan, a P&G veteran and IIM-Ahmedabad alumnus, is set to take over as P&G India's head from July 1, 2022. He aims to transform the company's operations in India to capitalize on the country's strong growth prospects in the FMCG market. Inspired by Satya Nadella, Vaidyanathan plans to change the company's culture and image, focusing on product superiority, innovation, and sustainability.

title: Low Volatility Stocks Usually Do Well—But There Are Traps
summary: A Northern Trust Asset Management paper warns that while low-volatility stocks generally outperform over time, they can present traps, particularly in real estate, utilities, and consumer staples sectors. Rising interest rates and economic vulnerability can negatively impact these types of low-volatility holdings. NTAM advises focusing on companies with strong cash flows, profitability, and solid balance sheets to mitigate these risks.

title: Microsoft (MSFT) Q3 revenue up 18%, earnings beat estimates
summary: Microsoft Corp. reported strong third-quarter 2022 results, with revenues increasing by 18% to $49.4 billion, surpassing analyst expectations. The company's net income also rose to $16.7 billion, or $2.22 per share, exceeding estimates. This performance was significantly driven by a 29% growth in server products and cloud services revenue, particularly Azure.

title: Android Can Now Sync with Windows PCs via Phone Link App, Like Apple Devices
summary: Android phones can now sync with Windows PCs through Microsoft's Phone Link app, enabling users to access notifications, messages, and calls directly from their computer, similar to how Apple devices operate. This integration, especially with Windows 11, allows for seamless management of smartphone features and even enables running Android native applications on the PC. The setup involves simple QR code scanning and ensures updated operating systems and apps for both devices.

title: Slack is definitely part of Salesforce now, and here’s proof
summary: Salesforce and Slack are launching "Salesforce Platform for Slack," allowing developers to build functionality that integrates Salesforce data into Slack. This move solidifies Slack's integration with its parent company, offering enhanced collaboration and automation capabilities while still maintaining compatibility with third-party business tools. Slack cofounder Cal Henderson provides a nuanced perspective on the acquisition, highlighting the benefits of working with Salesforce's thriving ecosystem.

title: Why AWS, Microsoft, Google, Visa Are Setting Base In Kenya
summary: Major tech companies like Amazon Web Services, Microsoft, Google, and Visa are establishing operations in Kenya due to its growing economy and increasing client demand for cloud computing services. Kenya's strategic location as East Africa's largest economy and a hub for Fortune 500 companies makes it an attractive market for expansion. This move is driven by the need to meet local data residency requirements and capitalize on the burgeoning African cloud computing industry.

title: HCL Technologies brings All Access Media Marketplace Platform
summary: HCL Technologies has launched its All Access Media Marketplace Platform at NAB Las Vegas, aiming to streamline content creation and distribution processes. Developed in coordination with Microsoft and CoreSite, the platform addresses workflow, application, and vendor discovery challenges for media creators. It features HCL Workflow Creator for building workflows, leverages Microsoft 365 for communication, and utilizes Azure Cloud for cybersecurity, aligning with MovieLabs 2030 Vision.

title: Advisors Are Powering Portfolios With Thematic ETFs
summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in companies poised to benefit from long-term transformative changes, are increasingly being adopted by financial advisors as a replacement for traditional sector funds. These ETFs offer more precise exposure to growth trends like blockchain, emergent food, robotics, and electric vehicles. Asset managers are expanding their thematic ETF offerings, with BlackRock (iShares) recently launching two new products in this space, highlighting a growing conviction in the long-term benefits of thematic investing despite some recent market volatility.

title: Telkom dan Microsoft Perkuat Kemitraan Strategis untuk Akselerasi Digitalisasi Indonesia – Indonesia News Center
summary: PT Telkom Indonesia (Persero) Tbk and Microsoft have signed a strategic partnership agreement to accelerate Indonesia's national digitalization, strengthen intelligent infrastructure, and speed up TelkomGroup's digital transformation. This collaboration, which continues a previous MoU from August 2021, aims to enable innovative and impactful services in accelerating Indonesia's digital transformation by combining Telkom's extensive infrastructure with Microsoft's global technology capabilities. The partnership also includes internal digital transformation for Telkom and its subsidiaries, focusing on cloud adoption, competency enhancement, and collaboration in developing national intelligent infrastructure.

title: Top 10 Growth Stocks by Market Capitalization
summary: This article defines growth stocks and explains how they differ from other investment types, highlighting their potential for capital appreciation and typical reinvestment strategies. It then presents a list of the top 10 large-cap growth stocks as of April 2022, detailing their market capitalization, forward P/E ratio, dividend status, and specific growth drivers for each company. The article also touches on general market performance of growth stocks and factors influencing their volatility.

title: Telkom and Microsoft Strengthen Strategic Partnership to Accelerate Indonesia's Digitalization
summary: Telkom and Microsoft have formed a strategic partnership to accelerate Indonesia's national digitalization, enhance intelligent infrastructure, and advance TelkomGroup's digital transformation. This collaboration, a continuation of an August 2021 MOU, aims to leverage Telkom's infrastructure and Microsoft's digital capabilities to offer innovative services and support Telkom's vision as a leading digital telecommunications company. They will jointly explore new market opportunities, develop intelligent infrastructure, and facilitate Telkom's internal digital transformation by increasing cloud-based service adoption and employee competence.

title: Covington Nabs Former Linklaters Antitrust Chief
summary: Christian Ahlborn, former global head of antitrust at Linklaters, is joining Covington & Burling's Brussels office as a partner. This move anticipates a surge in demand for antitrust legal advice as new EU rules, such as the Digital Markets Act, are set to curb the power of major technology companies. The increased complexity and data-intensive nature of competition investigations are driving the need for more specialized legal expertise in the sector.

title: Microsoft announces general availability of Microsoft Defender for Business designed to boost small and medium business (SMB) security
summary: Microsoft has announced the general availability of Microsoft Defender for Business, a new cybersecurity solution specifically designed for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). This offering aims to provide enterprise-grade endpoint security, including detection and response capabilities against ransomware and other sophisticated cyberthreats, to SMBs. The solution is available standalone or as part of Microsoft 365 Business Premium, addressing the growing concern among SMBs about cybersecurity risks.

title: Here’s why TELUS International is a buy right now
summary: Greg Newman of Scotia Wealth Management recommends TELUS International (TSX:TIXT) as a buying opportunity, despite its recent dip alongside the broader software sector. He highlights the company's strong growth rate, its trading below issuance price, and attractive valuation at 19x its 2023 multiple. Newman suggests investors consider building a position gradually, expecting rewards over the next two to three years.

title: 13 Companies Offering Quantum Cloud Computing Software Services
summary: This article lists and describes 13 leading companies that offer quantum cloud computing software services in 2025. It explains what quantum cloud computing is, highlighting its role in making expensive quantum hardware accessible to a wider audience, from researchers to enthusiasts. The article covers major players like IBM, Google, and Microsoft, as well as specialized quantum startups, detailing their cloud platforms and offerings.

title: Microsoft deepens investment in Canada with the opening of new headquarters and expansion of operations to empower economic growth – Microsoft News Center Canada
summary: Microsoft has opened its new Canadian headquarters in Toronto, marking a significant investment in the country's innovation economy. A new EY report indicates Microsoft's ecosystem supports nearly 300,000 jobs and contributes $37 billion to Canada's GDP. The company is also expanding its regional presence, supporting innovation projects, and a skills initiative that has trained over one million Canadians.

title: Embracing The Inevitable Move From Hardware To Services
summary: Dell Technologies is shifting its focus from hardware sales to software and services, as evidenced by its Dell Technologies World event. The company is expanding its Apex as-a-service portfolio, enhancing its storage software, and building multi-cloud solutions like Project Alpine to offer cloud-like consumption models and address the evolving needs of IT. This strategy aims to provide streamlined experiences, consistent data mobility, and robust cybersecurity solutions for on-premises and public cloud environments.

title: Microsoft signs 110MW PPA with AES to power California data centers
summary: Microsoft has signed a 20-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with AES to provide 110MWAC solar and 55MW, 4-hour storage renewable energy for its data centers in California. This agreement builds on previous collaborations between Microsoft and AES in Virginia and Chile, helping Microsoft achieve its goal of 100 percent renewable energy by 2025. The energy will partially match Microsoft's load at its California-based data centers, sourced from projects within the CAISO region.

title: Brookfield's venture capital arm cuts biggest check yet for Elon Musk's Twitter buyout
summary: Brookfield Asset Management's venture capital division, Brookfield Growth, has committed US$250 million to Elon Musk's US$7.1 billion equity raise for his acquisition of Twitter Inc. This investment marks the largest single check written by Brookfield Growth to date and stems from an existing relationship between the billionaire and the Canadian asset manager. The partnership also extends to other ventures, including a sustainable housing development in Austin, Texas, involving Musk's Boring Co. and Tesla Energy.

title: This World Password Day consider ditching passwords altogether
summary: On World Password Day, Microsoft urges users to consider ditching passwords entirely due to their inherent vulnerabilities and the rising frequency of password attacks. The article details how to enable passwordless sign-in for Microsoft accounts using various methods like the Microsoft Authenticator app or security keys. It also highlights the importance of multifactor authentication and offers tips for creating stronger passwords for accounts that still require them.

title: 7 Charts for Making Sense of These Markets
summary: This article analyzes current market volatility through seven charts, highlighting the Federal Reserve's aggressive interest rate hikes as a primary driver. It discusses the impact on both stock and bond markets, the underperformance of growth stocks versus value stocks, and offers insights on potential opportunities for investors amidst the market adjustments.

title: Kraft Heinz wants to solve its supply chain problems — in the metaverse
summary: Kraft Heinz is leveraging the metaverse and a partnership with Microsoft to enhance its supply chain efficiency. By creating "digital twins" of its facilities, the company aims to resolve logistical issues virtually, speed up operations, and ensure products like Lunchables and Velveeta reach shelves faster. This technological investment seeks to improve dependability and responsiveness to shifting consumer demands.

title: Holy-Moly Mortgage Rates Hit 5.64%, 10-Year Treasury Yield 3.12%, Long-Term Treasury Bond Fund Gets Massacred
summary: Mortgage rates have surged to 5.64%, the highest since 2009, as the 10-year Treasury yield rose to 3.12%. This has led to a significant increase in monthly mortgage payments, impacting housing demand. A long-term Treasury bond ETF has plunged, indicating a "bond massacre" for investors who previously viewed bonds as a conservative investment.

title: Meta is opening its first store as VR headsets inch closer to mainstream reality
summary: Meta Platforms is opening its first physical store to sell VR headsets like the Oculus Quest 2, despite its Reality Labs division facing significant losses. The company is betting heavily on the metaverse, viewing it as the solution to boost virtual reality adoption beyond the current low engagement among teens. Experts suggest that widespread VR adoption could be triggered by one or two "killer applications" and that Meta's strategy is to build the foundational platform for this new computing paradigm, even if profitability remains years away.

title: As pandemic videogame boom wanes, merger madness takes over
summary: The pandemic-driven boom in video games is subsiding, leading to a surge in mergers and acquisitions aimed at supporting stock prices. Despite a decline in usage, analysts like Cowen's Doug Creutz remain bullish on the sector due to M&A activity, highlighted by major acquisitions like Take-Two's purchase of Zynga and Microsoft's offer for Activision Blizzard. Electronic Arts is noted as one of the few large companies not yet involved in this M&A wave.

title: Intel Expands Atlanta Presence Across Software Development Team
summary: Intel is significantly expanding its software development team in Atlanta, planning to add over 100 new local jobs with six-figure salaries as part of a larger goal to create 500 jobs by 2024. This move reinforces Atlanta's growing reputation as a tech hub, alongside other major companies like Cash App, Nike, and Microsoft also establishing or expanding their presence in the city. The new roles will focus on areas like AI, cloud, and security, utilizing Atlanta's diverse technology community and talent base.

title: Ten Principles For Designing The Metaverse
summary: This article outlines ten principles for designing the metaverse, emphasizing that while the metaverse doesn't fully exist yet, companies should experiment with virtual 3D worlds. Author David Truog introduces "fusion" and "empiricism" as key principles, explaining how VR, MR, and AR experiences range from physical to entirely mediated, and stressing the importance of direct experience over speculation. The article also touches upon other principles like depth, attention, and magic, all grounded in human-centered design, to help organizations navigate the development of extended reality (XR) experiences.

title: Microsoft Collaborates with Peel District School Board to launch BuildAbility, a new world in Minecraft: Education Edition focused on accessibility and building inclusive spaces - Peel Board Office Site
summary: The Peel District School Board (PDSB) and Microsoft Canada have launched "BuildAbility," a new world in Minecraft: Education Edition designed to teach students about accessibility and inclusive design. This initiative encourages students and educators to identify and eliminate accessibility barriers, promoting empathy for individuals with disabilities. The program aligns with PDSB's multi-year accessibility plan and Ontario's Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, aiming to foster an accessibility-first culture in both online spaces and physical communities.

title: Kraft Heinz thinking outside the ketchup bottle, exploring paper packaging
summary: Kraft Heinz is partnering with UK-based Pulpex to develop paper-based packaging for its condiments, starting with the iconic Heinz Ketchup bottle. This initiative is part of Kraft Heinz's broader commitment to sustainability, including its goal to achieve carbon neutrality by 2050. The move reflects the company's efforts to innovate beyond traditional packaging to reduce environmental impact.

title: Slack and Teams are Replacing Business Emails, Report Shows
summary: A new report indicates that business communication is shifting from traditional email to real-time messaging apps like Slack and Microsoft Teams. This change is driven by a preference for less formal messaging, increased security features like end-to-end encryption, and the rise of hybrid work models. While email still serves for formal communications, chat apps are becoming the preferred method for daily interactions, and businesses are also adopting stronger cybersecurity measures to protect against email-based cyber threats.

title: Z by HP Powers Today’s Professionals to Create without Limitation
summary: HP announced new Z by HP solutions, including ZBook mobile workstations, Z displays, a Thunderbolt G4 Dock, and tools for data scientists, aimed at empowering creative and technical professionals. These products offer enhanced performance, seamless collaboration, and improved productivity for those working in diverse environments, from on-the-go to hybrid setups. Key features include powerful CPUs/GPUs, high-refresh-rate displays, secure docking, and a new Data Science Stack Manager to streamline workflows.

title: ConnectWise, N-able, Sophos Owner Thoma Bravo Raises $20B+ for Technology Acquisitions
summary: Private equity firm Thoma Bravo, known for owning ConnectWise, N-able, and Sophos, has successfully raised over $20 billion for its new Fund XV despite recent market turbulence. The firm plans to use these funds for further software acquisitions, with CEO Orlando Bravo emphasizing a profit-led approach over "growth at all costs." Thoma Bravo's substantial assets and investment strategy position it as a major player in the cybersecurity and MSP software market, competing with other private equity firms also actively investing in the tech sector.

title: Bill Gates Sells $940 Million of CN Rail Stock
summary: Bill Gates' investment firm, Cascade Investment, sold approximately $940 million worth of Canadian National Railway Co. shares between April 27 and May 12, reducing its stake in the company to about 9%. This sale is part of an ongoing reduction in ownership since May 2021. Gates' former wife, Melinda French Gates, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation also sold significant portions of their Canadian National shares.

title: Oracle Corp. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Oracle Corp. (ORCL) saw its stock rise 0.62% on Friday to $71.17, despite a generally positive day for the broader market with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also increasing. However, Oracle underperformed compared to some competitors and remained significantly below its 52-week high. Trading volume was also below its 50-day average.

title: Microsoft Channel Chief Rodney Clark To Leave After Over A Year In Role
summary: Rodney Clark, Microsoft's channel chief and a 24-year veteran of the company, is departing to take an executive officer role at a publicly traded company that partners with Microsoft. His successor will be announced at the annual Inspire partner conference in July. Microsoft's corporate vice president of global partner solutions, Nick Parker, stated that the company has a strong leadership team in place to ensure continuity for partners and programs.

title: Companies (and government) spending big to produce chips
summary: The Japanese government and companies are investing trillions of yen to revitalize the country's semiconductor industry, aiming to increase domestic production amid a global chip shortage. Through government grants from a 600-billion-yen fund, companies like Kioxia Holdings, TSMC, Sony Group, and Denso are constructing or upgrading factories to produce various types of semiconductors. This initiative seeks to establish a stable domestic supply chain and regain Japan's former prominence in the global semiconductor market, although some experts express concerns about potential interference with free competition due to government support.

title: Microchip Adds Real-Time Security to Its Root of Trust Silicon Tech
summary: Microchip has updated its Trust Shield root of trust product with the new CEC1736 controller, adding real-time security features like SPI bus runtime protection to monitor traffic between the CPU and Flash memory. This enhancement aims to prevent firmware attacks, which are increasingly common and often overlooked. The article highlights the growing industry focus on hardware-based root of trust solutions to secure servers and data centers from physical and remote threats, noting similar initiatives from tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Microsoft.

title: Microsoft TechSpark announces expansion to Jackson, Mississippi
summary: Microsoft's TechSpark initiative is expanding to Jackson, Mississippi, to address the digital divide and foster inclusive economic opportunities. The expansion will focus on digital skills training, computer science education, broadband connectivity, and digital transformation for nonprofits and startups, spearheaded by Jackson resident JJ Townsend. Key initial collaborations include supporting Jackson State University's Cybersecurity Readiness program, gener8tor Skills Accelerator, Innovate Mississippi’s CoBuilders Accelerator, and the Jackson Tech District Makerspace.

title: With multi-device FIDO credentials, you can now go all-in on passwordless | CXO Revolutionaries
summary: Apple, Google, and Microsoft's expanded support for multi-device FIDO credentials is set to revolutionize passwordless logins, addressing previous limitations of FIDO authentication like device-locked cryptographic keys and complex re-enrollment processes. This development, coupled with the Federal Zero Trust Strategy requiring phishing-resistant authentication, positions FIDO to become a prevalent, high-assurance authentication method for both consumers and federal use cases. The update promises seamless and secure access to FIDO credentials across multiple devices, even after device replacement.

title: Microsoft moves forward with massive corporate campus project in Atlanta
summary: Microsoft is proceeding with ambitious plans to develop a massive 90-acre corporate campus in Atlanta, Georgia, over the next decade, expecting to create thousands of jobs. The project, located at Quarry Yards, will dedicate 25% of the property to community projects like affordable housing and grocery stores, and Microsoft is actively seeking community feedback for the site's design with a strong focus on sustainability. Local politicians and community leaders have expressed enthusiasm for the economic and social benefits the campus will bring to Atlanta.

title: American Makes Microsoft Azure Preferred Cloud Provider for Airline Applications
summary: American Airlines has chosen Microsoft Azure as its preferred cloud platform for various airline applications, including airport taxiing decisions and managing operational data. This move follows previous partnerships with IBM, and American Airlines aims to leverage Azure's AI, machine learning, and data analytics to optimize customer experience and operations, particularly for gating decisions at its DFW hub. This decision positions American Airlines among other major U.S. carriers embracing cloud-first approaches for future resilience and innovation.

title: Nevada regulators grant investor Barry Diller limited license as MGM board member
summary: The Nevada Gaming Commission granted media mogul Barry Diller a two-year limited gaming license to serve on the MGM Resorts International board due to an ongoing federal investigation into his stock purchases. Regulators cited Diller's cooperation with the SEC regarding Activision Blizzard Inc. stock acquired before Microsoft's announced acquisition, which resulted in a $60 million profit for him and associates. Despite MGM's legal team arguing for a full license, and Diller denying any wrongdoing, commissioners opted for a limited license to allow time for the federal inquiry to conclude.

title: International Business Machines Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) stock fell 0.91% to $128.48 on Friday, May 20, 2022, underperforming the broader stock market, which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rise slightly. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for IBM, leaving its stock $17.51 below its 52-week high. While IBM declined, some competitors showed mixed performance, with Apple Inc. (AAPL) rising and Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Alphabet Inc. Cl A (GOOGL) falling.

title: WacoWatch
summary: Judge Albright granted Microsoft Corporation's motion to transfer a patent litigation case to the Western District of Washington, finding it to be a more convenient venue. The decision was based on an analysis of private and public interest factors, including the location of evidence and witnesses, and despite the plaintiff's argument about Microsoft's past venue practices. Three factors favored transfer, one disfavored it, and four were neutral.

title: Digital Marketing Software Market Giants Spending Is Going to Boom | Major Giants IBM, HubSpot, Microsoft
summary: A new study by HTF MI reveals that the worldwide digital marketing software market is expected to boom, with major players like IBM, HubSpot, and Microsoft driving growth. The research provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, drivers, and future prospects, covering segmentation by application (Large Enterprises & SMEs) and product type (On-Premises & Cloud). The report also highlights key market developments and includes a geographical analysis of the market.

title: U.S. company building AI supercomputer factory in Central Bohemia
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise is establishing its first European factory for next-generation high-performance computing and AI systems in Kutná Hora, Central Bohemia, Czech Republic. This new facility will manufacture custom-designed supercomputers primarily for scientific research, expanding HPE's global manufacturing footprint. The factory will focus on producing liquid-cooled HPC and AI systems, including HPE Apollo and HPE Cray EX supercomputers, with operations set to begin in summer 2022.

title: Microsoft Technology Center in Zurich officially opened
summary: Microsoft Switzerland has officially opened its Microsoft Technology Center (MTC) at its new headquarters in The Circle at the Zurich Airport. This 1,000 square meter space is designed to be an innovation hub where customers can envision and test new technology and industry-specific scenarios. The MTC represents a significant investment by Microsoft to drive innovation and co-innovation in Switzerland, built on over 30 years of local presence and partnerships.

title: Beware emailed PDFs containing Word docs exploiting old bugs
summary: HP cybersecurity researchers have discovered an email campaign distributing malware through PDFs that embed malicious Word documents, exploiting a four-year-old Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2017-11882). This unusual method leverages user trust in PDF documents to deliver the Snake Keylogger, highlighting that old vulnerabilities remain effective and emphasizing the need for patching, user education, and robust email security.

title: How Amazon, Microsoft, Costco and more Seattle-area brands rank by reputation
summary: Amazon ranked highest among Seattle-area companies in a recent Axios and Harris Poll survey assessing the reputations of the 100 most visible corporate brands in the U.S. Conversely, Costco, based in Issaquah, experienced a 17-place drop in the same survey. The article highlights how local companies performed in this national reputational ranking.

title: Apple's Steve Jobs: An Extraordinary Career
summary: This article chronicles the extraordinary career of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, highlighting how his vision propelled the PC revolution and Apple's success. It covers his early life, his initial departure from Apple, his ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, where he spearheaded groundbreaking products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. The piece concludes with his health struggles, resignation, and passing, emphasizing his lasting legacy as a visionary leader.

title: Qualcomm CEO sees growth in high-end phones even if economy slows
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon believes the company will continue to see growth in the high-end smartphone market, despite concerns about an economic slowdown. He noted that consumers are increasingly seeking "better phones" with more capabilities, and Qualcomm is gaining market share in premium devices. Amon also highlighted the potential for augmented reality to become as significant as smartphones, expanding Qualcomm's partnerships in the AR/VR space.

title: L’OFFICIEL Makes Its Arrival in the Metaverse with Virtual Land on The Sandbox
summary: Fashion magazine L’OFFICIEL has launched its presence in the metaverse with virtual land on The Sandbox, inspired by sci-fi films like Dune and Total Recall. This "L’OFFICIEL Land" is designed as an open-air exhibition and digital theatre to showcase the magazine's 100-year content history and future editorial focused on Web3 fashion. The initiative, led by Benjamin Eymère, CEO of L’OFFICIEL and Chief Metaverse Officer of AMTD group, marks a significant move into immersive digital experiences for the brand.

title: Johnson Controls Appoints Rodney Clark as Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer
summary: Johnson Controls (NYSE: JCI) has appointed Rodney Clark as vice president and chief commercial officer, effective June 1, 2022. Clark will be responsible for leading the company's global sales excellence and growth strategy, particularly focusing on decarbonization in smart, healthy buildings. He joins Johnson Controls from Microsoft, where he previously held significant roles in global partner sales and Internet of Things (IoT) sales.

title: What Is Web 3.0, and How Does It Impact Digital Marketers?
summary: Web 3.0 represents the next stage of internet evolution, offering enhanced data privacy, security, and a more personalized user experience through decentralization and blockchain technology. This shift will significantly impact digital marketers by changing data collection methods, improving user experience, and necessitating updates to current marketing strategies to leverage new data sources and address the move away from third-party cookies. B2B software marketers are advised to stay informed on trends, understand blockchain, and utilize customer data insights to prepare for this new era.

title: The Hanover Insurance Group’s automation CoE ensures successful solutions can scale
summary: The Hanover Insurance Group established an automation Center of Excellence (CoE) to drive digital transformation and improve productivity. By leveraging Microsoft Power Platform, particularly Microsoft Power Automate, the company has successfully scaled solutions across various departments, enhancing business decision-making, stakeholder relationships, and employee efficiency. The CoE aims to integrate automation into daily operations, demonstrating its success through use cases like the FastTrack renewal prep solution and a Surety Commission automation that prevented significant business disruption.

title: Global Data Annotation Tools Market Size, Share Forecast to 2033
summary: The global data annotation tools market was valued at USD 2.87 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 23.82 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 26.50%. Key growth drivers include the rapid penetration of AI in healthcare and massive data generation via advanced technologies, while the inaccuracy of some tools remains a restraint. Asia Pacific is expected to dominate the market, with North America also showing significant growth.

title: Threat Brief: CVE-2022-30190 – MSDT Code Execution Vulnerability
summary: Palo Alto Networks has released a threat brief regarding CVE-2022-30190, also known as Follina, a critical Microsoft Support Diagnostic Tool (MSDT) remote code execution vulnerability. This exploit allows attackers to bypass Office macro policies and execute code with the same privileges as the calling application, even potentially through zero-click methods. Palo Alto Networks details the attack mechanism, which leverages malicious Word documents with remote templates to execute PowerShell commands, and provides guidance for customers to implement protections using their product suite until Microsoft releases a patch.

title: Michal Braverman-Blumenstyk appointed as CTO for Microsoft’s new Security, Compliance
summary: Microsoft has appointed Michal Braverman-Blumenstyk as Chief Technology Officer for its new Security, Compliance, Identity, and Management Division. She will continue to serve as General Manager of Microsoft Israel R&D Center and her appointment is part of a comprehensive company-wide reorganization merging all security products into a single division. Braverman-Blumenstyk has a strong background in cybersecurity and artificial intelligence, having founded the Microsoft Cyber Security Center in Israel and significantly grown the Israel R&D Center's workforce.

title: Bill Gates on the Next 40 Years in Technology
summary: In an interview with PCMag, Bill Gates reflects on the past 40 years of technology since the original IBM PC and offers insights into the future. He discusses the ubiquitous nature of the personal computer, the challenges of climate change and political polarization, and the potential of AI and biological advances. Gates also shares his current technology usage and how it compares to early computing, highlighting the immense progress made.

title: MyDIGITAL and Microsoft commit to innovation, digital skills, and building economic resilience for Malaysia
summary: MyDIGITAL and Microsoft announced the MyDIGITAL GovTech Innovation partnership in Malaysia, focusing on enhancing digital skills for civil servants and strengthening the nation's economic resilience. This initiative builds on existing collaborations, aiming for widespread cloud adoption and digital literacy within the government by 2025. Microsoft reaffirms its commitment to Malaysia's digital transformation, celebrating 30 years in the country with goals to skill 300,000 people by 2024 and an additional 1 million Malaysians by the end of 2023.

title: Microsoft introduces Microsoft Entra to help customers secure access in a connected world
summary: Microsoft has launched a new product family called Microsoft Entra, which consolidates all of its identity and access capabilities. This includes Azure Active Directory, a new Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management (CIEM) solution called Microsoft Entra Permissions Management, and a decentralized identity offering, Microsoft Entra Verified ID. The initiative aims to provide a unified trust fabric for the digital ecosystem, securing access across various cloud environments and enabling privacy-respecting digital interactions.

title: Accenture, Microsoft and Avanade expand partnership to help organizations tackle their greatest sustainability challenges
summary: Accenture, Microsoft, and Avanade have expanded their partnership to help organizations address sustainability challenges, with an initial focus on achieving net zero and future plans for broader ESG issues. They will invest in co-developing carbon-efficient solutions and offer advisory services for reducing emissions, transitioning to new energy sources, and minimizing waste. The collaboration will target digital manufacturing transitions, low carbon energy transitions, sustainable IT with Microsoft Azure, and ESG measurement and analytics using the Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability.

title: A digital conflict between Russia and Ukraine rages on behind the scenes of war
summary: A behind-the-scenes digital conflict is ongoing between Russia and Ukraine, with the U.S. also conducting offensive cyber operations. Microsoft's Head of Customer Security and Trust, Tom Burt, details how the company has observed destructive cyberattacks leading up to and during the invasion, noting similarities between Russian cyber and physical military objectives. This "hybrid war" poses a threat of collateral damage to Western companies, highlighting the critical role of private sector insights and collaboration with governments.

title: Teresa Carlson returns to Microsoft
summary: Teresa Carlson has rejoined Microsoft as a corporate Vice President and executive-in-residence, nearly 12 years after her previous departure. Carlson, a prominent figure in government cloud computing, left Amazon Web Services where she led their public sector growth, and then had a brief stint as chief growth officer at Splunk before her recent move back to Microsoft. Her return marks a significant development in her career trajectory within the technology industry.

title: Nvidia TAO Toolkit update aims to simplify deep learning model development
summary: Nvidia has updated its TAO Toolkit platform to simplify and accelerate deep learning model creation for enterprise applications, making AI development more accessible and less time-consuming. The toolkit now includes new capabilities like "bring your own model weights" and integration with TensorBoard for better visualization, along with the option to deploy as-a-service with REST APIs. While other companies have also worked on simplifying AI pipelines, analysts note that Nvidia's TAO Toolkit, with its low-code nature and pre-trained models, aims to speed up time-to-market for AI models and reduce the need for extensive data science expertise.

title: Gen-trepreneur Z is making its mark on the future of small business
summary: A Microsoft Store report "Small Business State of Mind" highlights how Generation Z is redefining entrepreneurship. Gen Z small business owners prioritize social good, work unconventional hours, and are using platforms like TikTok for business advice, aiming for early retirement rather than traditional corporate paths. The article emphasizes their unique perspective on work, success, and the future of small business, despite challenges like inflation and data security risks.

title: DocuSign and Microsoft Deepen Relationship to Accelerate Anywhere Work
summary: DocuSign and Microsoft have expanded their global strategic partnership to enhance how people collaborate and reach agreements digitally, particularly in the "anywhere economy." This collaboration involves new integrations and features across Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, and Power Platform, aiming to streamline agreement processes, increase efficiency, and support remote work. Both companies will also deepen their use of each other's products to further automate internal workflows.

title: Why Has Microsoft Stock Been Falling; Will It Rebound? (NASDAQ:MSFT)
summary: Microsoft's stock has fallen 25% since its November peak, influenced by macroeconomic headwinds and a recent lowering of its fiscal fourth-quarter EPS and revenue guidance due to foreign exchange rates. Bears highlight these challenges, while bulls focus on the company's cloud business and other growth initiatives as potential rebound drivers. The article, written by Chuck Walston, explores these contrasting views on MSFT's future performance.

title: Microsoft announces four new employee workforce initiatives - Microsoft On the Issues
summary: Microsoft is implementing four new workforce initiatives to enhance employee relationships and workplace culture. These changes include removing non-competition clauses for most U.S. employees, strengthening protections against disclosure of alleged misconduct, increasing pay transparency by publicly disclosing salary ranges, and conducting a civil rights audit of its workforce policies. These initiatives aim to empower employees, foster a safe environment for concerns, and reinforce the company's commitment to diversity and inclusion.

title: Procter & Gamble’s CIO believes passion is contagious. His latest passion: digitally remaking the 185-year-old company
summary: Vittorio Cretella, CIO at Procter & Gamble, is leading a massive digital transformation of the 185-year-old company. P&G has partnered with Microsoft to leverage the Microsoft Cloud, aiming to accelerate its digital manufacturing platform and enrich the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). This collaboration seeks to improve productivity, speed up product delivery, boost customer satisfaction, and reduce costs, while also enhancing environmental sustainability through optimized energy and water consumption.

title: Dow dives 800 points, S&P 500 posts worst week since January after inflation hits 40-year high
summary: U.S. stocks fell sharply on Friday, with the Dow dropping 880 points and the S&P 500 completing its worst week since January, following a higher-than-expected inflation report for May. The consumer price index reached its highest level since 1981, showing an 8.6% year-over-year increase, fueling concerns about a potential recession and prompting fears of a more aggressive Federal Reserve. Tech stocks, banks, and cyclical stocks were particularly impacted as the 2-year Treasury yield surged above 3%.

title: P&G Levels Up Manufacturing With Machine Learning and AI
summary: Procter & Gamble is partnering with Microsoft to implement AI, machine learning, and edge computing across over 100 manufacturing sites globally to improve productivity, reduce costs, and increase consumer satisfaction. This multiyear relationship will leverage Microsoft Azure to digitize and integrate data, enabling real-time, data-informed decisions and innovations in areas such as energy use, downtime reduction, and product quality. The collaboration includes the creation of a Digital Enablement Office to incubate and deploy high-priority business scenarios.

title: Hack Into Quantum Computing Boom With QQQ
summary: Quantum computing, a disruptive technology with significant growth potential, is still in its early stages but offers various use cases. While some speculative companies exist, major players in this field are large-cap companies. The Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) provide investors with exposure to quantum computing through their holdings in established tech giants like Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Nvidia, and Honeywell.

title: Is the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) Right for You?
summary: The Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) offers investors diversified exposure to companies benefiting from the metaverse's growth. This article examines METV's top holdings, including Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Roblox, Microsoft, and Autodesk, highlighting their roles in the metaverse ecosystem. It also discusses the fund's expense ratio and the potential market size of the metaverse industry.

title: Google Agrees to Pay $118 Million to Settle Pay Discrimination Case (Published 2022)
summary: Google has agreed to pay $118 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging systematic underpayment of women. The settlement covers approximately 15,500 women employed by Google in California since September 2013 and also mandates external oversight of its pay practices. Google denied wrongdoing, stating the settlement was in the best interest of all parties involved after five years of litigation.

title: Google Engineer Claims AI System is Sentient; China's Tech Giants Flourish as Crackdown Eases
summary: A Google engineer has been suspended after claiming that the company's LaMDA chatbot system shows signs of sentience, which Google denies, citing confidentiality breaches. Meanwhile, China's major tech companies like Baidu, Tencent, and Alibaba are experiencing a surge in share value as Beijing eases its regulatory crackdown on the sector. Twitter has also introduced a new "people-first" content moderation process to improve reporting of offensive content, especially amid Elon Musk's previous concerns about fake accounts.

title: Oracle women stumble in pay bias suit while Google cuts a deal
summary: Women at Oracle Corp. face a setback in their pay disparity lawsuit as a judge tentatively revoked their class-action status, making it more difficult to proceed. Meanwhile, Google agreed to pay $118 million to settle a similar lawsuit alleging pay disparities for more than 15,000 female workers. The Google settlement includes provisions for independent oversight of hiring practices and pay equity studies.

title: How Jabil’s New HQ Facilitates Collaboration Between Worldwide Teams
summary: Jabil, a global technology solutions company with 260,000 employees worldwide, opened a new 175,000-square-foot headquarters in St. Petersburg, Florida, designed to foster collaboration. The facility, equipped with state-of-the-art Crestron technology, allows seamless communication between local and international teams. Clearwater, Fla.-based APG Technology was the audiovisual integrator for the project, ensuring a uniform and user-friendly experience across various collaboration spaces.

title: P&G taps Microsoft for cloud services to boost production efficiency
summary: Procter & Gamble has partnered with Microsoft to utilize its Azure cloud computing services and predictive analytics to enhance manufacturing efficiency, reduce costs, and improve product delivery. This collaboration aims to leverage digital manufacturing visibility and advanced analytics to optimize production processes for various P&G products globally. The initiative builds on four years of prior cooperation, focusing on better data utilization, automation, digital twins, and sustainability.

title: National Grid's CIDO reimagines operating models, IT strategy
summary: Adriana Karaboutis, National Grid's group chief information and digital officer (CIDO), discusses her approach to digitizing the company. She outlines a strategy that balances legacy IT with emerging technologies like AI, machine learning, and cloud computing, emphasizing a product operating model and agile methodologies. Karaboutis also highlights the CIDO's role in measuring and supporting sustainability initiatives and navigating the challenges of existing legacy infrastructure.

title: Procter & Gamble Partners With Microsoft To Transform And Accelerate Digital Manufacturing
summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) has partnered with Microsoft to advance digital manufacturing through cloud, AI, and digital transformation technologies. This multiyear collaboration aims to make manufacturing smarter by utilizing Microsoft Cloud, Azure data, and AI solutions to optimize P&G's manufacturing and packaging processes, initially focusing on diaper and paper towel production. The initiative seeks to improve predictive quality, reduce downtime, enhance supply chain efficiency, and explore the concept of an "industrial metaverse" for simulating manufacturing environments.

title: Microsoft Targets Salesforce Clients With New Tools to Aid Sales Reps
summary: Microsoft has introduced Viva Sales, a new program designed to integrate its Office and Teams applications with customer-relationship management (CRM) software, including its own and rival platforms like Salesforce. This initiative aims to attract Salesforce clients by allowing data synchronization between CRM systems and Microsoft's productivity suite. Viva Sales also incorporates AI tools to analyze sales calls, track customer sentiment, and provide actionable feedback to sales representatives.

title: ServiceNow Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: ServiceNow Inc. (NOW) stock fell 8.13% on Thursday, closing at $428.08, significantly underperforming the broader market which also saw declines. This drop ended a two-day winning streak for the company. ServiceNow's stock also underperformed compared to competitors like Microsoft, Qualcomm, and IBM, and closed $279.52 below its 52-week high.

title: Broadcom's acquire-and-axe history concerns VMware users
summary: VMware users and employees are expressing concern over Broadcom's acquisition of the company, fearing a repeat of Broadcom's historical strategy of acquiring and then divesting parts of companies. Broadcom's CEO, Hock Tan, intends to transition VMware to a subscription-based model and integrate Broadcom's infrastructure and security software. The acquisition timing also coincides with a trend where corporate IT shops are moving towards SaaS-based solutions, diminishing VMware's traditional market.

title: Is Juneteenth being observed by companies in the Dow industrials? Here's how some are handling it.
summary: This article explores how companies in the Dow industrials are observing Juneteenth, a federal holiday. While many Dow components are closed, some are open, and others offer employees the choice to take it as a paid holiday. The article also notes the relatively quick adoption of Juneteenth as a day off for exchanges compared to Martin Luther King, Jr. Day.

title: Why ESG is Becoming a Bigger Deal for Investors
summary: ESG (environmental, social, and governance) investing is rapidly gaining traction, with a record $649 billion poured into ESG-focused funds in 2021. Major players like BlackRock are emphasizing ESG, believing that companies deliberating on their societal role perform better, leading to long-term value for shareholders. While greenwashing concerns exist, new global standards and regulatory clarifications are emerging as ESG becomes a mainstream method for evaluating businesses and attracting investment.

title: The Hong Kong Management Association and Microsoft Hong Kong Showcase Technology Excellence, Innovation and Resilience – Microsoft News Center Hong Kong
summary: The Hong Kong Management Association (HKMA) and Microsoft Hong Kong (MSHK) recognized 14 Hong Kong companies for their innovative use of technology in the inaugural HKMA x MSHK Excellence in Digital Transformation. These companies demonstrated successful integration of various technologies, including cloud, big data, and AI, across five categories: customer engagement, employee empowerment, product transformation, operations optimization, and all-rounded innovation. The initiative highlights Hong Kong businesses' agility in leveraging technology to navigate challenges, seize opportunities, and enhance operational models.

title: Microsoft Office 365 has ability to ‘spy’ on workers
summary: Research has revealed that Microsoft Office 365 contains tools that allow employers to monitor staff emails and computer usage, raising privacy concerns. Despite Microsoft's 2020 changes to protect employee privacy by aggregating data, UCL research found that "content search" and "audit" tools still enable detailed individual surveillance. This has led to calls for Microsoft to increase transparency and notify employees when such monitoring tools are enabled.

title: YPF explora la Computación Cuántica para el desarrollo de la transición energética junto a Microsoft
summary: YPF is partnering with Microsoft to explore the use of quantum computing for developing energy transition sources and retraining its technical teams. The project aims to accelerate the adoption of renewable energy and reinforce YPF's commitment to sustainability. It began with a training program for YPF's Digital Technologies teams, led by Microsoft experts, to identify potential pilot applications in 2022.

title: MinIO extends Snowflake analysis to any data location
summary: MinIO has partnered with Snowflake to enable Snowflake analytics on data stored in any location, not just within Snowflake's own environment. This partnership allows customers to leverage external table support in MinIO for cost-effective analysis of large datasets without moving them to the cloud. This provides flexibility similar to recent Snowflake partnerships with Dell and Pure Storage, offering various options for on-premises or cloud-based data analysis.

title: Cloudflare recognized by Microsoft as a Security Software Innovator
summary: Cloudflare announced it has won the Security Software Innovator award at the 2022 Microsoft Security Excellence Awards. This recognition highlights Cloudflare's innovative approach to Zero Trust and security solutions through deep integrations with Microsoft, helping joint customers secure their web applications and employees. The article emphasizes the importance of Zero Trust in the modern threat landscape, detailing Cloudflare One's comprehensive capabilities.

title: Microsoft Viva Sales takes on Salesforce-Slack tandem
summary: Microsoft is set to launch Viva Sales, an AI-powered automation tool designed to integrate sales data from Microsoft 365 and Teams directly into CRM systems, challenging Salesforce's offerings. Viva Sales aims to streamline sales processes by reducing manual data entry and app-switching, particularly for remote workers. This move intensifies the rivalry between Microsoft and Salesforce, with both companies focusing on enhancing employee experience through AI and automation in sales.

title: BWT Alpine F1 Team turns big data into faster track times with data science platform built on Azure and Azure NetApp Files
summary: BWT Alpine F1 Team implemented a new cloud-hosted data science platform using Microsoft Azure and Azure NetApp Files to enhance race performance. The platform integrates racing, testing, and manufacturing data, emphasizing "fast data" to enable quick decision-making for engineers. This move has allowed Alpine to rapidly scale resources, manage costs effectively, and improve productivity.

title: Roe v. Wade overturned: Here are companies paying for abortion travel costs for employees
summary: Following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, numerous major U.S. companies announced expanded support and financial assistance for employees seeking abortions in states with new restrictions. This support often includes covering travel expenses for medical care unavailable in their home states. Corporations are responding to pressure from investors, customers, and employees, and are concerned about the impact on talent attraction and retention.

title: Disney, other U.S. companies offer abortion travel benefit after Roe decision
summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, numerous U.S. companies, including Walt Disney Co. and Meta Platforms Inc., announced they would cover travel expenses for employees seeking abortion services. This move comes as many states are expected to restrict or ban abortions, making it necessary for employees to travel to states where the procedure remains legal. Companies like Dick's Sporting Goods, Lyft, Yelp, Microsoft, Tesla, Apple, Alaska Air Group, Johnson & Johnson, OkCupid, Bumble, Netflix, and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are also offering similar benefits or legal protections.

title: Five9 Integration with Microsoft Teams Review
summary: This article reviews the integration of Five9 with Microsoft Teams, highlighting how it enhances customer service by connecting agents with subject matter experts in real-time. The integration offers features like agent-expert consultation and telephony connect, aiming to bridge communication gaps and improve customer experience. It details benefits such as full organizational collaboration, boosted CX, simplified directories, toll-free call transfers, and an improved employee experience.

title: HoloLens 2 helps Novo Nordisk employees see work in new ways
summary: Novo Nordisk is implementing Microsoft HoloLens 2 and related Dynamics 365 technologies to transform its manufacturing operations and employee training. This includes using augmented instructions for complex procedures and facilitating virtual site visits, significantly improving efficiency, onboarding, and reducing travel needs across its global facilities. The technology has been well-received by employees, leading to improved performance consistency and a more flexible workplace.

title: KPMG leads Digital Future of Work hackathon
summary: KPMG, in collaboration with Microsoft, BP plc, and the University of Cambridge Think Lab, hosted a two-day "Digital Future of Work" hackathon in London. The event focused on developing digital solutions for modern business challenges, including talent retention and hybrid work, aligning with the UN's Sustainable Development Goal of "Decent work and economic growth." The winning solution, "The Good Job Tool," helps design roles that meet candidate expectations and market demands, receiving investment and support from KPMG.

title: FabricScape: Escaping Service Fabric and Taking Over the Cluster
summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 researchers identified FabricScape (CVE-2022-30137), an important vulnerability in Microsoft's Service Fabric that allows Linux containers to escalate privileges and gain root access, potentially compromising an entire cluster. This vulnerability affects Azure Service Fabric and other Microsoft products using it, though certain managed multi-tenant services have runtime access disabled, preventing exploitation. Microsoft has patched the issue, and users not on automatic updates are advised to upgrade their Linux clusters.

title: Announcing the 2022 Microsoft US Award Winners
summary: Microsoft has announced the winners of its 2022 US Partner Awards, which complement the global Microsoft Partner of the Year program by highlighting US-specific partner impact.
