The awards recognize partners across various categories including Azure AI, Business Applications, Business Excellence, Modern Work & Security, Social Impact, and Industry-specific solutions. The announcement encourages partners to register for the upcoming Microsoft Inspire virtual event on July 19-20, 2022.

title: Watch: When Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer laughed at iPhone
summary: This article revisits a famous incident where then-Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer publicly dismissed the original Apple iPhone upon its launch. He criticized its high price and lack of a physical keyboard, believing it wouldn't appeal to business users. Years later, Ballmer admitted his mistake, acknowledging Apple's innovative business model and regretting Microsoft's slow entry into the hardware business for mobile devices.

title: BP, Shell To Pay Employee Travel Costs After Abortion Ruling
summary: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's reversal of Roe v. Wade, BP Plc and Shell Plc announced they would cover travel expenses for U.S. employees needing to access health services not available near their homes. This makes them the first major oil companies to implement such policies. Both companies maintain significant operations in Texas, a state expected to ban or severely restrict abortions, joining other corporate giants in expanding employee health benefits.

title: DevOps 2030: People, Practices, And Platforms
summary: DevOps has proven its value in software-intensive organizations by reducing time-to-market, increasing agility, and enhancing resilience. Looking ahead to 2030, the evolution of DevOps will focus on three key areas: people, practices, and platforms. Success will involve fine-tuning team integration, automating processes to free up human attention, and consolidating and expanding DevOps platforms to support new technologies like low/no-code apps and MLOps.

title: Will Zoom Stock Hit $1,500 a Share by 2026?
summary: This article examines Ark Invest's bold prediction that Zoom (ZM) stock could reach $1,500 per share by 2026. While Ark Invest's model, driven by factors like increased remote work, expanding unified communications market share, and revenue growth from new AI-driven products, suggests this possibility, the article highlights the significant discrepancy between Ark's projected 76% annual growth and Zoom's actual present growth rate of around 10-12%. Nanalyze questions the sustainability of such aggressive growth projections and advises caution for investors.

title: Up in the Cloud!
summary: This article by Uma Iyer discusses the evolving concept of cloud computing, moving beyond its abstract perception to its tangible impact on technology and data management. It explores the differences between cloud storage and cloud computing, highlights real-world applications and the benefits of scalability and ease of use, and addresses the critical issue of trust and security in cloud technologies through various models and programs like CSA STAR. The article concludes by looking at emerging trends like edge computing, indicating a bright future for cloud technology.

title: Enterprise AI and Machine Learning for Managers​
summary: This article introduces an eBook titled "Enterprise AI and Machine Learning for Managers," designed to provide frontline managers with a practical understanding of AI/ML. The book aims to equip managers with the knowledge to identify high-impact AI use cases, collaborate effectively with data scientists, and deploy AI/ML into production to gain a competitive advantage. Written by former McKinsey consultant and professor Nikhil Krishnan, the guide focuses on helping managers understand AI science, measure success, and build strong data science teams.

title: 8x8 Deploys XCaaS to 1,500 Hounslow Borough Council Employees
summary: 8x8 has deployed its XCaaS platform to 1,500 employees at Hounslow Borough Council, integrating Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS) and Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) with Microsoft Teams. This deployment aims to simplify communications, enhance efficiency for staff across various departments, and improve interactions with the borough's 270,000 residents. The initiative further solidifies 8x8's presence in the UK public sector, with XCaaS now used in over one-third of London's boroughs.

title: When Pentest Tools Go Brutal: Red-Teaming Tool Being Abused by Malicious Actors
summary: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has identified that Brute Ratel C4 (BRc4), a red-teaming and adversarial attack simulation tool designed to evade EDR and AV detection, is being actively abused by malicious actors, including techniques consistent with APT29. The tool's effectiveness was highlighted by a sample that received benign verdicts from all 56 VirusTotal vendors despite containing a malicious payload. The report details the technical analysis of how BRc4 is deployed, including DLL search order hijacking and process injection, and provides indicators of compromise to help organizations detect and protect against this evolving threat.

title: What's the Impact of the Strong Dollar on My Portfolio?
summary: The strong U.S. dollar, experiencing its biggest rally in 20 years, negatively impacts non-U.S. fund returns and profitability for American companies operating overseas. While it offers advantages for consumers traveling abroad, investors in international stocks and bonds face challenges due to unfavorable exchange rates and increased repayment costs for dollar-denominated debt. Despite the difficulties, some Morningstar analysts suggest currency hedging can minimize volatility, and others view the current currency weakness as an attractive buying opportunity for international funds.

title: N-able Acquires Spinpanel to Accelerate Cloud Strategy
summary: N-able, Inc. (NYSE: NABL) has acquired Spinpanel, a multi-tenant Microsoft 365 management and automation platform. This acquisition aims to help N-able partners address the challenges of labor scarcity and the shift to cloud services, particularly in managing Microsoft Cloud offerings. Spinpanel's technology will be integrated into N-able's product portfolio during the third quarter, providing partners and customers with enhanced tools for optimizing their Microsoft Cloud products and accessing a broader range of IT management and security solutions.

title: Amazon and Microsoft deny using Flickr pics for facial recognition, as suits test limits of privacy law
summary: Amazon and Microsoft are facing lawsuits alleging they violated the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act by using Flickr photos for facial recognition development without consent. Both companies deny using the IBM "Diversity in Faces" database for their AI algorithms after initial downloads. The cases highlight broader issues concerning the lack of a national privacy law, the trade-offs between privacy and AI accuracy, and jurisdictional complexities in the cloud era.

title: FMET Makes It Simple to Take Long View of Metaverse
summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) offers investors a way to engage with the growing metaverse theme, which is projected to be worth over $800 million by 2028. Despite a retrenchment in growth stocks, the long-term outlook for the metaverse remains compelling, especially with established companies like Meta Platforms, Alphabet, Apple, and Nvidia investing heavily in its development. FMET provides a diversified basket approach to this theme, including exposure to gaming and numerous mega-cap growth companies, with an affordable annual fee.

title: Fujitsu acquires ServiceNow partner Enable
summary: Fujitsu Australia has acquired Melbourne-based ServiceNow partner Enable Professional Services, marking its third acquisition in 15 months. This deal will grant Fujitsu access to Enable's ServiceNow advisory, consulting, and delivery services, along with its employees across multiple Asian countries. The acquisition aims to bolster Fujitsu's business applications focus under its new global Uvance brand, with Enable operating as a stand-alone Fujitsu company.

title: Gurus foresee 'virtual humans' enhancing many industries
summary: Experts predict that virtual humans, fueled by AI advancements, will revolutionize various industries such as retail, finance, and education. Production costs and development times for digital avatars are expected to decrease significantly, leading to widespread adoption. Companies like Baidu are already investing heavily in this technology, with the market in China projected to reach 10.24 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) by 2026.

title: In a Tough Quarter, Sustainable Strategies Struggled But Stayed Competitive
summary: In the second quarter, sustainable investing strategies faced headwinds as technology stocks, prominent in many ESG portfolios, declined, and energy stocks, often excluded, performed strongly. Despite this, many sustainable equity strategies, particularly those leveraging healthcare and consumer staples, managed to stay competitive and, in some cases, even outperformed the broader market. Over the long term, ESG-focused strategies continue to show strong performance relative to the market, indicating resilience despite short-term market shifts.

title: NetApp Ontap to be sole vSAN storage alternative in the cloud
summary: NetApp's Ontap-based cloud storage services will become the exclusive vSAN storage alternative officially supported by VMware's vSphere instances in the cloud. This partnership allows enterprise customers to extend VMware data stores in AWS, Azure, and Google clouds using Ontap as an external NFS data store, offering a more cost-effective solution than traditional vSAN. The move aims to provide a familiar and integrated storage experience for VMware customers migrating to the cloud.

title: Walmart Is Weaning Itself Off of Big Tech
summary: Walmart is developing its own cloud infrastructure to reduce its reliance on major tech companies like Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud. This strategic move aims to bring its data management and operational technology in-house. The article highlights Walmart's efforts to create its own internal cloud services for its extensive operations.

title: WaterNSW lays the foundation for HR automation using robust technology
summary: WaterNSW, a state-owned corporation, has implemented Microsoft Dynamics 365 Human Resources as a core component of its digital transformation, aiming to automate HR processes and enhance employee experience. This initiative, part of a three-horizon strategy, focuses on simplifying HR operations, improving data management, and providing self-service capabilities for its 1,000 employees. The transition to an agile delivery model allowed for rapid development and optimization of the HR case management platform, leading to better reporting and cost efficiencies.

title: Netflix names Microsoft as partner for ad-supported subscription plan
summary: Netflix has selected Microsoft as its technology and sales partner for its upcoming ad-supported subscription plan. This move comes as Netflix seeks to boost subscriber growth after experiencing losses, aligning with other streaming services that offer ad-supported tiers. Microsoft's strong advertising revenue and recent acquisition of Xandr Inc. make it a suitable partner, with Netflix's Chief Operating Officer emphasizing increased consumer choice and a premium advertiser experience.

title: Netflix Partners With Microsoft for New Advertising-Backed Option
summary: Netflix has selected Microsoft to be its technology and sales partner for its new lower-cost, advertising-backed subscription plan. This partnership aims to boost Netflix's revenue and subscription growth after the company reported its first subscriber loss in over a decade in the March quarter. The move also significantly benefits Microsoft's expanding advertising business.

title: Microsoft Inspire 2022: Empowering partners to drive transformation in every industry
summary: Microsoft Inspire 2022 is focused on empowering partners to drive digital transformation across various industries by leveraging the Microsoft Cloud. The company is offering four key value propositions—Build, Enrich, Unlock, and Scale—to help partners develop innovative solutions and address customer needs. The event features key speakers and sessions covering industry clouds, sustainability, and opportunities in healthcare, financial services, retail, manufacturing, and other sectors.

title: Tech Moves: Vimeo hires Seattle execs; Microsoft corporate VP retires; and more
summary: Vimeo has appointed Lynn Girotto as chief marketing officer and Ashraf Alkarmi as chief product officer, both based in the Seattle area. Microsoft corporate vice president Sam George is retiring after 25 years with the company. The article also details new hires at Absci, Amazon, SeekOut, RealNetworks, and TEAL, covering key personnel changes in the Pacific Northwest tech community.

title: Netflix, Microsoft And The Rapidly Blurring Lines Between Media And Tech
summary: The article discusses the increasing convergence of media and technology industries, highlighted by Netflix's partnership with Microsoft for its ad-supported tier and Disney's ad tech collaboration with The Trade Desk. It also points to Apple TV+'s exclusive streaming deal for Major League Soccer as another example of tech companies making significant moves into content. The author uses the metaphor of Certs mints to illustrate how Netflix and Microsoft are becoming "two industries in one," emphasizing the blurring lines between these sectors.

title: +25년간 쉼없이 배당준 배당귀족주 ETF ; NOBL ETF 투자에 대해 공부해보기
summary: This article discusses investing in the NOBL ETF, which focuses on Dividend Aristocrats—S&P 500 companies that have consistently increased dividends for at least 25 years. It explains the NOBL ETF's characteristics, including its investment strategy, current portfolio of 64 companies, and expense ratio, while noting its exclusion of major tech stocks like Apple and Microsoft. The author suggests it as a "peace-of-mind" investment during volatile market conditions.

title: Spectralink DECT handsets Microsoft Teams now supported with Teams SIP Gateway
summary: Spectralink DECT handsets are now officially certified and supported for Microsoft Teams via the Teams SIP Gateway, making Spectralink the first vendor to achieve this. This development allows organizations, particularly those in retail and manufacturing, to integrate DECT solutions directly with Teams Phone, removing the need for traditional PBXs or SBCs. The article details the architecture, benefits, and how to acquire Spectralink DECT solutions for Teams, highlighting their rugged design and specialized features suitable for various work environments.

title: Reddit Turns One Down Under
summary: Reddit is celebrating its one-year anniversary of expanding into Australia, which is now its fourth-largest user base. To commemorate this milestone, Reddit is launching its inaugural Australian consumer brand campaign, "Find your people," highlighting its diverse communities and increasing engagement with Australian users and brands. The platform has seen significant growth in local teams and user activity across various communities, including a successful partnership with the Australian Football League and effective ad campaigns for brands like Up.

title: Microsoft training AI in drones
summary: Microsoft is developing Project AirSim, a flight simulator for autonomous aircraft to train AI systems for drones in a virtual environment. This platform allows companies to test drone software in risky scenarios and adverse conditions, significantly reducing development time. The project, running on Azure, aims to provide a safe space for companies like Airtonomy to develop and test crucial flight routines for infrastructure inspection, and eventually offer a free option and pre-trained AI "building blocks."

title: Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty: The most flexible and comprehensive solution for digital sovereignty
summary: Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty is a new solution enabling public sector customers to use Microsoft Cloud while meeting compliance, security, and policy needs, including data residency and sovereign controls. It provides increased control over data and transparency into cloud operations and governance processes. The solution is partner-led and focuses on working with in-country partners to deliver customized cloud solutions that address unique government requirements.

title: Oracle and Microsoft announce availability of Oracle Database Service for Microsoft Azure
summary: Oracle and Microsoft have announced the general availability of Oracle Database Service for Microsoft Azure, enabling Azure customers to easily provision, access, and monitor Oracle Database services on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This collaboration extends their 2019 partnership on Oracle Interconnect for Microsoft Azure, simplifying multicloud operations for joint customers. The service bears no charges for usage, data egress/ingress between OCI and Azure, with customers only paying for consumed Azure or Oracle services.

title: Oracle and Microsoft Agree to Deepen Interoperability of Cloud Platforms
summary: Oracle Corp. and Microsoft Corp. have announced a deeper interoperability between their cloud platforms, enabling customers to more easily run projects across both services. This agreement reflects an evolving market where customers utilize multiple clouds and resist being locked into a single provider. The move acknowledges the multicloud environment, with about two-thirds of enterprise-level companies already using multiple cloud solutions.

title: Microsoft Teams back up for most users after global outage
summary: Microsoft Teams, used by over 270 million people globally, experienced an hours-long outage that disrupted services for tens of thousands of users worldwide. Microsoft attributed the disruption to a broken connection in a recent software update and confirmed that the service was largely restored. The company is actively monitoring for any lasting impacts and ensuring full recovery of all functions.

title: Microsoft Teams back up for most users after global outage
summary: Microsoft Teams has largely recovered from a global outage that affected tens of thousands of users. The disruption was attributed to a recent software update containing a broken connection to an internal storage service. The company is monitoring for any residual impact and ensuring all functions are fully restored.

title: Microsoft Opposes Summary Judgment for IRS in FOIA Suit
summary: Microsoft has filed an opposition to the IRS's motion for summary judgment in a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit, arguing that the IRS's search for responsive documents was inadequate. Microsoft asserts that the IRS failed to search for relevant records created by its contractors, Boies Schiller Flexner LLP and Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, LLP, and that the IRS's justifications for withholding documents are legally insufficient. The company contends that its contracts with these firms define contractor-generated work as IRS agency records and that the IRS exercised extensive control over these records.

title: Oracle adds new chapter for multicloud story through cloud database service with Microsoft
summary: Oracle and Microsoft have announced the availability of Oracle Database Service for Microsoft Azure, allowing Azure customers streamlined access to Oracle Database Services on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This partnership aims to simplify multicloud solutions for their shared enterprise customer base, building on a previous interoperability alliance from 2019. The new service enables Azure users to provision and monitor Oracle Database Services in OCI and build applications on Azure that connect to Oracle services such as Autonomous Database.

title: Enter Microsoft Viva Engage. A Real Social Network For The Enterprise?
summary: Microsoft has launched Viva Engage, a new application within Microsoft Viva, which is described as an "Instagram for the enterprise." This platform aims to provide a dedicated space for personal connections, employee belonging, and social interactions at work, separate from work-oriented collaboration tools like Slack or Microsoft Teams. The author argues that such platforms, which allow employees to share personal interests and engage in non-work-related discussions, can significantly benefit companies by fostering a stronger sense of belonging and making work more engaging.

title: Best Flash Drives: Kingston DataTraveler Max & Sandisk Extreme Pro
summary: This article reviews and compares eight USB 3.2 flash drives from top brands like Kingston, Sandisk, Lexar, Samsung, and Corsair, focusing on performance, design, and build quality. The Kingston DataTraveler Max is highlighted as the best overall for its speed and value, while alternatives like the Sandisk Extreme Pro and Lexar JumpDrive M900 are recommended for specific needs. The article also provides detailed benchmark analysis for movie and photo transfer speeds, as well as insights into throttling issues and physical durability.

title: CDHU Enhances Citizen Services by Leveraging the Cloud
summary: The Housing and Urban Development Company (CDHU) of São Paulo has adopted Microsoft's cloud technologies, including Dynamics 365, Power Apps, and Microsoft 365, to centralize its CRM processes and enhance citizen services. This digital transformation has created a robust customer service channel, improved data management, streamlined housing financing registrations, and enabled field teams to conduct diagnoses more efficiently. The initiative has significantly boosted agility, reduced manual work, and standardized services for the population.

title: What’s New in NVIDIA AI Enterprise 2.1 | NVIDIA Technical Blog
summary: NVIDIA AI Enterprise 2.1 is now generally available, offering an optimized and supported software suite for deploying and scaling AI applications across various environments. Key updates include advanced data science capabilities with NVIDIA RAPIDS 22.04, low-code AI model development using NVIDIA TAO Toolkit 22.05 with new features like REST APIs and pre-trained models, and expanded public cloud support for Red Hat OpenShift and Microsoft Azure NVads A10 v5 series. These enhancements enable affordable GPU sharing, optimized performance for deep learning inferencing, and flexibility for enterprises in managing AI infrastructure.

title: Tencent tops Apple, Sony to take gaming market crown
summary: Tencent Holdings has overtaken industry giants like Sony and Apple to become the leading company in the gaming market, primarily through over 180 strategic, smaller investments worldwide. Despite regulatory challenges in China and increased competition from large acquisitions by Microsoft and Sony, Tencent is intensifying its focus on overseas markets, planning a global release for its top game "Honor of Kings," as its value-added services segment remains highly profitable. The Chinese tech giant's diverse global portfolio has allowed it to achieve significant revenue figures even as it navigates a changing domestic and international landscape.

title: Lumen advances 5G technology with a simplified, holistic cybersecurity mindset
summary: Lumen embraced a simplified, holistic cybersecurity mindset to advance its 5G technology, partnering with Microsoft to address identity and compliance challenges during its cloud migration and system modernization. By implementing Azure Active Directory and other Microsoft Security solutions, Lumen has enhanced data protection, streamlined operations, and achieved greater transparency and automation in managing data access. This strategic move allows Lumen to focus on innovation while ensuring robust security and compliance for its cutting-edge 5G infrastructure.

title: Threat Brief: Microsoft Critical Vulnerabilities (CVE-2022-26809, CVE-2022-26923, CVE-2022-26925)
summary: This threat brief details three critical Microsoft vulnerabilities: CVE-2022-26809 (RPC Remote Code Execution), CVE-2022-26923 (Active Directory Privilege Escalation), and CVE-2022-26925 (Windows LSA Spoofing). Microsoft released patches in April and May 2022, and organizations are strongly advised to apply these updates as CVE-2022-26925 has been exploited in the wild. Palo Alto Networks customers receive protection through their Next-Generation Firewall and Cortex XDR.

title: How a Top T. Rowe Price Manager Is Positioned for a Continued Bear Market
summary: Justin White, manager of the T. Rowe Price All-Cap Opportunities Fund, is preparing his portfolio for a prolonged bear market by focusing on inflation- and recession-resistant stocks like Visa, Chubb, Veeva Systems, and Moderna. While he believes much of the market pain has already occurred, he anticipates further challenges, particularly given uncertainties surrounding a potential recession and the Federal Reserve's battle against inflation. White emphasizes individual stock selection over broad sector trends and has adjusted his portfolio by trimming growth stocks and increasing positions in more resilient companies.

title: Multi Echelon Inventory Optimization Market Analysis by Size,
summary: This report provides a detailed analysis of the global Multi Echelon Inventory Optimization (MEIO) market, covering its size, demand, future trends, challenges, and growth opportunities with forecasts up to 2030. It includes competitive analysis of key players like ToolsGroup and Manhattan Associates, segmentation by type (Software, Services) and application (Consumer Goods, Chemical, Retail, Manufacturing), and regional market breakdown. The study aims to offer insights into market dynamics, segmentation, and strategies for businesses and investors.

title: Lessons from Target’s cloud journey, a decade in the making
summary: Target embarked on its cloud journey a decade ago, moving beyond a simple "lift and shift" to a hybrid multicloud architecture supported by Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure. The retailer modernized its application stack with a microservices approach and open-source tools, overcoming initial cost challenges through significant investment in engineering and FinOps. Target plans to continue its hybrid strategy, leveraging public cloud for scalability and cost arbitrage, and private cloud for data-intensive and core business applications.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Robotic Process Automation
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Robotic Process Automation for the second consecutive year. This achievement underscores the significant momentum and expansion of their RPA offerings since 2019, including the integration of Power Automate for desktop into Windows and new features like hosted RPA bots and pay-as-you-go options. The company acknowledges the crucial contributions of its customers, community, and partners to the success of Power Automate.

title: PLDT activates trans-Pacific cable system seen to boost Philippine int’l data capacity
summary: PLDT Inc. has activated the Jupiter Cable System, a trans-Pacific cable network that will significantly boost the Philippines' international data capacity. This $136 million investment connects the Philippines to Japan and Los Angeles, tripling PLDT's international capacity to about 60 terabits per second. The system is expected to enhance internet speed and reliability for Filipinos, supporting the nation's digital economy and data-driven industries.

title: Microsoft Links Raspberry Robin USB Worm to Russian Evil Corp Hackers
summary: Microsoft has identified a potential connection between the Raspberry Robin USB-based worm and the Russian cybercrime group Evil Corp. The tech giant observed FakeUpdates malware delivered via Raspberry Robin infections, which subsequently led to actions resembling pre-ransomware behavior associated with Evil Corp (DEV-0243). While this marks the first evidence of post-exploitation activity for Raspberry Robin, the exact relationships between the involved threat actors remain unclear, according to experts.

title: How Novartis Is Finding The Path To Be A Digital Winner
summary: Novartis, a major pharmaceutical company, has faced challenges in its digital transformation despite significant investments. Initially, its efforts failed due to compartmentalized teams and a lack of business-wide integration of digital tools. However, during the Covid pandemic, pilot projects fostering collaboration between data scientists and business units, along with training front-line employees in data use, accelerated its digital progress, moving it towards becoming a digitally-enabled firm with a comprehensive AI application platform.

title: Webull Launches Expansion into Singapore
summary: Webull Corporation has officially launched its expansion into Singapore and the Asia-Pacific region. Through Webull Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd., residents can now trade U.S. and Hong Kong-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares with low commissions. This move follows Webull's success in the U.S. and Hong Kong, aiming to capture the growing mobile-first brokerage market in Asia-Pacific.

title: The Neocolonial Game Behind Musk-Bolsonaro Rendezvous
summary: This article criticizes Elon Musk's meeting with Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, framing it as a neocolonial act driven by resource acquisition and political manipulation. It highlights concerns about Musk's Starlink project in the Amazon, linking it to Brazil's nickel and lithium reserves, and questions the integrity of Bolsonaro's government in its dealings with foreign interests given its past dismantling of environmental monitoring bodies. The author suggests the collaboration serves to undermine Brazil's democratic processes and exploit its natural wealth for corporate gain.

title: How Capital One improves visibility into Snowflake costs
summary: Capital One has launched Capital One Software, a new business selling its internal cloud cost management and governance tools, with its first product, Slingshot, targeting the Snowflake platform. This move allows Capital One to monetize its extensive cloud migration work and helps other enterprises manage Snowflake costs and automate governance, while also showcasing Snowflake's capabilities for highly regulated industries. Slingshot has already helped Capital One reduce Snowflake costs by 27%, improve cost per query by 45%, and save over 50,000 hours of work.

title: Microsoft shares attack surface management solutions | Microsoft Security Blog
summary: Microsoft has launched two new security products, Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender External Attack Surface Management, to help organizations combat increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. These solutions provide deeper context into threat actor activities, enable organizations to map their digital environments from an attacker's perspective, and identify unknown or unmanaged internet-facing resources. Additionally, Microsoft Sentinel now offers enhanced detection and response capabilities for SAP environments.

title: Join our Microsoft Technologies Discipline
summary: EPAM is actively recruiting for its Microsoft Technologies Discipline, seeking over 3,500 .NET engineers, solution architects, and managers across more than 55 countries. The company highlights opportunities to work on innovative SaaS/PaaS cloud solutions, build CI/CD pipelines, and develop cloud-native applications for world-class clients, emphasizing professional growth and a strong AI community. EPAM showcases several projects, including those in oil and gas, IoT, life sciences research, and finance, applying technologies like .NET, Azure, Kubernetes, and GenAI.

title: Microstrategy Outperforms Every Asset Class and Big Tech Stock Since Adopting Bitcoin Strategy, Says CEO
summary: Microstrategy CEO Michael Saylor highlights that the company's bitcoin strategy has led to significant outperformance against all asset classes and major tech stocks since its adoption. Saylor attributes this success to bitcoin's digital property characteristics and its appeal as a safe haven asset. The company's unique approach involved acquiring substantial bitcoin holdings and integrating it into their corporate strategy.

title: A New Way to Connect Microsoft Teams with the Contact Centre
summary: This article discusses the growing need to integrate contact center capabilities with Microsoft Teams, especially for businesses seeking a unified platform for communications and customer service. It highlights the Voca Conversational Interaction Center by AudioCodes as a solution that offers "contact center lite" features and conversational AI, leveraging Microsoft's ecosystem. The piece also details three models for Teams contact center integrations (Connect, Extend, Power), with AudioCodes' Voca adopting the advanced "Power" model to enhance agent and customer experiences.

title: Press Release Details
summary: Bridgestone announced an expanded collaboration with Microsoft to accelerate its digital transformation and enhance its connected tires and mobility solutions globally. The partnership will leverage Microsoft Azure to integrate advanced tire analytics, benefiting global fleet and OEM customers by optimizing tire maintenance and retreading. This extended collaboration supports Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, specifically focusing on "Efficiency," "Extension," and "Economy" values through technical innovation in AI and machine learning.

title: Oracle lays off hundreds of employees
summary: Oracle has laid off hundreds of employees, primarily from its advertising and customer experience group, to prioritize its healthcare IT services and cloud businesses. This move comes after its recent $28.3 billion acquisition of Cerner Corp. and reflects a broader trend of tech companies making staffing adjustments amid an economic slowdown, shifting focus to high-priority areas like cloud infrastructure.

title: 7 Apps for a more productive ‘work from home’ experience
summary: This article reviews seven applications designed to enhance productivity for remote workers, moving beyond common tools like Zoom and Slack. It highlights apps such as Basecamp for project management, Freedom for distraction blocking, Krisp for noise cancellation, Evernote for digital note-taking, Simplish for to-do lists, Dashlane for password management, and Zapier for automating repetitive tasks. The author, Adarsh, provides a brief overview of each app's features and benefits for improving the work-from-home experience.

title: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins speak out about the state's new restrictive abortion law
summary: Large Indiana employers Eli Lilly and Cummins have voiced strong opposition to the state's newly passed restrictive abortion law. Eli Lilly stated the law would hinder its ability to attract diverse talent and force it to plan for employment growth outside Indiana, while Cummins expressed concerns about its impact on talent attraction and retention. Both companies emphasized that the law conflicts with ensuring women's full participation in the workforce and the need for inclusive environments.

title: Poly Rove DECT Wireless Phones now Microsoft Teams supported with Teams SIP Gateway
summary: Poly has announced that its Rove DECT Wireless IP Phone Solution is now certified for Microsoft Teams via the Teams SIP Gateway. This integration allows industries like retail and manufacturing, heavy users of DECT, to register their devices directly to the Microsoft Teams cloud SIP gateway, potentially eliminating the need for on-premises PBXs or certified Session Border Controllers. Poly's Rove 30 and Rove 40 handsets, along with B2 and B4 bases, are ruggedized, IP65-rated, and feature Microban antimicrobial protection, offering specialized solutions for various work environments.

title: Is the Vanguard MSCI International ETF (VGS) a good alternative to global tech shares?
summary: This article examines the Vanguard MSCI International ETF (VGS) as a potential alternative to direct investment in global tech shares. It highlights that VGS includes a significant allocation to information technology (21.5%) and offers broad diversification across 1,473 businesses globally with a low management fee of 0.18%. While VGS provides diversified tech exposure, the article also mentions other ETFs like iShares S&P 500 ETF and Betashares Nasdaq 100 ETF for greater or more targeted tech sector concentration.

title: Red Dead Redemption 2 & GTA V could be debuting on Xbox Cloud Gaming very soon, per Rockstar Support
summary: Rockstar Support's website suggests that Red Dead Redemption 2 and GTA V may soon be available on Xbox Cloud Gaming for users who own the digital versions and have an Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription. Microsoft had briefly listed the games in its Cloud Gaming catalog, leading to speculation of their imminent arrival. However, Microsoft later stated this was a bug and the listings have since been removed.

title: Security for next generation telecommunication networks
summary: Microsoft emphasizes security as a first principle in developing networks and systems for next-generation telecommunication, particularly 5G and 6G, by adopting a zero-trust security architecture. The article details how the disaggregated software components in modern telecom networks increase the attack surface and how Microsoft Azure's massive scale and sophisticated defenses, including AI/ML and cloud security capabilities, are leveraged to protect against threats to Open RAN architecture and critical infrastructure. It also addresses strategies for mitigating the impact of compromised systems through rapid detection and recovery mechanisms.

title: Microsoft Sues “Black Market” Software Distributor for TM and Copyright Infringement
summary: Microsoft Corporation has filed a lawsuit against The Search People Enterprises Ltd. and its principal for copyright and trademark infringement. The suit alleges the defendants are distributing "black market" access devices for Microsoft software, which they falsely advertise as genuine. Microsoft conducted over two dozen test purchases to confirm the illegality and is seeking a permanent injunction and damages.

title: Russian hacking group claims attack on Lockheed Martin
summary: A prominent Russian hacking group called Killnet claims responsibility for a DDoS attack that took Lockheed Martin's websites offline. The group stated its motivation was Lockheed Martin's provision of M142 High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems to Ukraine and also claims to have stolen information on Lockheed Martin employees. Lockheed Martin denies any system compromise, while Killnet's founder issued a chilling warning of escalating attacks.

title: How Microsoft secures its spot among the world's most valuable brands
summary: Microsoft has become one of the world's most valuable brands, with its brand value reaching $661 billion in 2022, securing the number four spot in Kantar BrandZ Most Valuable Global Brands. The company's success is attributed to its meaningful, different, and salient offerings, including superior cloud, software, and hardware products, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has focused on specific market segments, leading to a tenfold increase in brand value and solidifying its position as the world's most valuable business services brand, further boosted by the continued digital transformation and remote work trends.

title: Johnson Controls AI-enhanced OpenBlue Platform Cuts Microsoft's Beijing Campus Energy Footprint
summary: Johnson Controls partnered with Microsoft Beijing Campus to implement its AI-enhanced OpenBlue platform, resulting in significant energy savings of 27.9% and 98% uptime for key equipment. This collaboration helped the campus receive an energy-saving endorsement and subsidy from the Beijing Municipal Government, highlighting the potential of advanced technology for sustainable building operations and carbon emission reduction. The project demonstrates a successful application of AI and integrated building management systems in achieving net-zero goals.

title: Johnson Controls partners Microsoft Beijing Campus for optimization of building operations
summary: Johnson Controls has partnered with Microsoft Beijing Campus for the retrofit and optimization of its building operations, resulting in 27.9% energy savings and 98% key equipment uptime. This initiative, which involved implementing AI-enhanced OpenBlue Net Zero Buildings solutions and upgrading chiller systems, has earned the campus an energy saving endorsement and financial subsidy from the Beijing Municipal Government. It showcases how advanced technology can significantly reduce energy consumption and emissions in existing buildings, contributing to China's carbon neutrality goals.

title: Crawler Hints supports Microsoft’s IndexNow in helping users find new content
summary: Cloudflare's Crawler Hints now supports Microsoft's IndexNow protocol, allowing websites to proactively notify search engines of content changes. This "push" model for indexing aims to significantly reduce wasted crawler traffic, saving bandwidth and compute resources for both websites and search engines, and improving content freshness in search results. Cloudflare plans to default Crawler Hints on for all customers and hopes for wider adoption of the IndexNow standard by other major search engines like Google.

title: The Forrester Wave™: Anti-Money Laundering Solutions, Q2 2025
summary: SAS has been recognized as a leader in The Forrester Wave™: Anti-Money Laundering Solutions, Q2 2025 report. Forrester notes that SAS's recent acquisition of Hazy synthetic data software is expected to enhance its Financial Crime Compliance portfolio, especially with large data models and Generative AI. The report highlights SAS's AML solution as suitable for enterprises with existing SAS and data science skills, emphasizing its strong framework for quantifying ROI.

title: Why Microsoft Courts Nigerian Digital ID4D
summary: Microsoft Corporation has expressed its willingness to partner with Nigeria's Digital Identification for Development (ID4D) project, focusing on capacity development and data protection. This collaboration aims to support the ID4D project's efforts in strengthening digital identity and data security within Nigeria. The ID4D project coordinator, Musa Odole Solomon, welcomed Microsoft's interest, particularly in capacity building for ecosystem partners and in establishing robust data protection laws.

title: Microsoft, Traeger Grills and Young Living are cutting staff. Are Utah workers in for more layoffs?
summary: Several companies with a presence in Utah, including Traeger Grills, Young Living Essential Oils, and Microsoft, are implementing staff layoffs due to reduced consumer spending from inflation and ongoing supply chain issues. Traeger is cutting 14% of its global workforce, and Young Living is reducing staff by about 200 people. Despite these reductions, a labor economist from the University of Utah emphasizes that layoffs are still relatively rare in the current strong economy, with overall layoff rates remaining low compared to historic averages.

title: Up Close at Defcon 30 in Las Vegas
summary: PCMag staffers attended Defcon 30 in Las Vegas, a significant annual gathering for hackers, to gain insights into current cybersecurity trends and hands-on experience. The event, in its 30th year, facilitated networking among security professionals and provided a platform to discuss pressing issues like insider threats and Windows vulnerabilities. Staffers highlighted the conference's community-focused atmosphere and practical demonstrations.

title: Saudi PIF buys shares in Alphabet, Zoom and Microsoft in US shopping spree
summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) significantly expanded its US stock portfolio in the second quarter, investing approximately $40.8 billion. The fund acquired shares in major tech companies like Alphabet, Zoom Video, and Microsoft, alongside holdings in JPMorgan, BlackRock, Starbucks, Adobe, and others. The PIF, managing $620 billion in assets, is a key instrument in Saudi Arabia's strategy to diversify its economy away from oil.

title: Microsoft launches Dev Box preview for developers to try cloud-based workstations
summary: Microsoft has launched a public preview of its Dev Box service, offering developers cloud-based, ready-to-code workstations. Built on Windows 365, this managed service provides virtual machines with eight virtual CPUs and 32GB of RAM, supporting various IDEs and SDKs for cross-platform app development. Organizations can try 15 hours of free usage monthly, with consumption-based billing thereafter.

title: Should we break up Facebook to save it?
summary: Richard J. Shinder argues that Facebook's recent financial struggles and user dissatisfaction stemming from a degraded user experience, content moderation issues, and privacy scandals highlight the need for a drastic change. He proposes breaking up Meta Platforms into two entities: a "good bank" Facebook focusing on its original social media strengths and apolitical content moderation, and a "bad bank" Meta for its more experimental and data-driven ventures like the metaverse. This bifurcation, he suggests, would allow users to choose their preferred experience, maximize shareholder value, and potentially salvage the "good" aspects of the platform.

title: Capital Budgets Fall to 13% of IT Spending
summary: Capital budgets now constitute only 13% of IT spending in North American companies, a significant drop from 24% in 2013, as businesses shift away from on-premises infrastructure towards public cloud and subscription-based applications. While total IT spending is projected to increase, the allocation is largely towards operational expenditures like cloud infrastructure and applications rather than long-term capital investments. This shift brings flexibility but also risks of being locked into difficult-to-negotiate operational contracts.

title: Microsoft plans to shut down its Olympia Corp test lab for Windows Enterprise
summary: Microsoft Corporation is closing its 'Olympia Corp' program, a test lab designed for Windows enterprise users to test business-oriented features. Participants were notified via email about the impending shutdown, effective September 12, 2022, and advised to back up any data. Launched in 2017, the program helped Microsoft gather feedback for enterprise Windows versions, but the company now believes other simulations and demos can achieve similar goals.

title: Amazon (AWS) and Microsoft Data Centers in Virginia
summary: This article details the extensive presence of Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Microsoft data centers across Virginia, highlighting locations in both Northern Virginia and emerging areas like Boydton. It outlines specific data center sites, their sizes, and ongoing development plans for both companies, emphasizing their investments and strategic growth in the region. The report also covers their respective cloud regions, availability zones, and cloud on-ramps to support demand for compute, storage, and database capabilities.

title: Bill Gates Is Ecolab’s Largest Shareholder. He Just Scooped Up More Shares.
summary: Bill Gates, through his investment vehicles, has increased his stake in Ecolab, a company specializing in water-treatment, hygiene, and infection-prevention services. He is already the largest shareholder of Ecolab, which provides essential services related to public health and safety. This recent acquisition further solidifies his position as a major investor in the company.

title: Digital Health Market Is Booming Worldwide | GE Healthcare, Philips Healthcare, Qualcomm, Cerner Corp
summary: The Global Digital Health Market is experiencing significant growth, as detailed in a new research study by HTF Market Intelligence Consulting Pvt. Ltd. The report evaluates market risks, identifies opportunities, and offers strategic decision-making support, covering market trends, growth drivers, technologies, and investment structures. Key players in this booming market include GE Healthcare, Philips Healthcare, Qualcomm, and Cerner Corp, among others, with the market segmented by areas like mHealth, Tele Healthcare, Healthcare Analytics, and Digital Health Systems across more than 18 countries.

title: No, Microsoft did not acquire Sony
summary: A widespread but fake news report claimed that Microsoft had acquired Sony for $130 billion, including all its divisions like Sony Music and PlayStation. The article debunks this claim, citing the lack of credible media coverage, absence of official statements from either company, and the complex regulatory approvals such a deal would require.

title: Why the ESG/Tech Combination Is Important
summary: Broad market environmental, social, and governance (ESG) exchange-traded funds (ETFs) frequently have significant allocations to technology stocks. For example, the Invesco ESG Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQMG) has a technology weight of almost 62%. While some critics view this as over-reliance on tech, experts argue that technology companies often embody sustainable development principles, making them suitable for ESG funds despite facing scrutiny on issues like data privacy and energy consumption.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Data Integration Tools
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Data Integration Tools, signifying its ongoing commitment to comprehensive and cost-effective data integration solutions. The company's offerings, including Azure Data Factory and Azure Synapse Link, provide end-to-end data integration strategies to help organizations manage and gain insights from their rapidly growing data. This recognition highlights Microsoft's focus on empowering businesses to achieve competitive advantages and better outcomes through data.

title: Warner Music’s HipHopDX Asia Launches ‘The Regionals’ – The First-Ever Pan-Asia Cypher Series
summary: Warner Music's HipHopDX Asia has launched 'The Regionals,' a pioneering pan-Asia rap cypher series designed to globalize Asian hip-hop music. This initiative brings together top emcees from various Eastern markets and pairs them with acclaimed Western hip-hop producers like !llmind, Hit-Boy, and 9th Wonder. The project aims to showcase diverse talent, connect global hip-hop communities, and promote Asian hip-hop culture worldwide, with the first cyphers scheduled for release in September.

title: These Wide-Moat Stocks Are Gaining Ground
summary: Quality stocks with wide economic moats, including Microsoft and Amazon, have outperformed since the bear market low in June, despite still being undervalued. While narrow-moat stocks have seen stronger recent gains due to market-cap weighting of companies like Apple and Tesla, wide-moat companies are better positioned to handle inflation due to their pricing power. Overall, Morningstar analysts consider wide-moat stocks to be deeply undervalued compared to overpriced narrow-moat stocks.

title: What’s Changed: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Data Integration Tools
summary: Gartner's 2022 Magic Quadrant for Data Integration Tools highlights renewed momentum in the data integration market, driven by hybrid/multicloud needs, augmented data management, and data fabric designs. The report notes a shift from mega-vendors to niche players, with Informatica retaining its leadership position, and new entrants like AWS, Palantir, and Software AG joining the quadrant. Key market drivers include augmented data integration, increased data engineering requirements, and DataOps.

title: Rackspace’s intention not to sell leaves Wall Street wary
summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. has decided against selling the company and will instead pursue a restructuring plan, disappointing some Wall Street analysts. The cloud-computing services provider, the largest tech company in San Antonio, will realign into separate business units for private and public clouds. This decision led to a significant drop in share prices as analysts downgraded the stock, expressing skepticism about the standalone strategy's ability to create shareholder value quickly.

title: Compass CTO is out; brokerage laying off tech employees
summary: Compass has parted ways with its Chief Technology Officer, Joseph Sirosh, and is laying off a "small" number of tech employees and recruiters as part of a $320 million cost-cutting initiative. This move signifies a shift for Compass, emphasizing its role as a residential brokerage rather than solely a technology company, despite having invested heavily in its tech platform. Kendra Shimmell and Shay Artzi will take over some of Sirosh's duties, though his CTO position will not be directly replaced.

title: Microsoft Wants Uber Drivers’ Facial Recognition Privacy Case Tossed on Jurisdictional Grounds
summary: Microsoft has filed a motion seeking to dismiss remaining biometric privacy claims against it regarding Uber's use of its facial recognition technology, "Real Time ID Check." The company argues that Illinois courts lack general or specific jurisdiction over it as Microsoft is headquartered in Washington and incorporated in Delaware, and its technology does not specifically target Illinois. The plaintiffs, Illinois-based Uber drivers, allege that the consentless capture of their faceprints by Uber through Microsoft's technology violates the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA).

title: Cisco Delivers Enterprise Architecture for Voice, Video and Integrated Data Networks
summary: Cisco Systems announced Cisco AVVID (Architecture for Voice, Video and Integrated Data) for corporate enterprises, aiming to enable Internet-based business models with a lower total cost of ownership. This architecture is a culmination of Cisco's multiservice strategy, featuring IP infrastructure, call processing, New World applications, and intelligent IP-enabled clients. Cisco AVVID is designed to provide a flexible, converged network solution, highlighting network availability, open systems, and the rapid deployment of advanced applications.

title: Amazon not expected to bid for Electronic Arts, says CNBC
summary: CNBC reported that Amazon.com Inc is not expected to bid for Electronic Arts Inc, refuting an earlier report about a potential takeover. This news caused EA's stock to fluctuate, initially jumping 15% on the acquisition rumor before settling to a 4% gain. Amazon has recently been active in acquisitions, diversifying its business, while analysts see EA as an attractive target due to its strong licenses and metaverse potential, despite a struggling global gaming industry.

title: Making European Cloud Providers More Competitive
summary: Microsoft is introducing changes to its software licensing terms, effective October 1, 2022, to enhance the competitiveness of European cloud providers. These updates aim to make it easier for customers to port their software, ensure partners have access to cost-effective solutions, and empower partners to build hosted solutions efficiently. The initiative reflects Microsoft's commitment to a fair and open competitive cloud economy in Europe.

title: Microsoft Korea introduces its new startup program ‘Microsoft Launcher’
summary: Microsoft Korea launched "Microsoft Launcher," a new program designed to support local startups at every stage of their growth. This platform offers benefits valued up to ₩500,000,000, including technology, tools, Azure credits, and mentorship, aiming to address common challenges faced by Korean startups. The initiative is part of Microsoft's broader effort to foster a healthy digital ecosystem in Asia, a region recognized as a global innovation powerhouse.

title: How to Get Windows 11 on HP Computers
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to upgrade HP computers to Windows 11. It covers the benefits of upgrading, the essential system requirements, and step-by-step instructions for installation via Windows Update or the Windows 11 Installation Assistant. Additionally, it offers advice on preparing the computer for installation, backing up files, and troubleshooting common issues after the upgrade.

title: Data breach at Nelnet exposes 2.5 million student loan records
summary: Nelnet Serving, a division of Nelnet, experienced a data breach affecting 2.5 million student loan records from the Oklahoma Student Loan Authority and EdFinancial Services LLC. The breach, which occurred between June and July 2022, potentially exposed names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and Social Security numbers. Law enforcement and the U.S. Department of Education were notified, and several class-action lawsuits are reportedly being prepared despite the company offering identity theft protection services to affected individuals.

title: NetEase is acquiring Heavy Rain developer Quantic Dream
summary: Chinese tech giant NetEase Games is acquiring Quantic Dream, the developer behind titles like Heavy Rain and Detroit: Become Human, for an undisclosed amount. The acquisition marks Quantic Dream's first studio in Europe, with NetEase aiming to expand its global operations. Despite the acquisition, Quantic Dream will continue to operate independently, leveraging NetEase's resources to develop new narrative-driven games, including an upcoming Star Wars title.

title: China gaming giants Tencent, NetEase invest in foreign studios
summary: Chinese gaming giants Tencent and NetEase are expanding their investments in overseas studios due to a slowdown in the domestic market. Tencent will acquire a 16.25% stake in Japan's FromSoftware, while NetEase has acquired French developer Quantic Dream. These moves highlight a continued pivot by Chinese tech companies towards international markets amid domestic regulatory crackdowns.

title: DAX-ETF statt MSCI-World-ETF? Was der Fokus auf deutsche Aktien wirklich bringt
summary: This article examines whether a DAX-ETF is a viable alternative or supplement to an MSCI World-ETF for investors. The author argues that while German companies in the DAX have a long history, focusing solely on a DAX-ETF has historically yielded poorer returns compared to the more diversified MSCI World. Key reasons cited against a DAX-ETF include its historical underperformance, regional concentration, lack of tech companies, and limited number of constituent stocks.

title: Focus Cloud Acquires Microsoft, ServiceNow Cloud Talent Provider Cognitive Group -
summary: IT recruiting firm Focus Cloud has acquired Microsoft specialist Cognitive Group to form the new Focus Cloud Group. This expands Focus Cloud's recruitment expertise across major cloud platforms including Microsoft, Workday, SAP, Salesforce, and ServiceNow, as well as the life sciences and cybersecurity industries. The acquisition aims to build a global cloud recruitment powerhouse and grow internationally while benefiting shareholders and staff.

title: Export Development Canada leads Canadian business toward a sustainable—and prosperous—cloud-based future
summary: Export Development Canada (EDC) is utilizing Microsoft Azure Cloud Services to embed environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles into its core business and help Canadian companies become more sustainable. This digital transformation is reducing EDC's operational carbon footprint, improving disaster recovery, and enhancing employee productivity. Furthermore, EDC plans to use Azure's capabilities to track ESG metrics for its customers, encouraging decarbonization and supporting Canada's clean technology sector.

title: Self-driving tech firm Aurora mulls sale to Apple or Microsoft - Bloomberg News
summary: Aurora Innovation Inc CEO Chris Urmson is exploring strategic options for the self-driving tech company, including a potential sale to Apple or Microsoft, to navigate challenging market conditions. Other considerations include cost cuts, going private, spinning off assets, and acquiring smaller companies. The company's shares rose 15% on the news but have suffered significantly this year, and it recently delayed the delivery of its autonomous freight trucks.

title: Microsoft, VMware, Ivanti Lead UEM Market As Hybrid Work Takes Over
summary: Gartner's 2022 Magic Quadrant for Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) tools identifies Microsoft, VMware, and Ivanti as global market leaders, driven by the increasing adoption of cloud-based UEM and hybrid work models. The report highlights strengths and weaknesses of six key vendors—Microsoft, VMware, Ivanti, IBM, ManageEngine, and Matrix42—predicting that over 90% of clients will use cloud-based UEM by 2025. This shift is also expected to lead to the convergence of UEM and digital employee experience tools by 2027, significantly reducing human effort in endpoint management.

title: Software superpower India to join world data capitals: Microsoft Corp president Brad Smith
summary: Microsoft Corp president Brad Smith states that India has become a software superpower and is poised to become one of the world's great data capitals, joining the top 2-3 software economies globally. He highlights India's significant digital progress, its influential role in technology regulation, and its potential to export its software stack to other countries, citing initiatives like ONDC, Aadhaar, and UPI. Smith also notes that India's growing working-age population presents an opportunity amidst an aging workforce in other major economies, and he emphasizes Microsoft's commitment to supporting India's digital growth.

title: NetApp Gets Into The MSP Game Via Spot, CloudJumper
summary: NetApp is expanding its focus into managed services by leveraging its Spot and CloudJumper acquisitions, particularly targeting MSPs with a cloud-first approach. The company is bundling its technologies with Microsoft Azure services to offer fixed-price, per-seat managed services, aiming to provide predictable margins and scalability for MSPs. This strategy deviates from NetApp's traditional storage focus, with Spot PC and CloudCheckr's CMX platform now serving hundreds of MSPs.

title: How to Buy NASDAQ Stocks
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy NASDAQ stocks, detailing what the NASDAQ is, who owns its shares, and the pros and cons of investing in them. It covers the steps to purchase NASDAQ stocks, from fixing a budget and choosing a broker to placing an order, and includes a brief history of the exchange. The article also mentions specific index funds and prominent companies listed on the NASDAQ.

title: Insight Partners Backs Microsoft 365 Security Provider SaaS Alerts
summary: Insight Partners has invested $22 million in SaaS Alerts, a cybersecurity company that provides cloud application security software primarily to Managed Service Providers (MSPs). Founded in 2020, SaaS Alerts helps MSPs monitor and protect end-customers using various SaaS applications like Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce. This investment underscores Insight Partners' continued interest in the SaaS security and MSP software sectors, where it has several other related portfolio companies.

title: Single-Stock ETFs Are Mushrooming. What to Know.
summary: Direxion, a fund company, is expanding its single-stock ETF offerings with six new funds. These ETFs will allow investors to make short-term bullish or bearish bets on major tech companies like Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. This move signifies a further expansion in the fast-growing market of single-stock funds.

title: Will there be an end to the soda price increase? Not likely. Here's why.
summary: The cost of soda has increased by over 45% in less than four years, with a 12-pack now retailing for upwards of $7. Consumers like Rachel Hatfield and Rebecca Dunn find these prices "ridiculous" and "stupid money," forcing them to cut back on purchases despite inflation. The rise in prices is attributed to inflation, increased aluminum costs, and the lack of significant competition between beverage giants PepsiCo and Coca-Cola, suggesting that these price hikes are likely to continue.

title: Microsoft, Lululemon take lead scooping up downtown Vancouver office space in 2022
summary: In 2022, Microsoft Corp. and Lululemon Athletica Inc. have become major acquirers of downtown Vancouver office space, leading to a stabilization in the market despite increased vacancy rates due to new building deliveries. Microsoft secured 468,000 square feet, while Lululemon leased 237,000 square feet, indicating strong demand for top-quality office space. This activity suggests that the current abundance of office space in Vancouver's downtown core will likely be short-lived.

title: PLDT to build new Aurora cable landing station
summary: PLDT Inc. announced plans to construct a new cable landing station in Baler, Aurora, and integrate the Asia Direct Cable (ADC) system by 2024. These initiatives are part of a broader strategy to enhance the Philippines' information and communication technology infrastructure, specifically targeting the growing hyperscaler industry. PLDT aims to position the Philippines as a leading hyperscaler hub in the Asia-Pacific region by expanding its data center operations and subsea cable capabilities.

title: Microsoft integrates Israeli tech to upgrade video streaming quality
summary: Microsoft has launched new video streaming technology for its Teams platform, based on developments from Israeli firm Peer5, which Microsoft acquired a year ago. The technology, created at Microsoft's R&D center in Herzliya, aims to significantly improve the quality of large-scale live broadcasts without overloading networks, enabling organizations to host events for millions of users simultaneously. This integration is designed to support remote work environments by enhancing internal communication through high-quality virtual meetings and training sessions.

title: Ministry of Justice moves official website into Microsoft cloud
summary: The Ministry of Justice in Qatar has migrated its official website to the Microsoft cloud, in collaboration with the Ministry of Communications and Information Technology (MCIT). This move is part of Qatar's digital transformation plan, aligning with Qatar National Vision 2030, and follows the launch of Microsoft's first large-scale cloud data center in Qatar. The migration aims to accelerate digital procedures, promote legal culture, and develop the community.

title: 343 Industries founder Bonnie Ross resigns after leading Halo brand for 15 years
summary: Bonnie Ross, the founder of 343 Industries, has announced her departure from the company and Microsoft after 15 years, citing a "family medical issue." Her role will be split among three individuals as part of a restructuring to manage the Halo franchise's future development and cross-media efforts. This resignation follows other notable departures from the studio, including the Halo Infinite art director and several design leads.

title: Microsoft patches a new zero-day affecting all versions of Windows
summary: Microsoft has issued security patches for a zero-day vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2022-37969, impacting all supported Windows versions, including Windows 11 and earlier, and Windows Server. This elevation of privilege flaw, which allows attackers to gain system privileges, has been exploited in real-world attacks. Additionally, Microsoft addressed another zero-day, CVE-2022-23960, a cache speculation vulnerability affecting Windows 11 for ARM-based systems, as part of its monthly Patch Tuesday release.

title: Rigetti Expands QCS™ Availability to Microsoft’s Azure Quantum Platform
summary: Rigetti Computing has launched its QCS™ platform in Public Preview on Microsoft’s Azure Quantum platform, making its 80-qubit Aspen-M-2 and 40-qubit Aspen-11 quantum processors accessible to all Azure Quantum users. This integration allows developers to use Rigetti's proprietary Quil language and supports popular quantum programming frameworks, enhancing flexibility and performance optimization. Rigetti is also participating in the Azure Quantum Credit program, providing users with credits to explore its QPUs.

title: Author: Vamsi Nekkanti
summary: This page profiles Vamsi Nekkanti, an author at Sify, and highlights his article "Data Centers To Infinity and Beyond!". Nekkanti, a technology enthusiast from Visakhapatnam, focuses on the future of data centers, including innovative concepts like underwater and space-based data centers, and mentions Microsoft's patent for an Artificial Reef Data Center. The page also lists other recent articles discussing AI, deepfakes, and data center innovations.

title: Lawsuit Accuses Zillow, Microsoft Of ‘Wiretapping’
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Zillow and Microsoft in Washington state, alleging that Zillow's website uses embedded code from Microsoft and other vendors to "wiretap" visitors' online interactions. The suit claims this code records mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and other communications, creating a video replay of user behavior that violates privacy rights and the state's wiretapping statute. Plaintiffs are seeking civil remedies, while Zillow states it is reviewing the lawsuit and is transparent with its privacy policy.

title: Rigetti Announces New Partnerships and Details Multi-Year Roadmap Designed to Reach Quantum Advantage
summary: Rigetti Computing, Inc. announced an updated multi-year roadmap and key partnerships at its inaugural investor day, focusing on achieving Quantum Advantage. The roadmap includes the upcoming Ankaa™ 84-qubit system and Lyra™ 336-qubit system, alongside collaborations with companies like Keysight, NVIDIA, Bluefors, and Microsoft Azure to enhance hardware, software, and climate modeling applications. Rigetti is also expanding its Fab-1 facility and developing next-generation cryogenic platforms.

title: Expert: Zillow Privacy Lawsuit Has Wider Implications
summary: A civil lawsuit filed against Zillow Group Inc. and Microsoft Corp. alleges privacy violations through the collection of user interaction data on Zillow's website, including mouse movements and keystrokes. An expert in internet privacy law suggests this case, and similar ones, could force significant changes in how companies draft their privacy policies and collect user data. The core issue revolves around the vagueness of Zillow's privacy policy and whether it adequately discloses the extent of data collection to consumers.

title: How CrowdStrike plans to become a generational platform
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. has rapidly become a leading security firm, boasting over $2 billion in annual recurring revenue and a $40 billion market capitalization, with projections to reach $5 billion in revenue by mid-decade. The company's success is attributed to its architectural platform, strong go-to-market strategies, and strategic acquisitions, positioning it as a potential generational platform in the cybersecurity sector, despite broader market headwinds. The article delves into CrowdStrike's platform, customer spending momentum, and its focus on extended detection and response (XDR) as it aims to consolidate point products in the market.

title: The making of TSMC and its ecosystem
summary: TSMC has solidified its dominant position in the global wafer foundry market due to its advanced technology and strategic ecosystem development, securing orders from major IC designers worldwide. Despite global economic headwinds, TSMC projects significant revenue growth and continues massive expansion projects, fostering extensive collaborations with its supply chain partners through initiatives like the Open Innovation Platform. The company faces competition in advanced process nodes but maintains a leading edge through strong client relationships and continuous investment in technological advancements.

title: S&P 500 Buybacks Decline 21.8% as Financials Pull Back; 12-Months Buybacks Pass $1 Trillion for the First Time
summary: S&P 500 stock buybacks decreased by 21.8% in Q2 2022 to $219.6 billion from Q1's record $281.0 billion, primarily due to a pullback from Financials and Health Care sectors. Despite the quarterly decline, the 12-month expenditure ending June 2022 surpassed $1 trillion for the first time, reaching $1.005 trillion, a 65.0% increase from the prior year. An anticipated 1% excise tax on net buybacks in 2023 is not expected to significantly impact overall buyback activity in 2023, though some 2023 purchases may be accelerated into Q4 2022.

title: S|W: The SaaS Weekly – Adobe has $20 billion designs on Figma
summary: This edition of S|W: The SaaS Weekly highlights Adobe's significant $20 billion acquisition of Figma and Google facing a $25 billion lawsuit. The newsletter also covers various funding rounds for Canadian startups like GrowerIQ and Bright, leadership changes at Thinkific, and financial updates from companies like D2L and Byju's, alongside news of layoffs at GoStudent and Twilio.

title: Logitech G Elevates Game Streaming By Unveiling Cloud First Handheld Gaming Device
summary: Logitech G has launched the G CLOUD Gaming Handheld, a dedicated cloud gaming device developed in partnership with Tencent Games. This device is optimized for cloud streaming from Xbox Game Pass Ultimate and NVIDIA GeForce NOW, offering precision controls, a 1080p HD screen, and over 12 hours of battery life. It will be available in North America in October 2022 for a suggested retail price of $349.99, with a limited-time pre-order price of $299.99.

title: Schlumberger Launches Enterprise Data Solution
summary: Schlumberger announced the commercial release of its Enterprise Data Solution, powered by Microsoft Energy Data Services. This solution, aligned with the OSDU™ Technical Standard, aims to provide comprehensive subsurface data capabilities and enable data integration across multiple vendors. It supports data-driven decision-making, accelerates AI and digital solutions, and is designed to improve operational efficiency and advance initiatives like carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS).

title: 'Productivity paranoia': Microsoft study of corporate workplaces finds big disconnect in hybrid work
summary: A new Microsoft study reveals a significant "productivity paranoia" gap between employees and leaders regarding hybrid work. While 87% of employees feel productive, only 12% of leaders are confident in their employees' productivity, creating a disconnect that could threaten the sustainability of hybrid work. Microsoft suggests focusing on outcomes, clear communication, and continuous feedback, rather than monitoring hours, and is updating its Viva platform with features to address these challenges.

title: How a Top Fidelity Growth Fund Is Navigating This Market
summary: Fidelity Growth Discovery FDSVX, co-managed by Jason Weiner and Asher Anolic, has navigated 2022's challenging market for growth stocks better than many peers by focusing on "growth quality" rather than simply high-growth potential. The fund emphasizes companies with strong gross margins and predictable earnings growth, incorporating names like Microsoft, Coca-Cola, and UnitedHealth Group, which are not typically associated with aggressive growth funds. This strategy has helped the fund outperform its category over various timeframes despite the overall market downturn.

title: DPSP Group partners with Vivo to digitize the company's telephone service
summary: DPSP Group, parent company of Drogarias Pacheco and Drogaria São Paulo, has partnered with Vivo Empresas to digitize its telephone service. Vivo will provide a Virtual PABX system, enabling over 5,000 branches to operate from anywhere and integrate with collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams. This aims to optimize internal processes, improve efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction for the pharmaceutical retail giant.

title: Shrugging off crypto winter, Galaxy Digital’s Mike Novogratz calls inaugural NFT launch ‘building for the future’
summary: Despite the ongoing "crypto winter" and declining NFT sales, Galaxy Digital CEO Mike Novogratz remains bullish on NFTs, launching the company's inaugural collection of 3,210 digital collectibles. The NFTs, created in partnership with Time's Web3 initiative TIMEPieces, will be airdropped for free to employees and partners, with Novogratz emphasizing it as "building for the future" and a way to foster community spirit rather than a financial endeavor. He points to corporate adoption of NFTs by companies like Nike and the NBA as evidence of their long-term viability.

title: Ontario Power Generation enters agreement to sell clean energy credits to Microsoft
summary: Ontario Power Generation (OPG) has signed a 10-year agreement to sell clean energy credits from its hydroelectric dams and nuclear reactors to Microsoft. This deal marks OPG's first major customer for its clean energy credit trading business and will help Microsoft meet its goal of powering its operations with 100% zero-carbon energy by 2030, particularly for its data centers and offices in Ontario. The sale of nuclear-derived credits has, however, sparked debate among environmental groups and the Ontario Energy Board regarding the definition of "clean" energy and the potential implications of selling these credits outside the province.

title: OPG and Microsoft announce strategic partnership to power a Net-Zero future for Ontario
summary: Ontario Power Generation (OPG) and Microsoft have formed a strategic partnership in Canada to tackle climate change and promote sustainable growth in Ontario. Microsoft will purchase Clean Energy Credits (CECs) from OPG's carbon-free hydro and nuclear assets on an hourly basis, aiming for 100% carbon-free energy for its datacenters globally by 2030. The collaboration also includes co-developing an hourly energy matching platform, modernizing OPG's IT infrastructure with Microsoft Azure, and exploring digital co-innovation for OPG's Small Modular Reactor program.

title: 삼성자산운용, 월배당 'DIVO ETF' 한국판 출시
summary: 삼성자산운용이 미국 운용사 앰플리파이의 월배당 커버드콜 ETF인 'DIVO'의 한국판 상품을 출시한다. 이 상품은 'KODEX 미국 배당 프리미엄 액티브 ETF'로, 미국 우량 배당 성장주와 대표 성장주에 투자하며 주식 배당과 콜옵션 매도를 통해 월 배당 재원을 마련한다. 특히 개별 주식에 대한 전략적인 콜옵션 매도와 자산의 주가 상승에 따른 자본 이익 추구하는 액티브 전략이 특징이다.

title: Build real-time video and audio apps on the world’s most interconnected network
summary: Cloudflare announced Cloudflare Calls, a new product designed to simplify the development of real-time audio and video applications. This service abstracts away WebRTC complexities by leveraging Cloudflare's global network as a "super peer," offering a regionless, scalable, and secure solution for applications like video conferencing, interactive events, and privacy-first group sessions. Calls aims to solve common challenges with traditional WebRTC deployments, such as high latency, reliability issues with multiple participants, and the operational overhead of managing SFUs, by routing traffic through the closest Cloudflare data center and providing enhanced observability and privacy features.

title: Lazard Asset Management: 3 undervalued global equities
summary: Warryn Robertson from Lazard Asset Management identifies three undervalued global equities: eBay, Intel, and International Game Technology (IGT).
