He explains that eBay and Intel are attractively priced after significant declines, having refocused their strategies and invested heavily in R&D, respectively. IGT is highlighted for its strong, underappreciated earnings growth and low valuation compared to its peers.

title: Salesforce Inc. stock rises Wednesday, still underperforms market
summary: Salesforce Inc. stock rose 0.86% on Wednesday to $150.17, despite an overall positive day for the stock market where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also saw gains. However, the company's stock still underperformed several key competitors, including Microsoft, Adobe, and SAP SE. Salesforce Inc. remains significantly below its 52-week high.

title: Microsoft Corp. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) shares decreased by 1.48% to $237.50 on Thursday, still outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 fall by 2.11% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fall by 1.54%. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, but performed better than some competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and SAP SE ADR. Trading volume for Microsoft exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud ERP for Product-Centric Enterprises
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud ERP for Product-Centric Enterprises for the second consecutive year. This recognition is attributed to their Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute, with Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management and Dynamics 365 Finance highlighted for their cloud-native architecture, integration with the Microsoft technology stack, embedded analytics, and flexible manufacturing capabilities. Microsoft continues to invest in innovation to make Dynamics 365 smarter and more efficient for enterprises of all scales.

title: Lloyds Bank closing Copley data center in 2025
summary: Lloyds Banking Group plans to close its Copley, West Yorkshire data center in 2025 as part of a modernization strategy and ongoing cloud migration. The bank had previously announced a £60 million plan for its Halifax Bank unit's HQ, which included the closure of the Copley facility. Lloyds has been moving workloads to the cloud, signing a large deal with IBM in 2017 and later with Azure and Google Cloud.

title: TTAC: Overweight Tech & Energy, Underweight Industrials & Communication
summary: The FCF US Quality ETF (TTAC) focuses on free cash flow to select approximately 150 stocks, aiming to outperform the Russell 3000. As of late August, TTAC was overweight in technology and energy sectors and underweight in industrials and communication services. FCF Advisors prioritizes free cash flow metrics, which they believe are more difficult to manipulate than GAAP earnings, especially in the current challenging investment environment.

title: 'Ask the Expert' with Dominic Mailhot from Export Development Canada (EDC) – Microsoft News Center Canada
summary: This article features an interview with Dominic Mailhot, CIO of Export Development Canada (EDC), as part of Microsoft's "Ask the Expert" series. Mailhot discusses EDC's top priority: helping Canadian businesses become more competitive through strong sustainability practices and ESG goals. He highlights EDC's own digital transformation, including migrating to Azure, to lead by example in reducing environmental impact and supporting a low-carbon economy.

title: ServiceNow Continues to Grow Its Revenue, But Can It Catch Up to Microsoft?
summary: ServiceNow has experienced significant growth since its 2012 IPO, with its revenue increasing from less than $650 million to $5.9 billion in 2021, representing a 42.5% CAGR. While the company projects continued strong growth, it faces a challenge in catching up to Microsoft, which, despite a more modest 11% CAGR in the same period, has a much larger revenue base. ServiceNow, with its cloud-based digital workflow management platform, is a major player in its industry but is unlikely to surpass the much older and larger Microsoft by 2030.

title: How the NetApp/VMware Partnership Benefits Businesses in the Cloud
summary: The partnership between NetApp and VMware aims to help businesses overcome cloud overspending and improve storage solutions. It allows organizations to manage cloud storage consistently, integrate cloud services without major overhauls, and procure storage incrementally based on their needs. This collaboration offers flexibility and cost-efficiency for businesses utilizing cloud environments.

title: Identity Theft Monitoring Offered to Microsoft 365 Consumer Users
summary: Microsoft has launched a new Identity Theft Monitoring service for its U.S. Microsoft 365 Personal and Family subscribers, offered at no extra charge. The service is largely outsourced to Experian, which provides up to $1 million in insurance for stolen funds and $100,000 for identity theft. Users will need to provide personal information to Experian, not Microsoft, to utilize the monitoring service.

title: Building a Lifelong Learning Culture Is More Important Than Ever
summary: In an era of rapid technological advancement and job market evolution, fostering a culture of lifelong learning is crucial for both individuals and organizations. Automation and AI are transforming roles, necessitating continuous skill development to remain relevant. Employees are eager to upskill, and organizations that fail to provide learning opportunities risk losing talent, making lifelong learning a critical component for future success, emphasizing reward, leadership support, awareness, and technology enablement.

title: Walmart blazes trails with its enterprise ‘supercloud’
summary: Walmart has developed an enterprise "supercloud" called the Walmart Cloud Native Platform, which abstracts away differences between public cloud providers, its hybrid cloud, and edge computing nodes. This multicloud environment aims to optimize resource consumption, drive developer productivity, and enhance network resilience and data analysis. The company is also overhauling its legacy software infrastructure to containers, aiming for portability and resiliency over pure cost savings, and has been able to make 170,000 website adjustments monthly.

title: Equinix Expands to Indonesia with $74M Data Center Investment
summary: Equinix, Inc. is expanding its operations to Indonesia with a planned $74 million investment in an International Business Exchange (IBX) data center in Jakarta. This expansion aims to meet the growing digital needs of Indonesian businesses and multinational companies operating in the region, supporting the country's rapid digitalization and its emergence as Southeast Asia's largest digital economy. The new eight-story facility, set to open by the second half of 2024, will offer over 1,600 cabinets and more than 5,300 square meters of colocation space, strategically located in Jakarta's Central Business District.

title: Microsoft, Kyndryl deal connects Azure to IBM mainframe data
summary: Microsoft and Kyndryl have expanded their partnership to facilitate the transfer of mainframe data to Azure, enabling access to AI, web-based analytics, and machine learning capabilities. This collaboration aims to break mainframe data out of its silos by creating secure data pipes between IBM mainframes and Azure, leveraging Microsoft's Power Platform. The deal is seen as a strategic move benefiting both companies and allowing IBM to further its multi-cloud strategy while making mainframe data more accessible for modern application development.

title: ‘LofyGang’ hacking group targets Discord, Disney+ and Minecraft accounts
summary: Security researchers at Checkmarx Ltd. have exposed a hacking group named "LofyGang" responsible for over 200 malicious packages and thousands of installations in 2022. The group, believed to be Brazilian, targets online accounts to steal credit card information and premium accounts for services like Discord, Disney+, and Minecraft. LofyGang operates openly, promoting tools on GitHub and having a Discord server, highlighting the risks of untrusted open-source code.

title: Quantum Company Of The Week: IonQ
summary: IonQ is a leading quantum hardware manufacturer founded in 2015, specializing in trapped-ion technology to build powerful quantum computers. The company has launched two quantum computers, Harmony and Aria, and is set to release Forte in 2023, promising significant improvements in performance. IonQ has secured substantial funding, formed key partnerships with Microsoft and Hyundai, and became the first public pure-play quantum computing company listed on the NYSE in October 2021.

title: Game Pass generated nearly $3 billion in 2021, accounting for 30% of Microsoft’s gaming revenue
summary: Microsoft's Game Pass subscription service generated nearly $3 billion in console revenue in 2021, representing 18% of Microsoft's total annual gaming revenue and almost 30% of its games and services revenue. This data comes from a filing to the Brazilian regulator, CADE, days after it approved Microsoft's acquisition of Activision Blizzard. While PC earnings remain undisclosed, the service's performance compares to PlayStation's Network Services, which earned $3.64 billion in FY 2021.

title: Walmart Finance standardizes on Power BI for reporting and analytics
summary: Walmart Finance has standardized on Microsoft Power BI for its reporting and analytics needs, aiming to centralize, democratize, and optimize its vast financial data. This move allows associates, from leadership to analysts, easy access to comprehensive data, enabling efficient analysis and reducing time spent on data gathering. The implementation leverages Power BI's semantic models, combined with in-memory and DirectQuery modes, and incremental refresh, to provide scalable, cost-effective, and actionable insights across the organization.

title: Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF: Stash This ETF Away In Your Roth IRA (NASDAQ:QQQM)
summary: This article recommends investing in the Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQM) for a Roth IRA, especially given market turmoil. The author highlights why QQQM was chosen over the more common QQQ and discusses its appeal after significant price declines in quality tech stocks like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The author also mentions a collaboration with Hoya Capital for an income-focused investing service.

title: Zeek is Now a Component of Microsoft Windows
summary: Corelight announced that Zeek, a popular open-source network security monitoring platform, has been integrated into Microsoft Windows and Defender for Endpoint. This integration aims to enhance security teams' ability to respond to advanced threats by providing richer signals for threat hunting, improved IoT device discovery, and more powerful detection and response capabilities. The move highlights Microsoft's commitment to supporting open-source projects and expands Zeek's impact on endpoint defense.

title: Microsoft uses strategically located subsidiaries to lower taxes more than previously known, new analysis finds
summary: A new report by the Centre for International Corporate Tax Accountability and Research (CICTAR) alleges that Microsoft uses a network of subsidiaries, particularly in Ireland and Bermuda, to significantly minimize its tax liabilities globally. While not suggesting illegal activity, the analysis raises concerns about profit shifting and its impact on tax revenues in countries where Microsoft conducts substantial business. The report points out that despite high profit margins, some Microsoft subsidiaries report much lower margins, reducing taxable income.

title: Haleon to improve accessibility for visually impaired customers
summary: Haleon has partnered with Microsoft to enhance accessibility for blind and partially sighted customers using the Seeing AI app. The app allows users to scan barcodes of over 1,500 Haleon products, including Sensodyne and Centrum, to hear information like product names, ingredients, and instructions. This initiative aims to promote health inclusivity and greater independence for visually impaired individuals, with plans for global expansion and Android availability.

title: Microsoft - Gaming Global Taxes, Winning Government Contracts
summary: A new report by CICTAR alleges that Microsoft uses a complex network of shell companies in various tax havens, such as Bermuda, to significantly reduce its global tax obligations while simultaneously securing lucrative government contracts. The report highlights instances where Microsoft subsidiaries, despite reporting billions in profits, pay little to no tax, leading to public funds being diverted from essential services like healthcare and education. Public Services International calls for transparency in corporate taxation and suggests that governments should mandate global tax standards for companies seeking public contracts.

title: Seeing AI app launched by Haleon and Microsoft to enhance sight accessibility
summary: Haleon and Microsoft have collaborated to expand the functionality of the Microsoft Seeing AI app, making product labels more accessible for visually impaired individuals. This partnership will allow the app to provide detailed information on over 1500 Haleon products in the UK and US, enabling users to hear product names, ingredients, and usage instructions simply by scanning barcodes. The initiative aims to enhance independence for those with sight loss or low literacy.

title: Microsoft Office suite becomes Microsoft 365 app
summary: Microsoft Corporation announced that its productivity suite, including Office.com, the Office mobile app, and the Office app for Windows, will merge into a single "Microsoft 365 app." This change, featuring a new icon and more features, will roll out gradually starting November 2022. Existing accounts, subscriptions, and files will be automatically updated without disruption, and users can continue to access core applications like Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.

title: The female venture capitalist creating billions in a new world of work beyond the office
summary: Brianne Kimmel's Worklife Ventures has invested in 50 startups since 2019, with nine valued over $1 billion, focusing on the changing work/life balance and the future of remote work. Kimmel, whose venture is backed by Marc Andreessen and Zoom CEO Eric Yuan, plans to continue scaling businesses for freelancers and creators. Despite economic challenges, Worklife Ventures aims to support companies enabling productive work outside traditional office settings, a thesis Kimmel developed before the pandemic.

title: Microsoft Teams SIP Gateway to support analog phones via Analog...
summary: Microsoft announced at Ignite that its Teams SIP Gateway will support analog phones starting in early 2023, allowing existing analog endpoints like door access systems and elevator phones to connect to Teams. It will support specific Analog Telephone Adaptors (ATAs) from AudioCodes, Cisco, and Poly. Additionally, SIP Gateway will support Algo overhead paging devices, ringers, and speakers, also coming in 2023.

title: Microsoft to drive sustainability through the Microsoft Cloud
summary: Microsoft is expanding its Cloud for Sustainability platform with new ESG capabilities to help organizations track and reduce their environmental footprint. The updates include enhanced calculation methodologies for Scope 3 emissions, improved usability and security features in Microsoft Sustainability Manager, and an expanded data model for water and carbon data. Additionally, Microsoft has updated the Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure and introduced Environmental Credit Service to streamline the voluntary environmental markets.

title: Zillow Sued For Alleged Wiretapping
summary: Zillow is facing multiple class-action lawsuits in Pennsylvania, Washington, and Missouri, alleging violations of state wiretapping laws. These lawsuits, similar to prior cases against Facebook and Carrier IQ, claim that Zillow and its third-party vendors (like Microsoft) record users' mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and other interactions to create "session replays" without explicit consent. The article highlights the ongoing debate about consumer privacy versus the need for website analytics, and the implications of applying older wiretapping laws to modern web technologies.

title: ING uses Microsoft Security solutions to reimagine banking for a digital audience
summary: ING, one of Europe's top banks, has adopted Microsoft Security solutions to unify its fragmented IT landscape and enhance its security posture. This move addresses the challenge of coordinating global IT departments and managing a diverse technical environment, ultimately improving efficiency for security teams and providing greater visibility across its digital assets. The bank now leverages Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft 365 Defender, and other Microsoft solutions to proactively defend against cyberthreats and navigate a complex regulatory environment.

title: The 5x Spike & Now Implosion of Generac’s Stock Shows What Went Horribly Wrong in the Markets since Feb. 2020
summary: This article by Wolf Richter on Wolf Street examines the implosion of Generac Holdings (GNRC) stock, which surged five-fold from February 2020 to November 2021 before dropping 79% from its peak. Richter uses Generac as a case study to demonstrate the "stock-market craziness" fueled by factors like ZIRP and QE policies, and the subsequent brutal unwinding of these inflated valuations. The piece also criticizes Wall Street analysts for maintaining "buy" ratings even after significant stock drops, and discusses the broader implications for dip-buyers and the market as a whole.

title: Oracle Cloud adds NVIDIA chips, software to speed enterprise AI uptake
summary: Oracle has expanded its partnership with NVIDIA to integrate tens of thousands of NVIDIA's AI-processing graphics chips and software tools into Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This aims to accelerate enterprise AI adoption across industries like banking, healthcare, and manufacturing by offering powerful A100 and upcoming H100 GPUs. The collaboration will also provide access to NVIDIA software like RAPIDS Accelerator and Clara for enhanced AI performance, medical imaging, and drug discovery.

title: WithSecure adds Microsoft OneDrive protection to its cloud-based security platform
summary: WithSecure Corp. has expanded its cloud-based security platform, WithSecure Elements, by integrating Microsoft OneDrive for Business protection. This new feature enhances collaboration protection by continuously scanning and analyzing files uploaded or modified in OneDrive, leveraging real-time threat intelligence and isolated cloud sandboxing to detect and block malicious content. The move addresses the growing security concerns around cloud-based collaboration tools, which are becoming essential for many companies, especially with the shift to remote work.

title: Report: Oracle slows hiring, especially in Bay Area and Seattle, raising fears of deeper cuts
summary: Oracle has reportedly slowed its hiring efforts, particularly in the Bay Area and Seattle, leading to concerns about potential deeper job cuts. Hiring managers have been instructed to delay start dates for new recruits as the company significantly scales back its recruitment activities. This move by Oracle follows similar actions by other tech giants like Microsoft and Google, signaling a potential shift in the tech job market.

title: Enhancing your world with Augmented Reality
summary: This article explores the concept and vast applications of Augmented Reality (AR), distinguishing it from Virtual Reality (VR). It highlights how AR overlays digital information onto the real world, transforming industries from entertainment and retail to healthcare and agriculture. The author emphasizes AR's potential to provide real-time information and enhance human perception, making everyday tasks and specialized fields more interactive and efficient.

title: 7 Virtual Reality Stocks to Buy to Bet on the Coming VR Boom
summary: The virtual reality market is projected to grow significantly to over $227 billion by 2029, presenting a massive opportunity for investors, especially as many top VR stocks are currently oversold. This article highlights seven virtual reality stocks to consider, including the ProShares Metaverse ETF (VERS) for diversification, Roblox (RBLX) as a leading social metaverse platform, and tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT) and Apple (AAPL) that are heavily investing in VR/AR. Other recommendations include Nvidia (NVDA) for its Omniverse platform, Matterport (MTTR) for its exposure to "digital twins," and Unity Software (U) which recently received a "buy" rating.

title: Microsoft Corp Acquires 25-Acre Land in Pimpri-Waghere for Data Centre
summary: Microsoft Corp. has acquired a 25-acre commercial plot in Pimpri-Waghere, Pune, for approximately Rs 329 crore to establish a hyperscale data center. This acquisition from Finolex Industries is part of Microsoft's broader multi-city data center strategy for India, with the potential to develop a 100 MW cloud capacity data center. The company also has ongoing data center projects in Hyderabad, in collaboration with the Telangana government.

title: 2020s–Today
summary: The article details SLB's (formerly Schlumberger) strategic evolution from the 2020s to today, focusing on its commitment to decarbonization and a balanced planet. It outlines the company's shift towards new energy ventures like geothermal, clean hydrogen, and lithium while continuing to innovate in oil and gas with digital solutions and emissions reduction technologies. Key events include brand changes, major collaborations, and advancements in AI, energy storage, and sustainability, emphasizing SLB's net-zero emissions goal by 2050 and its push for diversity.

title: How self-healing endpoints are a solution to identities under siege
summary: Cyberattackers frequently target endpoints to steal identity data and compromise networks, with malware-free intrusions being particularly hard to detect. The growing threat has led to increased spending on endpoint security and the adoption of self-healing endpoints as a critical part of Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) frameworks. These systems, utilizing AI and firmware-based solutions, aim to proactively secure devices, reduce manual IT tasks, and improve overall resilience against identity-based attacks.

title: Taskbar Not Working? Here's How to Fix It
summary: This article provides various solutions for common Windows 10 taskbar issues, such as freezing, disappearing, or not responding. It covers fixes ranging from decluttering the taskbar and restarting the explorer process to updating Windows and using PowerShell commands. The guide also offers advice for more persistent problems like creating a new user account or performing a system restore.

title: Earnings Infographic: Microsoft (MSFT) Q1 revenue up 11%; earnings beat
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) reported stronger-than-expected earnings and revenues for the first quarter of fiscal year 2023, with revenues increasing 11% to $50.12 billion. Despite a drop in net profit to $17.56 billion or $2.35 per share, the results surpassed market projections. CEO Satya Nadella highlighted digital technology as a "tailwind" in challenging times, focusing on efficiency for customers and disciplined cost management.

title: Sony Electronics Announces the New LinkBuds S "Earth Blue" Noise Canceling Truly Wireless Headphones, Made from Water Dispenser Bottles¹ and New Model "LinkBuds UC for Microsoft Teams"
summary: Sony Electronics has announced new LinkBuds S "Earth Blue" noise-canceling truly wireless headphones, made from recycled water bottle materials, and a new "LinkBuds UC for Microsoft Teams" model. The "Earth Blue" LinkBuds S support Sony's "Road to Zero" environmental plan and will lead to a $500,000 donation to Conservation International. Additionally, a software update will bring multi-point connection to the LinkBuds series and WF-1000XM4 headphones, while the LinkBuds UC for Microsoft Teams offers enhanced features for online meetings.

title: Valence Security raises new cash to secure the SaaS app supply chain
summary: Valence Security has raised $25 million in a Series A round, bringing its total funding to $32 million, to enhance its SaaS app supply chain security platform. The company aims to address the growing risks associated with interconnected SaaS applications, including misconfigurations and data breaches. The new capital will be used for product development and to double its 25-person team.

title: iShares S&P 100 ETF OEF
summary: This article provides current pricing and key metrics for the iShares S&P 100 ETF (OEF), highlighting its stability and offering guidance on how to invest through eToro. It details that OEF aims to replicate the S&P 100, comprising 100 top US companies like Apple and Microsoft. The piece also includes a section on trending ETFs and educational resources for investors.

title: Microsoft and Sony in corporate battle over Call of Duty access
summary: Microsoft's pending $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard has ignited a corporate battle with Sony over the future of the popular Call of Duty franchise. Sony fears losing access to the "must-have" title on its PlayStation platform if Microsoft makes it exclusive to Xbox or GamePass. Regulators in the U.S., UK, and EU are scrutinizing the deal amid Sony's concerns, while Microsoft assures Call of Duty will remain on PlayStation for at least "several more years."

title: 2022 Diversity & Inclusion Report: Driving progress through greater accountability and transparency
summary: Microsoft released its 2022 Global Diversity & Inclusion Report, highlighting significant progress in workforce diversity and representation across various employee communities. The report emphasizes increased transparency with new data points on multiracial employees, military experience, workforce exits, and additional pay analysis. Microsoft reaffirms its commitment to pay equity and its Racial Equity Initiative goals, while also focusing on fostering an inclusive culture.

title: Chris Gondran of Home Bank - How to Protect Yourself and Your Assets from Cybersecurity Thieves
summary: Chris Gondran, Senior Vice President at Home Bank, offers critical advice on protecting digital assets from cybercriminals, emphasizing strong password practices, vigilance against social engineering scams (phishing, vishing, smishing), and the importance of multi-factor authentication and data backups. The article highlights how human error is a major cause of breaches and directs readers to resources like ic3.gov, stopthinkconnect.org, cisa.gov, and haveibeenpwned.com for further protection and reporting.

title: How SMBC Group became a leader in transformation and digital perseverance
summary: SMBC Group, one of Japan's largest banks, is undergoing a significant digital transformation by partnering with Microsoft as its strategic cloud provider. This collaboration aims to modernize IT infrastructure, develop innovative products like Embedded Finance and digital supply chain solutions, and support customers in achieving their sustainability goals. The article highlights how SMBC's approach to digital transformation also involves a cultural shift, emphasizing talent empowerment and a customer-first mindset, positioning them for "digital perseverance" in a changing industry.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Infrastructure and Platform Services
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Infrastructure and Platform Services for the ninth consecutive year, also placing furthest on the Completeness of Vision axis for the first time. The article highlights Microsoft's commitment to hybrid and multicloud solutions, continuous innovation in Azure services like Azure Arc and Microsoft Dev Box, and investments in mission-critical workloads and global datacenter expansion. It emphasizes Microsoft's dedication to helping customers optimize cloud investments and achieve their business goals amidst economic uncertainties.

title: Johnson Controls Honors Intaleq with OpenBlue Pioneers Award for First of Its Kind Stadium Technology at World Cup Qatar 2022
summary: Johnson Controls has awarded Intaleq with the OpenBlue Pioneers Award for creating a revolutionary connected digital command center for the World Cup Qatar 2022. This solution, powered by Johnson Controls OpenBlue and Microsoft Azure, transforms eight World Cup stadiums into one connected digital space, enabling real-time situational awareness and incident management. The technology utilizes "3D digital twins" for each stadium, providing live data on safety, comfort, and sustainability to significantly enhance security and operational efficiency during the event.

title: Newfold Digital completes MarkMonitor acquisition
summary: Newfold Digital has finalized its acquisition of MarkMonitor for $302.5 million from Clarivate Plc. MarkMonitor, a domain name registrar serving major brands like Amazon and Microsoft, will join Newfold Digital's portfolio, which includes Network Solutions and Register.com. This acquisition marks another instance of MarkMonitor changing private equity ownership, as Newfold Digital is backed by Clearlake Capital Group and Siris Capital Group.

title: A dash of flexibility enhances security at International Flavors and Fragrances
summary: International Flavors and Fragrances (IFF) is implementing a flexible security strategy, dubbed "security by obscurity," to secure its systems following a merger that doubled its size. Director of Information Security Architecture, Michael Strause, emphasizes identity as the primary defense in their zero-trust architecture, which was accelerated by the shift to hybrid work. IFF utilizes Zscaler Private Access (ZPA) for user-to-application segmentation, focusing on crown jewel applications and categorizing employees by function to manage complexity and ensure business agility.

title: Microsoft empowers partners to drive innovation in Sri Lanka and the Maldives
summary: Microsoft recently held its annual Partner Connect event in Colombo, Sri Lanka, to foster innovation and resilience within its partner network in Sri Lanka and the Maldives. The event emphasized enabling partners to help customers "Do More with Less" through five digital imperatives and recognized partners who successfully utilized Microsoft technologies. Microsoft also outlined its commitment to supporting governmental digital transformation and skills development in both countries.

title: SMBC partners Microsoft to integrate consumer and corporate businesses - Digital Transformation - Finance
summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation Group (SMBC) is collaborating with Microsoft to enhance its digital transformation, focusing on "embedded finance" to integrate consumer and corporate businesses. This partnership aims to develop B2B2C platforms that offer customized financial solutions, reduce costs, and deliver personalized customer experiences globally. SMBC is leveraging Microsoft's cloud platform to create diverse services, including a cloud-based solution for supply chain issues and AI engines for digitizing trade finance, while also investing in non-financial digital tools and employee upskilling.

title: Cultivating a culture of learning at Microsoft with Viva Learning
summary: Microsoft is using its internal deployment of Microsoft Viva Learning to foster a strong learning culture, empower employees, and drive innovation. This platform centralizes learning experiences within Microsoft Teams, providing personalized recommendations and integrating existing learning resources. By acting as "Customer Zero," Microsoft uses its own internal experiences to refine Viva Learning, ensuring it meets both employee and customer needs.

title: Raytheon Technologies collaborates with Microsoft to accelerate key business priorities through cloud adoption
summary: Raytheon Technologies is deepening its collaboration with Microsoft Corp. (Nasdaq: MSFT) to equip its employees with cloud-based tools, technologies, and platforms to enhance collaboration, optimize operations, and unlock intelligent insights. This partnership leverages Microsoft 365 and Azure to reduce data center footprint, accelerate speed-to-market for products, and drive data-driven insights through predictive analytics. The initiative aims to support business growth and operational excellence within the aerospace and defense company.

title: 3 Hydrogen Stocks That Will Make You a Millionaire by 2030
summary: This article identifies three hydrogen stocks – Plug Power (PLUG), Bloom Energy (BE), and Ballard Power Systems (BLDP) – as potential millionaire-makers by 2030. It highlights hydrogen's versatility and the growing $11 trillion hydrogen economy, driven by energy and transportation needs. Each company is presented with its unique strengths, such as Plug Power's electrolyzer business and Microsoft partnership, Bloom Energy's solid-oxide fuel cells and financial targets, and Ballard Power Systems' leadership in fuel cells for heavy-duty vehicles.

title: At the frontier of the energy transition
summary: Pentagreen Capital, a joint venture between HSBC and Temasek, plans to invest USD1 billion in sustainable infrastructure projects in Southeast Asia, focusing on "marginally bankable" projects that traditional finance sources deem too risky or too small. CEO Marat Zapparov explains that they will target renewable energy, energy storage, water and waste treatment, and sustainable transportation, including innovative solutions like battery swap models for electric vehicles and plastic waste recycling infrastructure. The venture employs blended finance, combining private sector capital with more risk-tolerant catalytic capital, and benefits from HSBC’s commitment to sustainability, expertise, and global network.

title: Trimble Construction Cloud Powered by Microsoft Azure Launches at Trimble Dimensions+ Conference
summary: Trimble has launched the Trimble Construction Cloud, powered by Microsoft Azure, at the Trimble Dimensions+ Conference. This industry-specific cloud platform aims to streamline construction projects by connecting project teams, data, processes, and stakeholders across all phases of construction. It serves as the new backbone for Trimble Construction One, enabling better design, construction, operation, and management of projects through improved collaboration and data visibility.

title: EdgeDB raises $15M for open source graph-relational database
summary: EdgeDB, a startup founded in 2019, has secured $15 million in Series A funding for its open-source graph-relational database technology. The platform, which uses PostgreSQL as its foundation and features a query language called EdgeQL, aims to resolve the object-relational impedance mismatch by treating objects as primary entities rather than tables. EdgeDB plans to use the funding to further develop its open-source technology and launch a cloud service in 2023.

title: Top 10 Quantum Companies Ranked According To Their Number Of Quantum Patents
summary: IPRdaily has released a ranking of the top quantum companies based on their number of quantum patents, highlighting the rapid growth and international participation in the quantum computing industry. IBM leads the list with 1323 patents, followed by Google and D-wave, demonstrating the significant investment by major tech firms. The analysis also revealed that the U.S., China, Japan, and Canada are the leading countries in patent holdings.

title: Insight sows seed of cloud analytics for GrainCorp
summary: Insight Enterprises has developed a new cloud infrastructure for GrainCorp, leveraging Microsoft Azure services to enhance its analytics capabilities. This infrastructure addresses previous ad-hoc data processes, improving efficiency and enabling more robust decision-making for GrainCorp, particularly in areas like freight optimization and supply-demand matching. The project has already demonstrated significant improvements in data consistency and the speed of critical operations.

title: Catawba County News
summary: Microsoft Corp. plans to invest a minimum of $1 billion in the phased development of four datacenters in Catawba County over the next 10 years. These datacenters, located in Conover, Hickory, and Maiden, are expected to create at least 50 new jobs. The announcement was made by leaders from Catawba County, the Cities of Conover and Hickory, the Town of Maiden, and the Catawba County Economic Development Corporation.

title: Microsoft plans to put $1 billion into 4 Catawba data centers
summary: Microsoft is investing at least $1 billion over 10 years to build four data centers in Catawba County, North Carolina. This project, spanning Conover, Hickory, and Maiden, is expected to create about 50 new jobs and has received economic development incentives from local authorities.

title: AMD launches data center CPU 'Genoa', taps Google, Microsoft as customers (Nov. 10)
summary: AMD has launched its fourth-generation EPYC processor, codenamed "Genoa," for data centers, securing major customers like Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle. This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

title: Has the promise of Enterprise Cloud Computing failed to deliver?
summary: The article discusses how the initial promise of enterprise cloud computing is facing scrutiny due to rising complexity, costs, and management challenges, despite its early benefits in streamlining IT infrastructure. It highlights issues like multi-cloud unwieldiness, vendor lock-in, and security concerns, emphasizing the need for disciplined planning and focused strategy to maximize cloud benefits and avoid poor ROI, especially with economic headwinds.

title: Reza Partners with Perfect Corp. to Enable Virtual Try On of Jewelry
summary: French jewelry brand Reza has collaborated with Perfect Corp. to integrate AI and AR virtual try-on technology into its new eCommerce website. This technology, featuring Perfect Corp.'s AgileHand, allows customers to virtually try on rings, earrings, and bracelets in real-time digital renderings. The partnership aims to enhance the luxury accessories shopping experience by providing an immersive and impactful virtual try-on solution.

title: Dow pops 1,200 points, S&P 500 jumps 5% in biggest rally in two years after light inflation report
summary: Stocks experienced their largest rally since 2020 following an October consumer price index report that suggested inflation might have peaked. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 1,201.43 points (3.7%), the S&P 500 rose 5.54%, and the Nasdaq Composite jumped 7.35%. This market reaction was driven by a lower-than-expected CPI reading, leading traders to anticipate a less aggressive stance from the Federal Reserve regarding interest rate hikes.

title: Amazon to lay off 10,000 people: Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly planning to lay off approximately 10,000 corporate and technology employees, focusing on its devices unit (including Alexa), retail division, and human resources. This move comes as the company, which recently froze corporate hiring, prepares for a potential economic downturn and slowing consumer demand, following similar job cuts by other major tech companies like Meta and Twitter.

title: Microsoft unveils ‘Supply Chain Center’ with Kraft Heinz, Daimler among customers
summary: Microsoft has launched a new "Supply Chain Center" and "Supply Chain Platform" to integrate various supply chain systems and offer a unified solution. Major customers like Kraft Heinz North America and Daimler Truck North America are already utilizing the technology to enhance agility and optimize costs. The new offerings aim to connect data across different ERPs and third-party solutions, including SAP and Oracle, providing a comprehensive view of the supply chain.

title: Looka Collaborates with Leading Business Platforms to Help Grow Small Business
summary: Looka, an AI-powered design and branding platform, has partnered with leading business platforms like Microsoft, HubSpot, and Square to offer exclusive discounts and tools to small business owners. Through Looka's Brand Kit Subscription, entrepreneurs can access over $3,000 worth of exclusive offers to help streamline and automate tasks, reducing the costs and challenges of launching and scaling a business. These offerings span marketing, sales, customer success, CRM, finance, and payroll, aiming to empower small businesses for success.

title: Kraft Heinz Bets (Even) Bigger On Supply Chain Visibility
summary: Kraft Heinz is deepening its partnership with Microsoft, adopting the new Supply Chain Center to enhance supply chain visibility through AI-backed capabilities. This initiative is part of a larger transformation to integrate and centralize data, allowing for more agile risk assessment and improved product delivery across North America. The platform leverages AI models for demand and supply insights, order management automation, and Teams integration to streamline operations and decision-making.

title: Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella visits Japan, encourages local organizations to ‘do more with less’
summary: Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella visited Japan to highlight how innovation and digital technology, particularly the Microsoft Cloud, are supporting the country's developers, startups, and companies. During his "Empowering Japan's Future" event, Nadella emphasized the importance of digital transformation for organizations to achieve more with fewer resources. He showcased Japanese innovations using Microsoft's Power Platform and collaborative efforts with startups like AGRIST Inc. to improve efficiency and productivity.

title: Microsoft Shareholders | Who Owns The Most Shares of Microsoft?
summary: This article identifies the major shareholders of Microsoft (MSFT) amidst a 27% year-to-date loss in its stock value. It details the top institutional holders like The Vanguard Group and Capital Group, as well as significant individual insiders including founder Bill Gates and CEO Satya Nadella, while also providing an overview of Microsoft's business segments.

title: Microsoft Cloud Summit 2022: Empowering customers in Malaysia to do more with less with Microsoft technologies
summary: The Microsoft Cloud Summit 2022 in Malaysia focused on how Microsoft Cloud technologies empower organizations to "do more with less." The event highlighted five key imperatives: being data-driven with Azure, delivering efficiency with AI and automation, innovating with a cloud developer platform, re-energizing the workforce with Microsoft 365, and protecting data with advanced security solutions. Microsoft also reiterated its commitment to upskill 1 million Malaysians by 2023 through various partnerships.

title: C.J. Mahoney
summary: C.J. Mahoney is the Deputy General Counsel for U.S. International Trade and National Security at Microsoft, a position he assumed in 2021 after serving as Deputy United States Trade Representative from 2018 to 2020. In his previous role, he was instrumental in negotiating the USMCA agreement, leading discussions on its landmark labor chapter, and overseeing USTR's digital trade portfolio. Mahoney's career also includes work as a partner at Williams & Connolly LLP and a clerkship for Associate Justice Anthony Kennedy.

title: Revealed: Just one in four Trump-endorsed candidates using Truth Social actively
summary: A Columbia Journalism Review investigation found that only 25% of candidates endorsed by Donald Trump for Senate, House, and gubernatorial elections actively use Truth Social. Despite this low engagement from even Trump-aligned politicians, the platform remains a significant hub for right-wing extremism and serves as Trump's primary communication channel after his ban from mainstream social media. The article highlights Truth Social's struggles with user attraction, advertising, and internal issues, alongside two federal investigations that jeopardize its future funding.

title: Nvidia beats revenue estimates, reassures investors
summary: Nvidia reported better-than-expected revenue for the fiscal third quarter, driven by strong demand for its AI and data-center chips, despite a slowdown in the PC market and challenges in China. The company's data center unit saw a 31% sales increase, offsetting a 51% drop in its gaming business. Although the fourth-quarter outlook was slightly below some estimates, it was sufficient to alleviate investor concerns, with CEO Jensen Huang acknowledging ongoing macroeconomic difficulties and COVID-19 restrictions in China.

title: Is DigitalOcean Stock a Buy Now?
summary: DigitalOcean is presented as a cloud infrastructure company targeting small businesses, developers, and startups, distinguishing itself from giants like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud with competitive pricing. The article highlights DigitalOcean's strong financial performance in Q3, including significant revenue growth and ambitious targets for 2024, despite considerations about stock-based compensation affecting free cash flow. The author concludes that DigitalOcean is a strong buy due to its rapid growth, reasonable stock valuation, and focused niche market.

title: Opinion | How One of the Country’s Most Storied C.E.O.s Destroyed His Legacy (Published 2022)
summary: The article discusses Jack Welch's regret over his choice of successor at General Electric, highlighting his belief that he prioritized charm over competence. Author William D. Cohan explores Welch's complex legacy, acknowledging his leadership while also criticizing his succession process. The piece delves into the debate surrounding Welch's impact on GE, questioning whether his focus on short-term profits ultimately harmed the company.

title: Kraft Heinz Taps Janelle Orozco for North American CPO Role
summary: Kraft Heinz has appointed Janelle Orozco as the North America Chief Procurement Officer, a newly created role aimed at enhancing the company's procurement capabilities in the region. Orozco, previously CPO at Diageo, will report to Carlos Abrams-Rivera and Marcos Eloi, and her appointment signifies a major step in transforming the procurement function into a competitive advantage for Kraft Heinz. This move is part of Kraft Heinz's broader strategy to expand its North America footprint and improve supply chain visibility through leadership additions and technological partnerships.

title: Microsoft offered Sony 10-year deal to keep Call of Duty on PlayStation, as regulatory pressure intensifies
summary: Microsoft has offered Sony a 10-year deal to keep Call of Duty on PlayStation, a move made as Microsoft seeks regulatory approval for its acquisition of Activision Blizzard. Sony has reportedly declined the offer and continues to oppose the deal, citing concerns about market competition and harm to consumers. Regulators in key markets like the UK, US, and EU remain skeptical about the acquisition.

title: Maria Alvarez Joins Alkami Board of Directors
summary: Maria Alvarez, VP, AI Platform, Cloud and AI Division at Microsoft Corporation, will join Alkami Technology, Inc.'s Board of Directors effective January 1, 2023. She brings extensive experience in product strategy, operational and technical excellence, and digital transformation. This appointment follows the departure of Merline Saintil and Mike Hansen from the board.

title: Microsoft signs 900MW PPAs for Ireland, 28% of nation's target for 2030
summary: Microsoft has secured Corporate Power Purchase Agreements (CPPAs) for 900MW to power its Irish data centers, covering 28% of Ireland's 2030 renewable energy target. This multi-year deal with Statkraft, Energia Group, and Power Capital Renewable Energy will bring new renewable power plants online, aiming for 100% renewable energy for Microsoft's Irish data centers by 2025. The announcement comes amidst challenges for data center development in Ireland due to power grid limitations.

title: As the world goes digital, datacenters that make the cloud work look to renewable energy sources
summary: The article discusses the parallel trends of digitization and the shift to renewable energy, focusing on how datacenters are integrating sustainable practices. It highlights Microsoft's significant role in this transition through power purchase agreements (PPAs) that support new renewable energy projects. The piece also details innovative approaches by Microsoft datacenters in Europe, such as using waste heat for district heating and employing batteries for grid services, underscoring their commitment to carbon reduction and a sustainable future.

title: Microsoft empowers microentrepreneurs with digital skills and tools; From Jeli to the world
summary: Microsoft, in partnership with social enterprise Me.reka and MyDIGITAL Corporation, launched the 'Program Digital Usahawan Jeli' to equip microentrepreneurs in Jeli, Kelantan, with digital skills. Five selected entrepreneurs underwent a three-month virtual mentorship program focusing on sales, social media, and partnerships, leading to significant business growth and inspiring the 'Digital Entrepreneur Skills and Tools in a Box Programme' to scale these efforts. This initiative aims to bridge the digital divide and empower Malaysians, with Microsoft having achieved over 60% of its target to upskill 1 million Malaysians by 2023.

title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF DFAC
summary: This article provides details on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC), including its current price, key metrics, and how to invest in it through eToro. DFAC aims to replicate the performance of the Russell 3000 Index and holds investments in over 2,500 US companies across various sectors, with major holdings like Apple and Microsoft. The ETF, established in 2007 by Dimensional Fund Advisors, had net assets of $14.7 billion as of September 2022.

title: Employers Rethink Need for College Degrees in Tight Labor Market
summary: A tight labor market is leading more employers, including Google, Delta Air Lines, and IBM, to reduce college degree requirements for certain positions, focusing instead on skills and experience. This trend has resulted in a decline in job postings requiring bachelor's degrees compared to pre-pandemic levels. The shift is influenced by high demand for workers, low unemployment, and efforts to address racial disparities in the workplace.

title: BlackBerry makes its QNX operating system available on AWS
summary: BlackBerry has announced the availability of its QNX operating system on Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud platform at AWS re:Invent 2022. QNX, a real-time operating system used in over 215 million vehicles globally, will enable automakers to develop and test new vehicle software more efficiently in the cloud using Amazon EC2 instances. This move also extends QNX's potential use to other industries like robotics, medical equipment, and aerospace.

title: Leveraging digital platforms to promote food security and sustainable agriculture in Africa
summary: IFC and Microsoft have partnered to support digital transformation in Africa's agriculture sector, focusing on promoting food security and sustainable agriculture. This collaboration aims to make digital tools and training accessible to small-scale farmers and agribusinesses, addressing inefficiencies and climate-related challenges. The initiative leverages tools like Microsoft's AgBot and Community Training applications to provide crucial information and enhance farming practices.

title: Phoenix Data Centers: Colocation in the Silicon Desert
summary: Phoenix, Arizona has become the United States' fourth-largest data center market, boasting over 90 data centers and 600 megawatts of commissioned power. This growth is fueled by lower power costs, abundant real estate, and tax incentives, attracting hyperscalers and major enterprises. The article details the market's key players, connectivity, and the factors contributing to its emergence as a Tier-1 data center hub, while also addressing challenges like climate and water usage concerns.

title: Amazon Launches Supply-Chain Software Service
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has introduced "AWS Supply Chain," a new cloud-based software service designed to help businesses manage and track cargo flows. This move positions Amazon as a competitor to existing supply-chain software providers such as Manhattan Associates and Blue Yonder, and follows a similar launch by Microsoft Corp. The service aims to assist businesses, especially the numerous small and medium-sized businesses in Amazon's marketplace, in gaining better control over their complex supply chains.

title: Amazon Launches Supply-Chain Software Service
summary: Amazon Web Services has launched a new supply-chain software service, entering the burgeoning tech sector that aims to bring more efficiency and visibility to goods management. This move positions Amazon alongside established players like Blue Yonder and SAP, as well as other big operators such as Microsoft, in helping businesses navigate supply chain disruptions. The service is expected to find a ready audience among the thousands of small- and medium-sized businesses on Amazon's third-party marketplace.

title: Spotify CEO renews attack on Apple after Musk's salvo
summary: Spotify's CEO Daniel Ek criticized Apple in a series of tweets, accusing the iPhone maker of stifling innovation and hurting consumers by giving itself every advantage. This attack follows similar criticism from Elon Musk regarding Apple's in-app purchase fees. Spotify has previously filed antitrust complaints against Apple over its 30% charge, which it claims artificially inflates its prices.

title: Salesforce shares tumble after surprise exit of co-CEO Taylor
summary: Salesforce Inc (CRM.N) shares fell by about 10% after co-CEO Bret Taylor's unexpected departure, raising concerns about the company's co-CEO leadership model and slowing revenue growth. This exit prompted at least 17 brokerages to cut their price targets on the stock, with analysts noting it as a significant blow given Taylor's leadership role in product development. Taylor's departure comes after just one year in the co-CEO role, leaving co-founder Marc Benioff as the sole top boss.

title: BJ’s connects advertisers with customers across channels
summary: BJ's Wholesale Club is launching its own retail media network called BJ’s Media Edge, in collaboration with Microsoft PromoteIQ. This platform aims to connect advertisers with its 6.5 million members across various channels, providing real-time analytics and data. This move follows a similar trend in retail, with Sam's Club also expanding its advertising solutions.

title: 7 Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy for the Next 10 Years
summary: This article highlights seven quantum computing stocks poised for significant growth over the next decade, as quantum computing transitions from theory to reality and disrupts various industries. It details how major tech companies like Alphabet, IBM, and Microsoft are leading the charge, alongside smaller players like Quantum Computing and Chinese giants Alibaba and Baidu, as well as Intel in hardware development. The author emphasizes the transformative power of quantum computing and the potential for these companies to be big winners.

title: Microsoft advances plan for new $400M Loudoun data center
summary: Microsoft is nearing final permit approval for a new $400 million data center project in Loudoun County, Virginia. The site plan for this fast-track development has already received approval. This initiative is part of a larger trend of data center development in the region.

title: How suite of dividend growth ETFs brings quality to the fore
summary: In an environment of market volatility and economic uncertainty, CI WisdomTree ETFs offer a quality-focused core portfolio solution that has outperformed benchmarks. These dividend growth ETFs, managed by CI Global Asset Management, employ a unique methodology to identify companies with strong fundamentals, sustainable earnings, and resilient business models, screening for quality and growth metrics. This strategy aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure and long-term stability by avoiding overvalued companies and focusing on profitable dividend payers, proving particularly effective during periods of economic contraction.

title: Microsoft Is Raising Price of New Xbox Games to $70 Starting Next Year
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) announced it will increase the price of its new Xbox games to $70 starting in 2023, following similar moves by other major gaming companies. This $10 price hike will apply to next-generation titles like Starfield, Redfall, and Forza Motorsport, with the company stating it reflects the content's scale and technical complexity. These games will also be accessible through Microsoft’s Game Pass subscription service.

title: Email hosting service Rackspace says it's facing a ransomware attack
summary: Rackspace Technology, a major cloud and email hosting provider, announced a ransomware attack is responsible for a widespread outage that has kept some customers from their email inboxes since Friday. The company revealed the attack may lead to revenue loss for its Hosted Exchange business and has hired a cyber defense firm to investigate, but cannot provide a restoration timeline. Customers are complaining about a lack of communication and support as they are advised to migrate to Microsoft 365, with concerns about data security and the difficulty of the migration process.

title: Artificial Intelligence and the Renaissance of Sports
summary: This article explores how artificial intelligence is revolutionizing the world of sports across various domains. It highlights AI's role in personalized training and diet plans, performance analysis, and player scouting and recruiting. The author provides examples such as LaLiga's Granada CF using AI for nutritional planning and professional leagues like the NBA and NFL utilizing sensors for performance tracking.

title: Building the future of retail with Adobe and Dynamics 365
summary: Microsoft is revamping its online store on microsoft.com by integrating Adobe Experience Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics 365, and Microsoft Azure. This initiative aims to improve content management efficiency for developers and create a more accessible and intuitive interface for customers. The shift replaces an internal custom solution with a suite of Adobe products linked to Dynamics 365 Commerce, hosted on Azure, to enhance scalability and adaptability for Microsoft Store's digital storefronts.

title: Pentagon Divides Big Cloud-Computing Deal Among 4 Firms (Published 2022)
summary: The Pentagon awarded lucrative cloud-computing contracts, potentially valued at up to $9 billion, to Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Oracle. This decision marks a significant shift from an earlier attempt to award a sole contract to Microsoft, which was later scrapped after legal challenges and a recognition that the department's needs had evolved. The new Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability project aims to provide comprehensive cloud services across all security domains and classification levels for the Defense Department.

title: F.T.C. Sues to Block Microsoft’s $69 Billion Acquisition of Activision (Published 2022)
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has sued to block Microsoft's $69 billion acquisition of video game maker Activision Blizzard, citing concerns that the deal would harm consumers by allowing Microsoft to withhold popular games like Call of Duty from rivals. This aggressive move by the F.T.C. signals a stronger regulatory stance against the expansion of big tech companies. Microsoft has stated its intention to fight the lawsuit in court to proceed with the acquisition.

title: Microsoft’s law firm diversity program: celebrating top performers in 2022
summary: Microsoft's Chief Legal Officer, Hossein Nowbar, discusses the company's 2022 Law Firm Diversity Program (LFDP) results. The program, now in its 14th year, incentivizes law firms to achieve diversity representation goals, focusing on increasing diversity in leadership roles. While overall progress in diverse partnership ranks saw modest increases, Microsoft celebrates firms that have significantly advanced diversity within their organizations.

title: Coca-Cola United automates over 50,000 orders in complex SAP invoicing process with Microsoft Power Platform
summary: Coca-Cola United has significantly improved its operational efficiency by using Microsoft Power Platform to automate two key processes: on-demand shipment requests and a complex SAP invoicing process. The automation reduced order validation time from half a day to seconds and enabled the processing of over 50,000 orders, freeing up valuable employee time and supporting strategic growth. The company plans to continue automating more business processes using Microsoft Power Automate RPA capabilities, further reducing labor costs and improving accuracy.

title: Microsoft acquires Lumenisity®, an innovator in hollow core fiber (HCF) cable
summary: Microsoft has acquired Lumenisity® Limited, a leader in hollow core fiber (HCF) solutions, to enhance its global cloud infrastructure and address the strict latency and security requirements of its Cloud Platform and Services customers. Lumenisity's HCF technology offers increased speed, lower latency, enhanced security, and lower costs compared to traditional fiber, benefiting sectors like healthcare, financial services, manufacturing, retail, and government. This acquisition will enable Microsoft to accelerate innovations in networking and infrastructure by integrating Lumenisity’s technology and expert team.

title: New Technique Discovered To Bypass Web Application Firewalls (WAF) Of Several Vendors
summary: Cybersecurity experts at Claroty have developed a new generic method to bypass Web Application Firewalls (WAFs) from multiple leading manufacturers. This technique was discovered while researching a SQL injection flaw in Cambium Networks' wireless device management platform. The bypass abuses the default JSON syntax support in SQL engines to craft new, less common SQLi payloads that can evade WAF detection.

title: Consoles and Competition
summary: This article traces the tumultuous history of the video game console market, highlighting the shifting dynamics between hardware manufacturers and third-party developers, from Atari's integrated system to Sony's third-party focus and later emphasis on exclusives. It critically examines the FTC's attempt to block Microsoft's acquisition of Activision, arguing that the FTC's concerns fail to understand the industry's dynamic evolution and Microsoft's innovative subscription-based business model, which offers a competitive alternative to Sony's platform-locking exclusive strategy. The author suggests that the FTC is trying to preserve an outdated model of competition and is missing the real antitrust issues concerning dominant storefronts.

title: Microsoft invests $2 billion in London Stock Exchange cloud deal
summary: Microsoft is acquiring a 4% stake in the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) for $2 billion, marking a significant cloud partnership between the two entities. This deal aims to integrate Microsoft's applications with LSEG's products, broaden its customer base, and explore shifting trading activities to the cloud. The move highlights the increasing convergence between Big Tech and financial services, drawing scrutiny from regulators concerned about concentration risks in cloud providers.

title: Microsoft, Nike and Common Energy partner on community solar in Oregon
summary: Microsoft, Nike, and Common Energy have partnered on the Skyward Community Solar project in Oregon, a 2.5 MWac facility now fully operational and subscribed. The project, owned by Standard Solar, will generate 3.6 million kilowatt-hours of clean energy annually, feeding into the Portland General Electric grid. Microsoft is the primary commercial subscriber, while Nike employees subscribe to the residential portion, and 10% of the generation is allocated to low- and moderate-income households receiving substantial bill discounts.

title: Annual Shareholders Meeting 2022
summary: Microsoft's Annual Shareholders Meeting for 2022 covered financial performance, strategic priorities, and shareholder proposals. Key topics included record financial results in FY22, investments in AI and cloud computing, and responses to shareholder concerns regarding diversity, fair chance employment, climate change, government contracts, and tax transparency. The leadership team also discussed balancing strategic growth with cost management and the ongoing FTC lawsuit regarding the Activision Blizzard acquisition.

title: Microsoft and Viasat Partner to Provide Satellite Internet Services to Underrepresented Communities in Africa
summary: Microsoft and Viasat have partnered to provide satellite internet services to 10 million people globally, with a focus on connecting 5 million people in Africa by 2025 through Microsoft's Airband initiative. The project, starting in Angola, Egypt, and Senegal, aims to bridge the digital divide by offering services like telehealth and distance learning. This collaboration leverages Microsoft's existing relationship with Viasat Azure Space and benefits from simplified licensing procedures in many African countries.

title: Microsoft and Viasat Partner to Provide Satellite Internet Services to Underrepresented Communities in Africa
summary: Microsoft and Viasat have partnered to provide satellite internet services to 10 million people globally, including 5 million across Africa by 2025, through Microsoft's Airband initiative. The project, which begins in Angola, Egypt, and Senegal, aims to bridge the digital divide by offering telehealth, distance learning, and other services. This collaboration also leverages the existing relationship between Viasat and Microsoft Azure Space to enhance satellite connectivity and address the lack of internet access in Africa, where simplified licensing procedures are facilitating global companies' efforts.

title: Microsoft announces the phased rollout of the EU Data Boundary for the Microsoft Cloud begins January 1, 2023
summary: Microsoft is initiating a phased rollout of its EU Data Boundary solution for public sector and commercial customers in the EU and EFTA starting January 1, 2023. This service enhancement will allow customers to store and process their data within the EU Data Boundary for Microsoft 365, Azure, Power Platform, and Dynamics 365, significantly reducing data flows outside of Europe. Microsoft also plans to provide detailed transparency documentation and a roadmap to assist customers with planning for this industry-leading data residency solution.

title: Microsoft names new Chief Sustainability Officer
summary: Microsoft has appointed Melanie Nakagawa as its new Chief Sustainability Officer, reporting directly to President & Vice Chair Brad Smith. Nakagawa, with nearly two decades of experience in environmental sustainability, will lead Microsoft's company-wide environmental initiatives, focusing on achieving carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste goals by 2030, accelerating innovation for customers, and partnering with external organizations for global sustainability progress. Her appointment comes as Microsoft accelerates its sustainability efforts amid increasing global climate challenges discussed at COP27.

title: MSCI ONE Aims to Help Investors Turn Data Into Insights
summary: MSCI Inc. has launched MSCI ONE, an open architecture technology platform built on Microsoft Azure, designed to help investors convert vast amounts of data into actionable insights for investment decisions. The platform provides next-generation quantitative investment analytics through a modern web-based experience, combining flexible distribution, cloud delivery, dashboards, and visualizations. MSCI ONE offers single-point access to various services like MSCI Risk Insights, Climate Solutions, and its Developer Community, emphasizing improved client experience through transformational technology.

title: T-Mobile Supports 5G Rollout with Azure Synapse Analytics and Power BI
summary: T-Mobile leveraged Microsoft Azure Synapse Analytics and Power BI to build a robust data lakehouse solution, significantly improving its data reporting and decision-making capabilities for its nationwide 5G network rollout. This centralized data architecture allowed T-Mobile to overcome challenges with disparate data sources, streamline supply chain and business-critical information, and enhance security and performance. The integration of Power BI with Azure facilitated the creation of powerful dashboards, enabling data-driven insights for procurement and supply chain management and supporting both strategic and operational users.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Global Industrial IoT Platforms
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Global Industrial IoT Platforms for the third consecutive year. The article highlights Microsoft's continued innovation and investment in industrial IoT, emphasizing key areas such as IT/OT convergence, cloud and on-site innovations, digital twins and the metaverse, security advancements, and collaborations with partners. Microsoft's Azure IoT platform aims to help industries improve operations to become more efficient, agile, and sustainable.

title: Gaming peripheral market size to increase by USD 5,610.35 million: APAC will account for 35% of the market's growth during the forecast period - Technavio
summary: The global gaming peripheral market is projected to grow by USD 5,610.35 million at a CAGR of 9.41% during the forecast period. The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is expected to contribute 35% of this growth, driven by an increase in gamers adopting gaming as a career. Key market drivers include the rising popularity of e-sports and advanced gaming consoles, while challenges include counterfeit products and high peripheral costs.

title: 7 Cybersecurity Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Long Haul
summary: This article identifies seven cybersecurity stocks and an ETF that investors should consider buying and holding for the long haul, despite the current tech downturn. The author emphasizes the increasing essentiality of cybersecurity for businesses and governments, projecting significant market growth. Featured companies include Palo Alto Networks, Microsoft, Palantir, Zscaler, CrowdStrike Holdings, and Fortinet, alongside the Global X Cybersecurity ETF (BUG).

title: Microsoft, Cisco Shareholder Votes Demonstrate Increasing Investor Demand for Tax and Offshore Transparency
summary: Shareholders of Microsoft and Cisco, representing hundreds of billions in investments, voted in favor of proposals calling for public country-by-country reporting on offshore operations and tax practices. This trend indicates growing investor demand for transparency regarding potential risks associated with opaque offshore activities. The FACT Coalition highlights that public country-by-country reporting is becoming inevitable due to global momentum and regulatory considerations in jurisdictions like the EU, Australia, and the U.S.

title: Netflix stock is up 65% since July, but the company faces 5 big problems in 2023
summary: Despite Netflix stock's 65% surge since July, a Wall Street analyst warns of significant headwinds for the company in 2023, including slowing subscriber growth and pressure on average revenue per user (ARPU). The recent ad-supported tier launch has seen struggles, with low adoption and missed viewership guarantees to advertisers. Additionally, the analyst suggests that regulatory actions, exemplified by the FTC's antitrust lawsuit against Microsoft, could impact Netflix's future acquisition prospects and advertising technology commitments.

title: Indoor Location Application Platforms Market is Booming Worldwide | Microsoft, Juniper Networks, Google, Infsoft
summary: A new study by AMA Research evaluates the Global Indoor Location Application Platforms Market, forecasting its trends and market size up to 2027. Key players include Microsoft, Juniper Networks, and Google, among others. The market is driven by increasing applications across beacon and BLE tags, and the inefficiency of GPS indoors, with opportunities in RFID tags and demand for contact-tracing solutions.

title: How King and its Candy Crush IP will help Microsoft boost its mobile revenues
summary: Microsoft is attempting to convince the US FTC that its acquisition of Activision Blizzard would boost its mobile revenues significantly, with its share of global mobile gaming revenues potentially increasing from 0.3% to over 4%. A key driver of this growth is King, the developer behind Candy Crush, which was the largest contributor to Activision Blizzard's mobile growth in Q3 2022. While the FTC has filed a lawsuit to block the $68.7 billion deal over monopoly concerns, Microsoft argues that the acquisition of a single game studio cannot disrupt a highly competitive industry.

title: How the industrial metaverse will transform manufacturing
summary: The industrial metaverse, leveraging virtual and augmented reality, is transforming manufacturing by enhancing training, collaboration, and the use of digital twins. Companies like Lincoln Electric are using VR for faster and more efficient welder training, while automotive manufacturers like Kia and Volvo are using metaverse technologies for remote design collaboration and virtual prototyping, significantly cutting down development time and costs. Despite challenges like high equipment costs and technical difficulties, the industrial metaverse is gaining traction, with its applications expected to expand beyond initial consumer-focused visions.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares MSCI ACWI Index Fund (ACWI), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It details the fund's portfolio as of July 31, 2025, highlighting major tech companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple among its top constituents. The report also includes performance metrics and historical data for ACWI.

title: Despite reports, HPE says it’s not holding acquisition talks with Nutanix
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. (HPE) has denied recent reports that it is in acquisition talks with Nutanix Inc., despite earlier speculation that Nutanix was exploring a sale and HPE had shown interest. This denial led to an almost 8% drop in Nutanix's shares. While acquisition talks between the two have ceased, the possibility of Nutanix being acquired by a private equity firm remains open, reflecting a trend of such firms acquiring enterprise software companies.

title: iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV), including its current stock price, key statistics, asset holdings, and dividend information. It highlights IGV's top holdings as of September 30, 2025, which include Palantir Technologies Inc, Microsoft Corp, Oracle Corp, Salesforce Inc, and ServiceNow Inc, and details the ETF's latest trades and historical performance data.

title: Stock Market Outlook 2023 for the Worst Performing S&P 500 Sectors
summary: This article provides an outlook for the worst-performing S&P 500 sectors in 2022, including Technology, Real Estate, Consumer Discretionary, and Communication Services. It analyzes the performance of key holdings within each sector, their outlook for 2023, and analyst sentiment with options market probabilities for recovery. The forecast indicates that a full recovery to previous highs for these sectors and their major constituents is unlikely in the short term.

title: Our Top 7 Tech Stock Predictions for 2023
summary: This article provides predictions for seven tech stocks in 2023, offering contrarian opportunities for daring investors given the equities sector's downturn. It highlights companies like Microsoft, Alphabet, Intuit, Meta Platforms, PayPal, ZipRecruiter, and IMAX, explaining why each could see an upside despite current economic challenges and market volatility. The author argues that prevailing negativity can create targeting opportunities for intrepid investors.

title: Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) Seems To Use Debt Rather Sparingly
summary: Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) has a healthy financial position, holding US$107.2 billion in cash against US$48.6 billion in debt as of September 2022, resulting in a net cash position of US$58.6 billion. Despite considerable liabilities, the company's large market capitalization of US$1.77 trillion and strong free cash flow generation (80% of EBIT, or US$63 billion) suggest that its debt load is not a major concern. The article concludes that Microsoft's disciplined use of debt and robust cash flow make it well-positioned to manage its financial obligations.

title: Alibaba CEO Daniel Zhang takes charge of cloud infrastructure business
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. CEO Daniel Zhang Yong is taking direct control of the company's cloud computing business unit, Alibaba Cloud, as part of an annual leadership reshuffle and a push for "aggressive development." This move emphasizes the company's increased focus on cloud computing as a key growth driver, especially given its third-largest global market share and competitive pressures in China, alongside a recent service outage. Zhang replaces Jeff Zhang, who will now lead Alibaba's DAMO Academy.

title: Four Ways Technology Will Drive Innovation In 2023
summary: This article outlines four key ways technology will drive innovation in 2023, particularly focusing on the Indian landscape. It highlights India's role as a global innovator, the increasing use of data services for problem-solving, the emergence of hyper-personalized customer experiences, and how sustainability concerns will accelerate cloud adoption. The author emphasizes data's central role in fostering innovation and addressing business challenges.

title: Microsoft (MSFT) Might Release Three Windows 11 Updates in 2023
summary: Microsoft is planning to release up to three Windows 11 updates in 2023, referred to as "Moment" updates, to deliver continuous innovation to users. These updates will introduce new features every few months, departing from the annual feature update schedule. Key improvements include enhancements to the Taskbar, a tablet-optimized experience, and updates to legacy interfaces with modern designs.

title: Does This Acquisition Make Microsoft a Bear Market Buy?
