title: Viasat real-time Earth antennas integrated on Microsoft Azure Orbital
summary: Viasat Inc. and Microsoft Azure Orbital have integrated Viasat's Real-Time Earth (RTE) ground service with Azure, making it accessible through the Azure marketplace. This collaboration equips five RTE sites with high-speed connectivity directly to the Azure cloud, offering commercial satellite operators secure, high-throughput, and low-latency connectivity for their spacecraft. The solution supports Ka-band transmissions, which are crucial for high downlink rates from remote sensing missions and aims to reduce data delivery times.

title: Amazon Launches AI Platform Aimed At Corporate Customers—Joining Google And Microsoft In AI Race
summary: Amazon has launched a new generative AI service called Bedrock, targeting corporate customers through its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud platform. This move distinguishes Amazon from competitors like Microsoft and Google, which have focused more on consumer-facing AI products. Bedrock offers tools for businesses to build and scale their own AI applications, including Amazon's Titan large language models and integrations with other AI startups, aiming to democratize AI development securely and efficiently for enterprises.

title: Bloomberg plans to integrate GPT-style A.I. into its terminal
summary: Bloomberg LP is developing its own AI model, Bloomberg GPT, similar to OpenAI's GPT, specifically trained on financial data. This AI will be integrated into Bloomberg's terminal software to automate financial tasks, such as answering company-specific questions, assessing market sentiment from headlines, and generating financial headlines. The initiative highlights a trend of specialized AI development beyond general models, leveraging proprietary financial datasets to enhance accuracy for industry-specific applications.

title: Chegg announces CheggMate, the new AI companion, built with GPT-4
summary: Chegg, Inc. announced CheggMate, a new AI-enhanced learning service built with OpenAI’s GPT-4, designed to provide personalized and interactive learning experiences for students. CheggMate will leverage Chegg's proprietary data and GPT-4's problem-solving capabilities to offer features like tailored quizzes, guided support, and recommendations. Early access for students is expected in May 2023.

title: Video Game Engine market Future Demand Competitive Situation and Emerging Trends with Historic Forecast 2023-2029-Unity Technologies, Epic Games, Chukong Tech
summary: A new report by Xherald mir provides a comprehensive analysis of the Video Game Engine Market, forecasting its growth from USD 2.14 billion in 2023 to expand at a CAGR of 26.76% by 2029. The report covers market segments, competitive landscape including key players like Unity Technologies and Epic Games, and factors driving and restraining market growth such as increased app store spending and the adoption of AR/VR technologies. It also provides a regional outlook and strategic insights for market stakeholders.

title: 2023 Phishing Report shows 47.2% more attacks
summary: The Zscaler ThreatLabz 2023 Phishing Report reveals a significant 47.2% surge in phishing attacks in 2022 due to increasingly sophisticated techniques and the use of AI tools. The report highlights that education was the most targeted sector, with Microsoft brands and crypto exchanges being frequently exploited. It recommends adopting a Zero Trust architecture and educating employees to combat these evolving threats.

title: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms 2023
summary: This article summarizes the 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, categorizing vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Visionaries, and Niche Players. It details the strengths and cautions for each vendor based on Gartner's evaluation of their ABI platforms. The report highlights Microsoft, Salesforce, and Qlik as leaders, and notes movements of several companies since the 2022 report, particularly Pyramid Analytics' jump to visionary status.

title: CBRE strengthens every employees’ engagement and productivity with Microsoft Viva
summary: CBRE has significantly improved employee engagement and productivity by adopting Microsoft Viva, addressing the challenges of a distributed workforce. The platform, integrated with Microsoft Teams, offers personalized communication through Viva Connections and fosters community building with Viva Engage, enabling easier access to information and promoting social interaction among employees globally. This implementation has streamlined internal communications, enhanced the employee experience, and provided accessible resources for CBRE's diverse workforce, whether they are in the office or on the go.

title: Google DeepMind: Bringing together two world-class AI teams
summary: Google and Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai announced the formation of Google DeepMind, merging Google Research's Brain team and DeepMind. This new unit aims to accelerate progress in AI development, focusing on building more capable, safe, and responsible general AI systems. Demis Hassabis will lead Google DeepMind as CEO, while Jeff Dean will take on the elevated role of Google's Chief Scientist, focusing on future AI research and multimodal AI models.

title: Semiconductor industry electricity consumption to more than double by 2030: study
summary: A Greenpeace East Asia report projects that global semiconductor manufacturing will consume 237 terawatt hours of electricity by 2030, more than doubling current levels and equating to Australia's 2021 electricity consumption. This energy-intensive industry, critical to the tech supply chain, is also set to emit 86 million tonnes of CO2e by 2030, exceeding Portugal's 2021 total emissions. The report criticizes major manufacturers like Samsung and TSMC for their reliance on fossil fuels and lack of ambitious 100% renewable energy commitments by 2030, urging them to accelerate renewable energy development in East Asia.

title: Job cuts in India just a small fraction of global layoffs: Recruitment firms
summary: Recruitment and staffing firms indicate that India is experiencing a significantly lower rate of job cuts compared to global layoffs, with only three to four professionals laid off in India for every 100 globally. While global layoffs, particularly in tech and startups, have been substantial, India's stable economic conditions make it a relatively safe zone for operations, and some companies are even considering relocating downsized operations to India for cost-effectiveness. This trend suggests that the Indian workforce, especially in the IT services sector, is largely shielded from mass layoffs, though some impact is felt in existing startups and the IT product sector.

title: Microsoft to shed more office space with Redmond sublease
summary: Microsoft is planning to sublease more office space in Redmond as part of its efforts to consolidate its office footprint and cut costs. This move aligns with a trend among other major tech companies in the Puget Sound area to reduce their physical office presence. The article also lists related instances of companies like UW, F5, and Salesforce making similar adjustments to their office space.

title: 3 reasons why the Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) could be an excellent investment for beginners
summary: The Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) is presented as an excellent investment option for beginners due to its significant diversification across major developed markets and sectors, its strong long-term performance, and its low management fees. The ETF offers exposure to over 1,400 businesses globally, including tech giants, and has historically delivered solid returns while keeping costs down.

title: LinkedIn Business Highlights from Microsoft’s FY23 Q3 Earnings
summary: LinkedIn's revenue increased by 8% year-over-year in Q3 FY23, with member growth accelerating for the seventh consecutive quarter, reaching over 930 million members globally. The platform saw significant expansion in India, a 73% increase in Gen Z student sign-ups, and continued success in Talent Solutions and Marketing Solutions. LinkedIn is also becoming a hub for AI learning and features, offering over 100 AI courses and new AI-powered tools.

title: Elon Musk Threatens to Sue Microsoft Over Alleged Illegal Data Usage
summary: Elon Musk is threatening to sue Microsoft, alleging unauthorized use of Twitter's data for training its AI models. This comes after Microsoft stopped supporting Twitter on one of its advertising platforms due to Musk's high asking price for API access. Musk claims Microsoft "trained illegally using Twitter data" and sees this as an opportune moment for legal action, especially given Microsoft's $10 billion investment in OpenAI and the integration of GPT-4 into Bing.

title: 2 Invesco ETFs That Offer Meta Exposure
summary: As Meta Platforms Inc. shares surge, two Invesco ETFs—the Invesco NASDAQ Internet ETF (PNQI) and the Invesco Dynamic Media ETF (PBS)—offer significant exposure to the company. Meta is the top holding in both ETFs, with weights of 9.1% and 6.6% respectively. Investors looking to capitalize on Meta's strong performance and broader internet or media sectors may consider these funds.

title: MDLIVE for Cigna cuts wait times by 50 percent by predicting changing healthcare demands with Azure Machine Learning
summary: MDLIVE for Cigna partnered with AIDAN Health to implement Azure Machine Learning for predicting patient demand, leading to a 50 percent reduction in patient wait times. This initiative helps balance medical professional workloads and optimize staffing, particularly critical after the surge in telehealth usage during the COVID-19 pandemic. The advanced forecasting models, developed on Azure, have also saved the company approximately $1 million each busy season in provider incentives.

title: Trimble partners with Microsoft to take on construction’s biggest challenges
summary: Trimble has partnered with Microsoft to develop Trimble Construction Cloud, built on Microsoft Azure, to address the construction industry's monumental challenges, including labor shortages and sustainability goals. This strategic alliance aims to connect disparate systems and data, facilitating digital transformation across construction, agriculture, and transportation. The cloud solution leverages Azure services to enable real-time collaboration, automate workflows, and integrate various technologies, aiming to improve productivity and decision-making for customers.

title: AWS Data Transfer Pricing: Solving Hidden Network Transfer Costs
summary: This article explains the complexities of AWS data transfer pricing, covering costs for data moving between AWS and the internet, as well as within the AWS cloud across regions and Availability Zones. It provides tips for reducing these costs, including optimizing infrastructure design and utilizing tools like NetApp Cloud Volumes ONTAP, which helps minimize data transfer through storage efficiencies. The aim is to help users avoid unexpected spikes in their monthly AWS bills by understanding and controlling hidden network transfer expenses.

title: Survey Signals Pause-and-Pivot Year for CEOs
summary: The 2023 Gartner CEO and Senior Business Executive Survey indicates a shift from short-term reactivity to cautious long-term planning, with CEOs focusing on business productivity and new three- to seven-year growth strategies. While growth remains a top priority, profitability is investors' primary concern, leading to an emphasis on multiyear productivity engineering and the creative application of new technologies like AI and automation. Talent and workforce issues, including shortages and compensation demands, continue to be significant challenges for executives.

title: Palo Alto Networks Unveils Its Cloud Next-Generation Firewall for Microsoft Azure Customers
summary: Palo Alto Networks has launched its Cloud Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) for Microsoft Azure, offered as a fully managed Azure-native ISV service. This solution integrates AI and ML-powered security to protect against known, unknown, and zero-day threats, allowing customers to safely migrate applications to Azure. It provides unparalleled security, ease of use, and consistent security management from on-premises to cloud environments, leveraging existing Panorama consoles.

title: IBM to pause hiring for certain jobs that could be replaced by AI
summary: IBM's CEO, Arvind Krishna, announced that the company will slow down or potentially suspend hiring for non-customer-facing roles, such as human resources, anticipating that AI and automation could replace about 30% of these jobs within five years. Despite this, IBM is actively hiring for revenue-generating and client-facing positions, asserting there is "no blanket hiring ‘pause’ in place." Krishna expects AI to replace many clerical white-collar jobs but believes the net increase in jobs related to AI development will be positive.

title: Msci Kld 400 Social Index: Meaning, Example, FAQs
summary: The MSCI KLD 400 Social Index is a market capitalization-weighted stock index that focuses on large, mid, and small-cap U.S. companies with high environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards, excluding certain industries. Founded as the Domini 400 Social Index, it was a pioneer in socially responsible investing. The index is reviewed quarterly and tends to be dominated by large American technology companies like Microsoft, NVIDIA, Alphabet, Tesla, and Visa.

title: Edgewell moves to SAP on Azure, strengthening BCDR and innovation on the Microsoft Cloud
summary: Edgewell Personal Care successfully migrated its entire SAP estate to SAP on Azure, enhancing business continuity, disaster recovery, and fostering innovation. This move, supported by Khoj Information Technology, Inc., allowed Edgewell to improve agility, reduce costs, and focus on strategic initiatives rather than datacenter management. The migration significantly boosted system availability and provided greater flexibility for scaling workloads and integrating diverse systems.

title: The era of generative AI: Driving transformation in banking
summary: This article discusses the transformative impact of generative AI on the banking industry, highlighting Microsoft's role through Azure OpenAI Service and AI-powered copilots. It focuses on how generative AI can enhance employee productivity, operational efficiency, and customer experience by empowering contact center agents and financial advisors, accelerating content and code generation, and ensuring responsible AI development. The author, Bill Borden, emphasizes the critical role of human supervision in leveraging these new capabilities responsibly and effectively within financial institutions.

title: xCloud generated less than $50 million for Microsoft in 2021, but estimated to become largest cloud gaming service in 2026
summary: Microsoft's xCloud service generated less than $50 million in 2021 but is projected to become the largest cloud gaming service by revenue in 2026, reaching $450 million. This prediction comes from a CMA report related to Microsoft's proposed acquisition of Activision Blizzard, highlighting divergent views on the cloud gaming market size among different research firms. The service's potential dominance was a key reason for the UK regulator's decision to block the Activision Blizzard deal.

title: Redwire, Microsoft, and Marvel are teaming up on a new STEM initiative
summary: Redwire, Microsoft, and Marvel are collaborating on a new STEM initiative to inspire students in space technology. As part of this effort, Redwire will 3D print a Microsoft Zune music player on the International Space Station, similar to the one seen in "Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3." This project aims to engage students with real-life space endeavors and educational materials about microgravity and additive manufacturing.

title: Hanumakonda: KTR inaugurates 4 new companies; IT sector hired 1.5L people last year
summary: Telangana's IT and Industries Minister K.T. Rama Rao inaugurated four new IT companies in Hanamkonda (LTI Mindtree, Genpact, HRH Next, and Hexad Solutions Pvt. Ltd.), encouraging local youth for employment and entrepreneurship. Hyderabad continues to be a major IT hub, attracting global companies and adding over 1.5 lakh jobs in 2021-22, with plans to expand IT growth to other districts like Mahabubnagar, Siddipet, Nizamabad, and Nalgonda. The state government's policy framework, infrastructure, and human capital access have made Hyderabad a preferred destination for IT companies.

title: Cisco Business Model
summary: This article details Cisco's business model, which has evolved from primarily selling networking hardware to offering high-technology services and software. It covers the company's financial performance, revenue streams from hardware sales, licensing, and services, and strategic initiatives like acquisitions. The article also provides a SWOT analysis, outlines key competitors, and discusses Cisco's customer segments and value propositions.

title: Google’s Quantum Computing Technology in 2024 Review
summary: This article reviews Google's quantum computing technology in 2024, tracing its journey from the Quantum AI Lab's launch in 2013 to its achievement of "quantum supremacy" in 2019. It details Google's core quantum technologies, offerings like QVM and Cirq, and its efforts in post-quantum cryptography. The piece also outlines Google's main competitors, funding sources, significant customers, and strategic partnerships, while acknowledging challenges such as the refined classical algorithms that have narrowed the 'quantum supremacy' gap.

title: New Global Blockchain Network of Networks for Financial Market Participants and Institutional Assets
summary: Digital Asset and a consortium of leading market participants have announced the launch of the Canton Network, touted as the financial industry’s first privacy-enabled interoperable blockchain network for institutional assets. This network aims to tackle the limitations of existing smart contract blockchains, such as privacy concerns, control compromises, and scalability issues. By providing a decentralized infrastructure connecting Daml-built applications, the Canton Network seeks to enable synchronized financial markets, allowing for reconciliation-free transactions and new product innovations for institutions like BNP Paribas, Goldman Sachs, and Deutsche Börse Group.

title: 5 major data backup trends to watch
summary: The article discusses five major data backup trends that IT professionals should monitor. These include the increasing role of AI in automating backup processes and ransomware recovery, the growing need to prepare for SaaS outages, and the challenges and solutions for protecting data generated by remote workers. Additionally, the article highlights a projected increase in data center jobs and a heightened demand for cybersecurity in relation to backup strategies.

title: Big Names Provide Ballast for Blockchain Technology
summary: Digital Asset, in partnership with over 30 companies, has launched the Canton Network, the financial services industry’s first privacy-enabled blockchain for institutional assets. This initiative, which includes firms held by the Invesco Alerian Galaxy Blockchain Users and Decentralized Commerce ETF (BLKC), addresses key blockchain issues like privacy and scalability, affirming the technology's long-term investment viability. The Canton Network enables atomic transactions for digital assets and payments, enhancing efficiency and reducing operational risk in financial services.

title: POKERSTARS VR LAUNCHES ON PLAYSTATION VR2
summary: PokerStars VR has launched on Sony's PlayStation VR2, marking the game's debut on Sony platforms. Developed with Lucky VR, the game leverages PlayStation VR2's advanced haptic feedback, real-time face, and eye tracking to enhance the social casino experience with games like poker, blackjack, and roulette. It is free to download and play, offering a realistic virtual casino environment to a new audience of VR players.

title: Microsoft bets big on Sam Altman-backed Helion to deliver clean fusion energy by 2028
summary: Microsoft has partnered with Sam Altman-backed Helion to purchase clean fusion energy by 2028, a highly ambitious goal given the current stage of fusion technology. Helion aims to achieve commercially viable fusion power in just five years using a "pulsed non-ignition fusion system" that directly generates electricity. While nuclear fusion remains challenging, with only one organization previously demonstrating a net energy gain, Helion believes it can meet this target despite its latest prototype, Polaris, still being under construction.

title: Here's how AI is driving the rise of GPU cloud specialists
summary: The rise of AI is fueling a new class of cloud providers specializing in GPU compute, offering lower prices than generalized cloud competitors. Companies like CoreWeave, Lambda Labs, RunPod, and Arc Compute are catering to AI workloads that require substantial GPU resources. These niche providers differentiate themselves through competitive pricing, strategic locations, and ready access to scarce compute resources amidst supply chain challenges.

title: Top 15 Coursera Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of Coursera's top 15 competitors and alternatives in the online learning market. It details each competitor's background, offerings, unique selling propositions, and how they differentiate themselves from Coursera, covering a wide range of platforms from tech-focused learning to creative arts and general education.

title: TCHP Outshines iShares S&P 500 Growth Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Blue Chip Growth ETF (TCHP) has significantly outperformed the iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW) year-to-date as of May 10, demonstrating the effectiveness of active management. TCHP's concentrated approach and focus on blue-chip companies with strong fundamentals, including significant stakes in companies like Microsoft and Meta, contribute to its strong performance. This highlights the potential for active ETFs to generate alpha, especially in volatile markets, when managed by experienced teams with robust research capabilities.

title: Layoffs may cause organizational drag: Gartner
summary: According to Gartner research, layoffs can negatively impact shareholder returns within three years by leading to reduced employee morale, increased turnover, and loss of customers, despite initial cost savings. The report highlights that businesses often face immediate costs like severance payments and long-term issues such as higher contractor costs and demands for increased compensation from remaining staff. Gartner suggests alternative cost-cutting measures like voluntary reduced hours, executive pay cuts, hiring freezes, and internal redeployment to avoid "organizational drag."

title: UAE Cybersecurity Council partners with Microsoft to host Middle East Cybersecurity Conference
summary: Microsoft and the UAE Cybersecurity Council are collaborating to host the Middle East Cybersecurity Conference on May 16-17, 2023, in Abu Dhabi. The event will gather CIOs, CSOs, and experts to discuss the evolving cybersecurity threat landscape, AI's future in cybersecurity, and data protection, aligning with the UAE's Digital Economy Strategy. Key speakers include H.E. Dr. Mohamed Al Kuwaiti and Microsoft executives, aiming to empower defenders against increasing cyberattacks.

title: Comparing Google’s Bard with ChatGPT: What’s wrong, what’s bright
summary: This article provides a critical comparison between Google's Bard and OpenAI's ChatGPT, highlighting Bard's shortcomings despite Google's vast data resources. The author, Satyen K. Bordoloi, describes issues with Bard's memory, translation capabilities, and tendency to "hallucinate" incorrect information, contrasting it with ChatGPT's more coherent, though sometimes less creative, outputs. The piece argues that while Google has the potential to create a superior AI, Bard still requires significant development to live up to expectations.

title: Land O'Lakes says US carbon farming payouts top $5 million in 2022
summary: Land O'Lakes' Truterra unit paid U.S. farmers $5.1 million in 2022 for sequestering 262,000 metric tons of carbon, an increase from $4 million in 2021. This program is part of a growing trend among agricultural companies like Corteva and Bayer to capitalize on demand for environmental credits by promoting sustainable farming practices. Microsoft Corp is a major buyer of Truterra's carbon removal credits.

title: Roundhill Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF With CHAT
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT), an actively managed ETF focusing on global companies leading in generative AI technology. The fund aims to offer precise exposure to generative AI, distinguishing itself from other AI-themed ETFs that may lack such specific focus. Top holdings include Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet, with expectations for the basket to expand as more companies genuinely contribute to the generative AI space.

title: Roundhill Investments Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT)
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT), providing investors with exposure to companies at the forefront of generative AI technology. The ETF aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing generative AI market, which Roundhill estimates to have a total addressable market of over $120 billion for enterprise software. CHAT is an actively-managed ETF with top holdings including Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Salesforce, offering investors a way to participate in this innovative and transformative technology.

title: Nazzic Keene to Retire as SAIC CEO, Toni Townes-Whitley Named Successor
summary: Nazzic Keene, CEO of Science Applications International Corp. (SAIC), is set to retire after a decade with the company. Toni Townes-Whitley has been named as her successor, effective October 2, 2023, and will join as CEO-elect on June 12 for a smooth transition. Townes-Whitley brings 35 years of technology experience, including leadership roles at Microsoft and CGI's federal arm.

title: Steve Jobs: Apple Cofounder and Tech Innovator
summary: Steve Jobs, co-founder of Apple Inc., was an American inventor, designer, and entrepreneur responsible for pioneering revolutionary technologies like the iPhone and iPad. The article details his life from adoption and early education to co-founding Apple, his departure and subsequent ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, ultimately shaping modern technology. It also covers his personal life, battle with pancreatic cancer, and lasting legacy after his death in 2011.

title: Is BULZ Stock a buy?
summary: The article discusses BULZ, the ticker symbol for the MicroSectors FANG & Innovation 3X Leveraged ETNs, which trades on NYSE Arca. It explains that BULZ is an unsecured debt security providing leveraged exposure to the Solactive FANG Innovation Index, tracking 15 large-cap US tech stocks, and is not suitable for "buy and hold" investments. The article also mentions Bank of Montreal as the issuer and highlights Barclays' recent decision to redeem some of its own ETNs due to past issues, noting this does not directly impact BULZ but provides context on the ETN market.

title: NVIDIA Collaborates With Microsoft to Accelerate Enterprise-Ready Generative AI
summary: NVIDIA announced the integration of its NVIDIA AI Enterprise software with Microsoft Azure Machine Learning to provide an end-to-end cloud platform for enterprise-ready generative AI. This collaboration offers Azure customers tools to build, deploy, and manage customized AI applications using over 100 NVIDIA AI frameworks, enhancing development speed and security for large language models. The integration leverages high-performance NVIDIA accelerated computing for AI model training and inference and also makes NVIDIA Omniverse Cloud available on Microsoft Azure.

title: In The Multicloud World, The Data’s The Thing
summary: Dell is expanding its Apex as-a-service portfolio, particularly focusing on data as the immutable center of the multicloud environment. The company emphasizes that while applications are ephemeral, data persistence and accessibility across various public and on-premises clouds are crucial for enterprises. Dell is positioning its storage-as-a-service offerings as a "Switzerland" for data, enabling organizations to achieve data gravity and avoid vendor lock-in, a strategy that executive John Roese states is paying off and differentiating Dell from competitors.

title: Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:HLAL) $HLAL
summary: The Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF (HLAL) tracks the FTSE USA Shariah index, focusing on Sharia-compliant US equities. The ETF, launched in 2019 and managed by Wahed, had a market capitalization of $736.85 million and assets under management of $758.04 million at the time of the article. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet, and it recently declared quarterly distributions.

title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

title: Microsoft shares fix for cameras not working on Surface laptops
summary: Microsoft has released a temporary fix for an issue preventing built-in cameras from working on some ARM-based Surface devices, including the Surface Pro X. The problem, caused by a buggy driver, affects devices running Windows 10 22H2 or Windows 11 21H2/22H2 with specific Qualcomm processors. While a troubleshooter is being deployed automatically, users can apply a manual registry edit or await a permanent driver update.

title: Lenovo ranks eighth in the Gartner® Supply Chain Top 25 for 2023
summary: Lenovo has achieved its highest ranking ever, placing eighth in the Gartner Supply Chain Top 25 for 2023, which recognizes global companies with excellent supply chain management. The company's digital transformation initiatives, including the use of advanced technologies and a global hybrid manufacturing footprint, have significantly improved efficiency and sustainability. Lenovo emphasizes supply chain security and has made commitments to reducing emissions, further solidifying its position as a technology leader.

title: Everything you need to know about Salesforce’s Q1 2024 financial results
summary: Salesforce, Inc. reported increased first-quarter adjusted earnings in Q1 2024 ($1.69 per share) and revenue growth of 11% year-over-year to $8.25 billion. All key operating segments showed growth, driven by its generative AI ecosystem including Einstein GPT, Slack GPT, and Tableau GPT. CEO Marc Benioff highlighted the role of the Salesforce GPT Trust Layer in securing customer data and enhancing enterprise automation.

title: What’s lurking in your email?
summary: Email remains the primary threat vector for cyberattacks, complicated by hybrid work and advanced AI-enabled threats. Cisco Secure Email offers a multi-layered defense, integrating with other security solutions like Cisco Secure Endpoint and Cisco Umbrella, to protect organizations from phishing, ransomware, and other email-based attacks. The solution also includes Cisco Secure Email Threat Defense for Microsoft 365 and will be a key component of the forthcoming Cisco Security Cloud, aiming to simplify security operations and provide comprehensive protection.

title: Apple, Microsoft Boost the S&P 500 Amid Tech Comeback
summary: Apple and Microsoft have significantly contributed to the S&P 500's gains in 2023, accounting for nearly half of the index's rise and reaching an all-time high combined weight of 14%. This tech rally, driven by robust earnings, has left many mutual funds underweight in major tech stocks. The article suggests leveraged ETFs like AAPU, MSFU, and TECL for traders looking to capitalize on the continued bullish trend in the tech sector.

title: High Beta ETF 'SPHB' Lifted by Heavy Tech Sector Tilt
summary: The Invesco S&P 500 High Beta ETF (SPHB) performed strongly in May, driven by its significant allocation to the information technology sector. While the broader S&P 500 saw modest gains, the S&P 500 High Beta index climbed 2.4% due to the tech sector's 9.5% rally. SPHB has also demonstrated strong long-term performance, outpacing the S&P 500 over a three-year period.

title: Cisco needs to simplify. Here’s how.
summary: This article analyzes Cisco Systems Inc.'s business, highlighting its strong financial performance but also customer complaints about product complexity. It outlines how Cisco can simplify its offerings in security, networking, and observability to integrate its diverse portfolio and improve customer experiences, drawing on market data and insights from analyst Zeus Kerravala. The report also previews what to expect from Cisco Live regarding AI, security strategy, collaboration, and environmental, social, and governance initiatives.

title: Microsoft Inks Deal With NVIDIA-Backed CoreWeave
summary: Microsoft has signed a deal with NVIDIA-backed cloud-computing startup CoreWeave, planning to invest billions to bolster its cloud computing infrastructure for AI operations. This partnership will provide Microsoft easier access to NVIDIA’s GPUs, which are crucial for AI-based applications like ChatGPT, and aims to give Microsoft a competitive edge against rivals like Alphabet Inc. and Amazon Web Services. CoreWeave’s access to over 45,000 GPUs and its status as an elite CSP in the NVIDIA Partner Network positions it as a key enabler for Microsoft's AI endeavors.

title: Microsoft-Activision Deal Divides Regulators
summary: The proposed $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard by Microsoft has received mixed reactions from global regulators. While the European Union and China, among other countries, have approved the deal, citing various concessions from Microsoft, the US Federal Trade Commission and the UK's Competition and Markets Authority remain opposed. The UK's CMA particularly fears the deal would negatively impact competition and innovation in the burgeoning cloud gaming market, making a lengthy appeal process likely.

title: How Microsoft Fabric aims to beat Amazon and Google in the cloud war
summary: Microsoft has launched Fabric, a new cloud data and analytics platform designed to consolidate and simplify data management and analysis for enterprises. Analysts believe Fabric offers Microsoft a significant advantage over competitors like Amazon and Google by unifying disparate tools into a single platform with OneLake, a single data lake. This integration aims to reduce complexity, lower costs, and improve data quality, addressing key pain points for customers and potentially leapfrogging other cloud providers in enterprise analytics.

title: Cisco debuts its Secure Service Edge offering and enhances app security
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has unveiled a new Secure Service Edge (SSE) offering called Cisco Secure Access, designed to help enterprises securely connect edge resources with features like zero-trust network access. Alongside SSE, Cisco announced Multicloud Defense for protecting cloud service workloads and significant upgrades to Panoptica, its cloud-native security development platform. These introductions aim to simplify security processes, enhance performance, and provide comprehensive protection across various enterprise environments.

title: Salesforce CEO Benioff shakes up executive team with new hires
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has announced significant changes to the company's executive management team, including new hires for chief revenue officer and chief marketing officer. This shakeup occurs six months after the departure of co-CEO Bret Taylor and follows recent layoffs impacting 10% of Salesforce's global workforce. The changes aim to address challenges highlighted by Benioff, including a difficult economic environment and questions about new hire productivity.

title: Netflix Collaboration Fuels IAS, DoubleVerify Stock Surge
summary: A collaboration between Netflix and ad verification companies Integral Ad Science (IAS) and DoubleVerify (DV) has led to a significant stock surge for both IAS and DV. The partnership focuses on tracking ad performance, preventing fraud, and optimizing advertising campaigns for Netflix's ad-supported service. Analysts anticipate triple-digit earnings-per-share growth for both companies, highlighting their strong potential in the growing ad verification market.

title: IT and Networking | Large Enterprise Solutions
summary: Lumen offers large enterprise IT and networking solutions designed to prepare businesses for the AI revolution, addressing the concerns of 86% of CIOs about network readiness. The platform provides secure, scalable, and high-performance connectivity, cybersecurity, and collaboration tools, leveraging a globally peered network and a programmable network fabric for AI workloads. Lumen also provides various infrastructure, connectivity, security, and communication services, featuring customer success stories and extensive resources for intelligent enterprise solutions.

title: Big Tech Is Outpacing Small-Caps Amid Market Rally
summary: Big Tech is significantly outperforming small-cap stocks in the current market rally, with the Direxion Daily Technology Bull 3X ETF (TECL) up nearly 120% compared to the Direxion Daily Small Cap Bull 3X Shares (TNA) 3.7% gain year-to-date. This outperformance, particularly by companies like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, is despite lingering recession concerns. The article questions the sustainability of Big Tech's rally while suggesting that small-cap funds like TNA could offer future upside.

title: A breakdown of AI ETFs as tech names burn bright
summary: This article breaks down various AI-focused ETFs available in Europe, examining their methodologies, holdings, and performance. It highlights the differences in how these ETFs define and capture the AI theme, from market-cap weighted exposure to companies involved in big data and NLP to equally-weighted exposure based on AI-related revenue or IP. The piece also compares their costs, liquidity, and correlation of returns, noting that while some are dominated by large tech names, others offer differentiated exposure to companies leveraging machine learning.

title: News | Big-Name Requirements Return To Lift South East Offices
summary: The South East office market is experiencing a significant uplift with over 2 million square feet of active requirements, driven by major corporations such as Microsoft, PepsiCo, and Wood Group. This surge in demand follows a quiet period and reflects a shift in sentiment towards in-office working, with many large companies seeking new or expanded premises. Savills reports a notable increase in requirements over 20,000 square feet, indicating a potential recovery in the region's office sector.

title: The 3 Best ETFs to Buy to Balance Risk and Reward
summary: This article recommends three ETFs for a balanced portfolio, particularly for those interested in a 60/40 equity-to-bond split. The suggested ETFs are the Vanguard Total World Stock Fund ETF (VT) for global equity diversification, the VanEck Morningstar SMID Moat ETF (SMOT) for small and mid-cap stocks with competitive advantages, and the Fidelity Total Bond ETF (FBND) for fixed-income exposure. The author references market analysis suggesting a potential rebound for the 60/40 portfolio and provides detailed insights into each ETF's holdings, performance, and expense ratios.

title: Accenture looks to power AI efforts with $3 billion investment
summary: Accenture announced a significant $3 billion investment over three years into its data and AI practice, aiming to double the number of employees working with AI to 80,000. This move highlights a broader industry trend of companies scaling up generative AI offerings, following similar initiatives by Microsoft, Alphabet, and Salesforce. The investment also comes after recent layoffs at Accenture and includes the launch of an "AI Navigator for Enterprise" tool to guide businesses in AI adoption.

title: Someone is posing as a fake security company to create malicious GitHub repositories
summary: An unidentified actor is creating malicious GitHub repositories under the guise of a fake security company, High Sierra Cyber Security, offering exploits for popular products but delivering malware. These threat actors went to great lengths to establish fake personas and accounts, even using real security researchers' headshots. VulnCheck researchers, who uncovered this activity, warn that security researchers are valuable targets and advise caution when downloading code from GitHub, recommending vetting code thoroughly or running unknown exploits in isolated virtual machines.

title: Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FDTX)
summary: The Fidelity Disruptive Technology ETF (FDTX) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund that invests in innovative businesses with the potential for market disruption, including global disruptive technology companies in areas like machine learning and AI. The fund launched in April 2020 and is managed by Fidelity, with assets under management of $191.07 million. FDTX holdings include major tech companies such as NVIDIA, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FMET)
summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target equal to its current share price of $36.66. The rating is based on 613 analyst ratings covering 20 companies (69.5% of the portfolio) over the past year. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like AMD, Alphabet, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: Reddit CEO Steve Huffman: 'It's time we grow up and behave like an adult company'
summary: In his first interview since a widespread subreddit blackout, Reddit CEO Steve Huffman defended the company's decision to charge third-party apps for data access, stating it's necessary for Reddit to be a sustainable business. He acknowledged the protest but maintained that the company would not reverse its plans, emphasizing that Reddit can no longer afford to give its valuable data away for free, especially with the rise of AI models leveraging it. Huffman views the move as an essential step for the 18-year-old company to mature and operate as an "adult company."

title: Microsoft Roars to New High: ETFs to Tap the Strength
summary: Microsoft's stock has reached new highs, driven by investor optimism surrounding its advancements in artificial intelligence. The article highlights Microsoft's significant market capitalization increase and its strategic investments in AI, particularly with OpenAI. Investors seeking to capitalize on Microsoft's strength can do so through several ETFs with substantial exposure to the tech giant.

title: JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (JUSA)
summary: This article provides a detailed review and aggregate rating for the JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA). MarketBeat calculates the ETF's "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating and a price target of $61.93 by analyzing the weight and individual analyst ratings of its underlying stock holdings. The review also lists the top 25 holdings with their respective analyst ratings, price targets, and potential upside.

title: What Does Logitech CEO’s Abrupt Departure Mean?
summary: Logitech International SA (NASDAQ: LOGI) stock fell 10% after its CEO of 10 years, Bracken Darrel, abruptly resigned on June 13, 2023, to pursue other opportunities. This sudden departure, combined with recent changes in CFO and COO, has raised investor concerns amid Logitech's ongoing financial struggles and a two-year downward stock trajectory. Citi downgraded Logitech's shares to Neutral, citing uncertainty caused by the lack of clear guidance and executive changes.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | Omnicom Group to Debut Tool Using Generative AI for Advertising Employees
summary: Omnicom Group is set to launch its first generative artificial intelligence tool, Omni Assist, for its advertising employees. Developed in partnership with Microsoft and using OpenAI's GPT models via Microsoft Azure, this virtual assistant will help with tasks like compiling audience insights and building media plans. The tool is currently in a beta testing phase before a wider rollout.

title: HP Enterprise Offers Access to Cray Supercomputers for Building AI Models
summary: HP Enterprise is positioning itself as a key player in the AI revolution by offering access to its Cray supercomputers for building AI models. This move follows the trend of generative AI boosting other tech giants. HP Enterprise hopes to join the list of companies benefiting from the growing demand for AI development.

title: H&R Block and Microsoft’s Industry-Leading Partnership Brings Powerful Generative Artificial Int...
summary: H&R Block is partnering with Microsoft to integrate Azure OpenAI services and generative AI technology into its tax experiences. This collaboration aims to provide faster, more consultative, and secure tax services, enhancing customer confidence and driving innovation. H&R Block is also part of Microsoft's AI 100, an exclusive group focused on developing AI solutions.

title: KT to invest $5.4 bn by 2027 to develop AI platforms
summary: South Korea’s telecom service provider KT Corp. plans to invest 7 trillion won ($5.4 billion) by 2027 to become the country’s leading AI service provider, focusing on the robot, healthcare, and education markets. The investment will be allocated to securing super AI source technologies, upgrading related infrastructure, and developing AI service models. KT aims to generate 1.3 trillion won ($1 billion) in revenue from five AI-related service areas by 2025: contact centers, logistics, robots, healthcare, and education.

title: Microsoft study: Small businesses intrigued by AI and the opportunities it brings
summary: A Microsoft study reveals that small businesses are highly interested in AI, with 79% wanting to learn more about its application. Despite optimism about AI's potential to address operational challenges like inflation and data security, many small business owners lack the knowledge and resources to implement it effectively. The study highlights the need for simple AI solutions to help small businesses innovate and overcome daily operational burdens.

title: Canadian Tire Corporation and Microsoft partner to advance retail innovation in Canada
summary: Canadian Tire Corporation (CTC) and Microsoft have announced a seven-year strategic partnership to drive retail innovation in Canada. CTC will migrate its IT infrastructure to Microsoft Azure and leverage Microsoft's cloud products, expertise, and AI capabilities to modernize operations, enhance customer experiences, and accelerate its digital transformation. This collaboration aims to improve efficiency, scalability, and foster co-innovation in the Canadian retail sector.

title: Amazon Is Spending $100 Million to Teach Cloud Customers About AI
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is investing $100 million in a new Generative AI Innovation Center. This initiative aims to help its cloud customers develop and deploy AI products, connecting them with AI and machine learning experts. The move positions Amazon to compete with Microsoft and Google in the rapidly expanding generative AI market, with early users including Highspot, Twilio, Ryanair, and Lonely Planet.

title: Artificial Intelligence Opens a Generational Opportunity for Chipmakers
summary: The article discusses how the expansion of AI applications, especially large language models, is creating a "generational opportunity" for chipmakers. It highlights the projected growth of AI chip spending to nearly $165 billion by the end of the decade, primarily benefiting GPU-based chip providers like Nvidia, and notes that the demand for specialized AI hardware is leading to significant investments, partnerships, and a potential "long capital investment cycle" for the semiconductor industry. The article also examines how this shift will impact traditional CPU providers and accelerate the replacement of legacy computing infrastructure with AI-first hardware.

title: Canadian Tire Corporation and Microsoft Partner to Advance Retail Innovation in Canada
summary: Canadian Tire Corporation (CTC) and Microsoft have announced a seven-year strategic partnership to modernize CTC's retail operations and enhance brand experiences across its group of companies. CTC will leverage Microsoft Azure to accelerate its digital transformation, improve customer experience, and explore co-innovation opportunities with AI and machine learning. This collaboration aims to drive retail innovation in Canada by combining CTC's understanding of Canadians with Microsoft's cutting-edge technology.

title: Top 10 cybersecurity companies in the US
summary: This article lists the top 10 cybersecurity companies in the US, providing a brief overview of the services offered by each. It highlights that as technology advances, the need for robust cybersecurity solutions grows due to increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. The companies featured specialize in protecting sensitive information for individuals, businesses, and governments through various solutions like endpoint protection, cloud security, and threat intelligence.

title: Mizuho Rolls Out Microsoft's Generative AI to All 45,000 Bank Staff in Japan
summary: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. is rolling out Microsoft's Azure OpenAI service to all 45,000 of its bank employees in Japan, making it one of the first financial firms in the country to adopt the generative AI technology. The bank hopes to harness the technology, with employees already submitting ideas on how to utilize it. This move positions Mizuho at the forefront of AI integration in the Japanese financial sector.

title: Breaking GPT-4 Bad: Check Point Research Exposes How Security Boundaries Can Be Breached as Machines Wrestle with Inner Conflicts
summary: Check Point Research (CPR) has demonstrated a new method, dubbed "double bind bypass," that can circumvent GPT-4's security protocols and extract sensitive information, such as illegal drug recipes. This technique exploits the AI's internal conflict between its programmed urge to provide information and its "censorship" reflex to suppress harmful content. By persistently posing as clueless, CPR researchers were able to nudge GPT-4 into revealing details it initially refused to share, highlighting the ongoing challenge of securing advanced AI systems.

title: Here's How to Rank the 3 Biggest Video Game Stocks
summary: This article analyzes the top three video game stocks—Take-Two Interactive Software, Activision Blizzard, and Electronic Arts—ranking them based on their recent performance, market position, and analyst outlook. It highlights Take-Two's strong performance driven by the Grand Theft Auto series, the ongoing acquisition saga of Activision Blizzard by Microsoft, and Electronic Arts' more subdued and volatile trajectory. The analysis provides insights for investors considering opportunities in the video game industry.

title: Corsair Darkstar Wireless review: 'An MMO mouse packed with intuitive extra features'
summary: The Corsair Darkstar Wireless mouse offers a unique six-button side panel and reliable tilt gesture controls, making it suitable for MMO and MOBA players despite a higher price point. While its design prioritizes comfort and versatility, especially compared to competitors like the Razer Naga V2 Pro, it does have some drawbacks such as rear side buttons being harder to hit, a learning curve, and shorter battery life. The innovative tilt controls, however, stand out as a killer feature that enhances its appeal.

title: Nuclear power to reduce Microsoft data centre carbon footprint
summary: Microsoft has signed a "landmark" hourly energy-matching agreement with Constellation Energy Corporation to significantly reduce the carbon footprint of its data center in Boydton, Virginia, bringing it closer to 100% carbon-free operation. The deal leverages nuclear power's environmental attributes, complementing existing wind and solar purchases, and utilizes Constellation's hourly carbon-free energy matching platform for tracking. This collaboration aims to revolutionize clean energy procurement by ensuring real-time matching of electricity needs with regional, clean, carbon-free sources, addressing limitations of traditional annualised renewable energy certificates.

title: Lawsuit says OpenAI violated US authors' copyrights to train AI chatbot
summary: Two U.S. authors, Paul Tremblay and Mona Awad, have filed a class-action lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the company misused their copyrighted works to train its ChatGPT AI system. They claim ChatGPT mined data from thousands of books without permission, including potentially from "shadow libraries," and can generate accurate summaries of their novels. The lawsuit seeks unspecified monetary damages for copyright owners whose works were allegedly misused by OpenAI.

title: Oakmark Equity and Income Fund: Second Calendar Quarter 2023
summary: The Oakmark Equity and Income Fund gained 5.3% in Q2 2023, outperforming the Lipper Balanced Fund Index, despite market concentration in large-cap tech stocks. The fund adjusted its portfolio by reducing technology exposure and initiating new positions, primarily in the healthcare sector, based on a value-driven investment philosophy. The article also reflects on the foundational principles of diversification in portfolio construction, influenced by economist Harry Markowitz, and the fund's approach to asset allocation.

title: Q3 Stock Market Outlook: Time to Batten Down the Hatches or Raise the Sail?
summary: Morningstar's Q3 2023 stock market outlook suggests that while the U.S. market is still undervalued, the margin of safety has decreased, and the rally in the first half of the year has been disproportionately driven by a few "Magnificent Seven" stocks. Investors are advised to take profits from overextended growth and technology stocks and reallocate to undervalued sectors like communications and real estate, and to value, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks. The outlook anticipates slower market gains due to economic slowdown, tight monetary policy, and reduced credit availability, but expects any future pullbacks to be relatively shallow.

title: Top 15 Autodesk Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the top 15 competitors and alternatives to Autodesk, a leading software provider for design, engineering, and construction. It details each competitor's offerings, market position, and key features, highlighting their specific advantages against Autodesk's products. The article covers a range of software solutions from CAD to 3D modeling, catering to various industries such as manufacturing, architecture, real estate, and construction.

title: Apple makes history as first $3 trillion company amid tech stock surge
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first company to reach a $3 trillion market value, driven by investor optimism about its expansion into new markets and a projected easing of interest rate hikes. This accomplishment is set against a backdrop of a broader tech stock surge, with other companies like Tesla and Nvidia also seeing significant gains due to advancements in AI and strategic partnerships. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance and its reputation as a safe investment, alongside the success of other tech giants.

title: Growth-Stock Funds Fly High but Fall Short in 2023′s First Half
summary: Growth-stock funds experienced a strong rebound in the first half of 2023 after significant losses in 2022, with large-growth funds doubling small-growth funds' gains. However, two-thirds of U.S.-centric active managers underperformed stylistically relevant indexes and passive rivals due to untimely sector allocations and light stakes in mega-cap stocks that drove market performance. International and emerging markets showed more modest gains, with some regions outperforming others, leading to a mixed bag for global stocks.

title: QuickBooks Payroll vs SurePayroll by Paychex: Which is better?
summary: This article compares QuickBooks Payroll and SurePayroll by Paychex for small and midsize businesses. While QuickBooks Payroll offers more scalability and flexible plan tiers with seamless QuickBooks Online integration, SurePayroll by Paychex is generally more affordable, provides broader third-party app integration, and offers more HR resources, including high-quality employee benefits. The comparison delves into pricing, features like time tracking, benefits administration, direct deposit, HR tools, customer service, and mobile app access, concluding that SurePayroll is often better for businesses prioritizing affordability and real-person support, while QuickBooks is suited for those already using its accounting software.

title: Top New Mobile Game Development Software You Should Know in 2023
summary: This article provides a guide to the top mobile game development software in 2023, including Unity, Unreal, HTML5, Godot, CryEngine, Cocos-2D, and Amazon Lumberyard. It highlights key features and popular games for each engine, aiming to help developers choose the best tools to create engaging and successful mobile games in a rapidly growing industry. The article emphasizes the importance of utilizing robust game engines and unique ideas to stay competitive.

title: HPE signs pact to produce $1 bn worth of servers in India
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed an initial pact with VVDN Technologies to produce high-end servers worth $1 billion in India over the next 4-5 years. This collaboration falls under the Indian government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for IT hardware and aligns with the "Make in India" initiative. Production is slated to begin in November, with HPE aiming to better serve Indian customers and capitalize on the country's growing digital economy.

title: D-Wave Quantum Regains NYSE Compliance, Continues Trading Amid Quantum Computing Advancements
summary: D-Wave Quantum Inc. has announced its regained compliance with the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) listing standards after its share price surpassed the $1.00 minimum requirement on June 30, 2023, and maintained an average closing price above this threshold for 30 trading days. As the world's first commercial supplier of quantum computers, the company will continue to trade on the NYSE. D-Wave provides quantum computing systems, software, and services to major organizations globally, specializing in both annealing and gate-model quantum computers.

title: Worldwide Public Cloud Services Revenues Surpass $500 Billion in 2022 -
summary: Worldwide public cloud services revenue reached $545.8 billion in 2022, a 22.9% increase from the previous year, with SaaS – Applications being the largest revenue source. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Salesforce Inc., Google, and Oracle remain the dominant providers, collectively capturing over 41% of the global revenue. IDC predicts continued growth in foundational cloud services, especially IaaS and PaaS, driven by digital transformation and AI integration.

title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (DFAC)
summary: This article provides a review and aggregate rating for the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC). Based on 801 analyst ratings across 25 companies (34% of the portfolio), DFAC has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $40.95. The report details the consensus ratings for individual holdings within the ETF, including top components like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: If You Like JEPI, You’ll Love This ETF With an 11.9% Dividend Yield
summary: This article introduces the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) as an alternative to the JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI), highlighting its 11.9% dividend yield. JEPQ invests in Nasdaq 100 stocks, focusing on tech giants, and uses a covered-call strategy similar to JEPI to generate income. While offering a high yield and potential downside protection, JEPQ has lagged behind a comparable Nasdaq ETF (QQQ) in total return during a bull market.

title: Jacobs Solutions Inc. builds Alluvial Platform with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI
summary: Jacobs Solutions Inc. has developed its Alluvial Platform using Microsoft Fabric and Power BI to overcome challenges with fragmented project data and accelerate solution delivery. This platform aims to provide scalable self-service BI solutions, enhance data management, and support diverse client solutions across engineering projects. Alluvial represents a strategic shift for Jacobs towards a data and AI-driven culture, improving efficiency and fostering innovation in the AEC industry.

title: Deutsche Bank, Commerzbank, ING Data Breached in MOVEit Hack
summary: Deutsche Bank AG, Commerzbank AG, and ING Groep NV experienced client data breaches due to a security flaw in the MOVEit file transfer tool. A criminal hacking group exploited this vulnerability, gaining access to thousands of clients' names and account numbers. The compromised data was held by an external data provider, Majorel, and the software flaw has since been patched.

title: Trading Analysis Tools - Analytics and Chart Tools
summary: FOREX.com offers a suite of trading analysis tools, including Performance Analytics powered by Chasing Returns to help traders refine their skills through behavioral science insights. The platform also provides advanced charts integrated with TradingView, offering extensive indicators, drawing tools, and customization options for technical traders. Additionally, users can access real-time market news from Reuters, expert market analysis, an economic calendar, and various alert features to keep track of market movements.

title: EA, Take-Two Have Merger Hopes After Microsoft-Activision Ruling
summary: Following Microsoft's successful court ruling regarding its acquisition of Activision Blizzard, the video game industry is seeing increased merger and acquisition speculation. This has particularly boosted the stock of Electronic Arts and Take-Two Interactive Software, as investors view them as potential acquisition targets in the wake of the favorable legal precedent. The ruling suggests a more open environment for consolidation within the sector.

title: Activision Blizzard (ATVI) to be removed from the Nasdaq-100 (QQQ) Indexes
summary: Activision Blizzard (ATVI) is set to be removed from the Nasdaq-100 Indexes by mid-July 2023, with The Trade Desk taking its place. This action follows Microsoft's recent court victory against the US Federal Trade Commission, clearing a significant hurdle for its $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard. The removal indicates the company's nearing departure from the publicly traded market as the acquisition progresses.

title: RSPT: Right Idea for More Balanced Approach to Tech
summary: The article discusses the growing concentration risk in the technology sector, highlighted by the outperformance of a few mega-cap stocks in the cap-weighted S&P 500 Information Technology Index. It presents the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF (RSPT) as a solution for investors seeking a more diversified approach, noting its strong year-to-date performance despite its equal-weighting strategy. The article suggests that historical trends of tech concentration reversing could favor equal-weighted strategies like RSPT.

title: Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com launches own large language model, ChatRhino
summary: Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com has launched its own large language model (LLM) called ChatRhino, aiming to integrate AI into various industrial applications. The company emphasizes that the value of LLMs will be realized through industrial use cases, with ChatRhino designed to offer targeted solutions across sectors like retail, logistics, finance, and healthcare by combining general data with intelligent supply chain data. This move reflects the broader Chinese tech industry's efforts to catch up with Western advancements in AI and aligns with the government's push to regulate LLMs.

title: Fortinet’s stock nabs record closing high, while Microsoft, Nvidia, Broadcom bids all fall short
summary: On Friday, July 14, 2023, several AI-related stocks, including Microsoft, Nvidia, and Broadcom, aimed for record closing highs, but only Fortinet succeeded. Nvidia and Broadcom shares initially rose following positive analyst feedback on their AI chip market potential but ultimately closed down. This illustrates the fluctuating nature of the tech rally driven by artificial intelligence.

title: It’s Not Just Earnings I’m Watching This Week
summary: Garrett Baldwin outlines key financial events and stock market trends he is watching this week beyond just earnings reports, including economic data, corporate takeovers, defense spending, and the Nasdaq 100 rebalancing. He highlights specific stocks and ETFs to monitor, such as Microsoft, Bank of America, Tesla, and Starbucks, offering insights on potential trading strategies like selling put spreads. The article provides a detailed week-ahead outlook for investors.

title: A breakdown of the changes to the Nasdaq-100 and what it means for mega-cap tech stocks
summary: Nasdaq announced a special rebalance of the Nasdaq-100 Index to dilute the weightings of mega-cap tech stocks that have outperformed in 2023. The seven largest stocks in the Nasdaq-100 will see their combined weighting reduced from 56% to 44%, with Nvidia and Microsoft being most affected. Historically, similar rebalances have not had a significant impact on stock prices.

title: Preparing for Upcoming QQQ Changes
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a special rebalance of its Nasdaq-100 Index (NDX), impacting ETFs like Invesco QQQ (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). This rebalance, effective July 24, aims to address concentration risk due to the large percentage holdings of tech giants like Microsoft and Apple. Investors can expect lower allocations to these mega-cap growth names, though the overall changes are not anticipated to be monumental, and the ETF structure should help avoid capital gains distributions for investors.

title: Teladoc expands Microsoft tie-up to document patient visits with AI
summary: Teladoc Health is expanding its partnership with Microsoft to integrate AI services into its telehealth platform, aiming to automate clinical documentation and ease the burden on healthcare staff. This collaboration, which includes Microsoft's Nuance Dragon Ambient eXperience powered by GPT4, is expected to help address administrative burdens and staff shortages in the healthcare profession. Teladoc's shares rose 6% in premarket trade following the announcement.

title: Microsoft and Meta expand their AI partnership with Llama 2 on Azure and Windows
summary: Microsoft and Meta are expanding their AI partnership, bringing Meta's Llama 2 large language models to Azure and Windows. This collaboration aims to democratize AI by providing developers with open models, enabling them to build generative AI tools and experiences. Azure customers can now fine-tune and deploy Llama 2 models, while Windows developers can run Llama locally, integrating AI safety features from both companies.

title: Redefining how we deliver the power of Azure to the edge
summary: Microsoft is redefining how it delivers Azure's cloud capabilities to the edge for customers with globally distributed operations. The article highlights Azure Arc's role in simplifying, securing, and managing hybrid environments, exemplified by Carnival Corporation's use of Azure to manage its complex physical environments across its cruise ship fleet. Microsoft is also partnering with infrastructure leaders like Dell Technologies to further simplify the adoption of cloud innovation at the edge and ensure consistent security and management across diverse environments.

title: Unstructured raises $25M for large language model data processing expansion
summary: Unstructured Technologies Inc. has secured $25 million in new funding to expand its operations for processing data for large language models. Founded by a former CIA analyst, the company provides a platform that converts unstructured internal data into LLM-compatible formats, addressing the challenge of scattered data for organizations. The funding round was led by Bain Capital Venture Associates LLC with participation from several other investors.

title: Nasdaq Is Reducing the Influence of Giant Tech Firms in a Popular Stock Market Index
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a "special rebalance" of its Nasdaq 100 index to reduce the dominance of giant tech firms like Apple and Microsoft. This move addresses the historic rally of a few mega-cap tech stocks, which have caused overconcentration in the index and potentially increased diversification risk for investors. The rebalance, effective July 24, will prompt funds tracking the index to adjust their portfolios, potentially leading to trading costs and minor market volatility.

title: Top 10 Contact Center as a Service Providers Companies, 2023
summary: The article identifies the top 10 Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) providers in 2023, highlighting their innovative solutions and recent developments. It details the growing market value of CCaaS, projected to reach USD 16.43 billion by 2030, driven by digital transformation and AI integration. The listed companies are instrumental in enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency across various industries.

title: Carnival Corporation deploys Azure Edge solution on its cruise ships
summary: Carnival Corporation has deployed Microsoft Azure Stack HCI on three of its cruise ships to enhance customer connectivity and improve vessel performance monitoring. This move aims to unify IT infrastructure across its diverse brands, simplifying processes and providing consistent service, maintenance, and cybersecurity. The system also integrates with Neptune software using Azure IoT Edge for real-time data analysis onboard.

title: TD Cowen: Record year expected as US data center leases hit 2.1GW in 90 days due to AI boom
summary: TD Cowen reports that the US data center market is poised for a record year, with 2.1GW of leases signed in the last 90 days, primarily driven by artificial intelligence demand. Hyperscalers like Google and Microsoft have secured massive deals, leading to a significant tightening of the leasing market and a shift towards pre-leasing capacity further in advance. This boom is expected to benefit companies like Equinix and Digital Realty, though supply chain issues with long lead times for equipment persist.

title: EPAM Named 2023 Microsoft Partner of the Year
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced it has won the 2023 Microsoft U.S. Azure Migration Partner of the Year Award and earned two finalist honors. The company was recognized for its exceptional use of Azure to help customers seamlessly migrate to the cloud and develop transformational applications using data and AI, specifically highlighting its work with Louis Dreyfus Company, Estée Lauder Companies, and Ahold Delhaize. This recognition underscores EPAM's dedication to delivering leading-edge enterprise solutions and mastering cloud complexities for global brands.

title: Microsoft Cloud strength drives fourth quarter results
summary: Microsoft announced strong fourth-quarter and fiscal year 2023 results, driven by significant growth in its Cloud services. The company reported substantial increases in revenue, operating income, net income, and diluted earnings per share, with Microsoft Cloud revenue reaching $30.3 billion, up 21% year-over-year. CEO Satya Nadella highlighted the company's focus on leading the new AI platform shift and helping customers maximize value from their digital spending.

title: Genpact, Microsoft collaborate to equip workforce with AI tools
summary: Genpact and Microsoft have partnered to provide Genpact's global workforce with access to Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service, aiming to integrate generative AI capabilities into their services for clients. This collaboration will support Genpact employees through comprehensive training and resources, leveraging large language models to enhance operational efficiencies and foster innovation across various industries. The initiative seeks to improve employee productivity and agility by developing AI-driven solutions for common enterprise challenges.

title: Announcing AI-Powered Threat Detection for NetApp
summary: SentinelOne has announced the general availability of AI-Powered Threat Detection for NetApp, a new component of their Singularity Cloud Data Security product line. This solution utilizes proprietary AI models to scan files on NetApp arrays for malware, preventing its spread, and integrates seamlessly with the SentinelOne management console. It aims to improve risk management, reduce recovery costs, and ensure compliance by offering high-performance, low-latency protection against novel and unknown malware.

title: Mutual Fund Top Trumps – TMT
summary: This article compares two TMT mutual funds, the BlackRock Next Generation Technology fund and the Janus Henderson Global Technology and Innovation fund, using the "Top Trumps" card game format. The comparison focuses on factors like 3-year performance, assets under management, alpha, volatility, ongoing charges, and information ratio. Ultimately, the Janus Henderson fund wins the comparison 4:2, and details on both funds' investment strategies, sector, and geographic breakdowns are provided.

title: Decisive Decade, Future Vision" TNL Mediagene’s 10th Anniversary Invites Taiwan Microsoft, Minister of Digital Affairs Audrey Tang, and Other Special Guests to Envision Future Trends
summary: TNL Mediagene celebrated its 10th anniversary with a forum and banquet, inviting distinguished guests from government, industry, and academia, including Vice Premier Cheng Wen-Tsan, Minister of Digital Affairs Audrey Tang, and Microsoft Taiwan General Manager Sean Pien. The event discussed the future of media, AI technology, geopolitics, and media values, reflecting on media's role in communication and its direction for the next decade. TNL Mediagene, formed by the merger of The News Lens and Mediagene Inc., is projecting to be the first Asian digital media group to go public in the US in Q1 2024.

title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar analyzes Alphabet's Q2 earnings, highlighting accelerated search growth, YouTube ad revenue recovery, and cloud service stability. The article maintains its Fair Value Estimate of $161 per share, rating Alphabet's stock as undervalued with a wide economic moat due to intangible assets and network effects, despite high uncertainty related to advertising reliance.

title: U.S. crude helps fix world’s key oil price
summary: The inclusion of U.S. WTI Midland crude in the Dated Brent benchmark has significantly improved its liquidity and stability. This reform addresses previous issues of supply scarcity and price manipulation, making the benchmark less susceptible to sudden shifts. The increased supply has also made it harder for individual traders to corner the market and has influenced pricing dynamics for other North Sea crudes.

title: AI and Data Centers: It’s About Keeping Cool
summary: A report from Citi Research highlights the critical role data centers play in the AI revolution, emphasizing that keeping servers cool will be vital. Despite historical predictions, data center energy use has been largely stable due to efficiency gains, but AI's computational demands are accelerating, making cooling and renewable energy increasingly important for sustainability. The article notes that while AI compute has outpaced Moore's Law, increased scrutiny of carbon emissions will drive higher corporate renewable PPAs.

title: Megatrends, PCs, Servers and Moore
summary: This article from Citi Research outlines three megatrends shaping the IT Hardware and Networking space: Generative AI, 400G and 800G transition, and Digital Transformation. It also discusses the normalization of the PC market, the growth of the server and storage markets, and the significant projected growth for the semiconductor industry, driven by "More than Moore" advancements. The insights are based on reports from Citi Research analysts Atif Malik, Asiya Merchant, and Andrew Gardiner.

title: Powering decision making at Microsoft by analyzing data with Microsoft Power BI
summary: Microsoft's Enterprise 360 Data Intelligence team developed training programs to empower employees to analyze data and build visualizations using Microsoft Power BI. These trainings shifted the responsibility of creating reports from the central data team to partner teams, reducing waiting times for data insights and improving overall data literacy and decision-making across the company. The initiative has enhanced efficiency, fostered a data-driven culture, and freed up the Data Intelligence team to focus on more complex support and data democratization efforts.

title: Cyber Mindfulness Corner Company Spotlight: DXC Technology
summary: This article showcases DXC Technology's "virtual first" approach to the workplace, which was implemented before the pandemic and allows over 90% of its workforce to work remotely. Mike McDaniel, President of Modern Workplace at DXC Technology, highlights how this approach fosters inclusivity, personalization, and leverages technologies like the Metaverse for collaborative equity. DXC also emphasizes comprehensive support for remote employees, including security training and specialized programs like the Dandelion Program for neurodivergent individuals, aiming to enhance productivity and employee well-being.

title: Thomson Reuters reports higher second-quarter revenue as it pushes forward generative AI tools
summary: Thomson Reuters reported strong second-quarter revenues, up 2% year-over-year to US$1.65-billion, while also exceeding analyst earnings estimates. The company is actively investing in generative AI tools, exemplified by its partnership with Microsoft and the acquisition of the legal AI startup Casetext for US$650-million, all while maintaining its full-year financial targets despite economic uncertainties.
