CEO Steve Hasker noted that multi-year contracts provide some insulation from headwinds, as the company plans to spend $100 million annually on AI capabilities, viewing it as an offensive rather than defensive strategy.

title: phoenixNAP Powers Expanded AI and Cloud Services with Energy-saving, Ampere-Based Servers from Hewlett Packard Enterprise
summary: phoenixNAP, a global IT services provider, is expanding its Bare Metal Cloud platform by deploying cloud-native HPE ProLiant RL300 Gen11 servers across its 18 data centers. These servers utilize energy-efficient Ampere® Computing processors, enhancing performance and energy efficiency for AI inferencing, cloud gaming, and cloud-native workloads. This expansion addresses the growing demand for data-intensive workloads while mitigating the environmental impact of increasing energy consumption in data centers.

title: Expedia Group says ‘surplus of top-tier tech talent’ is boosting its strategic hiring efforts
summary: Expedia Group is leveraging a "surplus of top-tier tech talent" in the market to bolster its strategic hiring initiatives, despite increased salary expenses. The company reported a significant profit in the second quarter of $385 million, up from a loss the previous year, even as overhead expenses rose. Expedia is focusing its tech talent on integrating its various brands onto a single platform, launching a unified loyalty program, and exploring generative AI for travel planning.

title: Top 20 Atlassian Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article identifies and details 20 major competitors and alternatives to Atlassian, an Australian software company known for products like Jira and Confluence. The list includes technology giants such as Microsoft, IBM, Google Workspace, and ServiceNow, as well as specialized platforms like GitLab, Asana, and Slack. Each competitor's founding year, headquarters, market focus, and key offerings are highlighted, demonstrating the diverse landscape of tools available for project management, collaboration, DevSecOps, and enterprise software.

title: AI as an investment theme
summary: The article discusses Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a significant investment theme due to its potential to revolutionize industries and create new opportunities. It highlights the booming AI sector, strong investor interest, and the impact of AI on technology stocks and the broader market. The author also touches upon various ways to invest in AI, including specific ETFs, and emphasizes the importance of careful research despite the vast potential.

title: Nvidia teams with Accenture and ServiceNow for AI program
summary: Nvidia, Accenture, and ServiceNow have partnered to launch "AI Lighthouse," a program aimed at helping ServiceNow customers quickly integrate generative AI tools. The initiative combines ServiceNow's automation platform, Nvidia's AI supercomputing and software resources, and Accenture's consulting expertise to develop and deploy custom generative AI large language models and applications. The program focuses on enhancing customer service, promoting self-service options, automating content generation, and boosting developer productivity.

title: Microsoft named a Leader and Positioned Furthest for Completeness of Vision in the 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Robotic Process Automation
summary: For the third consecutive year, Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Robotic Process Automation (RPA), achieving the furthest position for Completeness of Vision in 2023. This recognition is attributed to continuous innovation in Power Automate, driven by community engagement, customer feedback, partner contributions, and developer input. Key advancements include the integration of generative AI with Copilot, custom UI actions for Power Automate for desktop, expanded cloud API connectors, and the general availability of hosted RPA.

title: Video Game M&A Surges In Q2 2023 With Big-Name Mobile Deals
summary: Video game M&A activity saw a surprising surge in Q2 2023, reaching $5.99 billion in gross transaction value, the highest since Q1 2022. This increase was driven primarily by mobile gaming deals outside the US, despite the ongoing legal battle surrounding Microsoft's acquisition of Activision Blizzard. Notable transactions included Savvy Games Group's $4.90 billion acquisition of Scopely and Sega Sammy Holdings' $781.8 million deal for Rovio Entertainment.

title: Verizon to ‘sunset’ Blue Jeans vidconf platform
summary: Verizon is discontinuing its BlueJeans video collaboration platform, which it acquired in April 2020. The company cited "changing market conditions in a post-pandemic landscape" as the reason, shifting its focus to other priorities like 5G and mobile edge computing. The move highlights the intense competition in the video conferencing market and the impact of evolving work trends.

title: 9 customer journey mapping tools that can improve your CX
summary: This article explores nine customer journey mapping tools that can help companies improve their customer experience (CX) by analyzing customer paths across various channels. It discusses key features to consider when choosing a tool, such as ease of use, flexibility, collaboration, and integration capabilities. The article also provides a list of specific tools, ranging from simple diagramming software to comprehensive customer experience platforms.

title: Cloud as the ultimate digital enabler
summary: This interview with Elevance Health's CTO discusses their transformative cloud journey, highlighting lessons learned, drivers, benefits, and operating model evolution. They emphasize the importance of a systematic migration approach, agile methodologies, and cross-functional collaboration to maximize value from cloud adoption. The company achieved significant cost savings and enhanced member experiences by leveraging cloud-native capabilities and a product mindset.

title: Microsoft Azure meets growing needs of healthcare organizations, such as Mount Sinai Health System, with a highly scalable public cloud for Epic
summary: Microsoft announced that Epic clients, including Mount Sinai Health System, can now utilize Microsoft Azure Large Instances to run large Epic electronic health record (EHR) databases in the public cloud. This solution offers high scalability and dedicated resources, enabling healthcare organizations to manage significant database loads. The move aims to facilitate digital transformation, accelerate AI adoption, improve operational efficiency, and reduce infrastructure costs for healthcare providers.

title: Unlocking the potential of AI for the energy industry
summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming the energy industry by enhancing safety, accelerating subsurface characterization, and promoting sustainability through autonomous operations. To fully realize its potential, the industry must adopt open and secure data platforms, embrace rapid experimentation, integrate new technologies, and develop an AI-trained workforce. SLB is addressing these needs through open data initiatives, accessible AI tools, specialized innovation centers, and a "Domain Data Scientist" training program.

title: Artificial Intelligence at Morgan Stanley – Three Use Cases
summary: Morgan Stanley utilizes AI across three key areas to enhance its financial services: internal-facing chatbots for financial advisors, personalized client communication through recommendation systems, and an impact analysis tool for sustainable investing. The firm has partnered with OpenAI for its GPT-4-powered chatbot to drive efficiency and scale, while also leveraging machine learning for its Next Best Action system and the Morgan Stanley IQ® tool to align client portfolios with impact preferences. These AI initiatives aim to improve advisor efficiency, client engagement, and retention by providing tailored insights and investment options.

title: Amazon is removed from key list of climate-conscious companies
summary: Amazon has been removed from the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) list of companies taking action on climate goals due to its failure to set a credible target for reducing carbon emissions. This decision comes despite Amazon's 2019 pledge to eliminate or offset all carbon emissions by 2040 and a subsequent retraction of a 2030 zero-carbon delivery target. The removal raises questions about Amazon's standing among ESG investment funds, as its emissions have increased by 40% since 2019.

title: ERISA Case Against Cisco Over BlackRock TDFs Dismissed
summary: A federal court in California dismissed a breach of fiduciary duty lawsuit against Cisco Systems under ERISA, concerning its selection of BlackRock's LifePath target-date funds (TDFs). The judge ruled that underperformance alone over a short period does not constitute imprudence, especially without evidence of a flawed selection process. This dismissal is one of several similar cases brought by Miller Shah against various companies, all of which have been rejected on similar grounds.

title: UPDATE: San Jose delays decision on city-run power grid
summary: The San Jose City Council has unanimously voted to delay a decision on establishing a city-run power utility, "San Jose Power," until October 3. This deferral allows officials more time to analyze the complex financial and operational implications of creating such a utility, which aims to capitalize on new high-voltage transmission lines. The proposed utility is intended to serve key infrastructure sites and attract new businesses, potentially offering significant cost savings on electricity.

title: American Airlines Group – Digital Transformation Strategies
summary: American Airlines is heavily investing in digital transformation, with an estimated ICT spending of $1.7 billion for 2023, primarily focused on software and hardware. The company is leveraging disruptive technologies like AI, big data, blockchain, and cloud to enhance operations and improve customer service, as demonstrated by its use of a machine learning engine to optimize gate assignments. This strategy aims to improve efficiency and maintain a competitive edge in the travel industry.

title: Accelerate Insurance Claims Digital Transformation with Low-Code Platforms
summary: Leading insurers are increasingly adopting low-code platforms to accelerate development cycles for claims systems, moving from years to months. These platforms address challenges like evolving consumer digital expectations, workforce changes, and rising costs by enabling faster application development with minimal coding. Low-code solutions offer versatility for extending core systems without disruption, empowering insurers to create more personalized and efficient claims experiences.

title: Roblox faces a new class action lawsuit alleging it facilitates child gambling
summary: Roblox is facing a new class action lawsuit accusing it of facilitating child gambling through third-party sites using its in-game currency, Robux. Parents claim their children have lost thousands of Robux on these sites, which offer games like blackjack and roulette. Roblox denies affiliation with these sites but states it has teams investigating and may pursue action against them, while also acknowledging that its community standards prohibit simulated gambling.

title: The Next Big Things From SAP
summary: SAP recently unveiled significant innovations at its Sapphire event in Orlando, focusing on responsible AI integrated into business solutions, ledger-based accounting for carbon tracking (green ledger initiative), and industry-specific networks to enhance supply-chain resilience. These advancements aim to help customers transform their business models in the cloud, prioritize sustainability, and boost agility in an evolving future. SAP is also expanding partnerships, like with Google Cloud for open data, and committing to upskill two million people globally to meet the demand for SAP experts.

title: Markets News, Aug. 22, 2023: Tech Buoys Nasdaq; Nvidia, Banks Weigh on S&P 500
summary: U.S. markets closed mixed on August 22, 2023, with the Nasdaq Composite Index slightly up due to tech stocks, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average declined. Financial stocks were hit by another downgrade from S&P, affecting several regional banks. Companies like Boeing, Apple, and Baidu saw gains, while Merck, Nike, and several retailers experienced drops.

title: Silicon Labs Unveils the Series 3 IoT Platform, Pledges 100x Performance Gains for On-Device ML, AI - Hackster.io
summary: Silicon Labs has announced its new "Series 3" Internet of Things (IoT) platform, claiming a 100-fold boost in performance for on-device machine learning and artificial intelligence workloads. Built on a 22nm process, the platform promises performance, efficiency, and scalability gains, alongside multiple radio connectivity options and significant power improvements. The company also launched Simplicity Studio 6 with decoupled development tools and new chips for Amazon Sidewalk devices.

title: Yamaha’s CS-500 Now Certified for Microsoft Teams, Adding to Yamaha’s Portfolio of Certified Video Sound Collaboration Systems
summary: Yamaha has announced that its CS-500 Video Collaboration System is now Certified for Microsoft Teams, expanding its portfolio of certified video sound collaboration products. The CS-500 integrates a microphone and camera into a single device, featuring SoundCap Eye™ technology for focused audio capture and a 4K AI-enabled video camera with auto-framing. This certification ensures a seamless conferencing experience for Microsoft Teams in various meeting room sizes, similar to its predecessor, the CS-800.

title: Microsoft’s Python in Excel: A fusion enabling optimal data analysis and visualization
summary: Microsoft is integrating Python into Excel, allowing users to leverage Python's powerful data analysis capabilities directly within their spreadsheets. This feature, called Python in Excel, is available as a public preview for Microsoft 365 Insiders on Windows and includes access to popular Python libraries through a partnership with Anaconda. It aims to streamline data science workflows by enabling seamless execution of Python code within Excel, eliminating the need to switch between applications.

title: TAL’s MathGPT launches public beta: your next-gen math assistant
summary: TAL Education Group has launched the public beta of MathGPT, an innovative mathematical large model designed for global mathematics enthusiasts and research institutions. This LLM is China's first large model tailored for mathematics, offering problem-solving algorithms and explanations for problems ranging from elementary to high school levels. MathGPT supports both Chinese and English, is compatible with PC and mobile devices, and aims to transform educational landscapes by complementing traditional teaching with AI-assisted Q&A.

title: Best Back-to-School Marketing Campaigns (Plus the ‘Worst Ad Ever’)
summary: The article discusses the competitive landscape for back-to-school marketing in 2023, noting an expected 10% decrease in average spending per child. It highlights seven effective back-to-school marketing campaigns from various brands like Staples Canada, DoorDash, Dell, Amazon, Instacart, Bic South Africa, and Honda UK, showcasing diverse strategies from nostalgia to social responsibility. Additionally, it revisits top picks from previous years and comically points out an ad from East Hills Mall as "the worst commercial of all time."

title: YieldMax Launches Option Income Strategy ETFs on Microsoft and Disney
summary: YieldMax has introduced two new active ETFs, the YieldMax MSFT Option Income Strategy ETF (MSFO) and the YieldMax DIS Option Income Strategy ETF (DISO), designed to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on Microsoft and Disney stocks, respectively. These new ETFs, actively managed by ZEGA Financial, join a growing suite of YieldMax products that employ similar strategies on other major companies like Coinbase, Netflix, Meta Platforms, Alphabet, and Amazon.

title: P&G enlists IoT, predictive analytics to perfect Pampers diapers
summary: Procter & Gamble is leveraging IoT and predictive analytics, specifically Microsoft's IoT and edge analytics platform, to enhance the production of Pampers diapers. This initiative, dubbed Hot Melt Optimization, uses proprietary sensors and machine learning to predict and prevent manufacturing failures, significantly reducing waste and improving efficiency. The system has eliminated 70% of flawed diapers in 11 plants, showcasing the impact of data-driven innovation in manufacturing.

title: 'Not just money and math': Young people are willing to sacrifice returns for ESG
summary: New data reveals that young investors are increasingly willing to sacrifice investment returns to align their portfolios with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) causes they care about. This trend is particularly strong among Gen Z and millennials, with many prepared to underperform market averages for values-aligned investments. Behavioral scientists suggest this reflects a need for young people to signal their values and identity through their investment choices.

title: Hedge fund exposure to 7 biggest tech stocks at record high, Goldman Sachs says
summary: Hedge funds have reached record exposure to the seven largest tech stocks by market capitalization, according to Goldman Sachs data, driven by strong performance and the artificial intelligence theme. These seven stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, now constitute about 20% of the total net market value held by hedge funds tracked by Goldman Sachs. Despite the concentration risk, investment managers rationalize these allocations due to the significant returns generated by these companies this year.

title: Institute for National Defense and Security Research-Newsletter
summary: This article analyzes the impact of the Matsu undersea cable interruption on Taiwan's national defense and security, emphasizing the critical role of submarine cables for communication and military operations. It highlights how the CCP's grey zone tactics, including cable damage, undermine Taiwan's cyber resilience and calls for establishing comprehensive backup mechanisms, strengthening network infrastructure, and developing low-orbit satellites, drawing lessons from the Russo-Ukrainian War, to enhance Taiwan's communication system. The author stresses the need for integration between military and civilian technological capabilities and international cooperation to secure national communication.

title: Is Domo Stock a Buy Now?
summary: Domo's stock recently plunged 36% after its Q2 fiscal 2024 earnings report, despite narrower-than-expected losses and revenue slightly beating estimates. The cloud-based software company's growth has stalled due to macro headwinds, stiff competition from tech giants like Salesforce and Microsoft, and a shift to consumption-based pricing. The article advises investors to avoid Domo, citing its unprofitability, slowing growth, and crowded market, despite appearing cheap in valuation.

title: How space traffic in orbit could spell trouble on Earth
summary: The increasing number of satellites in Earth's orbit, particularly large constellations like Starlink, significantly raises the risk of catastrophic collisions and the generation of more space debris. Such collisions could disrupt vital services on Earth, including GPS, communication, weather forecasting, and intelligence gathering. The current patchwork of international regulations is insufficient to manage this growing traffic, highlighting the urgent need for a formal, coordinated global system to prevent disasters and maintain the functionality of orbital infrastructure.

title: New ZEGA ETFs Let Investors Play Disney and Microsoft on Easy Mode
summary: ZEGA Financial has expanded its YieldMax offerings by launching two new covered call ETFs: YieldMax MSFT Option Income Strategy ETF (MSFO) and YieldMax DIS Option Income Strategy ETF (DISO). These funds employ a synthetic covered call strategy on Microsoft and Disney stocks, aiming to provide monthly income and capital appreciation for investors seeking defensive positions in tech stocks. The ETFs allow for capped participation in price gains and are part of a trend of investors flocking to covered call funds amid a cautious market recovery.

title: Oxygen Top 45 ‘must-know’ UK IT Distributors
summary: IT Channel Oxygen has released its inaugural list of the top 45 "must-know" UK IT distributors, highlighting companies ranging from broadline giants like Westcoast and Ingram Micro to niche start-up VADs. These 45 distributors collectively generate over £14 billion in revenue, showing significant growth year-on-year despite challenges such as shrinking hardware sales and declining average selling prices. The report delves into market trends, M&A activity, and the shifting dynamics of the UK IT distribution landscape, including the surge in cloud and cybersecurity segments.

title: Enel Generación y Enel X sellan acuerdo con cruzados como “Partner Energético” del nuevo estadio de la UC
summary: Enel Generación and Enel X have partnered with Cruzados to make Universidad Católica's new stadium the first 100% sustainable sports venue in Chile. The agreement includes supplying 100% renewable energy for six years, implementing a solar autogeneration plant, and adding electric vehicle charging infrastructure. This initiative aims to significantly reduce CO2 emissions and serves as a model for future sustainable constructions in Chile.

title: Worker confidence in AI may be growing despite unease about the tech
summary: A Robert Half survey indicates growing worker confidence in generative AI, with 4 in 10 U.S. workers believing it will positively impact their careers, despite past unease regarding job displacement. The survey, conducted in May 2023, shows HR and tech professionals, along with younger generations, are particularly optimistic about AI's ability to automate tasks and increase efficiency. Companies are increasingly adopting AI for various functions, leading to a surge in demand for AI-related skills.

title: IXN: Not A True Global Tech Play
summary: The iShares Global Tech ETF (IXN) is criticized for its misleading name, as it is heavily concentrated in U.S.-based tech giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, comprising nearly two-thirds of its portfolio. Despite its "global" designation, over 80% of its holdings are U.S. companies, limiting true international exposure and sector diversification. This article highlights that IXN is not a genuinely global technology investment, potentially making investors vulnerable if they seek diversified international tech exposure.

title: SAP nabs new global AI head from Microsoft, Walter Sun
summary: SAP has appointed Walter Sun, formerly VP of Copilot Applied Artificial Intelligence at Microsoft, as its new global head of AI. This strategic hire underscores SAP's commitment to prioritizing AI, following recent collaborations with Microsoft and Google Cloud to integrate generative AI into its enterprise offerings. Sun, with his extensive background in AI and machine learning, is tasked with leveraging SAP's broad applications and customer base to transform business operations through innovative AI solutions.

title: Which companies are the best employers in Kentucky? UK and Norton top the Forbes list
summary: Forbes has released its fifth annual list of the best employers in Kentucky for 2023, based on employee surveys. The University of Kentucky topped the list, with several Louisville-based companies like Norton Healthcare, Baptist Health, and Humana also making it into the top 10. The ranking considered aspects such as working conditions, diversity, and company image.

title: HPE storage comes with full IT stack, hybrid cloud and as a service
summary: This article examines Hewlett Packard Enterprise's (HPE) strategy to position itself as a comprehensive IT stack provider, moving beyond just storage. It details HPE's Greenlake consumption model, which offers as-a-service options across on-prem and hybrid cloud environments, particularly highlighting the revamped Alletra MP storage line. The piece covers HPE's origins, market ranking, key storage products, and its approach to cloud, containerization, and various consumption models.

title: How Blackstone Sprinted Ahead of Its Peers in AI
summary: Blackstone, the world's largest alternative-asset manager, has aggressively integrated artificial intelligence into its operations thanks to a directive from CEO Stephen Schwarzman. The firm uses AI for risk assessment, demand forecasting, dealmaking, and optimizing its portfolio companies, leveraging its massive amount of proprietary data. While facing industry-wide concerns about cybersecurity and job displacement, Blackstone believes AI provides a significant competitive advantage and plans to double its assets under management with AI-driven strategies.

title: Qualcomm CEO says AI may breathe new life into smartphones: 'It could create a new upgrade cycle'
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon believes artificial intelligence could spark a new upgrade cycle for smartphones, revitalizing a market that saw a significant sales decline in 2022. Amon indicated that the company's upcoming Snapdragon Summit in October would showcase new AI-driven use cases from smartphone manufacturers. Unlike Nvidia's focus on data centers, Qualcomm aims to bring AI capabilities directly to devices, enhancing user experience and driving demand for new models.

title: SAP Versus Salesforce: Who’s World’s Third-Largest Software Vendor?
summary: This article analyzes the competition between SAP and Salesforce for the title of the world's third-largest software vendor, behind Microsoft and Oracle. While Salesforce recently surpassed SAP in quarterly revenue, SAP maintains a lead in trailing-12-month revenue. The author projects that SAP is poised to solidify its lead due to its faster-growing cloud business.

title: Microsoft crash dump exposed key that led to US cabinet email hacks, investigation finds
summary: A Microsoft internal investigation revealed that a consumer signing key, used by a China-based threat group to compromise U.S. cabinet email accounts, was exposed in an April 2021 crash dump. The threat group, Storm-0558, accessed a Microsoft engineer’s corporate account after the key was inadvertently moved from a secure production environment to a debugging environment. The incident has raised significant concerns about Microsoft's production environment security and detection capabilities.

title: If You Like SPHQ, Check Out Quality ETFs XMHQ and XSHQ
summary: The article highlights the growing popularity of quality ETFs, specifically discussing the Invesco S&P 500 Quality ETF (SPHQ) and suggesting that investors also consider mid-cap and small-cap quality ETFs like XMHQ and XSHQ for broader exposure. It emphasizes that quality factors are significant across all market capitalizations and details how these Invesco funds select their holdings based on quality scores. The piece also provides insights into the AUM and recent inflows for each ETF, noting the key differences and similarities in how they track quality within their respective market segments.

title: Seagate Game Drive for Xbox: Starfield Special Edition Released, RGB LED Lighting Included
summary: Seagate has released a Starfield Special Edition Game Drive for Xbox, featuring RGB LED lighting and available in 2TB and 5TB capacities. This officially licensed external hard drive, compatible with Xbox Series X|S and Xbox One, simplifies game storage with easy installation and no separate power cord. The drives are priced at $109.99 for 2TB and $169.99 for 5TB, including data recovery services and a one-year warranty.

title: History of Apple in Photos, From Steve Jobs Era to $3 Trillion
summary: This article chronicles the history of Apple, from its founding by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak to its recent achievement of a $3 trillion market cap under Tim Cook. It details key product launches like the Apple II, Macintosh, iPod, and iPhone, as well as leadership changes and challenges such as the recent DOJ lawsuit and declining iPhone sales in China. The article also touches upon Apple's expansion into services and new product categories like the Apple Watch and Vision Pro, alongside its pivot towards generative AI.

title: The pros and cons of the Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US ETF (VEU)
summary: The Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US ETF (VEU) offers extensive geographic and business diversification with over 3,700 holdings and a low annual management fee of 0.08%. While it provides exposure to many strong global companies outside the US, its exclusion of US businesses means it misses out on some top performers like Apple and Microsoft, which have driven higher returns in US-inclusive international ETFs. Despite this, VEU remains a strong choice for investors seeking broad non-US market exposure.

title: More writers sue OpenAI for copyright infringement over AI training
summary: A group of U.S. authors, including Pulitzer Prize winner Michael Chabon, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in federal court in San Francisco. They allege that OpenAI misused their copyrighted writings to train its AI-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, without permission. This is at least the third such class-action lawsuit by authors against OpenAI, with the plaintiffs seeking monetary damages and an injunction against OpenAI's business practices.

title: YieldMax Writes Call Options on JPMorgan Chase With JPMO ETF
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax JPM Option Income Strategy ETF (JPMO), an actively managed fund that aims to generate monthly income by writing call options on JPMorgan Chase & Co. Corp. (JPM). The fund seeks to harvest compelling yields with capped participation in JPM's price gains and is managed by ZEGA Financial with an expense ratio of 0.99%. This new ETF expands YieldMax's growing suite of option income strategy ETFs.

title: Developing and deploying AI responsibly: elements of an effective legislative framework to regulate AI
summary: Brad Smith, Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, testified before a Senate Judiciary subcommittee, endorsing the Blumenthal-Hawley framework for AI regulation. He shared Microsoft's principles for developing effective legislation, including prioritizing safety and security, ensuring compliance with existing legal protections, and using AI to improve government services. The testimony emphasizes accountability, building on existing efforts like the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, requiring "safety brakes" for AI in critical infrastructure, and implementing a "KY3C" (Know Your Customer, Cloud, and Content) framework.

title: New Coder 2.0 aims to elevate onboarding and productivity in the cloud era
summary: Coder Technologies Inc. announced the general availability of Coder 2.0, an open-source, cloud-native platform designed to enhance developer onboarding and productivity by shifting development to cloud-based workspaces. The new version introduces Dev Container support, a template registry, and integration with JFrog Artifactory and Xray, aiming to provide a local development experience with the security, power, and scalability of the cloud. Coder 2.0 seeks to streamline CDE workflows and remove productivity bottlenecks for developers and platform engineering teams.

title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Desktop as a Service
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the inaugural 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Desktop as a Service (DaaS), highlighting its commitment to providing secure and versatile cloud solutions for flexible work. The article discusses Microsoft's key DaaS offerings, Windows 365 and Azure Virtual Desktop, and recent innovations such as Windows 365 Frontline and enhanced security features like watermarking and confidential virtual machines in Azure Virtual Desktop. This recognition reinforces Microsoft's focus on empowering productivity for all employees through modern endpoint solutions.

title: Microsoft expands partnership with Oracle to bring customers’ mission-critical database workloads to Azure
summary: Microsoft and Oracle are expanding their partnership to enable Oracle's customers to run mission-critical database workloads on Microsoft Azure. This new offering, Oracle Database@Azure, allows customers to migrate existing Oracle databases "as is" to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and deploy them within Azure datacenters, providing seamless integration with Azure services like Azure OpenAI. The collaboration aims to reduce hurdles for cloud migration and allow businesses to leverage cutting-edge cloud services with their Oracle databases, improving efficiency and enabling new applications.

title: Microsoft and Oracle Expand Partnership to Deliver Oracle Database Services on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure in Microsoft Azure
summary: Microsoft and Oracle have expanded their partnership to launch Oracle Database@Azure, allowing customers direct access to Oracle database services running on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) in Microsoft Azure datacenters. This collaboration simplifies cloud migration, offers high-performance Oracle databases with Azure's services and AI capabilities, and provides a streamlined purchasing process through Azure Marketplace. The service aims to address challenges in multicloud architectures and is supported by a joint support model.

title: Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS), including its current stock price, key statistics like PE Ratio and Dividend Yield, and its top holdings as of August 31, 2025. The ETF has a portfolio value of $8.44 billion with top holdings including NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. It also presents historical performance data and breakdowns of asset allocation and sector exposure.

title: Sanofi uses the industrial metaverse to revolutionize training and operational efficiency
summary: Sanofi has implemented a pilot project using Microsoft HoloLens 2 with Dynamics 365 Guides and Remote Assist to revolutionize employee training and operational efficiency in its packaging workshop. This initiative has reduced training time for new operators from eight to six weeks, standardized training procedures, and enhanced troubleshooting capabilities through remote assistance. Sanofi plans to expand this solution across its value chain, leveraging the industrial metaverse for greater efficiency and innovation.

title: JPMorgan Global Select Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:JGLO) $JGLO
summary: This article provides details on the JPMorgan Global Select Equity ETF (JGLO), including its current price, holdings, and recent news. The ETF aims for long-term capital appreciation by investing in global equities across various market capitalizations, integrating ESG factors. Key statistics, expense ratios, major shareholders, and top holdings like NVIDIA and Microsoft are highlighted.

title: 29 African Startups in Healthcare Supply Chain Selected to Receive Funding and Impact Support from the Gates Foundation, MSD, and More
summary: The Investing in Innovation (i3) program, funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation and sponsored by Cencora, MSD, Microsoft, and Chemonics, has selected 29 African startups for its second cohort. These startups, operating in 21 African countries, will receive a $50,000 grant, market access, and tailored investment readiness support to enhance healthcare supply chains. The program aims to foster growth-focused partnerships and drive the commercialization of innovative solutions, building on the success of its first cohort which secured 24 contracts and strategic partnerships.

title: Morgan Stanley to Launch AI-Powered Assistant for Financial Advisers
summary: Morgan Stanley is launching an AI-powered assistant for its financial advisers and support staff, named the AI @ Morgan Stanley Assistant. This tool, based on OpenAI's GPT-4, provides quick access to 100,000 research reports and documents, aiming to enhance client interactions and efficiency. The bank also plans to introduce other generative AI tools, including "Debrief" for summarizing meetings and generating follow-up emails.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF
summary: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) offers investors global equity exposure to large and mid-capitalization developed and emerging markets. It tracks the MSCI All Country World Index, providing diversification across numerous countries and sectors efficiently. The ETF is marketed as a low-cost investment vehicle with potential for long-term growth, though it acknowledges inherent investment risks.

title: Intel pitches the 'AI PC' at software developer event
summary: Intel announced a new "Meteor Lake" chip, expected in December, that will enable generative AI chatbots to run directly on laptops without needing cloud data centers. This development, showcased at a software developer conference, aims to offer businesses and consumers greater privacy and faster AI responses for ChatGPT-style technologies. Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger positioned this as a "sea change moment" for tech innovation, with the company also announcing plans for a successor chip and partnerships in the AI space.

title: Oracle says it's ready to cater to Canadian banks' open banking needs
summary: Oracle has declared its readiness to support Canadian banks with their open banking requirements, offering technology solutions as Canada implements its framework. Oracle's executive vice president for financial services, Sonny Singh, noted that the company's existing financial products, used globally, are well-positioned for this shift. This comes as Canadian banks are increasingly partnering with fintech firms and Oracle, despite recent market value losses due to strong cloud-computing competition.

title: Continuing our commitment to further open source security
summary: JPMorgan Chase is dedicated to enhancing open source security due to its critical role in innovation and potential vulnerabilities. The company actively participates in initiatives like OpenSSF and collaborates with financial institutions to address supply chain threats. Recent efforts include focusing on security education for developers, securing OSS repositories, and fostering cross-collaboration for incident response.

title: Cisco to Buy Splunk, a Software Company, for $28 Billion (Published 2023)
summary: Cisco announced its largest acquisition to date, agreeing to buy data-analysis software company Splunk for $28 billion in cash. This move signifies Cisco's strategic shift further into higher-margin software subscriptions and away from its traditional networking hardware roots. The deal faces potential scrutiny from antitrust regulators, although both companies believe there is little product overlap.

title: 3 Quantum Computing Stocks That Should Be on Every Investor’s Radar This Fall
summary: This article highlights three leading quantum computing stocks: IBM, Alphabet, and Microsoft, emphasizing their significant advancements in the field. IBM is recognized for its quantum breakthrough with over 100 qubits. Alphabet's quantum computer can solve complex problems far faster than traditional supercomputers, while Microsoft is advancing quantum computing to revolutionize chemistry and materials science.

title: Indian auto insurance technology start-up Roadzen listed on Nasdaq
summary: Indian auto insurance technology start-up Roadzen has listed on Nasdaq, with its ordinary shares trading under the symbol "RDZN" and warrants under "RDZNW." The company, powered by artificial intelligence, achieved a pre-money equity value of $683 million and aims to lead the transformation of the auto insurance market through AI, mobility, and insurance. Roadzen's CEO, Rohan Malhotra, emphasizes the company's global vision and its commitment to building innovative AI technology for markets worldwide.

title: |‘Spider-Man No Way Home’ Figures Added to Hasbro's Marvel Legends Line
summary: Hasbro has unveiled a new wave of Marvel Legends action figures celebrating "Spider-Man: No Way Home," featuring the three live-action Spider-Men: Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland in his new suit. The collection also includes figures of MJ, Matt Murdock, and Sandman. These figures allow fans to commemorate the critically acclaimed film, which brought together the different generations of Spider-Man for an emotionally impactful storyline.

title: MSFU ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:MSFU
summary: This analysis details the Direxion Daily MSFT Bull 2X Shares (MSFU) ETF, designed to deliver 2x the daily price return of Microsoft stock. It highlights key statistics such as its $262.97M AUM, 2.56% dividend yield, and 0.99% expense ratio. The article also provides information on its structure, investment strategy, and recent performance, noting that the fund invests solely in cash.

title: Amadeus to Work with Microsoft and Accenture to Develop New Generative AI-Powered Integrations to Support Corporate Travel Management
summary: Amadeus announced a collaboration with Microsoft and Accenture to integrate generative AI into its Cytric Easy travel and expense platform. This partnership aims to develop new AI-powered tools, including a digital assistant for corporate travelers within Microsoft Teams and a plugin for Microsoft 365 Copilot, to streamline travel management and enhance the booking experience using natural language.

title: Is Datadog a Buyout Target After Cisco's Splunk Acquisition?
summary: After Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk, Datadog (DDOG) is being considered a potential buyout target, given its strong long-term growth potential and current undervaluation. Despite a recent stock dip due to a cautious near-term revenue outlook, Datadog's expanding product suite and critical role in cloud-based monitoring and analytics make it attractive to competitors or large tech companies looking to bolster their cloud software presence. The article suggests that a further drop in its stock price could increase the likelihood of takeover talks.

title: What Market Pros Saw Last Earnings Season That Spurred Investments in Tech and AI Stocks
summary: Market experts, managing over $67 billion, analyzed second-quarter earnings reports and concluded that the bull market has room to run, a U.S. recession is unlikely, consumer spending remains strong, and AI is not in a bubble but rather a significant driver of corporate profits. They identified key sectors and companies benefiting from current growth trends, including broad-market strength beyond AI and Big Tech, and a re-emerging strong momentum in European economies.

title: EPAM Expands Partnership with Microsoft - Becomes Globally Managed Enterprise Systems Integrator
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced its new status as a Microsoft globally Managed Enterprise Systems Integrator, significantly expanding its strategic partnership with Microsoft. This enhanced status allows EPAM to accelerate technology innovation and digital transformation for enterprises by leveraging cloud-native, AI, and data expertise, particularly through end-to-end solution design and implementation in the cloud. The company will focus on building intelligent applications powered by Azure OpenAI Service and growing Microsoft certifications, building on its recent recognition as the 2023 Microsoft U.S. Azure Migration Partner of the Year.

title: Silicon Hegemon: Could China Take Over Taiwan’s Semiconductor Industry Without Invading?
summary: This article explores the high-impact, low-probability scenario of China taking control of Taiwan's semiconductor industry, particularly TSMC, without a full-scale military invasion. It outlines hypothetical coercion tactics short of war, such as blockades and economic pressure, that could force Taiwan into ceding control. The authors propose military, regulatory, and policy responses for Taiwan and its allies to prevent such an outcome, emphasizing deterrence and preparedness to defend free passage to and from Taiwan.

title: Nuance Releases Generative AI Copilot for Clinical Documentation
summary: Nuance Communications, Inc., a Microsoft Company, has launched Dragon Ambient eXperience (DAX) Copilot, a generative AI-powered tool for clinical documentation. It streamlines the creation of draft clinical summaries from patient conversations, aiming to reduce administrative burden and clinician burnout. This solution integrates Nuance's conversational and ambient AI with generative AI models and is part of the broader Nuance Dragon suite.

title: Lumen CEO Kate Johnson on Seattle tech, AI, lessons from Microsoft, and ‘cloudifying’ telecom
summary: Lumen Technologies CEO Kate Johnson discusses her efforts to reinvent the telecom company by "cloudifying" its operations and adapting to the rapid growth of data and AI. She highlights Seattle's importance as a tech hub for Lumen's services and shares insights from her past experience at Microsoft, including their pilot program for Microsoft 365 Copilot to enhance productivity. Johnson also touches on cultural changes within Lumen to foster innovation and problem-solving.

title: Adobe hit with $33.8 million patent verdict over digital licensing technology
summary: A Delaware federal court jury has ordered Adobe to pay ViaTech Technologies nearly $33.8 million for infringing a patent related to digital licensing technology. ViaTech's lawsuit claimed Adobe's software activation system for products like Acrobat and Photoshop violated its patent for controlling access to digital content. Adobe expressed disappointment with the verdict and plans to explore post-trial and appellate options.

title: These 3 hiring companies reveal their tech stacks and business applications
summary: This article explores the technology stacks and business applications used by three companies: Chatham Financial, CubeSmart, and Fastmail. It provides insights into the tools these organizations leverage for product development, communication, and project management, offering potential job seekers a glimpse into their operational environments and culture.

title: Cisco-Splunk under the microscope: Joint customers weigh in
summary: Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk is viewed positively by joint customers, particularly for strengthening Cisco's security and observability portfolios, despite some concerns about potential pricing changes. A flash survey from Enterprise Technology Research revealed that while sentiment is generally favorable, especially regarding synergies and product overlap, customers expressed apprehension about higher prices and a 'brain drain' from Splunk. The deal is seen as a strategic move for Cisco to boost its software revenue and international presence, offering Splunk a lifeline after its challenging transition to a cloud and subscription model.

title: What is Joule? SAP’s new GenAI assistant
summary: SAP has introduced Joule, a new natural-language generative AI co-pilot designed to be embedded across its cloud enterprise portfolio. Joule aims to provide proactive and contextualized insights by analyzing data from SAP solutions and third-party sources, helping users work faster and achieve better business outcomes. SAP CEO Christian Klein emphasizes that Joule will understand user intent, leveraging SAP's extensive enterprise user base and a responsible approach to Business AI.

title: What’s Changed: 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Sales Force Automation Platforms
summary: This article summarizes Gartner's 2023 Magic Quadrant for Sales Force Automation Platforms, identifying 13 significant providers. It categorizes vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Niche Players, and Visionaries, detailing their key solutions, recent updates, and differentiating strengths. The report highlights Salesforce, Microsoft, and Oracle as Leaders, and discusses emerging trends like generative AI and enhanced mobile capabilities within SFA solutions.

title: Rockwell Automation goes cloud-native on Azure to bring modern software development paradigms to the factory floor
summary: Rockwell Automation, the world's largest industrial automation company, is leveraging Microsoft Azure, Visual Studio Code, and GitHub to deliver its first cloud-native application, FactoryTalk Design Studio. This move aims to bring modern software development practices like object-oriented programming and collaboration features to the factory floor, overcoming traditional limitations of on-premises applications. The partnership with Microsoft has enabled Rockwell Automation to enhance productivity, scalability, and security for its customers and its own development teams, significantly accelerating the delivery of new features and value.

title: ConocoPhillips goes global with digital twins
summary: ConocoPhillips is expanding its use of digital twin technology across its entire portfolio after initial successes in its major fields, aiming to optimize safety and efficiency. This global program, awarded a 2023 CIO 100 Award, helps the company maximize extraction from existing assets, manage volatile industry prices, and reduce GHG emissions. The company is developing a common platform and community to accelerate deployment and connect digital twins with other systems for greater insights and value.

title: Boeing PAC joins fleet of corporate givers to Kentucky Republican Party building fund
summary: Aerospace giant Boeing's PAC donated $100,000 to the Kentucky Republican Party's building fund, bringing the total corporate donations for the renovation and expansion of the Mitch McConnell Building to $2.75 million. This fund, allowed by a 2017 Kentucky law, accepts unlimited corporate contributions, unlike other political committees. The Republican building fund reported having over $2.7 million cash on hand, significantly more than the Kentucky Democratic Party's building fund.

title: theNET | Phishing keeps evolving
summary: Phishing attacks are continuously evolving, moving beyond simple malicious links to exploit human behavior across multiple communication channels. Attackers are increasingly adept at brand impersonation and bypassing email authentication checks, making traditional security measures less effective. Modern defense strategies emphasize cloud email security, layered protection, and a zero-trust model to combat these sophisticated threats.

title: Tech spared as bond selloff buffets stocks
summary: Treasury yields surged to their highest levels in over a decade as traders anticipated prolonged high borrowing costs from the Federal Reserve, impacting most US stocks. However, the Nasdaq 100, driven by major technology firms like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, saw gains. This market movement occurred despite a last-minute deal to avert a government shutdown, highlighting the market's primary focus on interest rate policy.

title: HR costs rise as data suggests tech can cut HR staff
summary: New research indicates that high-performing HR departments using technology operate with 40% fewer staff, despite rising HR operating costs due to wage hikes. While vendors promote generative AI for augmenting HR tasks, some experts advise caution and skepticism regarding its immediate impact on staff reduction. Top-performing companies invest more in HR tech, modernizing IT architecture, advanced analytics, and collaboration tools, contributing to their efficiency.

title: Moat Investing Rises Above Magnificent Seven Dominance
summary: The article discusses how Morningstar's Moat Index outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date despite being underweight the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap stocks. It highlights the growing concentration risk posed by these seven technology giants and explains how equal-weighted Moat strategies offer a solution through diversification and attractive valuations. The article also details the September index reconstitutions for both the Moat and SMID Moat Indexes, noting shifts away from growth stocks and into more value-oriented sectors.

title: Loan Servicing Software Market is Booming Worldwide with a High CAGR
summary: The Loan Servicing Software Market is experiencing rapid growth, projected to achieve a 16.5% CAGR between 2023 and 2030. This market, driven by digital transformation and AI integration, provides financial institutions with solutions for loan origination, payment processing, and compliance. Key players include Fiserv, FIS, and Black Knight, with challenges primarily involving integration complexities and regulatory compliance.

title: Over 800k Flagstar Bank Customers Impacted by Third Data Breach Since 2021
summary: Flagstar Bank is notifying over 800,000 customers about a data breach stemming from vulnerabilities in the MOVEit file transfer software used by its third-party vendor, Fiserv. This marks the third data breach for Flagstar since 2021, with previous incidents also involving file transfer hacks and impacting personal customer information. The bank is offering affected customers a complimentary two-year identity monitoring service through Kroll.

title: Why CXOs should go all-in on Cybersecurity Awareness Month
summary: Cybersecurity Awareness Month, observed every October, offers CXOs a critical opportunity to re-evaluate and strengthen their organization's security posture. It's not merely a promotional event but a chance for strategic introspection, emphasizing user training, technology adoption like AI, and adherence to foundational security practices such as strong passwords and regular updates. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency’s (CISA’s) "Secure Our World" program further reinforces the importance of fostering a security-first culture that extends to every user, both within and outside the workplace.

title: Microsoft Plans to Contest IRS Claim It Owes $28.9 Billion in Back Taxes
summary: Microsoft is planning to appeal an IRS decision stating the company owes at least $28.9 billion in back taxes. The dispute centers on how Microsoft allocated income and expenses among its global subsidiaries between 2004 and 2013. Microsoft announced its disagreement with the notices of proposed adjustment in a regulatory filing.

title: PacBio Announces Complete Computational Workflow for Human Whole Genome Sequencing Data Analysis
summary: PacBio has announced the availability of its WGS Variant Pipeline, a complete and standardized computational method for analyzing HiFi whole genome sequencing (WGS) data. This new software integrates PacBio and third-party tools into a single workflow, allowing customers to detect various variant types, along with methylation and phasing data, more efficiently than before. The pipeline aims to streamline data analysis, enable deeper biological discovery, and is accessible through several bioinformatics analysis platforms.

title: IWF ETF: Be Cautious Of High Valuations (NYSEARCA:IWF)
summary: The iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) tracks large-cap U.S. growth stocks and has its largest holdings in Apple and Microsoft. While it generally underperformed some growth ETFs like QQQ, it surpassed others like SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 Growth ETF due to differences in its holdings and sector exposure. The article advises caution due to potentially high valuations.

title: Amdocs Enables Migration of Classic Applications to Azure and Oracle Cloud for Service Providers
summary: Amdocs has announced that service providers can now migrate their classic Amdocs applications to Microsoft Azure or Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This is made possible by the expanded partnership between Microsoft and Oracle, which delivers Oracle database services on OCI within Azure data centers. This development allows service providers to integrate Amdocs classic applications with newer solutions and accelerate their hybrid cloud journeys.

title: Generative AI Delivers a Boost to Cloud Computing
summary: Cloud computing companies are experiencing a rebound after a difficult period, driven by improving fundamentals and the increasing importance of generative AI. Spending on cloud computing is projected to rise by nearly 22% in 2023, and cloud providers are capitalizing on this trend by integrating AI-incorporated solutions and monetizing AI-as-a-service. This surge in AI adoption is expected to lead to better-than-expected revenue and profit growth for companies in the cloud computing sector.

title: Microsoft’s Activision Buy Extends Nadella’s Decade of Deals
summary: Microsoft has completed its $75 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard, marking the largest deal in its nearly 50-year history and the biggest in a decade under CEO Satya Nadella. This acquisition solidifies Microsoft's position in various sectors, including videogames and artificial intelligence, despite facing a 21-month-long regulatory review process.

title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team discovered a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel for airstrike alerts. This malicious version, distributed via a deceptive website, collects sensitive user data including call logs, SMS, contacts, and account information, while mimicking the original app's functionality. Users who installed the Android version from `redalerts[.]me` are advised to delete it immediately and reinstall the authentic app from the Google Play Store.

title: Microsoft Closed Its Activision Deal. These Stocks Stand to Gain.
summary: Microsoft has successfully closed its Activision acquisition, a deal that faced significant regulatory scrutiny and required concessions on streaming rights. This massive tech acquisition is seen as an indicator of the global regulatory environment, and its completion suggests potential gains for certain stocks. The article implies that other companies or sectors might benefit from the precedent set by this landmark deal.

title: Gartner Identifies the Top 10 Strategic Technology Trends for 2024
summary: Gartner has announced its top 10 strategic technology trends for 2024, emphasizing the need for organizations to act boldly amidst technological disruptions and socioeconomic uncertainties. The trends, presented at Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo 2023, highlight the increasing role of AI, sustainability, and intelligent automation in driving business value and resilience. Key themes include the democratization of Generative AI, AI Trust, Risk and Security Management, AI-Augmented Development, and the emergence of Machine Customers and Industry Cloud Platforms.

title: Will AI Take Your Job? Answer: Yes, No & Awesomely So
summary: This article explores the impact of AI on jobs, arguing against the doomsday predictions of massive layoffs. While acknowledging that job displacement will occur, the author, Satyen K. Bordoloi, emphasizes that AI will also create new opportunities and redefine the job market, leading to increased productivity and a higher standard of living in the long run. The piece encourages individuals to adapt and leverage AI for personal and professional growth, viewing it as an opportunity rather than a threat.

title: Five reasons why... WPP has merged VMLY&R and Wunderman Thompson
summary: WPP has merged its agencies VMLY&R and Wunderman Thompson to form VML, creating the world's largest creative company. This strategic move aims to offer clients a comprehensive "one-stop shop" for brand experience, customer experience, and commerce, particularly leveraging advancements in AI. The merger also seeks to simplify client relationships, minimize duplication of capabilities, and provide strong competition against consulting firms.

title: Lululemon Athletica to join the S&P500 next week
summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. (Nasdaq:LULU) is scheduled to join the S&P500 on October 18, replacing Activision Blizzard (Nasdaq:ATVI) following its acquisition by Microsoft Corp. This announcement led to a nearly five percent spike in Lululemon's share price after hours, as index fund managers are expected to add the company's shares to their portfolios to replicate the S&P500. The S&P Global index committee includes companies based on size, with Lululemon's market capitalization around US$47.8 billion and its share price up 16.8 percent year-to-date.

title: Authors sue Meta, Microsoft, Bloomberg in latest AI copyright clash
summary: A group of authors including former Arkansas governor Mike Huckabee and Lysa TerKeurst have filed a class-action lawsuit against Meta, Microsoft, and Bloomberg, alleging unauthorized use of their copyrighted works to train AI systems. The lawsuit claims the companies used the "Books3" dataset, which contains pirated books, to train their large language models. The authors are seeking unspecified money damages and an injunction to prevent further misuse of their work.

title: Ask AI Guru: Genpact turns to generative AI to help employees learn
summary: Genpact, a NYSE-listed professional services company, has rolled out an internal generative AI chatbot called "AI Guru" to enhance employee learning and development. This tool, powered by Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service, helps employees with tasks such as IT migration, test case generation, and management advice, and provides 24/7 access to collective expertise from internal "master gurus." The initiative aims to improve employee retention and efficiency by offering personalized and constantly updated learning content, addressing a bottleneck in their traditional learning methods.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Franklin Templeton Optimistic About Emerging Markets
summary: Chetan Sehgal, lead portfolio manager of Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust (TEMIT) and senior managing director at Franklin Templeton, expresses optimism about emerging markets, citing digitalization, high-tech firms, and renewable energy. He highlights strong performance in South Korea, Taiwan, and India, with specific stock picks like PAYTM, Policy Bazaar, and Zomato. Sehgal also sees recovery in Brazil and stabilization in China's economy, emphasizing the increasing awareness and improvement in ESG issues across emerging markets.

title: Allstate’s cloud-first approach to digital transformation pays off
summary: Allstate is undertaking a comprehensive digital transformation by adopting a cloud-first approach, rebuilding its core applications on the cloud rather than migrating legacy systems. This strategy has significantly reduced claims processing time and enhanced customer experience, with the company leveraging a multicloud architecture including AWS, Google BigQuery, and Microsoft Azure for various workloads and generative AI applications. The transformation has involved re-engineering business processes and overcoming cultural challenges within the organization under the leadership of EVP and CIO Zulfi Jeevanjee.

title: Apple to spend $1 billion on AI in catch-up move: report
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly investing $1 billion to accelerate its efforts in generative artificial intelligence. This move comes after the company was reportedly "caught off guard" by the rapid rise of AI tools like ChatGPT last year. The investment aims to integrate generative AI technology across all Apple products, with the initiative being led by senior vice presidents John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi.

title: Pfizer to pay $2M to resolve allegations that the company underpaid women
summary: Pfizer will pay $2 million to resolve allegations of pay discrimination against 86 women, stemming from a U.S. Department of Labor review covering 2015-2016. The Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) alleged that Pfizer violated Executive Order 11246 by paying these women less than comparable male employees. While Pfizer agreed to the settlement, stating the discrepancies were minor and from seven years ago, the company denied revising its compensation system as stated by the DOL, asserting its commitment to equitable pay practices.

title: The Top 50 SaaS CEOs of 2023
summary: The Software Report has announced its Top 50 SaaS CEOs of 2023, recognizing leaders for their strategic vision, tactical execution, and inspiring leadership. The awardees include individuals who have significantly grown their companies, driven digital transformation, and developed advanced software solutions across various industries. The selection process involved nominations from industry experts and in-house research, highlighting CEOs like Craig Courtemanche of Procore, Therese Tucker of BlackLine, and Darren Roos of IFS for their remarkable achievements.

title: Capri Sun serves ‘epic’ kid moments on Dude Perfect, family-focused platforms
summary: Capri Sun has launched a new campaign titled "It's Gonna Be Epic," focusing on children's perspectives of small, everyday occurrences that feel monumental to them. This campaign, created by Goodby Silverstein & Partners with media by Starcom’s Publicis 57, is the brand's first major effort since reducing sugar in its formula last year and targets co-viewing and co-listening families across various streaming and audio platforms. It emphasizes "kid-in-charge" moments through three new spots that will run from October 2023 through March 2024.

title: Top 10 Cloud Security Companies
summary: The cloud-managed services market is projected to reach US$164 billion by 2027, driven by increasing enterprise demand and a lack of IT expertise, while cloud security is poised for substantial growth to US$170.51 billion by 2032 due to rising cyber threats. This article lists the top 10 cloud security companies in 2023, based on market capitalization, including industry giants like Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon, highlighting their key offerings and recent developments in cloud security. The companies presented offer diverse solutions, from comprehensive cloud platforms and cybersecurity infrastructure to data analytics and application security.

title: More tech coming for Schwab advisors
summary: Following the merger of Charles Schwab Corp. and TD Ameritrade, Schwab is enhancing its technology offerings for the thousands of registered investment advisors who custody nearly $4 trillion in client assets with the firm. Despite acknowledging an "imperfect" transition process, Schwab executives are emphasizing new additions like digital onboarding, e-signature tools, and an expanded no-transaction-fee fund platform, while cautiously exploring AI applications primarily for client service improvements rather than financial advice.

title: Microsoft, Rockwell Automation to bring Gen AI to robotics development
summary: Microsoft and Rockwell Automation are expanding their partnership to integrate Generative AI into robotics development, aiming to boost productivity and accelerate time-to-market. Their first joint effort involves adding Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service to Rockwell's FactoryTalk Design Studio, enabling engineers to use natural language for code generation and task automation. This collaboration seeks to streamline industrial automation processes, with potential future applications in quality management, failure analysis, and workforce training.

title: Nasdaq 100 outlook: How will Amazon earnings impact AMZN stock?
summary: Amazon is set to release its Q3 earnings, with analysts expecting significant revenue and EPS growth driven by easier year-over-year comparisons and improved margins. The focus will be on Amazon Web Services (AWS) and its growth stability, especially in light of AI advancements, as well as the recovery of e-commerce operations and the performance of Prime Day sales. Although Amazon shares are currently at five-month lows, the company's advertising revenue continues to show strong growth, and the overall outlook for Big Tech earnings remains critical amidst economic uncertainty.

title: Understanding Artificial Intelligence (AI) | Infographic
summary: This infographic provides an overview of Artificial Intelligence (AI), a transformative technology impacting various aspects of life. It delves into the different subsets of AI and illustrates its diverse applications. Readers can download a PDF version for a comprehensive look at how AI is revolutionizing industries.

title: |Intapp and Microsoft deliver AI-based solutions built on Azure to financial and professional services firms
summary: Intapp and Microsoft are partnering to deliver AI-based solutions built on Azure for financial and professional services firms, a $3 trillion market. These firms have complex relationship structures, partner-led operations, and strict compliance needs, which standard software solutions don't adequately address. Intapp's specialized SaaS offering, powered by Azure and Azure OpenAI, aims to harness the intelligence of professionals through AI to enhance deal execution, risk management, and client engagement.

title: Microsoft will hold a 27% stake in OpenAI and gain access to its AI models.
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have finalized a new agreement where Microsoft will acquire a 27% stake in OpenAI, valued at $135 billion. This agreement grants Microsoft access to OpenAI's AI technology, including AGI models, until 2032. OpenAI has also committed to investing an additional $250 billion in purchasing Azure services.

title: Insight 2023: NetApp Enhanced Data Pipeline for Hybrid Multi-Cloud AI and Advancements on Secure Storage Vs. Ransomware
summary: NetApp announced significant portfolio updates at Insight 2023, focusing on enhancements to its data pipeline for hybrid multi-cloud AI and advancements in secure storage against ransomware. The company is simplifying AI data pipeline deployment across hybrid multi-cloud environments, while also strengthening data protection with features like autonomous ransomware detection and an extended Ransomware Recovery Guarantee. These innovations aim to address the dual challenges of leveraging AI opportunities and mitigating ransomware threats for customers.

title: Rentokil Initial enhances security
summary: Rentokil Initial, a global business services company, has significantly enhanced its security compliance, tracking, and reporting across over 25,000 IT assets worldwide by integrating Lansweeper IT asset management software with its existing ServiceNow platform. This upgrade addresses gaps in their asset management strategy, provides comprehensive visibility of IT assets, identifies potential vulnerabilities, and ensures compliance with regulations like GDPR, particularly benefiting their mergers and acquisition strategy. The deployment of Lansweeper across regional data centers and its integration with ServiceNow has transformed their IT landscape, moving from manual processes to streamlined, accurate, and proactive security measures.

title: Microsoft Edged Out Its Cloud Computing Competitors in Q3
summary: Microsoft's cloud computing service, Azure, reported 29% growth in Q3, outperforming competitors Google Cloud and Amazon Web Services, largely due to strong demand for its AI capabilities. The article suggests inverse ETFs like MSFU, CLDL, and UBOT for traders looking to capitalize on Microsoft's continued competitiveness in cloud computing and the growth potential of AI. Microsoft's significant investment in OpenAI contributed to a 13% revenue jump, highlighting its strategic focus on integrating AI across its technology stack.

title: 7 Quantum Computing Stocks That AI Will Send Soaring
summary: This article highlights seven quantum computing stocks poised for growth due to increasing AI integration. It identifies key players from ETFs and pure-play companies to established tech giants and semiconductor firms, explaining how their involvement in quantum computing, often alongside AI and cloud technologies, positions them for future success. The piece also notes the resurgence of interest in quantum computing as interest rates stabilize.

title: MOAT: History May Not Repeat Itself (BATS:MOAT)
summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (MOAT) has a strong historical track record and reasonable fees but is significantly underweight in big tech. Given a bullish view on big tech, the author suggests MOAT may underperform the S&P 500 going forward. The article provides an overview of the ETF's strategy and the author's financial disclosures.

title: Blackstone, Vista acquire SaaS platform as The Riverside Company scores exit
summary: Funds associated with Blackstone and Vista Equity Partners have acquired Australia-based Energy Exemplar (EE), a provider of simulation software for the energy industry, from The Riverside Company for A$1.57 billion ($1 billion). This exit for The Riverside Company will enable EE to accelerate investment in its SaaS platform, which optimizes energy operations and planning. The deal leverages the combined expertise of Blackstone in energy and Vista in software to drive EE's growth in supporting grid reliability and decarbonization.

title: Bilibili Announces Most Popular Scientific Topics of 2023 among China's Young Generations
summary: Bilibili announced the "2023 Top 5 Most Popular Scientific Topics on Bilibili" among young Chinese generations, including AIGC, room-temperature superconductors, brain-computer interfaces, black holes, and controllable nuclear fusion. The platform hosted a "Super Science Night" event featuring renowned scientists and Bilibili creators to engage young audiences with science. Science and knowledge-related content is the second most searched category on Bilibili, accounting for 41% of total viewing time, with over 243 million users viewing such content in the past year.

title: SolarWinds hack explained: Everything you need to know
summary: The SolarWinds hack, identified as one of the largest cybersecurity breaches, involved malicious code injected into its Orion IT monitoring software, affecting thousands of organizations including the U.S. government. Suspected Russian nation-state hackers, referred to as Nobelium, gained widespread access, leading to significant investigations and legal actions against SolarWinds. The incident highlighted critical vulnerabilities in supply chain security and spurred new cybersecurity initiatives and regulations, such as the mandating of Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs).

title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.

title: Starbucks raising wages at least 3% starting Jan. 1
summary: Starbucks announced it will raise wages for its U.S. retail hourly partners by at least 3% starting January 1, 2024, with more tenured employees receiving higher increases up to 5%. This move is part of an expanded benefits package and comes without anticipated menu price increases. The company stated these investments aim to improve the partner experience, reduce turnover, and enhance customer connection scores.

title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Distributed Hybrid Infrastructure
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the newly introduced 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Distributed Hybrid Infrastructure (DHI). This recognition highlights Microsoft's innovation in cloud-to-edge products like Azure Arc, Azure Kubernetes Service, and Azure Stack HCI, which aim to provide cloud capabilities and agility in edge environments. Organizations like Carnival, COOP, and SKF are already leveraging these Azure technologies to streamline global operations, improve security, and reduce carbon footprint, demonstrating the real-world benefits of Microsoft's DHI offerings.

title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Utilized by Microsoft for Bing Conversational Search
summary: Oracle announced a multi-year agreement with Microsoft to support the growth of AI services. Microsoft is leveraging Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) AI infrastructure, alongside Microsoft Azure AI infrastructure, for inferencing AI models powering Bing conversational searches. This collaboration, utilizing the Oracle Interconnect for Microsoft Azure, enables Microsoft to scale its AI compute capacity to meet increasing demand.

title: Commvault re-integrates Metallic service into expanded security cloud
summary: Commvault Systems Inc. has launched Commvault Cloud, an integrated cloud offering that combines its SaaS and packaged software products with a focus on cyber resilience, AI for threat diagnosis, and recovery. This new platform re-integrates the Metallic Cloud Storage Service and introduces Arlie, an AI "co-pilot," to simplify cybersecurity tasks and enhance recovery capabilities. The service also includes features like Cloudburst Recovery, Cleanroom Recovery, and Platinum Resilience, aiming to address the increasing complexity of cyber incidents and ensure rapid data recovery.

title: Houston Innovation Awards winners revealed at 2023 event
summary: The 2023 Houston Innovation Awards announced winners across 13 categories, recognizing top innovators and startups in the city. The event, held in partnership with Houston Exponential, also honored Brad Burke with the Trailblazer Award and featured categories such as BIPOC-Owned, Female-Owned, Hardtech, Digital Solutions, Social Impact, Sustainability, Life Science Businesses, Corporate of the Year, DEI Champion, Ecosystem Builder, Mentor of the Year, and People's Choice. The awards highlight significant contributions to various industries and the startup ecosystem in Houston.

title: Better Home & Finance And Infosys Unveil 'Mortgage As A Service' Platform
summary: Better Home & Finance, in partnership with Infosys, has launched a "Mortgage as a Service" (MaaS) platform designed to streamline mortgage processing for banks. CEO Vishal Garg likens the platform to Amazon's AWS, aiming to standardize technology across the industry by offering a single digital solution for all stages of the mortgage process. The platform is intended to reduce the cost of originating mortgages, a figure that has paradoxically risen despite increased digitization efforts in the industry.

title: NetApp Extends Partnership with Microsoft Empowering Customers to Achieve More Through Cloud Adoption
summary: NetApp has renewed its collaboration with Microsoft, extending its offerings to Azure users with enhanced cloud storage, data management, and CloudOps solutions. This partnership brings innovations like Azure NetApp Files (ANF) and Spot by NetApp's CloudOps solutions to the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, aiming to optimize storage and compute, accelerate cloud adoption, and improve operational efficiency for customers. The collaboration focuses on delivering enterprise-grade performance, data protection, and cost-effective scalability for various workloads, including AI and Kubernetes.
