title: What to expect at HPE Discover Barcelona: Join theCUBE Nov. 29-30
summary: SiliconANGLE Media's theCUBE will cover HPE Discover Barcelona from November 29-30, focusing on Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co.'s advancements in AI, hybrid cloud, and edge computing. Analysts Rob Strechay and Rebecca Knight will discuss HPE's strategy, including its GreenLake platform for large language models and strategic partnerships, building on previous announcements and investments. The event will highlight HPE's commitment to innovation in AI, security, and cloud solutions, providing insights into the future of enterprise technology.

title: NetApp doubles down on Microsoft Azure partnership
summary: NetApp has strengthened its partnership with Microsoft Azure, aiming to boost its public cloud revenues which are growing despite hybrid cloud declines. This collaboration involves enhancing four key software products: Azure NetApp Files (ANF), Cloud Volumes ONTAP, BlueXP, and Spot, with ANF seeing significant improvements in features and regional availability. The updated Spot offering, integrated with Azure Kubernetes Service and other NetApp products, uses AI/ML to optimize Azure compute, promising increased efficiency and reduced operational complexity for cloud management.

title: Microsoft Earnings Beat: What’s Next?
summary: Microsoft recently reported earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by a 13% revenue surge year-over-year and significant growth in its Azure cloud segment, fueled by AI consumption. The article discusses opportunities for traders through Direxion's leveraged and inverse ETFs for Microsoft and other tech sectors, while also addressing skeptical views on Microsoft's valuation and recent acquisition. It highlights the potential for continued growth due to AI investments but also notes risks like high valuation compared to the S&P 500 and the impact of its Activision Blizzard acquisition.

title: 10월 하락장 맞춘 AI ETF…엔비디아보다 '이것' 담았다 [글로벌마켓나우 플러스]
summary: LG AI연구원과 인공지능 전문 자산운용사 크래프트 테크놀로지스가 협력하여 'LG 크래프트 AI 파워드 US 라지캡 코어 상장지수펀드'(LQAI ETF)를 뉴욕증권거래소에 상장했습니다. 이 ETF는 엔비디아보다 구글과 마이크로소프트 비중을 높이고, 크래프트 테크놀로지스의 AI 위험 예측 지표를 통해 10월 하락장과 11월 초 반등을 예측하는 정확도를 보였습니다. 내년 시장 전망에서는 넷플릭스를 최선호 종목으로 꼽고, 기술주와 헬스케어 비중을 높이며 부동산 섹터는 편입하지 않고 있습니다.

title: Fair Isaac Corp. Stock Outperforms Competitors Despite Losses On The Day
summary: Fair Isaac Corp.'s stock outperformed its competitors despite losses on the day. The article also briefly covers Unity Software's Q4 financial results, noting higher-than-expected revenue but a quarterly loss per share.

title: How Microsoft’s Legal Legacy Shapes the Antitrust Case Against Google (Published 2023)
summary: The ongoing Google antitrust trial frequently invokes the landmark Microsoft antitrust case from the 1990s as a legal precedent. Both the Justice Department and Google draw parallels and distinctions, with the judge acknowledging the Microsoft case as a guiding framework. The core of the comparison lies in alleged monopolistic practices, the impact of "network effects" in digital markets, and the potential remedies for perceived anti-competitive behavior.

title: AMD Brings New AI and Compute Capabilities to Microsoft Customers
summary: AMD and Microsoft are collaborating to integrate AMD's AI and compute technologies into Microsoft's cloud and PC ecosystems. This includes the AMD Instinct MI300X accelerator powering new Azure AI-optimized virtual machines and 4th Gen AMD EPYC processors enhancing general-purpose, memory-intensive, and compute-optimized VMs. Additionally, AMD is expanding its Ryzen AI presence in PCs for generative AI experiences.

title: Key Considerations for 2024 Corporate Tax Planning, Including Capitalizing Technology Expenditures, and the IRS’ Use of Artificial Intelligence
summary: This article provides key considerations for 2024 corporate tax planning, focusing on the capitalization of specified research or experimentation (SRE) costs, bonus depreciation changes, and the IRS's new initiatives using AI to enhance tax compliance. It details interim guidance on SRE expenditures, the phase-out of bonus depreciation, and the halt on new ERTC credits. The piece also highlights the IRS's intensified scrutiny on high-income earners, large corporations, and partnerships through AI-driven enforcement, while aiming to protect ordinary taxpayers from scams.

title: Amdocs to create telco-specific GenAI models using Nvidia service
summary: Amdocs will utilize Nvidia's AI foundry service to develop custom large language models (LLMs) specifically for the global telecommunications industry. This collaboration aims to provide telcos with enhanced performance and optimized resource utilization through specialized GenAI capabilities, building on a prior partnership between Amdocs and Microsoft. The development emphasizes the growing industry need for tailored AI solutions to address sector-specific challenges, as highlighted by other major telecom players developing their own GenAI models.

title: Byte by byte
summary: Big Tech companies have significantly influenced the negotiation of the EU's AI Act, primarily by lobbying to undermine or limit regulation of foundation models and "high-risk" AI. Despite initial proposals for stricter oversight, intensive lobbying efforts by tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI, coupled with support from certain EU member states, have led to a weakening of proposed regulations, including the adoption of self-assessment mechanisms for risk and the exclusion of military applications. This report highlights how the industry's considerable financial and lobbying power has prioritized corporate profits over fundamental rights, environmental concerns, and independent oversight, raising questions about the effectiveness of regulating Big Tech.

title: ServiceNow launches major expansion to Now Assist generative AI portfolio with new capabilities to transform
summary: ServiceNow has significantly expanded its Now Assist generative AI portfolio, integrating new capabilities into the Now Platform to enhance user experiences and boost productivity. The new solutions, including Now Assist in Virtual Agent, flow generation, and Now Assist for Field Service Management, are designed to accelerate digital transformation by enabling conversational self-service, streamlining workflow development, and improving field service operations. These additions demonstrate ServiceNow's commitment to secure, enterprise-wide generative AI, leveraging both general-purpose LLMs and their purpose-built Now LLM.

title: Legendary value investor's firm launches new fund targeting ‘quality' stocks
summary: Legendary value investor Jeremy Grantham's firm, GMO, has launched its first active ETF, the GMO U.S. Quality ETF, focusing on "quality" stocks. Managed by Tom Hancock, the fund targets companies that can sustainably deploy capital at high rates of return, primarily in technology, healthcare, and consumer staples, offering potential tax advantages and ease of trading for clients. The ETF aims to identify "battleship companies" with strong balance sheets and competitive moats.

title: 3 Best Long-Term Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next 10 Years
summary: This article identifies three long-term stocks — ASML Holding N.V., Axon Enterprise, and General Electric — that are well-positioned to benefit from secular tailwinds over the next decade. ASML is a critical player in semiconductor manufacturing, Axon leads in public safety technology, and General Electric is poised for growth in air travel. The author suggests these companies offer sustainable growth opportunities beyond popular AI and cloud computing themes.

title: AI Boosting Payments Efficiency & Cutting Fraud
summary: J.P. Morgan highlights how artificial intelligence is enhancing payment efficiency and reducing fraud, especially within non-bank financial institutions. The article discusses AI's role in boosting productivity, cutting costs, and improving fraud detection, citing examples from J.P. Morgan and Microsoft. It also emphasizes the importance of data governance and ethical considerations for a successful AI rollout.

title: OpenAI’s Misalignment and Microsoft’s Gain
summary: The article discusses the dramatic events surrounding Sam Altman's firing from OpenAI and subsequent move to Microsoft. It highlights the inherent instability of OpenAI's non-profit structure, arguing that this misalignment of incentives ultimately benefited Microsoft, which gained key talent and intellectual property without acquisition costs. The author reflects on the implications for the AI landscape, suggesting Microsoft is now in a dominant position and raising questions about OpenAI's future and the motivations behind the board's decision.

title: Gaming Industry Charts a Rebound in 2023
summary: The gaming industry is experiencing a significant rebound in 2023 driven by strong console sales, new game releases, and innovations in mobile and cloud gaming. This recovery, following a dip in 2022, is further supported by anticipation of increased M&A activity and strategic moves by major players like Microsoft, Apple, and Netflix, positioning the sector for continued growth into 2024. The article highlights strong U.S. sales, hardware as a primary catalyst, and key factors like M&A and technological advancements shaping the industry's future trajectory.

title: KPMG in Canada and Microsoft Canada launch Operational Risk Skills Development Centre to support Quebec’s Innovation Economy – Microsoft News Center Canada
summary: KPMG in Canada and Microsoft Canada have launched the Operational Risk Skills Development Centre in Quebec, a $1.7M three-year initiative to provide free digital skills training and certifications. The center will focus on cybersecurity protection and the responsible implementation of generative AI for businesses and governments. The project aims to address the significant concerns Canadian CEOs have about cybersecurity breaches related to generative AI and the increasing demand for cybersecurity skills.

title: Diamond Sleet supply chain compromise distributes a modified CyberLink installer
summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence has uncovered a supply chain attack by the North Korea-based threat actor Diamond Sleet (formerly ZINC). This attack involves a modified CyberLink application installer that includes malicious code and has impacted over 100 devices globally. Microsoft has taken steps to protect customers, including notifying CyberLink and adding the malicious certificate to its disallowed list, while also providing detection and mitigation guidance.

title: OpenAI Says Sam Altman to Return as CEO in Dramatic Reversal
summary: Sam Altman is set to return as CEO of OpenAI after being ousted less than a week prior, following intense negotiations and pressure from investors like Microsoft and nearly all of the company's employees. The agreement includes a new initial board of directors led by Bret Taylor, with Larry Summers and Adam D’Angelo, and an internal investigation into the conduct that led to Altman's dismissal. This dramatic reversal comes after widespread calls for Altman's reinstatement and highlights the significant influence of investors and employees on the future of the AI startup.

title: Which Cloud Stocks are Benefitting from the AI Boom?
summary: While cloud computing is fundamental to AI, some cloud-focused funds have underperformed despite the AI rally, suggesting a potential future bounce. The global cloud infrastructure market is dominated by Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, who are heavily investing in capacity and partnerships for generative AI. These "Magnificent Seven" companies have shown significant gains driven by the AI boom, indicating that the benefits of the AI-cloud synergy are largely accruing to the major infrastructure providers.

title: Which Cloud Stocks are Benefitting from the AI Boom?
summary: Despite cloud computing being fundamental to AI programs, some cloud funds, like WisdomTree’s Cloud Computing Fund, have not fully capitalized on the AI rally. This article explores why emerging cloud stocks have lagged, highlighting that the primary beneficiaries are the major cloud infrastructure providers such as Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, which are part of the "Magnificent Seven" and have seen significant gains. It also discusses how these cloud leaders are preparing for future demands from generative AI and quantum computing through increased capital expenditure and strategic partnerships.

title: Chinese EV maker Nio sees AI, robots replacing 30% of workforce by 2027
summary: Chinese EV manufacturer Nio plans to replace 30% of its workforce with AI and robots by 2027 to boost efficiency and cut labor costs. The company, which recently laid off 10% of its staff, also aims to reduce managerial positions by 50% by 2025 through the increased use of AI in manufacturing decisions. This strategic shift underscores Nio's push towards greater automation and competitiveness in the electric vehicle market.

title: Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued After Earnings?
summary: Nvidia's recent earnings exceeded expectations, driven by strong demand for its GPUs in the AI sector, though the company anticipates a decline in China revenue due to U.S. export restrictions. Morningstar maintains a "fairly valued" assessment for Nvidia stock with a fair value estimate of $480 per share, acknowledging its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs. Despite a Very High Uncertainty Rating due to competition and potential reliance reduction by hyperscale vendors, Nvidia is expected to remain a market leader in AI semiconductors for years.

title: Market Leaders Collaborate with Synopsys to Realize Gains of Generative AI Across Synopsys.ai Full EDA Stack
summary: Synopsys announced the expansion of its Synopsys.ai EDA suite to integrate generative AI capabilities across its full stack, aiming to significantly enhance engineering productivity for chipmakers. This initiative, starting with Synopsys.ai Copilot, builds on its existing AI-driven optimization solutions. Leading companies like AMD, Intel, and Microsoft are collaborating with Synopsys to leverage these GenAI tools for various aspects of chip design, from RTL creation to formal verification.

title: Gartner Hype Cycle Shows AI Practices and Platform Engineering Will Reach Mainstream Adoption in Software Engineering in Two to Five Years
summary: Gartner's Hype Cycle for Software Engineering 2023 indicates that AI-augmented software engineering, AI coding assistants, and platform engineering will achieve mainstream adoption within two to five years. By 2027, 50% of enterprise software engineers are expected to utilize machine learning-powered coding tools, significantly boosting productivity and allowing focus on higher-value tasks. Platform engineering is also highlighted as crucial for managing technological complexity, with 80% of software engineering organizations projected to establish platform teams by 2026.

title: Forrester names Microsoft Intune a Leader in the 2023 Forrester Wave™ for Unified Endpoint Management
summary: Microsoft Intune has been recognized as a Leader in the 2023 Forrester Wave™ for Unified Endpoint Management, Q4 2023 report. The report highlights Intune's platform approach in simplifying management, reducing costs, and transforming experiences with AI and automation, enabling businesses to foster creativity and innovation while ensuring security. Intune's capabilities in exceptional digital experience, cost-efficient management, and foundational threat prevention are detailed, showcasing its strategic strength and the role it plays in aiding organizations to apply Zero Trust security principles.

title: Salesforce soars 8% as cost cutting shows its ‘brand new religion’ to increase profits
summary: Salesforce stock soared over 8% after announcing a profit forecast that exceeded analyst estimates, driven by its aggressive cost-cutting measures, including workforce reductions and a focus on increasing profit margins. CEO Marc Benioff's strategy, dubbed a "brand new religion," has pleased Wall Street despite some concerns about revenue growth, as the company integrates acquisitions and introduces AI features.

title: Tom Sosnoff's Portfolio Update and Two Trade Ideas
summary: This article details Tom Sosnoff's current investment portfolio, which includes both long and short positions in various stocks and futures, as well as pairs and delta-neutral trades. It also presents two specific trade ideas: a bullish ZEBRA trade on TLT and a bearish put diagonal spread on QQQ, providing details on their mechanics and rationale. The author, Michael Rechenthin, explains the thinking behind these investment choices.

title: Can digital watermarking protect us from generative AI?
summary: This article explores the use of digital watermarking and other authenticity technologies like Content Credentials to combat misinformation and copyright infringement from generative AI. It delves into the history of watermarking, current industry efforts, and alternative punitive tools like Nightshade being developed by researchers to protect content creators. The piece also discusses the challenges of regulation and widespread adoption of these protection measures.

title: Big tech is changing in China, and so are its hiring plans
summary: China's big tech companies are undergoing significant changes in their hiring strategies due to slowing growth, corporate restructuring, and intensified market competition. After periods of massive expansion and subsequent layoffs, firms like Alibaba and Tencent are now focusing on "efficiency enhancement" and higher-skilled positions. The new hiring directions primarily target overseas business expansion and artificial intelligence development, reflecting a strategic shift in the industry.

title: One ETF that focuses on employee happiness is outperforming the market this year
summary: An ETF focused on companies with high employee happiness, the Harbor Human Capital Factor U.S. Large Cap ETF (HAPI), has outperformed the S&P 500 this year, climbing nearly 25% compared to the benchmark's less than 20% gain. The fund's strategy is based on Irrational Capital's extensive employee surveys and publicly available data, creating a "human capital score" that is considered a novel and uncorrelated investment factor. While the large-cap HAPI fund includes major tech stocks contributing to its performance, a similar small-cap ETF (HAPS) is also available, offering potential for future growth.

title: This Growth-Focused ETF Makes for an Excellent Long-Term Investment
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) is highlighted as an excellent long-term investment, having seen a 46% gain this year due to its focus on growth stocks. QQQ tracks the NASDAQ-100 Index, which includes 100 of the largest non-financial companies on the NASDAQ, with a heavy concentration in technology giants like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The ETF offers investors diversified exposure within the tech sector and has historically shown strong performance, more than doubling in value over five years, making it suitable for maximizing returns.

title: Online Meeting App Market is Booming Worldwide | Adobe Systems, Microsoft, Cisco WebEx, Google, Logitech International
summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics predicts significant growth for the global Online Meeting App Market, driven by increasing R&D spending and the adoption of high-ability broadband services. The study, which includes major players like Google, Microsoft, and Zoom, highlights market opportunities such as strategic alliances and mobile video conferencing. Key trends identified are the emergence of cloud-based applications and the integration of advanced technologies.

title: 美股科技股“疯牛”行情未完待续? AI仍是引爆明年行情的关键
summary: Wedbush機構預計美股科技股在2024年將上漲至少20%，主要得益於人工智能（AI）和雲計算支出的大幅增長。分析師將AI比作互聯網的“1995時刻”，預計未來十年AI支出規模將達到1萬億美元，並點名英偉達和微軟將引領這一趨勢。儘管宏觀經濟不確定性存在，Wedbush仍堅定看好科技行業，並預計納斯達克100指數明年在高科技巨頭帶領下將大漲20%以上。

title: Generative AI: writing, editing and designing in 2023
summary: This article analyzes the growth of generative AI platforms in 2023, specifically focusing on OpenAI, Character.ai, and Wondershare. It highlights OpenAI's significant audience growth and the impact of its tools like DALL.E and ChatGPT. The piece also details the user demographics and market reach of Character.ai and Wondershare.com, noting a strong adoption among Gen Z users for these new AI technologies.

title: AMD's highly anticipated MI300X AI chips hit general availability along with new Ryzen 8040 CPUs
summary: AMD announced the general availability of its new Instinct MI300X series data center AI accelerators and the Ryzen 8040 Series chips for smaller devices, marking a significant push into the AI market to challenge Nvidia. The MI300X series focuses on generative AI workloads and HPC performance, supported by launch partners like Microsoft, Oracle, and Meta, while the Ryzen 8040 CPUs bring AI capabilities directly to personal computers. AMD also introduced the ROCm 6 open software stack and previewed next-generation "Strix Point" CPUs, emphasizing AI as a top priority.

title: AMD releases new chips to power faster AI training
summary: AMD announced the availability of its new MI300 chips, including the Instinct MI300X accelerator and Instinct M1300A APU, designed to power demanding AI training and large language models. The company also unveiled the Ryzen 8040 mobile processors, which will bring enhanced AI capabilities to laptops in 2024. These releases position AMD to compete more directly with Nvidia in the booming AI chip market.

title: Microsoft Annual Shareholders Meeting 2023
summary: The 2023 Microsoft Annual Shareholders Meeting featured discussions on record financial results for FY23, strategic investments in AI, and responsible technology development. Key executives, including Satya Nadella, Amy Hood, and Brad Smith, addressed topics ranging from AI integration across product lines and revenue generation plans to safeguards against AI misuse, partnerships, and commitments to privacy and social responsibility. The meeting also covered the election of directors and various shareholder proposals.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Unified Communications as a Service for the fifth year in a row
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS) for the fifth consecutive year. The company was particularly noted for its strong ability to execute and completeness of vision, driven by continuous investment in Microsoft Teams and the introduction of advanced AI capabilities like Microsoft Teams Premium. Teams Premium offers features such as intelligent recap, advanced security, and branded experiences, enhancing collaboration and productivity for over 10,000 customers.

title: Just 3 of the "Magnificent Seven" Have Outperformed the S&P 500 Since 2022. Here's the One I'd Buy for 2024 and 2025.
summary: This article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks against the S&P 500 since 2022. It identifies Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple as the only three that have outperformed the S&P 500. The author argues that Alphabet, despite underperforming recently, is the best buy for 2024 and 2025 due to its valuation, potential in digital advertising, and cloud segment growth.

title: AMD debuts new chip with eye to rival Nvidia product
summary: AMD has unveiled its new MI300 accelerator chips, aiming to compete with Nvidia in the burgeoning artificial intelligence (AI) chip market. CEO Lisa Su projected the AI chip industry could exceed $400 billion in the next four years, and mentioned Microsoft, Oracle, and Meta Platforms as customers. The company claims its new chip outperforms Nvidia's H100 in AI inference and matches it in training capabilities.

title: Shinichi Ata
summary: This article provides a leadership profile for Shinichi Ata, President & CEO of SB Technology Corp. It details his extensive career background, including key roles at Microsoft and SoftBank, and outlines his current goals for SB Technology, focusing on significant growth, technological advancement, and fostering employee engagement to contribute to the Information Revolution.

title: AMD launches MI300, claims AI performance lead over Nvidia ...
summary: AMD has launched its Instinct MI300 series data center AI accelerators and ROCm 6 software stack, claiming a significant performance advantage over Nvidia's H100. The MI300 series comes in two variants, MI300x and MI300A, offering substantial memory and processing power for AI and high-performance computing. AMD highlights strong demand and adoption by major tech companies, positioning the MI300 as a leading solution for generative AI.

title: Epic, Elevance Health CEOs make a pitch for harnessing data, AI to support providers
summary: Epic and Elevance Health are collaborating to leverage massive clinical data and AI to support healthcare providers, aiming to improve patient outcomes and reduce administrative burdens. Epic is developing a "Best Care Choices for My Patient" tool and integrating generative AI with Microsoft, while Elevance Health uses its Health OS and AI to close care gaps and enhance provider directory accuracy. Both companies emphasize data integration and AI's role in creating "hyperconnectivity" and a more personalized healthcare experience.

title: SLB Collaborates with Northern Lights JV and Microsoft to Digitalize Carbon Capture and Storage Value Chain
summary: SLB, Northern Lights JV, and Microsoft have signed an MoU to develop and optimize integrated cloud-based digital solutions for the carbon capture and storage (CCS) value chain. This collaboration aims to scale up CCS technologies by leveraging SLB's Delfi™ digital platform and Microsoft Azure to create cost-efficient digital workflows. The initiative supports the urgent need for CCS to meet global net-zero ambitions and positions Northern Lights as a crucial provider of CO2 transport and storage services.

title: Cooperation with ASML in Netherlands becomes important for AI chip war
summary: The article highlights the growing importance of the Netherlands, specifically ASML, in the global semiconductor supply chain, especially with the surge in demand for AI chips. South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol's visit, accompanied by top executives from Samsung and SK Group, underscores the critical need for cooperation with ASML, the sole manufacturer of essential photolithography systems. This alliance is seen as crucial for the success of the South Korea-U.S.-Japan semiconductor partnership as the high-performing chip market expands rapidly due to AI.

title: DGRW Has Hallmarks of a Great Dividend ETF
summary: The WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) is highlighted as a strong dividend ETF due to its focus on companies' return on assets and return on equity, rather than just yield. This strategy has allowed DGRW to outperform the S&P 500 and other dividend indexes, especially in high-interest-rate environments. The ETF's approach aims for reduced volatility, potential capital appreciation, and diversification, including a significant allocation to tech stocks.

title: Financial Outlook 2024: Navigating Choppy Waters
summary: Andy Watts, EVP & CFO at Brown & Brown Insurance, outlines five key themes for businesses to consider in 2024, given the volatile economic landscape. These themes include adapting to high interest rates, addressing labor availability challenges, leveraging technology, investing in workforce upskilling, and navigating the ongoing impacts of a post-COVID economy. The article emphasizes the importance of agility and proactive planning to succeed in an unpredictable market.

title: Molecular Modeling Market Size, Growth, Trends & Demand Report by 2033
summary: The global molecular modeling market is projected to grow from USD 1.45 billion in 2025 to USD 5.11 billion in 2033, at a CAGR of 17.1%. This growth is driven by the increasing frequency of chronic diseases, leading to medication development, and significant R&D investments by pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies. Advancements in computational technologies, such as high-performance and quantum computing, are creating new market opportunities, while a shortage of skilled experts remains a key restraining factor.

title: IIT-BBS placement drive sees 200 offers in 10 days
summary: IIT-Bhubaneswar has received close to 200 job offers within the first 10 days of its ongoing placement drive, which began on December 1. This includes four international offers and a highest domestic package of Rs 64 lakh per annum. Despite a challenging global economic climate, the institution's placements are showing positive momentum, with computer science students receiving the most offers.

title: Google, AES, others to create marketplace for 24/7 hourly renewable energy certificates
summary: Google, AES, Microsoft, and Constellation have partnered with LevelTen Energy to create a marketplace for "granular certificates," a new type of energy attribute certificate that tracks renewable energy generation on an hourly, location-specific basis. This initiative aims to provide more accurate signals for renewable energy development and help corporate and utility buyers meet 24/7 clean energy goals. The platform is expected to launch next year, offering both forward and spot markets for these certificates.

title: New KV-Botnet Targeting Cisco, DrayTek, and Fortinet Devices for Stealthy Attacks
summary: A new botnet, dubbed KV-botnet by Lumen Technologies' Black Lotus Labs, is leveraging compromised firewalls and routers from Cisco, DrayTek, Fortinet, and NETGEAR to facilitate covert data transfer for advanced persistent threat actors, including China-linked Volt Typhoon. The botnet consists of two complementary clusters (KV and JDY) and has been active since at least February 2022, primarily targeting edge devices in enterprises. The malware operates entirely in-memory, making detection difficult but vulnerable to power cycling, though re-infection remains a risk.

title: Arm boss's chilling warning humans WILL lose control of AI as he demands 'override'
summary: Rene Haas, CEO of Arm Holdings, has issued a stark warning that humans risk losing control over AI and calls for an "override" or "backdoor" to shut down advanced AI systems. He believes AI will profoundly change every aspect of life within the next five to ten years, echoing the company's past role in the smartphone revolution. This comes as Microsoft invests significantly in UK data centers for AI development, with a focus on safety alongside economic benefits.

title: Call translation services new battleground in AI race
summary: The coming year will see a surge in real-time phone call translation services driven by AI, with Korean tech firms like SK Telecom and Samsung Electronics entering the fray. SK Telecom is launching its "A. call translator" service within its existing AI app, supporting four languages initially and expanding to 11. Samsung, conversely, plans to integrate its generative AI model "Samsung Gauss" directly into its new Galaxy S24 phones, offering on-device translation without external internet access, emphasizing privacy and reduced latency.

title: The Best Artificial Intelligence ETFs of 2023
summary: The article highlights the top-performing Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETFs of 2023, with returns as high as 71%. It details five specific AI ETFs, including thematic funds that invest in companies benefiting from AI growth and AI-powered funds that use AI to select stocks, identifying key holdings and year-to-date performance for each.

title: 5 Reasons to Avoid Microsoft (MSFT) Right Now
summary: This article outlines five reasons why investors should consider avoiding Microsoft (MSFT) stock at the moment, despite its historical success. The reasons include the stock being currently expensive compared to its historical price-to-earnings ratio, past unaddressed ethics issues, strong competition in its Azure cloud computing segment, potential for antitrust challenges, and the highly competitive nature of the generative AI market following its ChatGPT investment. However, the article also acknowledges Microsoft's long-term strength and suggests waiting for a stock pullback to a more favorable price-to-earnings ratio.

title: Midmark improves performance by up to 75 percent by migrating and running Oracle on Azure
summary: Midmark Corporation, a healthcare solutions provider, significantly improved its system performance by up to 75 percent through the migration of its business-critical Oracle environments to Microsoft Azure. Facing an explosion of on-premises data and aging infrastructure, Midmark utilized Azure DevOps pipelines and Terraform code to achieve greater flexibility, scalability, and better hardware. This successful transition has prompted Midmark to explore further Azure capabilities and adopt a cloud-first roadmap, benefiting employee perception and overall operational efficiency.

title: Ally Financial empowers customer service associates to focus on human engagement by using Azure OpenAI Service
summary: Ally Financial is leveraging Microsoft Azure and Azure OpenAI Service to enhance its customer service operations. By automating manual tasks like note-taking and call summarization with generative AI, Ally empowers its customer service associates to dedicate more time to direct human engagement with customers. This initiative has already reduced post-call effort by 30 percent and aims for a 50 percent reduction, leading to improved customer experience and associate satisfaction.

title: Banco de Chile y DDB: “Fuimos el primer banco en Fortnite”
summary: Banco de Chile and DDB won two awards, a gold and a bronze, at the Effie Awards Latin America for their "Teletón & Fortnite" campaign. The initiative involved creating a Banco de Chile branch within the Fortnite game to encourage younger audiences to donate to the Teletón charity event. This innovative approach resulted in significant engagement, with users spending over 43 minutes per person interacting with the brand experience.

title: Vice president of Advanced Micro Devices Jason Banta: ‘Computers with artificial intelligence will be able to anticipate our needs’
summary: Jason Banta, AMD's Vice President and General Manager, discusses the future of AI in computing, emphasizing its transformative potential to make computers more personal and anticipatory of user needs. AMD recently launched its MI300 accelerator, aiming to popularize smart laptops from 2024, with a significant inflection point expected in 2025. Banta also addresses the challenges of integrating AI into mobile devices, the importance of a diverse semiconductor supply chain, and the environmental considerations in AI development.

title: Microsoft closes on 1,030 acres of land in Mount Pleasant; company is now largest land owner in TID 5
summary: Microsoft has significantly expanded its landholdings in Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin, with the purchase of an additional 1,030 acres, bringing its total to 1,345 acres. This acquisition makes Microsoft the largest landowner in Tax Incremental District No. 5. The company plans to invest billions more into constructing new data centers, reinforcing its commitment to developing a major technology hub in the region.

title: Use XMHQ, XSHQ to Fill Gaps in Quality SMID-Cap Exposure
summary: The article discusses how investors can use Invesco's S&P MidCap Quality ETF (XMHQ) and S&P SmallCap Quality ETF (XSHQ) to gain exposure to quality mid- and small-cap companies, complementing the Invesco S&P 500 Quality ETF (SPHQ). Both XMHQ and XSHQ have seen significant inflows due to market uncertainty, offering access to high-quality firms based on return on equity, accruals ratio, and financial leverage ratio. These funds introduce sector tilts, with SPHQ, for instance, overweighting IT, energy, and consumer staples.

title: Top 6 Portfolio Moves for 2024
summary: This article outlines six key portfolio adjustments investors should consider for 2024, focusing on balancing risk and maximizing returns. Recommendations include rebalancing portfolios, increasing bond allocations due to improved yields, optimizing cash holdings for competitive yields without over-allocating, maintaining inflation protection, and considering exposure to small-cap stocks. These moves aim to address market shifts, inflation concerns, and current valuation disparities.

title: T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF): A Promising Active Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF) has shown significant growth since its launch in June 2023, accumulating over $600 million in assets. The fund focuses on high-quality U.S. large-cap companies, employing a bottom-up stock selection approach with top holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. TCAF's investment philosophy emphasizes strong performance in areas like technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary.

title: Palo Alto Networks Completes Acquisition of Talon
summary: Palo Alto Networks has finalized its acquisition of Talon Cyber Security, an Israeli startup specializing in secure browser technology for enterprises. The deal, valued at $625 million, aims to integrate Talon's enterprise browser into Palo Alto's Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) suite to enhance security for both managed and unmanaged devices. This strategic move is intended to provide robust protection against malware, phishing, and keylogger attacks, while also offering data security controls within the browser and streamlined IT administration.

title: Erich Andersen
summary: Erich Andersen is the Vice President and Deputy General Counsel for Microsoft Corporation, leading the company's Intellectual Property Group. He is responsible for managing Microsoft's patent, trademark, and copyright portfolios, securing IP protections for its substantial R&D investments, and overseeing the company's patent licensing program. Andersen has been with Microsoft since 1995, contributing significantly to strategic relations and previously leading legal and government affairs in EMEA.

title: Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:ZZZ)
summary: The Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF (ZZZ) is an exchange-traded fund launched on December 28, 2023, by Onefund, which invests in a 75/25 blend of US large-cap stocks and front-month CME Bitcoin futures. It does not directly invest in Bitcoin's spot price. The ETF has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy," assets under management of $6.02 million, and a net expense ratio of 1.01%.

title: The Apple Effect: Magic Leap Founder’s Previous Company Launches App on Vision Pro
summary: Stryker's Mako, a robotic surgery group initially founded by Magic Leap's founder Rony Abovitz, has launched its myMako app on Apple Vision Pro. This move highlights "The Apple Effect" where Apple's market influence draws companies into specific product categories. The article compares Vision Pro's appeal to Magic Leap's earlier struggles and notes how Apple's stability and ecosystem are encouraging XR-adjacent companies to adopt its platform.

title: IBM Stock Gains After Cuts to Marketing and Communications Staff—Key Level To Watch
summary: IBM shares saw gains after the company announced job cuts in its marketing and communications division, part of a "workforce rebalancing" to prioritize AI and hybrid cloud skills. This move aligns with other major tech companies reducing staff amid macroeconomic uncertainties. Investors are closely watching the $206.22 level, which represents IBM's all-time high, as a potential resistance point for the stock.

title: Microsoft, P&G, Unilever and Walmart among 239 companies to miss net-zero deadline
summary: The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) has reclassified 239 companies, including Microsoft, P&G, Unilever, and Walmart, as "commitment removed" for net-zero goals because they failed to submit science-based targets for validation within the 24-month deadline. A significant obstacle for these companies is the difficulty in reducing Scope 3 emissions, which relate to indirect emissions from customers and supply chains. Despite the reclassification, these major corporations maintain that they are continuing to pursue aggressive emissions reduction targets and are in various stages of reviewing or updating their strategies to align with SBTi standards, with many still holding near-term validated targets.

title: Markets Showing Signs of Bubble on Big Tech, Crypto Surge, BofA’s Hartnett Says
summary: Bank of America’s Chief Investment Strategist Michael Hartnett believes that the current market surge in "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks and cryptocurrencies exhibits characteristics of a bubble. He points to re-accelerating inflation, soft growth, and the narrowness of gaining assets as signs of a "bubble mentality."

title: Oracle Adds New GenAI Features to Cloud Applications and Expands Microsoft Partnership
summary: Oracle has announced new generative AI features for its Fusion Cloud Applications Suite, designed to enhance employee and customer experiences by leveraging AI within its software-as-a-service applications. Additionally, Oracle is expanding its partnership with Microsoft by making Oracle Database@Azure available in more regions, including its debut in Europe, to meet increasing demand for its cloud products and services, which constitute a significant portion of its revenue.

title: Apple Highlights Stryker, an NJIT Alum's Medical Tech Group, for VisionPro App
summary: Apple has recognized Stryker Corp., a medical technology company led by NJIT alumnus Robert Cohen, for its innovative MyMako app utilizing the new VisionPro mixed-reality headset. This app allows surgeons to plan and simulate robot-assisted knee replacement operations in 3D, enhancing surgical planning, in-operation information access, and resident training. Cohen envisions future integration of AI to further optimize implant selection and placement based on patient data within this mixed-reality framework.

title: Nasdaq listing is a strategic move aimed at bringing value to Noventiq: CEO Hervé Tessler
summary: Noventiq CEO Hervé Tessler discusses the company's strategic move to list on Nasdaq from the London Stock Exchange, highlighting the benefits of increased access to capital and enhanced brand prestige. He emphasizes Noventiq's strong presence in emerging markets, its partnerships with tech giants like Microsoft and AWS, and its focus on digital transformation, cybersecurity, and AI solutions. Tessler also addresses challenges like navigating diverse global regulations and the imperative for continuous innovation in the rapidly evolving technology sector.

title: Who is Jeff McMillan? Morgan Stanley’s First Head of AI
summary: Morgan Stanley has appointed Jeff McMillan as its first Head of AI, a strategic move to coordinate the firm's AI strategy and capitalize on AI opportunities in digital finance. McMillan, previously Chief Analytics and Data Officer, will work with business units to integrate AI, overseeing projects like the GPT-4 chatbot rollout for bankers. This appointment underscores Morgan Stanley's commitment to AI, projecting significant growth in AI power demand and exploring its positive impacts, including sustainability.

title: 2026 Global Market Outlook: Minds, machines, and market shifts
summary: T. Rowe Price's 2026 Global Market Outlook emphasizes the transformative power of AI, while also highlighting the persistent challenges of valuation, inflation, and geopolitical uncertainty. The outlook suggests investors balance exposure to AI leaders with opportunities in cyclical and international markets, requiring agility and a global perspective. It details prospects across economic, equity, fixed income, private, and Chinese markets, along with asset allocation strategies.

title: NVIDIA Blackwell Platform Arrives to Power a New Era of Computing
summary: NVIDIA has announced the Blackwell platform, a new GPU architecture designed to power the next generation of AI and accelerated computing. The Blackwell platform promises to enable real-time generative AI on trillion-parameter models with significantly reduced cost and energy consumption compared to its predecessor. Major cloud providers and AI companies, including Amazon Web Services, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, Tesla, and xAI, are expected to adopt Blackwell.

title: Top 10 Enterprise Data Management Companies | Best Accuracy and Security
summary: This article identifies and profiles the top 10 Enterprise Data Management (EDM) companies, highlighting their solutions, market presence, and recent developments. It also provides an overview of the EDM market, including its drivers and projected growth from USD 97.5 billion in 2023 to USD 281.9 billion by 2033. The featured companies include industry giants like IBM, SAP, Oracle, SAS, Informatica, Microsoft, Intel, Toshiba, Teradata, and AWS, each playing a significant role in helping organizations manage and leverage their data effectively.

title: The Next Big Theme: March 2024
summary: This article highlights key trends and developments across several transformative themes in March 2024, including advancements in humanoid robotics, continued growth in electric vehicle sales despite headwinds, breakthroughs in generative AI for audio and video, the scaling up of the global hydrogen industry, and AI-driven innovation in telemedicine and digital health. It also addresses the intensification of ransomware attacks and fortifying cybersecurity defenses. The article concludes by presenting performance and sales growth expectations for these themes, referencing corresponding ETFs.

title: Mind Meld: Brain-Computer Interfaces Unlock the Future (2024 and Beyond!)
summary: This article explores the rapidly advancing field of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs), detailing their evolution from early EEG-based systems to modern, embedded devices like those being developed by Neuralink. It highlights current applications in healthcare for restoring motor function and communication, as well as in gaming and cognitive enhancement. The author also addresses significant challenges including technical limitations, ethical concerns around privacy and consent, and issues of accessibility and affordability, while predicting a future where BCIs are smaller, more integrated, and significantly amplify human capabilities.

title: Google, Microsoft, and Nucor partner for new energy tech PPAs
summary: Google, Microsoft, and Nucor Corporation are collaborating to develop and fund early-stage clean energy projects, including advanced nuclear, geothermal, and long-duration energy storage. They aim to reduce risk for developers by aggregating demand and designing new commercial models to bring these technologies online by the early 2030s. The companies have issued an RFI for potential projects in several US regions, with an initial focus on the US to prove the demand aggregation and procurement model for these advanced technologies.

title: Meta, Microsoft, X and Match join Epic Games' battle against Apple
summary: Meta Platforms, Microsoft, X, and Match Group have joined Epic Games in accusing Apple of violating a court-ordered injunction regarding payments in its App Store. The companies claim Apple is in "clear violation" by making it difficult for developers to direct consumers to alternative, cheaper payment methods for digital content. Apple, however, maintains it has fully complied with the injunction, arguing it protects consumers and its ecosystem.

title: Microsoft, Google, Nucor partner on initiative to spur emerging 24/7 clean power technologies
summary: Microsoft, Google, and Nucor have launched the Advanced Clean Electricity initiative to accelerate the development and deployment of reliable, low- or zero-carbon electricity generation technologies like advanced nuclear, next-generation geothermal, and long-duration energy storage. The initiative aims for widespread commercial deployment by the early 2030s and aggregates the three companies' demand for clean electricity to spur first-of-a-kind and early commercial projects. Projects must be located in the U.S., with a preference for the PJM region, and meet specific evaluation criteria, including a potential capacity factor above 50% and project capacity above 50 MW.

title: Clean Electricity: Microsoft, Google and Nucor Join Forces
summary: Microsoft, Google, and Nucor are collaborating to overcome barriers in developing clean electricity technologies. They plan to establish new business models and aggregate their demand to accelerate first-of-a-kind projects such as advanced nuclear, next-generation geothermal, clean hydrogen, and long-duration energy storage. The companies are issuing an RFI for potential projects in the US, aiming to bring clear customer voice to policymakers and develop new tariff structures.

title: DOJ faces tough road in Apple antitrust lawsuit
summary: The Department of Justice's antitrust lawsuit against Apple faces significant challenges, despite drawing parallels to the successful Microsoft case two decades ago. Antitrust specialists highlight key differences and legal precedents that could make it difficult for the DOJ to prove Apple's monopolistic conduct and force product redesigns. Apple is expected to argue that its "walled-garden" approach benefits consumers and that the government's market definition is flawed.

title: Get to know QQQ: Charting 25 years of performance
summary: The article celebrates 25 years of the Invesco QQQ ETF, tracing its evolution from its launch in 1999 to becoming one of the largest ETFs today. It highlights key milestones such as reaching significant assets under management (AUM), changes in sponsorship and naming, and its consistent outperformance compared to the S&P 500. QQQ is presented as a proven way for investors to gain exposure to highly innovative, non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq-100 Index.

title: Wait, Did Europe Really Outperform the U.S. over the Last Three Years?
summary: This article challenges the common perception that European equities perpetually lag behind their U.S. counterparts. It highlights how a selection of 11 European "GRANOLAS" companies have delivered returns surprisingly similar to the U.S. Magnificent 7 over the past three years. Additionally, the WisdomTree Europe Hedged Equity Fund (HEDJ) has demonstrated significant outperformance against the S&P 500 and comparable European ETFs, attributed to its stock selection process, dividend yield weighting, and exporter tilt, all while trading at a valuation discount.

title: How to invest in ChatGPT stock?
summary: This article explains that direct investment in ChatGPT stock is not possible as its developer, OpenAI, is not publicly traded. Instead, it suggests investing in publicly traded companies heavily involved in AI technology, such as Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), Perion Network (PERI), and C3.ai (AI). The article details each company's relevance to AI and provides an investment strategy for AI stocks, emphasizing diversification and monitoring financial health.

title: FTEC: Concentration Increases Volatility (NYSEARCA:FTEC)
summary: The Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC) offers strong tech exposure, performing similarly to VGT. However, 45% of its assets are concentrated in Microsoft, Apple, and NVIDIA, which could lead to increased volatility despite a projected 7% upside by year-end 2024. The author, Ricardo Fernandez, highlights the importance of an investor's goals and definition of "tech" when choosing an ETF.

title: The World’s Craziest Data Centres
summary: This article explores some of the most unusual and innovative locations for data centres around the world, highlighting how they adapt to extreme environments and leverage natural resources for cooling and sustainability. Examples include data centres built in disused paper mills, floating on barges, submerged underwater, nestled in Arctic cold, within former military bunkers, and even beneath churches. These unconventional approaches demonstrate creative solutions to the ever-increasing demand for data storage and processing.

title: Zoom spreads AI across collaboration services
summary: Zoom has launched Workplace, an AI-powered collaboration hub that integrates chat, phone, video, and email services, including its AI Companion at no extra cost. This move positions Zoom to compete with rivals like Microsoft and Google, which charge for similar AI services, and addresses enterprise concerns about data control and privacy in AI applications. The company also announced improvements to its video conferencing platform and a partnership with Avaya to expand its reach.

title: A new product allows 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven. Traders should be wary.
summary: A new exchange-traded product allows investors to make 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven group of stocks, raising concerns among analysts. While levered exchange-traded products are gaining popularity, traders are warned about the significant risks associated with such highly leveraged instruments. ETF analyst Todd Sohn from Strategas Securities notes that this product appears to be the most heavily levered available in any developed market.

title: Take-Two Buys Gearbox, Company Behind The Borderlands Franchise, From Embracer For $460 Million
summary: Take-Two Interactive Software has acquired Gearbox Entertainment, known for the Borderlands franchise, from Embracer in an all-stock deal valued at $460 million. This acquisition deepens Take-Two's relationship with Gearbox, giving it full ownership of popular IPs like Borderlands and Tiny Tina's Wonderlands. Randy Pitchford will continue to lead Gearbox as a studio within Take-Two, with several new projects and sequels already in development.

title: These Growth Funds Continued to Shine in Q1 2024
summary: Growth equity funds, especially those with exposure to AI-related stocks, continued their strong performance in Q1 2024, significantly outperforming value funds. While large-cap growth led the way, small- and mid-cap growth funds also fared well. International growth funds also saw gains, although they lagged behind their US counterparts.

title: ED 24-02: Mitigating the Significant Risk from Nation-State Compromise of Microsoft Corporate Email System
summary: CISA Emergency Directive ED 24-02 addresses the significant risk posed by the Midnight Blizzard nation-state actor's compromise of Microsoft corporate email accounts, which exfiltrated correspondence from Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies. The directive mandates agencies to analyze compromised email content, reset credentials, and secure Microsoft Azure accounts by specific deadlines. CISA will provide support and monitor compliance to mitigate the threat.

title: Cybersecurity Faces Transformation from Generative AI
summary: Generative AI presents a double-edged sword for cybersecurity, simultaneously enabling more sophisticated cyberattacks and providing powerful tools for defense. Despite economic headwinds, global cybersecurity spending is projected to grow significantly, driven by the escalating threat landscape and the need to integrate AI-powered defenses. This transformation is expected to create opportunities for market consolidation and continued growth in the cybersecurity sector, appealing to investors looking beyond obvious AI beneficiaries.

title: Today’s top business headlines: GE's breakup, Microsoft Teams cut from Office, recalled car fire risks
summary: This article covers major business news including General Electric's breakup into three public companies, Microsoft's decision to unbundle Teams from Office due to regulatory pressure, ongoing fire risks with recalled Hyundai and Kia vehicles, NASA's citizen science projects for the upcoming solar eclipse, and SLB's recent $8 billion acquisition of ChampionX. These headlines highlight significant shifts in corporate structures, regulatory impacts on tech giants, public safety concerns in the auto industry, and consolidation trends in the energy sector.

title: The Rise of FinOps and GreenOps
summary: The article introduces FinOps and GreenOps as crucial methodologies for optimizing cloud usage in terms of both cost and environmental sustainability. It explains how FinOps acts as a financial advisor for cloud spending, ensuring cost transparency, right-sizing resources, and governance, while GreenOps focuses on reducing the environmental impact by optimizing resource utilization, selecting eco-friendly resources, and leveraging renewable energy. The piece emphasizes that these two approaches, working together, are essential for responsible cloud adoption and creating a brighter, greener future.

title: Bank of Montreal runs risk calculations twice as fast and at 30% less cost with Azure
summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) successfully migrated its market risk management platform to Microsoft Azure, achieving significant performance improvements and cost reductions. The bank now runs risk calculations twice as fast and at 30% less cost compared to its previous setup, enabling enhanced risk management capabilities. BMO also plans to scale its core processing capacity significantly and explore further use of AI and machine learning with Azure.

title: Winners of the 2024 IoT Evolution Industrial IoT Product of the Year Awards Announced
summary: TMC and Crossfire Media announced the winners of the 2024 IoT Evolution Industrial IoT Product of the Year Award, recognizing the most innovative products and solutions powering the Industrial Internet of Things. The award highlights products influencing efficiency and productivity gains for customers, with nominations based on availability for deployment within the past twelve months. Winning this award provides significant recognition, increased visibility, and marketing opportunities for the recipient companies.

title: Alger Launches 2 Transparent Actively Managed ETFs
summary: Fred Alger Management has launched two new transparent actively managed ETFs: the Alger AI Enablers & Adopters ETF (ALAI) and the Alger Concentrated Equity ETF (CNEQ). ALAI focuses on companies involved with artificial intelligence, while CNEQ invests in large-cap growth companies, with a significant allocation to technology. These additions bring Alger's ETF family to five funds, building on their earlier entry into the ETF market in 2021.

title: Alger Concentrated Equity ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Alger Concentrated Equity ETF (CNEQ), which was created on April 5, 2024. The ETF focuses on large-cap companies with high growth potential, maintaining a concentrated portfolio of no more than 30 stocks. The reported holdings include major technology companies like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Amazon, alongside others across various sectors.

title: If I Could Only Buy and Hold 1 Vanguard ETF For the Next 5 Years, This Would Be My Top Pick
summary: The article recommends the Vanguard Large-Cap ETF (VV) as a top choice for a buy-and-hold strategy for the next five years due to its low expense ratio, focus on U.S. large-cap stocks like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, and its ability to provide broad diversification. It highlights the ETF's simplicity and stability, making it easier for investors to hold through market volatility compared to individual stocks or more specialized thematic ETFs. The author also notes the subtle differences and near-identical returns between VV and the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, with a slight preference for VV.

title: Westcon-Comstor Brings AudioCodes Live for Microsoft Teams to EMEA
summary: Westcon-Comstor will distribute AudioCodes' Live Platform for Microsoft Teams to partners across EMEA, enhancing UCaaS and CCaaS offerings. This white-labelled platform, hosted by AudioCodes and managed by Westcon in Microsoft Azure, provides comprehensive calling, contact center, and recording capabilities for Microsoft Teams. The collaboration aims to empower channel partners to boost revenues by offering a hosted voice solution and expanding the reach of AudioCodes' Microsoft Teams experience.

title: Google introduces new Arm-based AI chip for data centers
summary: Google has announced its new custom-designed Arm-based AI chips, called Axion Processors, for data centers. These processors are designed to offer better performance and energy efficiency compared to x86 chips and general-purpose Arm chips. The Axion Processors will be available to Google Cloud customers later this year and are already in use in some of Google's own services.

title: Adobe leverages Microsoft Azure to streamline the customer experience, harnessing the power of connected cloud services.
summary: The partnership between Adobe and Microsoft integrates Adobe’s enterprise solutions with Microsoft’s cloud services, aiming to improve customer experiences and drive AI transformation. This collaboration focuses on streamlining cloud infrastructure, enhancing data management, and accelerating business outcomes through seamless integrations and innovative AI capabilities. The alliance has shown significant economic impact, including a 251% ROI over three years and improved productivity for IT, security, and marketing teams.

title: Suzuki embraces Azure OpenAI Service, supercharges AI use with five apps, and sparks ideas for AI in business
summary: Suzuki Motor Corporation is rapidly adopting generative AI through the Azure OpenAI Service to drive new initiatives and improve efficiency. The company has developed five unique multilingual web applications, including NAGI and NAMI, to expand AI usage across various departments. Suzuki aims to streamline operations, reduce manual tasks by 80%, and leverage AI to overcome challenges in a rapidly changing automotive industry.

title: Implications of AI for cloud computing companies
summary: The article explores the significant impact of AI on cloud computing companies, noting that while AI has driven much of the equity market's recent gains, particularly for the "magnificent seven" tech companies, the broader economic benefits may take time to materialize. It highlights cloud computing as a key beneficiary, where AI integration can enhance operational efficiency, decision-making, cybersecurity, and personalized services. The piece also introduces the Fidelity Cloud Computing UCITS ETF as a means for investors to gain exposure to this theme.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE), which was established on April 11, 2024. The fund actively manages a portfolio primarily focused on large-cap equities, similar to the S&P 500 and Russell 1000, and sells call options to generate income, aiming for current income and potential capital growth. The list provided shows its top holdings include major technology and financial companies such as NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.

title: Microsoft says Georgia may be overestimating data center load growth
summary: Microsoft has filed comments with the Georgia Public Service Commission, challenging Georgia Power Company's modeling methods for load growth, arguing they undervalue renewable energy and overestimate data center demand through 2030. The tech giant, which has significant data center operations in Georgia, is concerned that the utility's approach includes speculative projects and relies on a high-end load forecast, potentially leading to excessive and carbon-intensive generation procurement. Microsoft is advocating for greater transparency, a focus on projects with firm commitments, and better integration of renewable energy sources and storage solutions in Georgia Power's resource planning.

title: Terry Smith – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article analyzes Terry Smith's top 10 holdings based on his latest 13F-HR filing as of December 31, 2023. His portfolio has a market value of over $23.8 billion, with 65.29% concentrated in his top ten positions. Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Stryker Corp are among his largest holdings.

title: Arrow Debuts Copilot for Microsoft 365 Services for Partners
summary: Arrow Electronics has made Copilot for Microsoft 365 available to its channel partners in EMEA through its ArrowSphere Cloud platform and is providing a dedicated enablement program to support them. Since its launch, Arrow has implemented over 9,000 licenses of Copilot for Microsoft 365, demonstrating the tool's potential for boosting productivity and creativity using generative AI and natural language processing. The company, which distributes Microsoft in over 20 European countries confirms its commitment to helping resellers master this technology with over 300 certified specialists.

title: Adobe Explores OpenAI Partnership as It Adds AI Video Tools
summary: Adobe is exploring partnerships with third-party generative AI tools like OpenAI's Sora for its Premiere Pro video editing software, while also integrating its own Firefly AI model. This move aims to address concerns about AI's impact on its core business and offers users more options for AI-generated video content. Adobe also emphasized its commitment to AI ethics and transparency regarding content created with third-party models.

title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

title: 1 Stock-Split ETF That Could Turn $500 Per Month Into $1 Million, With Help From Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) recently undertook a 6-for-1 stock split, making its shares more accessible to a wider range of investors. This ETF, which has outperformed both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100, holds top tech stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Alphabet, benefiting significantly from the burgeoning AI industry. The article suggests that a consistent monthly investment of $500 into IGM has the potential to grow substantially, potentially reaching $1 million or more over the long term, especially if the current high growth rates driven by AI continue.

title: JEPI: Do Not Make The Same Mistake Two Years In A Row, Buy This Covered Call ETF Instead
summary: The article compares the 2023 and Q1 2024 performance of three S&P 500 covered call ETFs: JEPI, XYLD, and SPYI, highlighting their total returns and tax efficiency. It argues that SPYI consistently outperformed its peers due to its strategy of writing out-of-the-money covered calls and favorable Section 1256 tax treatment. The author advises investors to choose SPYI for better price appreciation, income, and tax efficiency, particularly given the S&P 500's continued rally.

title: Why Microsoft extended Windows 10 support for schools for $1
summary: Microsoft announced that schools can extend Windows 10 security updates for as little as $1 per computer, a decision influenced by advocacy from PIRG and Green Century Capital Management. This move is praised for its environmental benefits by extending the life of computers and reducing e-waste, though advocates suggest the affordable extension should be rolled out to all users, not just schools. The concerns stem from the impending end-of-life for Windows 10, which could render up to 400 million computers obsolete and significantly increase electronic waste.

title: Rockwell Automation and its PartnerNetwork™ Ecosystem Present Artificial Intelligence, Autonomous Operations, 5G and Cybersecurity in Driving Digital Transformation at Hannover Messe 2024
summary: Rockwell Automation, in collaboration with its PartnerNetwork™ ecosystem, will showcase advanced technologies like AI, autonomous operations, 5G, and cybersecurity at Hannover Messe 2024. The presentation will focus on driving digital transformation, modernizing operations, and achieving sustainability goals for industrial companies. Key highlights include AI integrations with Microsoft, NVIDIA Omniverse Cloud APIs for digital twins, secure industrial environments with Cisco, and private 5G connectivity with Ericsson and ODVA.

title: Controllers hunt for workarounds to ‘continuous’ reporting, accountant shortage
summary: Controllers are facing increased demands for more frequent reporting and data, exacerbated by an ongoing shortage of skilled accounting professionals. To address these challenges, many firms are outsourcing work to accountants in other countries like India and leveraging technology, including traditional AI, to automate routine tasks such as accounts payable and receivable. While generative AI is being used for basic tasks like drafting memos, controllers emphasize the need for accuracy, which current generative AI struggles with, but acknowledge AI skills will be crucial for future professionals.

title: Here's What's Happening in Markets: April 19
summary: Markets are showing uncertainty as investors react to a weaker-than-expected Q2 forecast from Netflix, causing tech stocks to dip. Meanwhile, cryptocurrency ETFs are gaining momentum as the highly anticipated Bitcoin halving approaches, with many watching to see if this event will trigger a familiar rally in Bitcoin prices given the current market dynamics.

title: D-Wave Quantum Computer: Commercial QC Pioneer [2024]
summary: This article chronicles the history and technological advancements of D-Wave Systems, recognized as the world’s first commercial quantum computer startup. It details D-Wave's quantum annealing approach, the evolution of its quantum machines from D-Wave One to Advantage2, and its cloud services. The article also addresses the ongoing debate regarding whether D-Wave's annealing machines are truly quantum computers.

title: Microsoft CEO to meet with Samsung, SK Hynix, LG, SK Telecom chiefs
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and founder Bill Gates will meet with leaders of Samsung, SK Hynix, LG, and SK Telecom at the MS CEO Summit 2024 in May to discuss strengthening AI partnerships. The meetings aim to explore joint development of AI chips, cooperation in AI technology for consumer electronics, and business tie-ups in 5G telecommunications and cloud sectors. This gathering signifies an increasing trend of Big Tech firms seeking AI business collaborations to gain a competitive edge.

title: Microsoft Partners with Korea’s ‘Tech Big 4’ for AI Collaboration
summary: Microsoft is collaborating with Korea's "Tech Big 4" – Samsung Electronics, SK hynix, LG Electronics, and SK telecom – to enhance AI service competitiveness. This partnership aims to leverage Korea's expertise in semiconductors and communication technologies, with discussions ranging from AI semiconductor development to integrating Microsoft's AI services into consumer devices and strengthening telecommunications cloud services. The collaboration is expected to be mutually beneficial, expanding Microsoft's market influence and Korean companies' AI capabilities.

title: The Coca-Cola Company and Microsoft announce five-year strategic partnership to accelerate cloud and generative AI initiatives
summary: The Coca-Cola Company and Microsoft have announced a five-year strategic partnership, with Coca-Cola committing $1.1 billion to the Microsoft Cloud and its generative AI capabilities. This collaboration aims to accelerate cloud adoption, foster innovation, and improve productivity across Coca-Cola's global operations. The company has already migrated all applications to Microsoft Azure and is exploring generative AI-powered digital assistants to enhance customer experiences and streamline operations.

title: Coca-Cola pours $1.1B into Microsoft cloud, generative AI partnership
summary: The Coca-Cola Company is expanding its partnership with Microsoft, investing $1.1 billion into the tech giant's cloud and generative AI services. This move will involve testing Azure OpenAI Service and Copilot for Microsoft 365 across various business functions to enhance customer experiences, streamline operations, and identify growth opportunities. Coca-Cola was reportedly an early adopter of generative AI, having already gained nearly a year of experience with ChatGPT Enterprise in its workflows.

title: Streamline controls with Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty
summary: Microsoft has released an update to Microsoft Cloud for Sovereignty, aimed at helping public sector organizations navigate cloud adoption, security, and compliance. The update introduces new guardrails, tools, and guidance to simplify the configuration of sovereign controls and expand best practice implementation. Key features include codified architectures, assessment and policy management tools, and reference architectures for using Azure OpenAI Service with sovereign landing zones.

title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FEPI)
summary: The REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) currently holds a "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating based on 625 analyst ratings covering 15 companies within its portfolio. The ETF has an aggregate price target equal to its current share price of $41.35, with specific holdings showing potential upsides. Top holdings include Micron Technology, Meta Platforms, Apple, NVIDIA, and Intel, each with their individual consensus ratings and price targets provided by analysts.

title: Microsoft and Apple are safer bets than the U.S. government? Bond investors seem to think so.
summary: Bond investors perceive Microsoft and Apple's bonds as safer than U.S. Treasury debt, with these corporate bonds trading at negative spread levels to Treasurys. This unusual market phenomenon is partly attributed to record annuity sales driven by investors planning for retirement. The article highlights that despite the general rule of being compensated for investment risk, these tech giants' bonds are an exception.

title: Microsoft (MSFT) reports higher revenue and profit for Q3 2024
summary: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) announced an increase in its Q3 2024 earnings year-over-year, driven by strong performance across its main operating segments, particularly the cloud business. Revenues for the quarter reached $61.86 billion, up from $52.86 billion in the prior-year period. Net income also rose to $21.9 billion, or $2.94 per share, compared to $18.3 billion, or $2.45 per share, in the comparable quarter of the previous year.

title: Microsoft reports 51% rise in Q3 gaming revenue thanks to Activision Blizzard, but console sales plummet
summary: Microsoft announced a significant 51% increase in its Q3 gaming revenue, primarily attributed to the acquisition of Activision Blizzard and strong performance of Call of Duty titles. However, this growth was contrasted by a 31% drop in Xbox hardware revenue due to lower console sales. The company noted record game streaming hours and console usage, with expectations for continued gaming segment growth in Q4, despite anticipated declines in hardware revenue.

title: The Estée Lauder Companies and Microsoft increase collaboration to power prestige beauty with generative AI
summary: The Estée Lauder Companies (ELC) and Microsoft have expanded their global strategic relationship by creating an AI Innovation Lab. This collaboration aims to leverage Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service to empower ELC's prestige beauty brands, enhance consumer connections, accelerate market entry with local relevance, and drive faster product innovation. The initiative deepens a partnership that began in 2017, which previously included the development of ELC's Voice-Enabled Makeup Assistant for visually impaired users.

title: Microsoft leaves office space in Morrisville as company moves to consolidate leases
summary: Microsoft has reduced its office footprint in Morrisville, North Carolina, by ending a lease for approximately 39,000 square feet and seeking to sublease an additional 20,000 square feet. This move is part of a broader company strategy to consolidate office leases and create higher density workspaces. The change reflects a trend among major tech companies to reduce office space post-pandemic, contributing to high office vacancy rates in the Raleigh-Durham area.

title: Why I Just Added This Ultra-High-Yield Dividend ETF to My Retirement Account
summary: The author recently added the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) to their retirement account, aiming for consistent passive income and price appreciation with lower volatility. JEPQ offers a high dividend yield (9.2% over 30 days, 10.8% over 12 months) through a strategy of holding a high-quality equity portfolio and selling out-of-the-money call options on the Nasdaq-100 index. This approach is designed to generate monthly income and provide relatively steady returns, making it an attractive investment for retirement planning.

title: From Washing Machine To TV And Fridges, Reliance Is Coming For India’s Electronic Market With WYZR
summary: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) is entering the Indian consumer electronics market with its new brand, Wyzr, aiming to compete with established players like Samsung and LG. Wyzr's website is live, initially offering air coolers, with plans to expand into a wide range of home appliances. RIL is reportedly finalizing production agreements with Mirc Electronics and Dixon Technologies, with Wyzr products expected to be affordably priced and distributed through Reliance Retail and other channels.

title: Korean Air validates its UAM system in world first operations demonstration
summary: Korean Air has successfully conducted the world's first comprehensive urban air mobility (UAM) operations demonstration, collaborating with partners like Hyundai Motor Company and Incheon International Airport Corporation. Over five weeks, the airline validated its UAM Operation Control System and Traffic Management System, utilizing a 5G aviation communication network to link e-VTOL aircraft with operating systems. This demonstration involved 10 different scenarios to test system functionality and performance, marking a significant step towards developing Korea's UAM ecosystem.

title: Microsoft Korea hosts AI Tour in Seoul illuminating the future of AI technology
summary: Microsoft Korea hosted the ‘Microsoft AI Tour in Seoul’ to provide insights into AI technology for developers and organizations, emphasizing AI's role in business transformation and new opportunities. The event highlighted Microsoft's commitment to supporting the developer community, introducing the Korean version of Copilot for Microsoft 365, and showcasing successful AI implementations by companies like LG Electronics and SK Innovation using Azure OpenAI Service.
