The airline experienced hundreds of millions of dollars in damages and widespread operational disruptions due to a faulty CrowdStrike software update that crashed Microsoft Windows systems earlier this month. Delta was particularly hard hit, with operations down for nearly a week and an estimated $500 million in costs from flight cancellations and refund requests.

title: Opinion: Microsoft and AMD earnings show that AI remains a game of optics
summary: The article discusses how recent earnings reports from Microsoft and AMD highlight that the AI investment landscape is heavily influenced by optics. Wall Street favors hardware companies that can provide precise revenue contributions from AI products over software developers that do not disclose AI-specific revenue. This trend explains why chip makers' valuations have risen more significantly than those of many software companies.

title: Is There Value In Small Cap Value Versus Large Cap Growth
summary: This article challenges the assumption that the significant outperformance of large-cap growth stocks versus small-cap value stocks is due for an imminent reversal based solely on price ratios. The author argues that such a comparison is flawed because the two categories are vastly different, akin to comparing orange juice and coffee. By examining sector breakdowns, earnings growth, and specific company examples like Microsoft and Robert Half, the article contends that a meaningful analysis must incorporate earnings growth, which often justifies the higher valuations of growth stocks.

title: Invest Like Congress: 2 ETFs to Add to Your Watchlist

The piece also delves into the ongoing ethical debate surrounding congressional stock trading, discussing concerns about conflicts of interest and insider information, urging investors to proceed with caution despite the ETFs' unique appeal.

title: Efficiency inside and out: EPAM streamlines communications for teams and clients with Copilot for Microsoft 365
summary: EPAM, a global software engineering and business consulting firm, integrated Copilot for Microsoft 365 to enhance internal communication and productivity across its 55 countries of operation and over 50,000 employees. This adoption allowed EPAM to not only streamline its own workflows, such as summarizing meetings and emails, but also to guide its clients through their own AI integration journeys while maintaining stringent data security. The company plans to expand its use of Copilot and emphasized the importance of a "Champions network" for successful AI adoption.

title: Microsoft to build $482.5 million data center in Castroville
summary: Microsoft plans to construct a new $482.5 million data center in Castroville, Texas, expanding its significant presence in the San Antonio area. This new facility will be a 244,676-square-foot colocation center, adding to two other data centers currently under construction by the company. The expansion highlights the region's appeal for data centers due to power and fiber infrastructure, but also raises questions about power supply with local utility CPS Energy.

title: Insight Enterprises achieves 93% Microsoft Copilot use rate, streamlining business operations to pave the way for customer success
summary: Insight Enterprises has achieved a 93% Microsoft Copilot use rate, significantly enhancing productivity across various departments like sales, finance, and human resources. title: Microsoft 365 Backup now GA with API for SaaS recovery
summary: Microsoft has made its Microsoft 365 Backup and Microsoft 365 Archive services generally available, offering customers direct backup and archiving for SaaS applications like Exchange and SharePoint. Analysts highlight the critical need for such backup solutions given the widespread reliance on Microsoft 365 applications and the shared-responsibility model common in SaaS.

summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. and Microsoft Corporation announced an expanded partnership to bring sophisticated cloud, AI, and analytics capabilities to the U.S. Defense and Intelligence Community. This collaboration will deploy Palantir's platforms (Foundry, Gotham, Apollo, AIP) in Microsoft Azure Government and classified clouds, utilizing Azure OpenAI Service for critical national security missions. The initiative aims to enable AI-driven operational workloads across various defense and intelligence verticals, pending government authorization.

summary: The digital pathology market is projected to grow significantly from USD 937.8 Million in 2023 to USD 2,082.7 Million by 2032, driven by advancements in imaging technology, the rising prevalence of chronic diseases, and increased adoption of telepathology and remote diagnostics. Key factors include AI integration in diagnostics, supportive government initiatives, and investments in healthcare infrastructure. The report highlights regional growth in North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Latin America, and the Middle East and Africa, driven by similar technological and healthcare advancements.

summary: Bardeen, a San Francisco-based provider of AI agents, has secured an additional $3 million in funding from strategic investors Dropbox Ventures and HubSpot Ventures, bringing its total funding to $22 million. Founded in 2020 by Artem Harutyunyan and Pascal Weinberger, the company offers an AI agent platform that automates repetitive knowledge work using natural language. This new investment will support Bardeen's goal of developing a fully autonomous AI platform to build time-saving automations, building on its rapid growth to over 300,000 users and 1,000 paying customers.

summary: KT Corp., South Korea's largest telecom provider, reported a 14.3% drop in Q2 operating profit due to pay raises and bonus payments despite steady revenue. The company is transitioning to a B2B service model, focusing on media and AI, with its cloud, data center, and property units contributing positively to earnings. However, its content platforms, payment processing, and satellite broadcaster segments experienced revenue declines.

summary: P&G is committed to building a superior employee experience for its Business and Technical Associates (BTAs) by understanding their unique perspectives, fostering continued learning, and tailoring benefits to increase their sense of belonging. Despite this, analysts maintain a "Buy" recommendation with a high price target, and NICE has initiated substantial share buyback programs to boost investor confidence.

They are particularly worried about dynamic pricing leading to increased costs for consumers and the use of cameras on ESLs for facial recognition to gather sensitive data for personalized offers. Kroger has defended its use of ESLs, stating the goal is to lower prices for customers and that any suggestion otherwise is untrue, though it did not address privacy concerns in its public statement.

Infillion's founder and executive chair, Rob Emrich, and CFO, Angela Birkemeier, highlighted the team's dedication and rapid innovation.

This initiative reinforces ANZ's commitment to safely integrating AI into its operations to enhance customer and employee experiences and drive business outcomes.

title: 19th August – Threat Intelligence Report

This integration reduces time-to-execute, minimizes data entry errors, and increases data accuracy and access, contributing to enhanced business visibility and revenue generation.

summary: Aerospace component manufacturer Unusual Machines has partnered with HP to integrate Multi Jet Fusion (MJF) 3D printing into its production of First-Person View (FPV) drones. This collaboration aims to boost the performance and durability of their drones while supporting domestic manufacturing by producing components in the U.S. using resilient TPU materials. The move is expected to control costs and enhance product quality, making FPV drone parts virtually indestructible.

title: Microsoft revamps reporting structure to give better visibility into cloud consumption revenue
summary: Microsoft has updated its quarterly revenue guidance and reporting structure to provide clearer insights into its growing cloud infrastructure business. These changes involve moving certain services between segments, such as Office productivity software and Windows commercial products into Productivity and Business Processes, and refining the "Azure and other cloud services" metric to better reflect consumption-based revenue. While the overall revenue forecast remains stable, these adjustments aim to align reporting with how the business is managed and enhance transparency for investors, although some analysts express minor concerns about understanding core commercial subscriptions.

title: AI Stock Eyes Early Buy Trigger As Profits Surge
summary: Powell Industries (POWL), an AI-related stock, is approaching an early buy trigger despite a recent midday dip. The company, which specializes in electrical energy management and data center solutions, saw its profits surge by 149%. This AI stock is currently an IBD 50 Stocks To Watch pick.

title: Palo Alto Networks’ Growth Soars due to High Cybersecurity Demand
summary: Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ: PANW) exceeded Wall Street's expectations with a 12% rise in quarterly revenue to $2.19 billion, driven by strong demand for cybersecurity solutions. The company anticipates annual revenue between $9.10 billion and $9.15 billion for fiscal 2025 and announced a $500 million expansion of its share repurchase program, reflecting confidence in its financial stability. Despite recent industry outages, CEO Nikesh Arora reassured customers about Palo Alto's distinct update process, leading to a surge in its shares and a record market capitalization of $121 billion.

title: Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:QDVO)
summary: The Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF (QDVO) invests primarily in large-cap equities, specifically holding long exposure to the Russell 1000 Growth Index and employing covered call options. The ETF, launched on August 22, 2024, aims to provide a balanced approach to growth and income with a current dividend yield of 10.78% and assets under management of $606.95 million. Its top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet.

title: Teladoc Health: Transforming telehealth operations with Copilot
summary: Teladoc Health has revolutionized its telehealth operations by implementing Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365, leading to significant time savings, increased efficiency, and improved employee satisfaction. Copilot automates routine tasks, streamlines processes, and enhances client service response times, setting a new standard in the telehealth industry. The integration of Copilot with other Microsoft tools like Power Automate and Power BI has further optimized client interactions and project management.

title: QDVO Top Holdings List & Exposure (Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF (QDVO). It lists the top 25 stock holdings, led by NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, which collectively account for a significant portion of the fund. The report also provides a comprehensive breakdown of QDVO's geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures, indicating a strong focus on the U.S. technology sector.

title: Stay Flexible With the AI Trade With These 2 ETFs
summary: This article discusses being flexible with AI stock market trades using leveraged and inverse ETFs from Direxion. It highlights the Direxion Daily AI and Big Data Bull 2X Shares (AIBU) for bullish trends and the Direxion Daily AI and Big Data Bear 2X Shares (AIBD) for bearish turns, allowing traders to adapt to short-term market volatility especially with the forthcoming U.S. presidential election.

title: What Are the Benefits of Windows 11 for AI?
summary: Windows 11 leverages AI and machine learning to enhance productivity, security, and user experience across various applications. Key benefits include optimized UI with features like Snap Assist and Copilot, improved performance in productivity tools and gaming, and robust cybersecurity measures. HP and other manufacturers are integrating these AI capabilities into their hardware, such as the HP Spectre and ENVY lines, to deliver powerful and secure computing experiences.

title: 3 Dividend-Growth Stocks That a Top-Rated Vanguard Fund Manager Likes
summary: This article highlights three dividend-growth stocks favored by Peter Fisher, lead manager of the Vanguard Dividend Growth fund (VDIGX), which holds Morningstar’s Gold Medalist Rating. These stocks are Microsoft (MSFT), Stryker (SYK), and Apple (AAPL), chosen for their strong operating performance and ability to grow dividends annually by more than 8%. The fund manager and Morningstar analysts provide an in-depth look at each company's financial health, economic moats, and stock valuations.

title: IBM Is America’s Worst Big Tech Company
summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) is highlighted as America's worst big tech company, lagging significantly behind competitors like Microsoft in market capitalization, revenue, net income, and cloud market share. The article attributes IBM's decline to missing major technological shifts such as PC operating systems, e-commerce, search, and smartphones. This has resulted in a much smaller market presence and lower financial performance compared to leading tech giants.

title: Top 10: Quantum Computing Companies
summary: This article highlights the top 10 quantum computing companies in 2024, detailing their unique approaches, key innovations, and applications. From D-Wave Systems' quantum annealing to IBM's superconducting transmon qubits, the list covers a diverse range of technologies and their impact on fields like drug discovery, financial modeling, and AI. The companies are ranked based on their contributions and advancements in the competitive quantum landscape.

title: Yelp sues Google, alleging a search engine monopoly that promotes its own reviews
summary: Yelp has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, accusing the tech giant of using its search engine monopoly to unfairly promote its own local search and advertising services. The lawsuit, filed in federal court in San Francisco, claims Google manipulates search results to keep users within its own ecosystem, preventing them from using rival platforms like Yelp, which it argues offer higher quality reviews. This action comes after a federal judge's ruling in August that Google violated US antitrust law with its search business.

title: Synergist Technology Partners with Microsoft to Drive Ethical and Compliant AI Innovation
summary: Synergist Technology has announced a strategic partnership with Microsoft to advance the development and deployment of ethical and compliant AI solutions. This collaboration aims to integrate Synergist's AI governance expertise with Microsoft's cloud infrastructure to help businesses navigate the complexities of AI regulations. The partnership, which includes Pliant Consulting and Maureen Data Systems, seeks to empower organizations to responsibly leverage AI while ensuring trust and transparency.

title: AI is revolutionizing the way we support corporate functions at Microsoft
summary: Microsoft Digital, the company's internal IT organization, is demonstrating how AI is transforming corporate functions like HR, legal, and real estate by improving operational efficiencies and accelerating growth. They highlight an AI-powered document lifecycle management platform that significantly reduces the time for document digitization and contract generation, as exemplified by a successful implementation with Xbox. Many capabilities of this internal solution will be integrated into SharePoint Premium, making Microsoft Digital "Customer Zero" for external product development.

title: Intapp director Ralph Baxter sells over $1.35m in company stock
summary: Intapp director Ralph Baxter sold over $1.35 million in company stock through a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan, while also exercising options to acquire additional shares. These transactions provide insight into insider trading behavior, occurring as Intapp reported strong Q4 and year-end 2024 results with significant cloud ARR growth. Despite the insider sales, Intapp shows promising financial metrics and is expected to become profitable this year, according to InvestingPro Insights.

title: Classroom quitters to industry leaders: These 14 billionaires dropped out of school to chase success
summary: This article highlights 14 billionaires who dropped out of formal education to pursue their entrepreneurial dreams and achieve significant success. It spotlights individuals like Ralph Lauren, Oprah Winfrey, Bill Gates, and Mark Zuckerberg, detailing their early career decisions and current net worth according to Forbes. The piece illustrates that success is not always linear and can be achieved through ambition and unconventional paths.

title: Semicon Taiwan draws top executives
summary: Semicon Taiwan, an annual international semiconductor exhibition, will host top executives from leading technology firms in Taipei. The event, taking place from Wednesday to Friday, will feature a CEO Summit and an AI Chip Fireside Chat, with discussions centered on how semiconductors drive global technological innovation amid the AI revolution. Over 1,100 exhibitors and 85,000 visitors are expected, with participation from 12 countries and a first-time US pavilion.

title: New F1 driver Colapinto shows off power of Argentina's 'unicorns'
summary: New Formula 1 driver Franco Colapinto is being backed by Argentinian "unicorns" Globant and Mercado Libre, showcasing a new era of sponsorship by digital native companies. Bizarrap, a superstar DJ, played a crucial role in securing this funding, shifting sponsorship to active partnerships where tech firms like Globant provide services to F1 teams. This collaboration highlights the growing influence of Latin American tech talent and companies in global sports.

title: This type of index fund might help you after the S&P 500’s concentration peaks
summary: The S&P 500's concentration in a few large-cap technology stocks is nearing peak levels, with 19.6% of the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust concentrated in Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia as of August 30. This high concentration runs counter to the diversification often sought by index fund investors, as a decade ago the top three holdings made up only 7.9% of the fund. The article suggests that while S&P 500 index funds have been beneficial, the current concentration might prompt investors to consider alternatives for broader risk management.

title: Global data center industry to emit 2.5 billion tons of CO2 through 2030, Morgan Stanley says
summary: The global data center industry is projected to emit approximately 2.5 billion metric tons of CO2-equivalent emissions by 2030, according to Morgan Stanley research. This surge is driven by hyperscalers like Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon expanding AI and cloud computing. The emissions are expected to accelerate investments in decarbonization solutions, clean power, and green building materials, as tech companies strive to meet climate pledges.

title: Why First Solar Stock Gained 30% This Year
summary: First Solar stock has gained 30% year-to-date, outperforming its competitor Enphase Energy. This performance is attributed to solid financial results, strong demand for its solar panels, and significant benefits from U.S. government incentives under the Inflation Reduction Act. The company's focus on domestic manufacturing and rising tariffs on Chinese imports further bolster its position.

title: Baidu rebrands Ernie Bot as Wenxiaoyan in China to stand out from AI rivals
summary: Baidu has rebranded its flagship AI app, Ernie Bot, as "Wenxiaoyan" in China to differentiate itself in the competitive AI market. The new name highlights its function as a "new search" assistant, with Baidu focusing on user value and integrating AI capabilities into various products. The app allows for text, speech, and image-based searches, along with AI functions like text-to-image generation and customized agents.

title: Salesforce set to buy cloud data backup specialist Own Co. for $1.9B in cash
summary: Salesforce Inc. has announced its acquisition of Own Co. (formerly OwnBackup Ltd.), a cloud data backup specialist, for $1.9 billion in cash. This acquisition marks Salesforce's first significant M&A deal since a committee tasked with such evaluations was eliminated two years ago, following pressure from activist investors concerning profitability. The deal, expected to close by January 2025, reflects a substantial discount from Own's 2021 valuation and is intended to enhance Salesforce's data protection offerings for its customers, especially in the context of growing AI initiatives.

title: Exclusive: Vista and Blackstone in talks to acquire software maker Smartsheet, sources say
summary: A private equity consortium, including Vista Equity Partners and Blackstone, is in talks to acquire Smartsheet, a U.S. workplace collaboration software maker with a market value of about $7 billion. A deal could be signed in the coming weeks, and Smartsheet's shares jumped nearly 10% on the news. This potential acquisition highlights a resurgence in private equity dealmaking, especially in technology, following high interest rates in 2023.

title: Global Sustainable Funds Are Up Nearly 11% This Year
summary: Global large-cap sustainable funds have seen an average return of 10.9% year-to-date, slightly lagging broader market indices. The top-performing sustainable funds, however, have achieved significantly higher returns, largely driven by investments in companies like Nvidia and Constellation Energy. This article details the largest and best-performing global large-cap sustainable funds, highlighting their strategies and key holdings.

title: Breakingviews - Blackstone $16 bln AI deal borrows from SPAC boom
summary: Blackstone's $16.1 billion acquisition of data center operator AirTrunk uses valuation methods reminiscent of the SPAC boom, incorporating "run-rate EBITDA" which includes future client commitments and capital expenditure for committed projects to justify the deal's size. While these techniques inflate the enterprise value significantly compared to traditional metrics, they enable the sellers to achieve a much higher valuation. A standard valuation method would show an 87 times EBITDA multiple, which is a figure difficult for any buyer to state publicly.

title: Could Electricity for Bitcoin Mining Become Energy for AI?
summary: U.S. technology companies are increasingly acquiring energy assets from Bitcoin miners to power their rapidly growing AI and cloud computing data centers, leading to a surge in electricity demand. This shift is turning some crypto miners into energy providers for AI, with companies like Amazon purchasing facilities and others like Core Scientific leasing their power-connected infrastructure to AI firms. Experts predict that a significant portion of Bitcoin mining power capacity will transition to AI needs by 2027, highlighting a profitable new direction for the cryptocurrency mining industry.

title: Stanley Druckenmiller – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article details Stanley Druckenmiller's top 10 holdings as of his latest 13F-HR filing (June 30, 2024). It highlights the companies and financial figures, emphasizing the value of 13F documents for investors tracking superinvestors. The total market value of his portfolio discussed is $2,917,218,000, with over 61% concentration in these top ten holdings.

title: 4 charts on the US rotation out of growth and tech stocks
summary: The article discusses the recent rotation in the US stock market, with investors shifting away from high-flying growth and tech stocks, particularly those tied to AI, towards value stocks in sectors like financials, healthcare, and consumer defensive. This shift is attributed to overvaluation in AI plays and attractive valuations in value and small-cap stocks, with economists foreseeing rate cuts and a soft landing. While major tech companies that led the market rally are now seeing declines, value stocks and defensive sectors are supporting the market's overall performance.

title: Salesforce CEO Reveals its ‘Next Big Transformation’
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff announced that the company's "next big transformation" will be a "hard pivot" towards autonomous AI agents, with a new platform called ‘Agentforce’. This shift will be the centerpiece of their Dreamforce conference and a subsequent world tour, aiming to deploy AI agents for thousands of customers. Benioff is convinced that AI is the future of CRM despite skepticism from some analysts regarding the company's ability to deliver on these "game changing plans."

title: CMS notifies 946K individuals of third-party data breach
summary: CMS has notified nearly 946,000 Medicare beneficiaries about a third-party data breach that potentially exposed their protected health information. The breach, which occurred in May 2023, involved vulnerabilities in Progress Software's MOVEit transfer software used by CMS contractor Wisconsin Physicians Service Insurance Corporation (WPS). Although WPS applied a patch and initially found no exploitation, new information in May 2024 revealed that an unauthorized actor had accessed and copied files containing personal identifiable information.

title: Oracle Expands Multicloud Capabilities with AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure
summary: Oracle is significantly expanding its multicloud capabilities by collaborating with AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. This initiative allows customers to directly access Oracle Database services running on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure within the data centers of these major cloud providers. The offerings, including Oracle Database@AWS, Oracle Database@Azure, and Oracle Database@Google Cloud, aim to simplify cloud migration, enable multicloud deployments, and provide a unified experience for customers.

title: iLearningEngines and Exult Global Announce Strategic Partnership to Implement Low Code AI Platform on Microsoft Azure and Fabric to Accelerate AI Use Cases Deployment
summary: iLearningEngines and Exult Global have formed a strategic partnership to deploy iLearningEngines' low-code AI platform on Microsoft Azure and Fabric. This collaboration aims to accelerate the development and deployment of AI use cases for enterprises, leveraging Fabric's unified analytics and Azure's cloud capabilities. The partnership expects to reduce IT infrastructure complexities, enhance scalability, integration, and security, and ultimately accelerate innovation and time to market for AI-powered applications.

title: Massive Microsoft data center brings new promise - and water and energy challenges - to Racine County.
summary: A new Microsoft data center in Racine County, spanning 1,347 acres, is under construction with a planned investment of $3.3 billion by 2026. While promising technological advancement, the project raises significant concerns regarding its substantial water and energy demands, and the limited number of new jobs it will create compared to the original Foxconn project. Local officials, including Racine Mayor Cory Mason, express anxieties about potential environmental impacts and the exacerbation of socioeconomic disparities in the region.

title: [UPD] Microsoft Gaming to lay off 650 people as part of post-acquisition optimization and team structure alignment
summary: Microsoft Gaming is set to lay off approximately 650 employees, primarily in corporate and supporting functions within Activision Blizzard, as part of post-acquisition team structure alignment. This follows previous job cuts earlier in the year, but CEO Phil Spencer stated that no games, devices, or studios will be canceled, and no executive roles will be cut. Activision Blizzard has been a significant driver of Microsoft's gaming revenue growth, contributing $5.72 billion in FY24.

title: Intel reaches deal to make chips for US military
summary: Intel Corp. has secured a deal expected to provide up to $3.5 billion in federal grants to produce semiconductors for the Pentagon under a secretive program called "Secure Enclave." This initiative aims to establish domestic production of advanced chips for military and intelligence applications, adding to Intel's potential $8.5 billion in grants and $11 billion in loans from the Chips and Science Act. Despite recent financial struggles and a reevaluation of its manufacturing ambitions, Intel was chosen due to its status as the only American maker of advanced processors, showcasing the Biden administration's commitment to domestic sourcing for critical defense technology.

title: With Salesforce push, AI Agents, agentic AI overload looms
summary: Salesforce is heavily promoting AI agents, or "Agentforce," at its upcoming Dreamforce conference, joining other enterprise tech giants like ServiceNow and Oracle in a rapidly expanding market for agentic AI. This push suggests a shift from generative AI to more autonomous AI agents that can make decisions and automate processes, potentially disrupting existing software revenue models and user interfaces. While the concept of AI agents has been around, significant venture capital and enterprise adoption are expected to accelerate its development and implementation across various business functions.

title: Total Recall? What Infosec Teams Can Learn From Microsoft’s Misstep
summary: This article discusses the privacy concerns raised by Microsoft's "Recall" app, highlighting its initial "opt-out" implementation and the subsequent backlash. It explores the benefits of such AI-enabled tools for productivity while emphasizing the significant privacy risks, especially in enterprise settings. The author provides lessons for companies considering similar technologies, focusing on opt-in policies, risk assessment, secure configuration, data minimization, employee training, and incident response.

title: Microsoft announces quarterly dividend increase and new share repurchase program
summary: Microsoft's board of directors declared an increased quarterly dividend of $0.83 per share, representing a 10% raise. Concurrently, the company approved a new share repurchase program authorizing up to $60 billion in buybacks with no expiration date. The annual shareholders meeting is scheduled for December 10, 2024, and will be held virtually.

title: SHÁKA RASHEED JOINS OPEN OPPORTUNITY FUND AS GENERAL PARTNER TO LEAD CAPITAL DEVELOPMENT
summary: Sháka Rasheed has joined Open Opportunity Fund as General Partner, tasked with leading capital development and portfolio value creation. Rasheed brings extensive experience from top financial services and technology companies like Bridgewater, Lazard, Citadel, J.P. Morgan, Salesforce, and Microsoft. Open Opportunity Fund, which invests in software companies founded by diverse entrepreneurs, is currently raising its second fund after successful exits from its first $100 million fund.

title: Securing US elections from nation-state adversaries
summary: Brad Smith, Microsoft's Vice Chair & President, testifies before the Senate Intelligence Committee about sophisticated and persistent nation-state threats to US elections in 2024. He details cyber influence operations from Iran, Russia, and China, including the deceptive use of generative AI, and outlines Microsoft's efforts in combating these threats through content provenance, deepfake detection, and safeguarding election infrastructure. The article concludes with policy recommendations for federal "deepfake fraud statutes" and legislation to protect elections from deceptive AI.

title: Kramer to show Microsoft Teams Rooms systems at UCX
summary: Kramer will be showcasing its new Microsoft Teams Rooms (MTR) certified systems and products from its Ashton Bentley acquisition at the UCX London exhibition. This event marks Kramer's first major trade show since acquiring Ashton Bentley and AVoIP specialist ZeeVee. Additionally, Kramer's cloud-based platform, Panta Rhei, designed for managing and monitoring AV systems, will also be featured.

title: Infosys deploys central identity and access management platform based on Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift
summary: Infosys has deployed a central identity and access management platform utilizing Keycloak hosted on Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift to enhance user experience, scalability, and security for its external stakeholders and employees. This solution provides seamless access across applications with single sign-on, supports various authentication flows, and has significantly improved performance processing over 200,000 daily transactions with zero downtime. The platform's robust security features and flexibility for customization have made it a transformative solution for Infosys's digital transformation journey.

title: Rezolve Ai Welcomes Microsoft and BlackRock’s $30 Billion Investment in AI Infrastructure as a Game-Changer for the Industry
summary: Rezolve Ai applauds Microsoft and BlackRock's $30 billion investment in AI infrastructure, seeing it as a pivotal development that will accelerate AI adoption and innovation. The company believes this investment will provide the necessary technological foundation for AI innovators to scale and deliver sophisticated solutions more rapidly, benefiting Rezolve Ai's own AI-driven commerce solutions like braincommerce and braincheckout. Rezolve Ai anticipates this infrastructure upgrade will enhance its ability to drive hyper-personalization and lead the transformation of the commerce sector through advanced AI capabilities.

title: Nuclear plant Three Mile Island is reopening to power Microsoft AI's growing energy demands
summary: Constellation Energy Corp. has announced a 20-year deal to supply Microsoft with nuclear power by reopening the Three Mile Island plant, which will address Microsoft AI's massive energy demands with carbon-free electricity. This move is expected to create 3,400 jobs, add over 800 megawatts to the grid, and contribute significantly to Pennsylvania's GDP and tax revenue, though it still requires federal regulatory approval. This development is seen as a major step in the energy and technology sectors for powering AI data centers while focusing on carbon-free solutions.

title: Former Three Mile Island nuclear power plant set to restart operations
summary: Constellation Energy announced a deal with Microsoft to restart Unit 1 of the former Three Mile Island nuclear power plant, which was shut down five years ago. The reactor is expected to be online by 2028 and will supply power for Microsoft's data centers. While some community members and watchdog groups express concerns about safety, local leaders highlight the potential economic boosts and tax revenue for the area.

title: Is the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF a Millionaire Maker?
summary: This article discusses the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) as a potential "millionaire maker" investment. It highlights the ETF's historical performance, its focus on growth stocks within the Russell 1000 index, and its exposure to both large and mid-cap companies. The author suggests that while not perfectly balanced, IWF offers a good chance to outperform the broader market over the long term.

title: IBM & Microsoft Expand AI Accessibility for Tech Skills Gap
summary: IBM and Microsoft have strengthened their partnership by launching three new Experience Zones in Romania, USA, and UK to improve AI accessibility and bridge the global technology skills gap. These centers offer hands-on access to advanced technologies like generative AI and hybrid cloud solutions, leveraging IBM's consulting expertise and Microsoft's cutting-edge technologies. The initiative aims to help businesses navigate the digital landscape and accelerate digital transformation.

title: Environmental groups call on Microsoft to power Mount Pleasant data centers with clean energy
summary: Environmental groups are urging Microsoft to power its Mount Pleasant data center campus with renewable energy, warning that the expansion of data centers could hinder clean energy progress in Wisconsin. They point out that these data centers are driving increased energy demand, potentially leading We Energies to build more natural gas infrastructure, thereby locking the state into fossil fuels for decades. The groups emphasize the need for large utility customers like Microsoft to actively advocate for around-the-clock clean energy solutions in Wisconsin rather than relying on emissions-matching purchases from other states.

title: This Microsoft Analyst Is No Longer Bullish; Here Are Top 5 Downgrades For Monday
summary: This article details the top five stock downgrades on Monday, including surprising shifts in analyst sentiment for major companies. A notable change comes from DA Davidson, which downgraded Microsoft from Buy to Neutral, setting a new price target. Other major companies like General Motors, GoDaddy, Ulta Beauty, and S&P Global also received downgrades from various analysts, impacting their stock outlooks.

title: The Benefits of the latest Android OS on Microsoft Teams Rooms and Devices
summary: This article discusses the importance of the Android OS version running on Microsoft Teams Rooms, Panels, and Phones, even though these are appliance-like devices. It highlights how the Android version impacts security, performance, and crucial support lifecycles, and explains how updates and support are managed for these specialized Android devices. The article also touches on the role of chipsets and OEMs in determining upgrade paths and support duration for Teams Android devices.

title: Microsoft announces $1.3 billion USD investment in Cloud and AI infrastructure supporting inclusive growth through technology and skilling programs in Mexico
summary: Microsoft is investing $1.3 billion over three years in Mexico to enhance its Cloud and AI infrastructure and promote digital and AI skills, aiming to train 5 million people. This initiative, announced by CEO Satya Nadella, will increase local computing capacity, foster innovation, support digital transformation for small and medium-sized businesses, and address societal challenges in health, connectivity, and sustainability. The investment reinforces Microsoft's commitment to Mexico's inclusive economic growth and reflects its role in making AI widely accessible and beneficial.

title: Will Windows Server 2025 spark VMware migrations?
summary: Microsoft aims to attract VMware customers with the upcoming Windows Server 2025 by leveraging enhancements to its Hyper-V virtualization platform, attractive cloud migration discounts, and the Azure Stack HCI platform. This strategic move coincides with customer concerns following Broadcom's acquisition of VMware and its subsequent changes to licensing and product offerings. The article highlights new features in Windows Server 2025 like GPU partitioning, dynamic CPU compatibility, and improved security, positioning them as viable alternatives for organizations seeking stability and innovation away from VMware.

title: Talos discovers denial-of-service vulnerability in Microsoft Audio Bus; Potential remote code execution in popular open-source PLC
summary: Cisco Talos' Vulnerability Research team recently disclosed critical vulnerabilities, including two in Microsoft products and three in OpenPLC. The Microsoft vulnerabilities involve a denial-of-service in the High-Definition Audio Bus Driver and a memory corruption issue in the Pragmatic General Multicast server, both of which have been patched. The OpenPLC vulnerabilities, one of which could lead to remote code execution, also resulted in denial-of-service risks through crafted Ethernet/IP requests.

title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights: Constellation Energy, Cameco and Vistra
summary: This article from Zacks Investment Ideas highlights Constellation Energy, Cameco, and Vistra as key nuclear energy stocks that investors should consider, especially given the high energy demands of the AI sector. It details recent developments for each company, including Vistra's acquisition of Energy Harbor and new power purchase agreements, Cameco's impressive uranium segment performance, and Constellation Energy's deal with Microsoft to restart a nuclear plant. The article suggests that while AI is driving demand, the energy consumption aspect offers a compelling investment angle in these nuclear energy providers.

title: InterDigital, IBD Stock Of The Day, Flirts With Buy Point After Analyst Day
summary: InterDigital (IDCC) has been named IBD Stock Of The Day and is approaching a buy point of 140.60 after an analyst day. The stock has a Composite Rating of 95/99 and is forming a shallow flat base. The article also mentions widespread investment in AI stocks by major tech companies.

title: Shipping zero trust to global ports
summary: Given the critical role global ports play in the economy and their increasing vulnerability to cyberattacks, security for their IT infrastructure, especially IoT and OT devices, is paramount. Recent incidents like the Australian port breach and the discovery of unexpected communications equipment in cranes highlight the urgent need for enhanced security measures. Adopting zero trust principles can help secure these crucial systems by improving device visibility, enabling secure communication, and managing third-party access, thereby safeguarding against disruptions and espionage.

title: AI Mania in Fine Fettle: ETFs to Invest
summary: The article discusses the resurgence of AI investment fervor on Wall Street, driven by strong performances from companies like Micron and significant investments from tech giants such as Microsoft and Amazon. It highlights how these developments are boosting the AI sector and suggests several AI-focused ETFs, including BOTZ, ROBT, and WISE, for investors looking to capitalize on this trend. The piece also touches upon NVIDIA's fluctuating valuation and its central role in the AI infrastructure.

title: 3 of Joel Greenblatt’s Top 5 Stocks Are S&P 500 ETFs. These Are the 2 Stocks That Aren’t
summary: Joel Greenblatt, known for "The Little Book That Beat the Market," runs Gotham Asset Management, which holds over 1,400 stocks. While three of his top five holdings are S&P 500 ETFs, the remaining two are mega-cap stocks Nvidia and Microsoft. Greenblatt has notably increased his positions in both NVDA and MSFT, despite some other investors reducing their stakes, demonstrating a contrarian value investing approach.

title: KT Corporation and Microsoft take ‘giant step’ to accelerate AI innovation in Korea
summary: KT Corporation and Microsoft Corp. announced a five-year multibillion-dollar strategic partnership to accelerate AI innovation and digital transformation in Korea. The collaboration focuses on developing customized AI solutions, launching Korean sovereign cloud services, establishing an AX-specialized service company, advancing AI R&D capabilities, and migrating KT's IT workloads to Microsoft Azure. This initiative aims to benefit over 650,000 businesses and 17 million consumers by deploying advanced AI services and upskilling KT employees.

title: Lisa Su on AMD’s Strategy for Growth and the Future of AI
summary: Lisa Su, CEO and chair of AMD, discusses the company's growth strategy, focusing on its strong position in high-performance computing and pervasive AI. She highlights AMD's successful acquisitions and partnerships, its latest MI300X AI chip often compared to its competitor Nvidia's, and addresses the "AI bubble" skeptics by emphasizing AI's transformative potential over the next five years. Su also touches on geopolitical considerations, and the importance of increasing women's leadership in the tech industry.

title: KT strikes multibillion-dollar AI deal with Microsoft
summary: South Korean telco KT Corp. has formed a five-year strategic partnership with Microsoft, valued at billions of dollars, to collaboratively develop AI and cloud services for both the Korean market and international needs. This deal includes a $450 million usage fee for Microsoft to utilize KT and KT Cloud infrastructure. The partnership will focus on customized AI model development, a secure sovereign cloud platform, and establishing an AI transformation company, alongside fostering the domestic AI ecosystem.

title: Microsoft, KT join forces to propel Korea’s AI innovation
summary: Microsoft Corp. and KT Corp. have announced a five-year multibillion-dollar strategic partnership to drive AI transformation in South Korea. The collaboration involves KT providing network infrastructure and data centers worth $450 million to Microsoft for 15 years, and the companies will jointly develop customized AI services, a Korea-specific version of Microsoft’s Phi small language models, and secure public cloud services. This partnership aims to accelerate AI adoption in Korean businesses and expand into global enterprise AI markets, with KT also planning to establish an AI transformation service company called AI Total Service.

title: Workday takes on Microsoft in race to transform corporate finance
summary: Workday has introduced Workday Assistant, an AI companion for finance and HR workflows, positioning itself against Microsoft's Copilot for Finance. This move is part of Workday's "Illuminate" framework, aiming to transform corporate functions with AI-powered experiences. Both companies are aggressively infusing AI into their enterprise software offerings to enhance productivity and customer value.

title: 13 Charts on Q3′s Roller-Coaster Rally for Stocks and Bonds
summary: The third quarter of 2024 saw a "white-knuckle ride" for investors, with stocks reaching record highs and bonds in the green, largely driven by the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts. A significant rotation from tech stocks to value and small-cap stocks occurred, alongside a rally in bond markets and outperformance of dividend stocks. Despite a brutal selloff in August, equities ended the quarter up over 6%, with further rate cuts anticipated.

title: Trade Like a Member of Congress With These 2 ETFs
summary: This article discusses two ETFs, the Subversive Unusual Whales Democratic ETF (BATS: NANC) and the Subversive Unusual Whales Republican ETF (BATS: KRUZ), which track the stock trades of U.S. members of Congress. The NANC ETF, heavily invested in technology stocks like NVIDIA and Microsoft, has seen a 50% increase since its inception, while the KRUZ ETF, with a more diversified approach including financial and industrial sectors, has risen 25%. Both ETFs offer investors a way to potentially benefit from the "insider knowledge" of Congress members regarding government and legislative impacts on the stock market.

title: Comment: Engineers should be collaborators and not competitors with AI
summary: Adam Auerbach from EPAM Systems argues that engineers should view AI as a collaborative tool rather than a competitor. He emphasizes that AI, through tools like Microsoft Copilot and AI Agents, can automate tedious tasks and accelerate the software development lifecycle, allowing engineers to focus on higher-value activities. The article stresses the importance of training, change management, and prompt library platforms for successful AI integration within engineering teams.

title: Microsoft and Rezolve AI partner to drive global retail innovation with AI-powered commerce solutions
summary: Microsoft and Rezolve AI have announced a strategic partnership to bring AI-powered commerce solutions to retailers globally. Rezolve AI's Brain Suite will be powered by Microsoft Azure and distributed via Azure Marketplace, with Microsoft providing extensive go-to-market and co-sell support. This collaboration aims to enhance customer engagement, streamline operations, and drive growth for retailers through advanced AI capabilities.

title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Desktop as a Service | Microsoft 365 Blog
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Desktop as a Service (DaaS) for the second consecutive year, highlighting its commitment to innovation, scalability, and security in cloud-based solutions. The article emphasizes Microsoft's comprehensive offerings like Windows 365 and Azure Virtual Desktop, along with its extensive ecosystem including Intune, FSLogix, Microsoft Entra, and Copilot, for empowering flexible work and IT modernization.

title: Transforming drug discovery: Novo Nordisk uses the power of AI and Azure with Microsoft Research
summary: Novo Nordisk partnered with Microsoft Research to create an AI platform on Azure, aimed at accelerating drug discovery and development. The collaboration has enabled Novo Nordisk to leverage AI capabilities across various use cases, leading to published results in predictive AI models for cardiovascular disease risk detection. This initiative amplifies Novo Nordisk's culture of innovation and utilizes their extensive historical data with advanced AI to drive precision medicine.

title: 2 Dividend Growth ETFs That You Can Count on for Recurring Income for Decades
summary: This article highlights two dividend growth ETFs, the WisdomTree U.S. Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) and the Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG), as excellent long-term investment options for recurring income. It details their holdings, expense ratios, dividend yields, and past performance, making a case for why they are suitable for investors looking for consistent cash flow. The author also provides a brief comparison between the two, noting DGRW's slightly higher returns due to more tech exposure and VIG's greater safety and diversification.

title: Oracle's Larry Ellison is getting richer, rivaling Musk, Bezos, Zuckerberg
summary: Oracle co-founder and CTO Larry Ellison's net worth has surged to an estimated $208 billion, placing him firmly among the wealthiest individuals globally, behind Elon Musk and ahead of Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos, according to Forbes. His wealth growth is largely attributed to his substantial 41% stake in Oracle, whose stock has seen a 63% increase year-to-date. Oracle's strong financial performance, with reported revenues of $13.3 billion and net income of $2.9 billion in Q1, contributes significantly to this rise, with forecasts suggesting Oracle could reach a $1 trillion market capitalization by 2030.

title: OpenAI partners with AMD; multi-billion-dollar chip orders may ignite computing power race!
summary: OpenAI has partnered with AMD to purchase tens of billions of dollars worth of AI chips, potentially acquiring a 10% equity stake in the chipmaker. This strategic alliance aims to accelerate OpenAI's AI infrastructure development and ignite a computational power race, with AMD's stock surging significantly after the announcement. The deal involves OpenAI deploying AMD's upcoming MI450 chip and committing to substantial computing power investments for future AI advancements.

title: Direxion Debuts Netflix, TSM Single Stock ETFs
summary: Direxion has launched four new single stock ETFs providing leveraged and inverse exposure to Netflix (NFLX) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM). These funds, Direxion Daily NFLX Bull 2X Shares (NFXL), Direxion Daily NFLX Bear 1X Shares (NFXS), Direxion Daily TSM Bull 2X Shares (TSMX), and Direxion Daily TSM Bear 1X Shares (TSMZ), are designed for active traders seeking tactical exposure to these "hottest names" in the retail space. The ETFs reset daily and are intended for short-term trading, as highlighted by Direxion's managing director, Edward Egilinsky.

title: Denso’s development of “human-like” robots using Azure OpenAI Service as the brain to help robots and humans work together through dialogue
summary: Denso is developing "human-like" robots that can understand human language and autonomously act, powered by Microsoft's Azure OpenAI Service. This initiative aims to overcome the inflexibility of traditional robots and enable seamless human-robot collaboration in diverse environments. The collaboration between Denso's hardware and software expertise and Microsoft's generative AI technology is set to revolutionize robot applications.

title: Teradata harnesses its history of parallel architecture for AI era
summary: Teradata is leveraging its long history with parallel architecture and partnerships with cloud giants like Google and AWS to position itself as a sustainable hybrid AI platform. CEO Steve McMillan highlights their patented approach of moving the query engine to the data and efficient orchestration of workloads. The company also announced a new partnership with Nvidia to combine Teradata's software with Nvidia's GPU infrastructure for AI innovation.

title: Have you tried? Autodesk Construction Cloud Data Connector & Microsoft CoPilot - Digital Builder
summary: This article discusses leveraging Artificial Intelligence in construction by integrating Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) data with Microsoft Copilot. It highlights how ACC's Data Connector can transfer project data into the Microsoft Fabric framework, enabling users to utilize Copilot for quick insights. The key benefits include saving time, centralizing data, and gaining access to AI capabilities for more informed decision-making in construction workflows.

title: This ETF Is a Backdoor AI Play
summary: The article suggests that nuclear power, represented by the VanEck Uranium and Nuclear ETF (NLR), is becoming a "backdoor AI play" due to the increasing energy demands of AI infrastructure. Major AI players like Microsoft and Amazon are already securing nuclear power agreements for their data centers, highlighting nuclear energy's reliability and sustainability benefits for the AI boom. While some uncertainties remain regarding future data center energy demands, the overall trajectory for nuclear power supporting AI is seen as positive.

title: AMD Unveils CPU & GPU Solutions in AI Leadership Bid
summary: AMD announced new high-performance AI accelerators, networking solutions, and the latest generation of EPYC processors at its 2024 Advancing AI event, aiming to intensify competition with Nvidia in the growing AI chip market. The company projects the data center AI accelerator market to reach US$500 billion by 2028 and is committed to delivering open innovation through expanded silicon, software, network, and cluster-level solutions. These new offerings, including the Instinct MI325X accelerators and fifth-generation EPYC CPUs, are designed to meet the increasing demand for powerful and efficient AI computing.

title: Data centers tap pipelines for onsite natural gas generation
summary: Data centers are increasingly seeking direct pipeline connections for onsite natural gas generation due to grid connection delays and competition for power. Pipeline giants like Kinder Morgan, Williams Co., and Energy Transfer are in commercial discussions with data center operators, indicating significant potential demand for natural gas. This trend is driven by the soaring electricity demands of technology giants for cloud services and AI, with data centers projected to consume up to 9% of U.S. electricity generation by 2030.

title: ManpowerGroup Talent Solutions builds strategic workforce planning solution with Power Platform
summary: ManpowerGroup's Talent Solutions practice developed a strategic workforce planning (SWP) solution using Microsoft Power Platform to streamline HR data analysis and consulting. This customized solution, built in two months, integrates various data sources, Power BI, and Azure OpenAI Service to provide a comprehensive view of workforce needs, saving costs and enabling consultants to focus more on individual career coaching rather than data compilation. The platform has been successfully deployed, accelerating planning processes and is set for global expansion within ManpowerGroup.

title: After Vistra Stock’s (VST) 200%+ Move This Year, Is There More Potential Upside Before Q3 Earnings?
summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) stock has surged over 200% this year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 Utilities index. The article suggests further upside potential before Q3 2024 earnings, driven by increasing energy demand for AI data centers, strategic acquisitions expanding its nuclear and renewables capacity, and robust hedging strategies securing profitability. The company is also benefiting from a tech exodus to Texas, its home state, and federal production tax credits for nuclear power.

title: US Stock Market Outlook Q4 2024: Will the Great Rotation Persist?
summary: This Morningstar Q4 2024 outlook discusses the potential persistence of the market "great rotation" from big-cap growth to value and small/mid-cap stocks. Analysts Dave Sekera and Preston Caldwell provide insights into market valuations, sector performance, top stock picks, and the US economic outlook, including inflation and interest rate expectations, maintaining a soft landing forecast. The article emphasizes undervalued opportunities in certain sectors despite the overall market trading at a premium.

title: Nuclear Power Makes a Comeback as Data Centers Adapt to Rising Power Demands
summary: Data centers are experiencing exponential growth, leading to a significant increase in their power demands. To meet these demands sustainably, and given the intermittency of renewable sources like wind and solar, nuclear power is re-emerging as a viable solution, especially with hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google investing in it. The article discusses various strategies data centers are employing, including revisiting decommissioned nuclear plants and utilizing small modular reactors, while also addressing the public perception challenges surrounding nuclear energy and the need for new cooling technologies.

title: Nvidia notches record close, could unseat Apple as most valuable company
summary: Nvidia's shares closed at a record high, positioning the AI chipmaker to potentially surpass Apple as the world's most valuable company. Strong demand for its AI processors propelled Nvidia's market value to $3.39 trillion, just shy of Apple's $3.52 trillion, while analysts predict significant revenue growth due to AI data center spending.

title: Schneider Electric charges into the future with Microsoft Copilot for Sales
summary: Schneider Electric is leveraging Microsoft Copilot for Sales to enhance its global sales operations by automating tedious administrative tasks and improving data quality in Salesforce CRM. The deployment, which is already in use by 5,000 employees across 30 countries with a target of 10,500 users by the end of 2024, enables sales teams to focus more on customer engagement. Key benefits include AI-assisted contact creation, lightning-fast email summaries, and accurate meeting recaps, leading to increased efficiency, higher customer satisfaction, and improved data accuracy.

title: Bridging the Digital Divide: Our Enduring Commitment to Global Connectivity
summary: Microsoft is refreshing its Digital Equity Data Dashboard to provide updated information on broadband gaps across the United States, helping policymakers address the digital divide. The article highlights the success of Microsoft's Airband Initiative in expanding connectivity in rural U.S. communities and its global partnerships, while also calling for continued collaboration from all sectors to connect the 2.6 billion people who remain offline. It emphasizes the importance of government investments, innovative technologies, and strategic partnerships to achieve global connectivity and digital equity.

title: Amazon goes nuclear, to invest more than $500 million to develop small modular reactors
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is investing over $500 million in nuclear power, specifically in small modular reactors (SMRs), to meet the massive energy demands of its expanding generative AI services and achieve net-zero carbon emissions. This move includes agreements with Dominion Energy in Virginia and Energy Northwest in Washington state to develop and utilize SMRs. Amazon's Climate Pledge Fund is also a lead investor in a $500 million financing round for SMR developer X-energy.

title: The enterprise guide to AI governance
summary: This article provides an enterprise guide to AI governance, emphasizing the importance of accountability, transparency, and explainability for building trustworthy AI systems. It highlights how effective governance is crucial for adopting generative AI to capture business value while mitigating risks like bias and compliance issues. The guide offers actionable recommendations and illustrates core concepts with case studies from organizations like IBM and Australia Post.

title: Invest in the A.I. Ecosystem with AGIX
summary: This article introduces the KraneShares Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AGIX) as a way to invest in the entire AI ecosystem. It highlights the rapid growth of AI, led by companies like Nvidia and ChatGPT, and explains AGIX's strategy of investing across AI hardware, infrastructure, and applications. The ETF uses a unique AI-score to identify relevant companies and aims to capture both current AI leaders and future winners through quarterly index reviews.

title: Why this money manager sees FirstService, Novo Nordisk and Microsoft as good long-term holdings
summary: Money manager Paul Harris of Harris Douglas Asset Management Inc. focuses on long-term investments in high-quality businesses, holding onto a core group of about two dozen stocks. He emphasizes that less than 4% of stocks drive most market gains and seeks companies with strong balance sheets and high returns on invested capital. His firm's top holdings include FirstService Corp., Novo Nordisk A/S, and Microsoft Corp., which he continues to buy for new clients due to their strong fundamentals and growth prospects in property management, diabetes/weight-loss drugs, and AI respectively.

title: Which AI Stocks Are Leading the Market to New Highs?
summary: After a sharp selloff, AI-related stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon, are once again driving the market to new highs, contributing significantly to recent gains. While the Morningstar Global Next Generation Artificial Intelligence Index is up 30.94% this year, it's also trading at a premium, raising questions about future growth and prompting Morningstar strategists to suggest opportunities might lie in undervalued small-cap and value stocks. This current tech-led recovery mirrors earlier market trends since October 2022, with technology and financial services sectors consistently leading the way.

title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Reports 47% Revenue Growth Driven by AI Demand; Mizuho Raises Price Target to $220 Amid Promising Collaboration with OpenAI
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) reported a 47% increase in revenue, driven by strong demand for AI semiconductor solutions and VMware, leading Mizuho to raise its price target to $220. The company's collaboration with OpenAI on custom AI chips is expected to unlock a significant market opportunity, with analysts believing Broadcom's AI potential is undervalued. The broader market remains bullish on AI-linked stocks, though high valuations and global events pose some risks.

title: Nucor eyes opportunities in the energy sector
summary: Nucor has established Nucor Energy Solution Services to expand into the energy sector, providing steel products and services to major energy consumers and generators. The company is focusing on leveraging its recycled-content steel in areas like solar arrays, data centers, and electric vehicle production, emphasizing sustainability and decarbonization. Nucor is also exploring partnerships for EV battery recycling and repurposing decommissioned coal plants to bring more clean energy to the grid.

title: XMAG Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:XMAG
summary: The Defiance Large Cap ex-Mag 7 ETF (XMAG) is a passively managed fund that provides exposure to stocks similar to the S&P 500 Index, but excludes the seven "Magnificent 7" companies (Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Nvidia, Meta, and Tesla). Its objective is to offer a broader and more balanced representation of the US equity market, with holdings weighted by free-float market capitalization from the 700 largest stocks. As of the article, XMAG has $136.62 million in assets under management, and its price has shown a 16.07% increase over the past year.

title: Rackspace Technology® records $40M in total cost of ownership savings by pivoting from SPLA to Azure Arc
summary: Rackspace Technology achieved $40 million in total cost of ownership savings by migrating from SPLA to Azure Arc. This pivot optimized SQL licensing, improved Extended Security Updates delivery, and enhanced hybrid cloud operations, resulting in increased reporting proficiency and customer satisfaction. The company aims for 80% growth in Azure Arc consumption within the next three years, leveraging Azure Arc for unified management and new cloud services.

title: Two Indian-origin leaders among top 10 highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs
summary: Two Indian-origin CEOs, Nikesh Arora of Palo Alto Networks Inc and Satya Nadella of Microsoft Corp, are among the top 10 highest-paid S&P 500 company leaders. Arora ranks second with over $151 million, while Nadella is tenth with over $48 million. This highlights a significant disparity between CEO pay, which has surged by over $4 million in a decade, and the average US worker's wage increase of $18,240 during the same period.

title: Axon enhances public safety and cuts report-writing time in half using Draft One, an AI-powered solution built on Microsoft Azure
summary: Axon Enterprise developed Draft One, an AI-powered solution built on Microsoft Azure, to significantly reduce the time police officers spend on report writing. This generative AI tool transcribes body-worn camera audio and drafts comprehensive police reports, cutting report-writing time by up to 82% in some agencies. The initiative aims to free up officers for more community engagement, improve work-life balance, and enhance public safety while maintaining report accuracy and accountability through robust safeguards.

title: BlackRock broadens active ETF shelf with AI and tech funds
summary: BlackRock has launched two new active ETFs, the iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF and the iShares Technology Opportunities Active ETF, to expand its offerings in the artificial intelligence and technology sectors. These new funds aim to provide investors with enhanced access to the growing AI industry and rapidly evolving technology trends, capitalizing on the dynamic growth seen in the active ETF space. BlackRock believes these ETFs will help investors seize significant investment opportunities within the new technology supercycle.

title: CDW rolls out Microsoft 365 Copilot to 10,000 employees, reporting 85% productivity gains
summary: CDW Corporation has successfully rolled out Microsoft 365 Copilot to 10,000 employees, reporting significant productivity gains. Surveys indicate that 88% of users improved work quality, 77% completed tasks faster, and 85% experienced increased productivity. CDW utilized extensive training and internal collaboration through Microsoft Viva Engage to facilitate this digital transformation and develop firsthand expertise for advising its customers on AI adoption.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms for the sixth consecutive year, reflecting the trust of millions of users building on Microsoft Power Platform. The article highlights successful customer case studies where Power Platform, especially with the integration of Microsoft Copilot and robust governance features, has driven significant savings and efficiency gains. This recognition underscores Power Platform's role in modernizing application estates and enabling rapid development for the AI age.

title: Ingram Micro IPO: Major tech product distributor debuts on NYSE today after pricing its stock within target range
summary: Ingram Micro Holding Corporation, a major behind-the-scenes tech product distributor with $48 billion in net sales last year, is making its initial public offering on the NYSE today, October 24, 2024, under the ticker symbol "INGM". The shares were priced at $22, within its target range, and the company is offering 18.6 million shares, raising approximately $255 million from its direct share sales. This marks a return to the public market for Ingram Micro, which was previously listed on the NYSE until 2016.

title: Accelerating financial services transformation with AI
summary: Microsoft is accelerating financial services transformation through AI, showcasing new tools like Meeting Prep for Financial Services, developed with LSEG, to enhance productivity and decision-making for professionals. The article highlights how AI is being used across the industry to improve customer experience, empower employees, manage risk and compliance, enhance security, and modernize core systems. Microsoft emphasizes its commitment to responsible AI, security, and privacy as financial institutions embrace these technological advancements.

title: Is Deckers Stock a Buy After Its Beat-And-Raise Quarter?
summary: Deckers Outdoor (DECK) stock rose after reporting strong fiscal second-quarter earnings, beating expectations and raising its full-year profit forecast, driven by significant growth in its Hoka brand. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Buy" rating for DECK, anticipating further upside based on continued product innovation and market segmentation. The company's robust performance year-to-date and positive outlook for the holiday season suggest ongoing growth potential.

title: Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) and L3Harris Forge Strategic Partnership to Boost AI-Driven Defense Capabilities for Enhanced National Security
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) and L3Harris Technologies have formed a strategic partnership to enhance AI-driven defense capabilities and accelerate L3Harris’ digital transformation. This collaboration aims to leverage their complementary strengths—L3Harris's sensors and software-defined systems with Palantir's AI Platform (AIP)—to provide resilient connectivity and enable faster decision-making for warfighters. The partnership is highlighted within the context of significant AI advancements in 2024, with a focus on governance and the potential for AI to drive substantial value across industries.

title: Nvidia overtakes Apple as world's most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable company, driven by robust demand for its specialized AI chips. Its market value briefly touched $3.53 trillion, exceeding Apple's $3.52 trillion, as the tech trio including Microsoft remains in a close race for top market capitalization. The surge in Nvidia's stock is fueled by the ongoing boom in generative AI and strong investment in AI capabilities across various companies.

title: Leading Supply-side Partners Including FreeWheel, Index Exchange, Magnite, Microsoft Monetize and PubMatic Integrate European Unified ID to Maintain Relevant Advertising
summary: Numerous supply-side partners, including FreeWheel, Index Exchange, Magnite, Microsoft Monetize, and PubMatic, are integrating the European Unified ID (EUID). This open-source identity solution, developed by The Trade Desk, aims to help marketers and publishers maintain relevant advertising in Europe, particularly as the industry moves beyond third-party cookies. EUID utilizes hashed email addresses and encrypted identifiers for a privacy-conscious, interoperable identity framework.

title: AI investment heat remains strong, with major asset management giants rushing to seize opportunities through etf deployment.
summary: AI investment continues to surge, prompting asset management companies to launch new ETFs focused on artificial intelligence. Over a third of the more than 20 AI-named ETFs were released this year, with their combined assets reaching $4.5 billion. Major tech companies like Microsoft and Amazon are engaging in an "AI arms race," significantly increasing capital expenditure in the sector, while venture capital funding for AI startups is also seeing a substantial boost.

title: COPT plans 1GW data center campus outside Des Moines, Iowa
summary: COPT Defense Properties is planning a 1GW data center campus near Des Moines, Iowa, after acquiring a 365-acre land parcel for $32 million. The campus is projected to include 15 buildings totaling 3.3 million square feet and will serve an existing Fortune 100 cloud computing tenant. This expansion leverages Des Moines' attractive land values, power availability, renewable energy access, and tax incentives.

title: Gail Evans Obituary - Charlotte, NC
summary: Gail Evans, a distinguished executive who held leadership roles at Eastman Kodak, Bank of America, HP, Microsoft, Mercer, and Disney, passed away on October 29, 2024, at the age of 65. Born in Rochester, NY, she diligently pursued education, earning degrees from Hobart William Smith, Nazareth University, and the University of Rochester. Evans was celebrated for her professional achievements and her dedication to her family, leaving behind a legacy of warmth, joy, and purpose.

title: Gartner names Dynamics 365 Customer Insights a Leader - Microsoft Dynamics 365 Blog
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner Magic Quadrant for B2B Marketing Automation Platforms for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Insights for the third consecutive year. This recognition highlights Microsoft's advancements in leveraging data, AI, and automation to deliver personalized customer experiences, particularly through innovations like Copilot and unified customer data across sales, marketing, and service functions. The platform aims to break down data silos and enable businesses to create more meaningful and scalable customer engagements globally.

title: 2 Small Modular Reactor Stocks Racing to Power AI Data Centers
summary: The growing demand for clean energy to power AI data centers has created a significant market for small modular reactor (SMR) technologies. NuScale Power and NANO Nuclear Energy are two companies at the forefront of this trend, developing SMRs and portable microreactors, respectively. While NuScale faces skepticism from short sellers, both companies are positioned to benefit from the increasing need for scalable and efficient nuclear energy solutions.

title: Microsoft names Jay Parikh as a member of the senior leadership team
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella announced that Jay Parikh is joining Microsoft's senior leadership team, reporting directly to Nadella. Parikh previously served as global head of engineering at Facebook (now Meta) and CEO of Lacework, bringing extensive experience in scaling technical teams and driving innovation. His role will focus on enhancing Microsoft's capabilities in security, quality, and AI innovation.

title: Dimensional US High Profitability ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the Dimensional US High Profitability ETF (DUHP), which focuses on large-cap US stocks selected for high profitability. The ETF, established on February 23, 2022, has over $10.7 billion in AUM and 158 holdings. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Eli Lilly, and Microsoft, with a section also listing recent changes in its portfolio.

title: Tech layoffs tracker 2024: Dropbox, Meta, Tidal cut jobs in October
summary: In October 2024, significant tech companies like Dropbox, Meta, and Tidal continued to announce job cuts, affecting thousands of employees. While these layoffs are devastating, overall numbers for tech layoffs in 2024 are down compared to 2023, with 142,532 employees laid off from 484 companies this year versus 264,220 from 1,193 companies last year. Dropbox, for instance, cut 20% of its workforce, citing softening demand and an overly complex organizational structure.

title: WEC Energy Group plans another $2.1 billion in renewables by 2029, hints at more data centers
summary: WEC Energy Group plans to make $28 billion in capital investments from 2025 to 2029, an increase largely driven by economic growth in southeastern Wisconsin and increased demand from data centers. The new plan includes an additional $2.1 billion for renewable electricity generation, bringing the total to $9.1 billion, with a significant increase in battery storage capacity. The company also anticipates further data center development inquiries beyond current Microsoft projects.

title: Board of Investment okays B66.9bn digital outlay
summary: Thailand's Board of Investment (BoI) has approved 66.9 billion baht in new investments for data centers and electronic businesses, aiming to establish the country as a regional digital innovation hub. Key approvals include projects from Quartz Computing (a Google subsidiary) and Digitalland Services, both targeting hyperscale data centers in Chon Buri. Additionally, the board approved a 6.1-billion-baht investment for Cheng Yi Technology to produce components for printed circuit boards.

title: Constellation Reports Third Quarter 2024 Results
summary: Constellation Energy Corporation reported strong Q3 2024 financial results, with GAAP Net Income of $3.82 per share and Adjusted Operating Earnings of $2.74 per share. The company raised its full-year 2024 Adjusted Operating Earnings guidance and announced a 20-year power purchase agreement with Microsoft to support the restart of the Crane Clean Energy Center. This agreement highlights Constellation's commitment to providing reliable, emissions-free energy, particularly for the growing data economy.

title: Tech Moves: Qualtrics taps Microsoft vet as CFO; F5 names innovation chief
summary: This article details several significant personnel changes in the Pacific Northwest tech community. Key moves include Rachita Sundar joining Qualtrics as CFO after a long tenure at Microsoft and HubSpot, and Kunal Anand transitioning from CTO to Chief Innovation Officer at F5. Additionally, former Microsoft CMO Chris Capossela has joined Lumen Technologies' board, and former First Mode executives have launched Sol Zero Group, a new venture focused on supporting companies solving important problems.

title: VMware Cloud Foundation Delivers a Private Cloud Platform to Accelerate Customers’ AI, Sovereignty and Security Outcomes
summary: Broadcom's VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) is enhancing its private cloud platform with new Advanced Services, ecosystem partnerships, and modernization programs to accelerate AI adoption, national digital sovereignty, and cybersecurity. These advancements aim to provide customers with a simple, secure, and cost-effective private cloud solution that supports generative AI application development, cyber resilience, and local data requirements. Key announcements include expanded data services, enhanced cyber recovery and security features, and new programs to facilitate private cloud transformation and certification.

title: UiPath to deliver AI-powered automation for Deluxe
