Additionally, the city is revitalizing its downtown area to attract businesses and improve walkability.

title: Apple approaches $4 trillion valuation as investors bet on AI momentum
summary: Apple is nearing a $4 trillion market valuation, driven by investor enthusiasm for its AI enhancements, which are expected to boost iPhone sales. The company has outpaced Nvidia and Microsoft in this race, with a roughly 16% share jump since November adding about $500 billion to its market cap. Analysts anticipate a rebound in iPhone revenue in 2025 as Apple integrates generative AI technology across its devices.

title: A Look Back at Automation Software Stocks’ Q3 Earnings: UiPath (NYSE:PATH) Vs The Rest Of The Pack
summary: This article reviews the Q3 earnings of several automation software companies, focusing on UiPath (PATH). UiPath reported an 8.8% year-on-year revenue increase to $354.7 million, exceeding analyst expectations, but its stock saw a slight decline. The report also highlights Microsoft (MSFT) as having the best Q3 with a 16% revenue increase, and Pegasystems (PEGA) as having the slowest Q3 despite a significant stock price increase post-earnings.

title: Hyperscale Data, Inc. (GPUS) Secures $25M for AI – Centric Data Center Expansion
summary: Hyperscale Data, Inc. (GPUS) has secured up to $25 million in funding from Ault & Company to expand its Michigan data center for high-performance computing, specifically to support AI solutions. The company is transitioning to focus exclusively on owning and operating AI-centric data centers, offering colocation, hosting, and AI-related software platforms. This funding will also address existing debt and provide working capital, emphasizing the growing investment in AI infrastructure.

title: Microsoft Corp (MSFT) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article provides a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Microsoft Corp (MSFT) to determine if its stock is undervalued. The analysis concludes that based on the DCF model, MSFT is currently overvalued, with a negative margin of safety. Financial data and calculations for forecasted free cash flows, terminal value, enterprise value, and equity value are presented.

title: Richard Parsons, prominent executive who held top posts at Time Warner and Citigroup, dies at age 76
summary: Richard Parsons, a highly respected Black executive who led Time Warner and Citigroup during challenging periods, has died at 76 from cancer. Known for his skilled negotiation and crisis management, Parsons helped restore Time Warner's standing after the AOL merger and steered Citigroup back to profit after the subprime mortgage crisis. His passing was mourned by organizations like Lazard and the NBA, highlighting his legacy as a transformational leader.

title: Evolving the device experience at Microsoft
summary: Microsoft is transitioning its employee device management from a traditional on-premises model to a modern, cloud-based approach using Microsoft Intune, Azure Active Directory, and co-management strategies. This shift supports hybrid work, enables employees to be productive from anywhere on various devices (Windows, Android, iOS, macOS), and enhances security through Zero Trust principles and verified device health. The migration addresses challenges by using Hybrid Azure AD joined devices as a bridge and standardizing management practices.

title: Bitwise proposes ETF for companies holding over 1000 Bitcoin
summary: Bitwise has proposed a new Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF), the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF, which aims to invest in publicly traded companies holding at least 1,000 Bitcoin. The ETF will weight holdings based on the market value of the companies' Bitcoin reserves, rather than their overall market capitalization. This initiative reflects the growing integration of cryptocurrency into traditional financial markets as more public companies acquire Bitcoin to boost stock valuations.

title: Gaxos.ai Inc. (GXAI) Launches Meshy 4 Update to Revolutionize 3D Modeling Workflows for Game Developers
summary: Gaxos.ai Inc. (GXAI) has released Meshy 4, an update to its Gaxos Labs platform, designed to enhance 3D modeling for game developers using AI-driven generative geometry. This update allows for dynamic mesh topology, polycount management, and improved geometry quality, aiming to accelerate asset creation. The generative AI market is projected to reach $1.3 trillion by 2032, driven by demand for AI products and infrastructure, with significant impact on various industries including gaming.

title: Jim Cramer on Apple Inc. (AAPL): ‘It Was The Dumb Money That Was Getting Out, Because Warren Buffett Or One Of His Assistants Made Us Nervous’
summary: Jim Cramer discussed Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) within the context of AI stocks and overall market performance in 2024. He highlighted that "dumb money" sold off Apple shares due to investor nervousness, potentially influenced by Warren Buffett, despite strong underlying sales. Cramer also offered trading advice for 2025, emphasizing the importance of not getting intoxicated by gains, having the fortitude to hold stocks during tough times, and carefully staging buy orders.

title: Costco defends DEI program as other major retailers drop controversial diversity push
summary: Costco's board of directors is urging shareholders to vote against a proposal to eliminate its Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) program, defending its practices as beneficial for employees, members, and suppliers. This comes as other major retailers have scaled back similar initiatives following a Supreme Court ruling and warnings from state attorneys general. The National Center for Public Policy Research is challenging Costco's DEI program, alleging it involves discriminatory practices and rebrands rather than eliminates problematic policies.

title: Growth ETFs Shaken Up as Tech Takes on Value Blush
summary: Growth ETFs like IVW are undergoing significant rebalancing as major tech companies such as Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Adobe are no longer considered pure growth stocks by S&P Global. This reclassification means investors will see a smaller allocation to these tech giants in growth ETFs and a larger presence of financial firms like Berkshire Hathaway and JPMorgan Chase, which are now being given a "growth blush". The rebalancing also shifts tech's share in the S&P Value Index significantly, with these companies now having their weightings divided between growth and value characteristics based on S&P's multifactor analysis.

title: 10 Best Performing Dividend ETFs In 2024
summary: This article identifies the 10 best-performing dividend ETFs in 2024, noting the significant growth in the global ETF market and increased investor interest, particularly among US retail investors. It details the methodology for ranking these ETFs, focusing on year-to-date share price performance and top holdings, and provides in-depth analysis for the top two funds. The article also discusses the impact of Federal Reserve rate-cutting cycles on dividend ETF inflows and cautions investors about the risks associated with high-yield ETFs.

title: Intermedia Unite Review
summary: Intermedia Unite offers comprehensive voice administration features, chat, file syncing, and video calls, making it a strong unified communications platform. Despite a slightly less straightforward contact import process and a Chrome-only web app, its competitive pricing, AI Assistant, and excellent call routing earned it an Editors' Choice award. The service performs reliably, providing crisp phone calls, clear video, and an intuitive interface across desktop, web, and mobile platforms.

title: Assessing Five9 Stock's 2025 Comeback Potential After 48.3% Drop in 2024
summary: Five9 (FIVN) experienced a significant stock decline of 48.3% in 2024, but its strong Q3 financial results, strategic focus on AI, and robust enterprise customer growth suggest a potential comeback in 2025. The company's innovative AI capabilities, strategic partnerships with companies like Microsoft, and positive outlook for 2025 positions it as an attractive investment opportunity despite recent market headwinds.

title: Management That Matters: 2024’s Best U.S. Companies Unveiled
summary: The 2024 Drucker Institute Company Rankings identify the 30 best-managed U.S. companies based on corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, financial strength, social responsibility, innovation, and employee engagement. Apple secured the top spot as the best-managed company with an effectiveness score of 92.1, surpassing Microsoft from its 2023 position. Nvidia and Microsoft followed, with Intel and MasterCard rounding out the top five, demonstrating the importance of innovation and financial strength for high effectiveness scores.

title: Wall Street declares war on the Associated Press
summary: Gannett and Reuters have partnered to provide news content, challenging the long-standing cooperative model of the Associated Press. This move is seen as an attempt to financialize journalism further by targeting AP's customers (local, regional, and national publishers) with a more commercially focused service, potentially undermining AP's non-profit structure and journalistic integrity. The article suggests this strategy is a continuation of the trend to consolidate and cheapen news production, threatening the diversity and quality of local journalism.

title: Tech Stocks Are Still Pricey. Dip Buyers Should Look Here Instead.
summary: Investors seeking "buy-the-dip" opportunities might be advised to look beyond tech stocks, which remain expensive despite recent market fluctuations. Instead, the article suggests considering other sectors, highlighting that the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund has seen a significant drop from its recent high. This implies that industrial stocks may present a more attractive dip-buying opportunity than pricey tech stocks.

title: Cybersecurity pioneer, tech CEO Amit Yoran dies 'unexpectedly' amid cancer battle
summary: Amit Yoran, the CEO of Tenable Holdings and a prominent figure in cybersecurity, has died unexpectedly at 54 after a battle with cancer. Yoran was a visionary leader, having also founded the U.S. Computer Emergency Readiness Team (US-CERT) program at the Department of Homeland Security. His leadership transformed Tenable into a global leader in exposure management, and his contributions to the cybersecurity field are described as indelible.

title: Intel Extends Leadership in AI PCs and Edge Computing at CES 2025
summary: At CES 2025, Intel unveiled new Intel Core Ultra (Series 2) processors, including the 200V series with Intel vPro for businesses, and 200HX and H series for creators and gamers, all featuring cutting-edge AI enhancements and performance improvements. Intel also introduced a new suite of processors for edge computing, showcasing significant performance gains in AI workloads compared to previous generations and competitors. The availability of these new systems is set to begin in January and February 2025, with further releases planned for the first half of the year.

title: Qualcomm’s New Chips Will Bring AI to $600 Personal Computers
summary: Qualcomm Inc. introduced its new Snapdragon X Platform chips at CES 2025, designed to power AI-capable personal computers priced as low as $600. These chips feature an 8-core Oryon central processor, a graphics component, and a dedicated AI chip, and will run Microsoft Corp.'s Copilot+ software. Major PC manufacturers like Dell Technologies Inc. and Lenovo Group Ltd. are expected to release laptops using this new technology in early 2025.

title: Ambiq and ThinkAR Disrupt the AR Glasses Industry with the AiLens
summary: Ambiq and ThinkAR have partnered to launch AiLens, a lightweight pair of smart AR glasses designed for everyday wear. These glasses weigh only 37 grams, offer over 10 hours of battery life, and are powered by Ambiq's ultra-efficient Apollo4 System-on-Chip (SoC) and ThinkAR's advanced AI capabilities. AiLens aims to redefine the AR glasses market with features like an AI-powered personal assistant, real-time language translation, and seamless integration with various platforms.

title: EWC, Canada ETFs Rise Amid Trudeau Resignation
summary: The iShares MSCI Canada ETF (EWC) rose following the announcement of Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's resignation, citing a need for new leadership. The article also discusses why Canadian ETFs have historically lagged behind U.S. equity ETFs like SPY, attributing it to factors such as sector concentration, innovation, market size, currency effects, venture capital, and global influence.

title: United States AI in Agriculture Research Report 2024:
summary: The U.S. AI in Agriculture market, valued at USD 351.09 Million in 2023, is projected to reach USD 705.74 Million by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 12.30%. This expansion is driven by the increasing demand for food security and the adoption of AI technologies like predictive analytics and AI-as-a-Service for enhanced productivity and sustainability in farming. Key players in this market include IBM, Granular, Microsoft, Deere & Co, and Valmont Industries.

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" list of stocks, initially identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the U.S. stock market's performance. It suggests that these stocks, including giants like Nvidia, are poised for continued revenue growth in 2025. The piece also mentions other groups of dominant stocks, such as "BATMMAAN," which includes Broadcom and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: AI in 2025 – Intuit Tech Leaders See Beyond the Hype
summary: Intuit tech leaders anticipate that 2025 will be a pivotal year for AI, marked by both excitement and skepticism. They emphasize that customer control, trust, and practicality will drive the success of AI-powered experiences, including agentic AI, code-free solutions, and enhanced security measures. The article highlights key areas such as balancing autonomous efficiency with user authority, the importance of AI governance, the convergence of fintech and AI, and the emerging threat of deepfakes.

title: Lumen’s Finke Charts Network, AI Intersections
summary: Josh Finke, Lumen's Senior Vice President, Public Sector, discusses how Lumen Technologies is supporting Federal government agencies with network modernization and AI integration. The company is focusing on simplifying technology consumption, enhancing network security and reliability, and building an "AI fabric" to support data-intensive AI workloads. Finke also shared Lumen's perspective on Federal AI policy, emphasizing a multi-phase approach to AI adoption for measurable outcomes.

title: Increased Fund Ownership Could Boost Microsoft’s Stock
summary: Increased investment fund ownership in Microsoft could drive a bullish trend for its stock in 2025. Despite underperforming the S&P 500 in 2024, strong fund buying and anticipated positive earnings, fueled by AI investments, suggest potential gains. Analysts expect continued revenue growth and a favorable market environment for tech giants.

title: Fundsmith Equity Underperforms for the Fourth Year | Morningstar UK
summary: Fundsmith Equity, the UK's largest fund, underperformed global markets for the fourth consecutive year in 2024, despite an 8.9% growth rate compared to the MSCI World Index's 20.8%. Fund manager Terry Smith attributed this to extreme market concentration in a few tech stocks and made several portfolio changes, including selling Diageo, McCormick, and Apple, and buying Atlas Copco and Texas Instruments. The article also discusses Smith's views on passive funds' role in AI growth and notes that despite recent underperformance, Fundsmith Equity still boasts strong long-term returns since its inception.

title: Board Games Market Set for Dynamic Boom as Key Players Hasbro • Mattel • Asmodee • Ravensburger
summary: Coherent Market Insights Pvt. Ltd has released a new report titled "Board Games Market 2025-2032," providing an in-depth analysis of market trends, growth drivers, challenges, and opportunities. The report identifies key players such as Hasbro, Mattel, Asmodee, and Ravensburger, and segments the market by various board game types. It aims to help businesses forecast profitability and make strategic decisions by offering insights into market dynamics, regional outlook, and competitor landscapes.

title: Efficient control with Microsoft Fabric optimizes hydrogen production at OGE
summary: OGE, a gas grid operator, is using Microsoft Fabric to optimize green hydrogen production at its Krummhörn compressor station. This platform creates a digital twin of the production process, integrating real-time electricity prices and internal data to calculate the cost of hydrogen and optimize production plans. This has led to a significant reduction in manufacturing costs, demonstrating a crucial step towards efficient and sustainable energy transitions in Germany.

title: Lead Generation World 2025: Key Takeaways and Trends Shaping the Future
summary: This article summarizes key takeaways from the Lead Generation World 2025 conference, highlighting innovation, compliance, and long-term growth as critical pillars for success. It discusses trends in navigating the changing landscape, the evolution of affiliate marketing towards data-driven brand building, and strategies for success in mortgage lead generation. The author emphasizes adaptability, embracing new tools, and creating value as essential for thriving in the lead generation space.

title: Where Brazil's big financial institutions are investing in tech
summary: Brazilian banks are significantly increasing their technology investments to enhance customer service, boost efficiency, and compete with fintechs. Key areas of focus include database integration, cloud adoption, and especially artificial intelligence solutions. Major banks like Itaú Unibanco, Bradesco, Santander Brasil, and Banco do Brasil are allocating substantial resources to AI, cloud migration, and developing "superapps" to personalize services and streamline operations.

title: Why Have Dividend Stock Strategies Been Underperforming?
summary: Dividend stock strategies significantly underperformed the broader market in 2024, with the Morningstar Dividend Composite Index rising 15.7% compared to the Morningstar US Market Index's 24.1% gain. This underperformance is primarily attributed to the low exposure to technology stocks within dividend strategies, as tech and communication services were the best-performing sectors. Additionally, many top market contributors like Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon have low or no dividend payouts, favoring share repurchases as a way to return cash to shareholders, which dividend indexes typically do not capture.

title: Bold collaboration is key for business leaders to make an impact on sustainability
summary: The article emphasizes that bold collaboration between the private and public sectors is crucial for addressing global sustainability challenges, despite economic headwinds. It highlights three core principles for successful partnerships, stressing that companies can mitigate risks and drive impactful solutions through joint efforts, supported by government resources and capital. Examples like Eastman's advanced recycling facilities and Microsoft's partnership to restart a nuclear reactor demonstrate how such collaborations can de-risk investments and yield significant environmental and economic benefits.

title: The Top Thirty Companies That Diamond Owes Money To
summary: Diamond Comic Distributors has declared bankruptcy, owing over $31.7 million to its top thirty non-secured creditors. Penguin Random House is the largest creditor with a $9.2 million debt, demonstrating the impact of its subdistribution deals for Marvel, Dark Horse, and IDW. The list also reveals significant debts to various toy companies like Bandai and Hasbro, and graphic novel/comic publishers such as Simon & Schuster and Titan, indicating a broad financial fallout across the industry.

title: AI revolution: Impact Series
summary: The article discusses the significant infrastructure demands of the AI revolution, highlighting the strain on resources like power, land, water, and materials due to increasing AI adoption and model training. It emphasizes the need for responsible and sustainable development of AI, examining energy consumption by data centers and the growing demand for "firm" and "dispatchable" power sources like nuclear and geothermal energy. Authors Christian Keller, William Thompson, and Akash Utsav contributed to this Impact Series report from Barclays Research, stressing the global challenge of balancing economic opportunities with environmental sustainability in AI development.

title: A look ahead: Experts weigh in on 2025 cybersecurity trends
summary: Radware's security experts predict key cybersecurity trends for 2025, highlighting the increasing role of AI in both crafting exploits and developing countermeasures. The article discusses threats like AI-enhanced attacks on ISPs, the need for unified security platforms, the emergence of "cyber lawfare," the growing importance of threat intelligence against hacktivists, and the rise of API attacks. It also emphasizes how AI will be crucial in improving API security, urging organizations to invest in AI-driven platforms and robust threat intelligence.

title: Biden Administration Releases Executive Order on AI Infrastructure
summary: The Biden Administration issued an Executive Order on AI Infrastructure on January 14, 2025, aimed at advancing U.S. leadership in AI, preventing reliance on foreign infrastructure, and promoting clean energy for AI operations. The EO directs federal agencies to lease federal land for private-sector construction of "frontier AI infrastructure," including data centers and clean energy facilities, by specified timelines. It also outlines requirements for expedited permitting and upgrades to electricity grid infrastructure to support these AI developments.

title: CHAT: Generative AI Stocks Still Look Relatively Attractive
summary: The Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) has seen a significant rise of +13.60% since October 2024, outperforming the S&P 500. The ETF maintains a diversified portfolio with key holdings in NVIDIA, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms. While previously rated a "Strong Buy," the rating has been revised to "Buy" due to macroeconomic uncertainties, even as it appears between fair value and "slightly undervalued."

title: Microsoft and OpenAI evolve partnership to drive the next phase of AI
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI are continuing their strategic partnership, focusing on future AI developments and Stargate. The agreement, valid through 2030, maintains Microsoft's access to OpenAI's IP, revenue sharing, and exclusivity on OpenAI's APIs. Microsoft remains a major investor, while OpenAI has made a new, significant Azure commitment, and the exclusivity on new capacity has shifted to a right of first refusal for Microsoft.

title: Top 100 Healthcare IT Consultant Project Areas in Highest Demand: Leading Advisory Firms Driving the Future of Healthcare Innovation
summary: Black Book Research has identified the top 100 healthcare IT advisory services projected to be in highest demand for 2025, based on over 5,100 engagements and insights from 900 healthcare sector respondents. The analysis, which evaluated firms on six Key Performance Indicators including business impact, regulatory compliance, and client satisfaction, highlights critical project areas like cybersecurity, EHR optimization, and AI strategies. Leading advisory firms such as Accenture, Deloitte, PwC, and Huron Consulting Group are recognized for driving innovation and delivering significant impact across these key domains.

title: Should Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: The Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) is a passively managed large-cap value ETF with $719.98 million in assets, sponsored by Invesco. Launched in 2005, it focuses on dividend-paying companies that have increased dividends for over 10 consecutive years, with top holdings including Broadcom, Apple, and Microsoft. PFM has a Zacks ETF Rank of 3 (Hold) and an expense ratio of 0.52%, and its performance has shown year-to-date gains of 2.90% and a 19.34% increase over the last year as of January 22, 2025.

title: IUSV: A Cost-Effective Value ETF With Strong Downside Protection And Growth Potential
summary: The iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV) offers cost-efficient value investing with a portfolio of 700 U.S. large-cap and mid-cap value stocks and a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its significant allocation to large-cap stocks and a 23% exposure to the technology sector, including Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, contribute to strong downside protection and growth potential. IUSV has demonstrated superior performance, outperforming its peer IWD with a 167.2% total return over the past decade and 597.5% since inception.

title: 7 Best Content Distribution Platforms to Grow Your Reach
summary: This article identifies and compares seven top content distribution platforms for 2026, including Buffer, Bynder, Distribution.ai, Hootsuite, Mailchimp, RELAYTO, and StoryChief. It emphasizes the critical role of content distribution in ensuring content is seen by a wide audience, especially with evolving search engine algorithms and AI. The article also provides guidance on how to choose a suitable platform, highlighting factors like UI, usability, integrations, and cost, alongside advice for building an effective content distribution strategy.

title: Renewables developer NextEra is investing in gas generation
summary: NextEra, a major renewables developer, is increasing its investment in gas generation through a partnership with GE Vernova, while simultaneously reaffirming its commitment to clean energy projects. The company plans to use gas generation for capacity to meet rising demand, especially from data centers and industry, complementing its rapidly expanding renewables portfolio. NextEra is also exploring restarting a previously shuttered nuclear plant, Duane Arnold, to provide firm clean power.

title: Marc Benioff says that from now on CEOs will no longer lead all-human workforces—enter the new era of AI coworkers
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff stated at the World Economic Forum that current CEOs will be the last to lead exclusively human workforces, predicting a future where AI agents act as "digital workers." He emphasized that AI will support human employees to achieve higher productivity rather than replace them, a sentiment echoed by Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei who foresees AI surpassing human capabilities by 2026 or 2027. While employers like Salesforce are readily adopting AI agents, the concept faces resistance from workers concerned about job displacement, as seen in the backlash received by HR company Lattice.

title: Exclusive: White House in talks to have Oracle and U.S. investors take over TikTok
summary: The White House is negotiating a deal where Oracle and U.S. investors would take majority control of TikTok's global operations, with Oracle overseeing the app's algorithm, data collection, and software updates. This plan aims to address concerns about the app's Chinese ownership by minimizing ByteDance's stake and ensuring American oversight. This negotiation follows a congressional mandate for TikTok to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a nationwide ban.

title: Microsoft plans 1 million-square-foot data center in Kenosha
summary: Microsoft is planning a massive 1 million-square-foot data center in Kenosha County, Wisconsin, building on its existing presence in the region. The tech giant acquired 240 acres for this project, adding another major data center development to Southeast Wisconsin. This expansion comes as Microsoft continues to develop a data center complex in nearby Mount Pleasant, Racine County.

title: XLK vs. QQQ: Which Tech ETF Should You Buy for Your Portfolio?
summary: This article analyzes two popular tech-heavy ETFs, XLK and QQQ, comparing their long-term performance, expense ratios, dividends, and portfolio allocations. While both have outperformed the S&P 500, XLK focuses purely on tech, making it more concentrated in top tech stocks like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. QQQ offers broader diversification by including communication services and consumer cyclicals, which may appeal to investors seeking less concentration in the top three tech giants.

title: RBC’s plans to marry large language and transaction models
summary: RBC is integrating its in-house AI models, trained on vast financial data, with large language models developed by a local rival to Microsoft and Google. This initiative aims to address a paradox in banking where generative AI excels more in language tasks than complex numerical calculations, despite the technology's potential to revolutionize financial operations. The bank is exploring how these combined AI capabilities can be applied to both internal processes and customer-facing services.

title: FY25 Q2 - Press Releases - Investor Relations
summary: Microsoft announced strong second-quarter fiscal year 2025 results, driven by significant growth in Microsoft Cloud and AI. The company reported $69.6 billion in revenue, a 12% increase, and a 17% increase in operating income to $31.7 billion. Microsoft's AI business alone surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $13 billion, marking a 175% year-over-year increase.

title: Reimagine Enterprise Productivity with Wipro’s Custom Approach for Microsoft 365 Copilot
summary: Wipro, a long-time Microsoft partner, offers a custom approach to integrating Microsoft 365 Copilot, tailoring the AI companion to specific user profiles and organizational needs. This customization aims to boost not just productivity but also creativity, driving better business outcomes across various roles like sales, development, and management. Wipro leverages its expertise, AI Lab, and ongoing support to maximize the benefits of Microsoft 365 Copilot, transforming how enterprises operate and empowering the workforce.

title: No Jitter Roll: Kore.ai Adds AI Agents, Intermedia Debuts Contact Center for Teams
summary: This "No Jitter Roll" roundup details product news in enterprise communication. It highlights Kore.ai's new AI for Process platform with custom AI agents and Intermedia's Contact Center for Microsoft Teams. Other updates include Twilio's RCS features, RingCentral's Studio and AI Clips, and NUWAVE's Teleport migration product, alongside reports on AI agents, business service automation, and contact center technology dissatisfaction.

title: Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, UPS, IBM, Nvidia, Comcast, Las Vegas Sands, American, and More Movers
summary: The article reports on the market movers of the day, including major tech and airline companies. Stocks experienced volatility but ultimately closed higher on Thursday, influenced by mixed tech earnings reports and comments regarding tariffs.

title: For Georgia Power, there’s no sign of load growth slowing down
summary: Georgia Power's latest Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) reveals a significant acceleration in load growth, driven primarily by new economic development projects and data centers. The utility now anticipates 8.2 gigawatts of growth by winter 2030-31, and up to 9,400 MW by winter 2034-35, a substantial increase from previous forecasts. To meet this demand, Georgia Power plans to delay coal plant retirements, deploy more renewables and battery storage, upgrade existing gas and nuclear plants, and build out its transmission infrastructure.

title: Microsoft, CoreWeave Join Princeton U. & NJ As Founding Partners In AI Hub
summary: Microsoft and CoreWeave are partnering with Princeton University and the state of New Jersey to establish the NJ AI Hub. This initiative, aiming to invest over $72 million, will focus on fostering AI innovation, research and development, and workforce training. The Hub will be located in West Windsor and seeks to position New Jersey as a leader in artificial intelligence technology.

title: Technology in 2025: The Cycle Rolls On
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a robust technology sector in 2025, driven by AI momentum and a broadening of opportunities beyond the "Magnificent 7." The article highlights investment potential in hyperscalers, software companies benefiting from AI, and ASIC semiconductor manufacturers, emphasizing the importance of active management to identify growth areas.

title: Lean Into Dividend-Paying Firms With Dividend Growth ETF TDVG
summary: Amid market uncertainty and potential rate cut delays, investors may benefit from focusing on durable, dividend-paying firms. The T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth ETF (TDVG) actively invests in large-cap companies with strong dividend and earnings growth, healthy balance sheets, and competitive advantages. This research-driven ETF offers a strategic option for those seeking both dividend income and equity upside.

title: Telefônica Brasil enhances its AI-powered call center assistant with Azure
summary: Telefônica Brasil has significantly enhanced its AI-powered call center assistant, Aura, by implementing Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service and other Azure AI tools. This initiative, named I.Ajuda, aims to optimize and scale call center operations, providing personalized service to its 115 million network users in Brazil. The integration of Azure services has enabled the company to reduce average handling time for individual customers by 9% and improve overall customer satisfaction through AI-powered insights for its 23,000 customer service agents. Telefônica Brasil plans to further develop speech analytics and voice transcription capabilities to continuously improve its service quality.

title: February 2025 Android Updates by Google; Severe Flaws in VMware & Microsoft’s Azure AI
summary: Google has released its February 2025 Android security updates, patching 46 vulnerabilities, including the actively exploited Kernel flaw CVE-2024-53104 and a critical Qualcomm WLAN vulnerability. Concurrently, VMware addressed multiple vulnerabilities in its Aria Operations products, with CVE-2025-22218 being the most severe information disclosure flaw. Microsoft also issued patches for critical vulnerabilities in Azure AI Face Service (CVE-2025-21415, an authentication bypass) and Microsoft Account (CVE-2025-21396, a privilege escalation flaw), urging users and organizations to apply updates promptly to mitigate risks.

title: Qualcomm and Arm shatter Wall Street’s targets, but investors aren’t too happy
summary: Qualcomm and Arm both reported strong quarterly earnings and optimistic guidance that surpassed Wall Street's expectations, yet their stock prices fell in after-hours trading. Qualcomm saw record revenue driven by strong smartphone, automotive, and IoT chip sales, while Arm's revenue climbed due to the rapid adoption of its Armv9 architecture. Despite impressive growth and positive outlooks from both companies, investors appeared dissatisfied, with concerns about Qualcomm's licensing business and Arm's flat licensing revenue from its more profitable platform.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Increases Stake in Tyler Technologies Inc
summary: BlackRock, Inc. increased its stake in Tyler Technologies Inc by acquiring an additional 713,164 shares, bringing its total holdings to over 4 million shares. This transaction, valued at $576.64 per share, reflects BlackRock's continued interest in the software industry and Tyler Technologies' strong growth potential, despite its current overvaluation according to GuruFocus. Other notable investors also hold positions in Tyler Technologies, highlighting broader market confidence in the government software solutions provider.

title: Intermedia COO On Kicking Off 'Record Year' In Sales And The Massive Microsoft Teams Opportunity For Partners
summary: Intermedia COO Jonathan McCormick describes 2025 as another record year for UCaaS product sales, driven by strategic initiatives in 2024. The company is capitalizing on a significant opportunity to integrate its advanced voice capabilities directly into Microsoft Teams, targeting businesses not using Microsoft's PBX for voice. Intermedia is also actively incorporating AI into its platform to offer practical use cases that boost efficiency and improve customer experience for partners and their clients.

title: Investigation Alert: Nano Nuclear Energy, New Fortress Energy, Domino’s Pizza and GitLab Inc - Johnson Fistel, LLP Encourages Long-Term Investors to Reach Out for More Information
summary: Johnson Fistel, LLP is investigating potential violations of federal and state laws by officers of Nano Nuclear Energy, New Fortress Energy, Domino’s Pizza, and GitLab Inc. The investigations stem from recent class action lawsuits alleging misleading statements and failures to disclose material facts to investors. Long-term shareholders of these companies are encouraged to contact Johnson Fistel, LLP for more information regarding these potential claims.

title: HPE begins notifying data breach victims after Russian government hack
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has started informing individuals impacted by a 2023 cyberattack, which the company attributes to Russian government hackers. The breach involved the theft of personal information, including Social Security numbers, driver’s license data, and credit card numbers, from HPE's email and SharePoint systems hosted by Microsoft. HPE confirmed the incident in January 2024, identifying the threat actor as "Midnight Blizzard" and noting that the stolen data primarily affected employees in cybersecurity and business teams, along with a small number of customers.

title: InCharge Energy Leadership Update

Despite challenges such as the high cost of game development, the industry is thriving due to evolving consumer preferences and the versatility of gaming experiences.

The utility wants to implement measures like 15-year power contracts, exit fees, and minimum capacity payments for large data centers to protect existing customers from stranded costs if these facilities consume less power than expected or leave the state. Watchdogs and state regulators are reviewing the proposal to ensure it adequately protects ratepayers, while the Data Center Coalition views the terms as discriminatory.

Microsoft is offloading some of OpenAI's substantial data center requirements to other partners like SoftBank and Oracle, especially after the "Stargate Project" announcement, while still maintaining its own CapEx targets. This move ensures that the immense capital expenditures for AI infrastructure are conserved and merely redistributed among various partners.

The findings highlight Black Basta's preference for known vulnerabilities with publicly available exploits and their strategy of targeting high-revenue companies in legal, financial, healthcare, and industrial sectors. This underscores the critical importance of prompt patching and mitigations for widely used applications and devices.

The lawsuit, Maxwell v. Amazon.com, Inc., draws parallels to prior SDK class actions, but introduces MHMDA's broad private right of action for violations related to "consumer health data," which includes precise location information. Businesses are advised to be mindful of SDK use as more states enact privacy legislation and similar lawsuits are expected.

The strategy can enhance tax efficiency and align portfolios with personal values but requires significant oversight and may not always outperform traditional index funds.

(COIN), and cryptocurrency ETFs (IBIT, ETHA), with an even-weighting methodology and quarterly rebalancing.

summary: Microsoft has issued a response to an unsolicited "mini-tender" offer from TRC Capital Investment Corporation to purchase up to 300,000 shares of Microsoft's common stock at $391.00 per share. Microsoft states it is not affiliated with TRC, does not endorse the offer, and remains neutral, advising shareholders to exercise caution and consult with financial advisors. The company also highlights that mini-tender offers are not subject to the same SEC disclosure and procedural requirements as larger tender offers.

title: F5 CEO: AI Is Sparking Cloud Repatriation Movement
summary: F5 CEO François Locoh-Donou states that enterprises are increasingly repatriating application workloads from the cloud due to rising costs and data sovereignty concerns, with AI accelerating this trend. An IDC report suggests 80% of organizations will see some level of repatriation within the next year, indicating a shift towards hybrid and multi-cloud strategies rather than full cloud adoption. This movement creates significant opportunities for partners to help businesses manage and secure applications across diverse environments efficiently.

title: Taboola Reaches 10 Years in Serving Advertising on Microsoft Properties Including MSN, Edge Browsers and Windows Experiences; Taboola Now Serves Advertising Across Microsoft Productivity Suite, Including Outlook Mail and Office 365
summary: Taboola has announced a ten-year milestone in its partnership with Microsoft, serving advertising on properties like MSN, Microsoft Edge, and Windows experiences. The collaboration has expanded to include advertising within Microsoft's productivity suite, such as Outlook Mail and Office 365. This expansion offers advertisers access to premium destinations and a broader reach across Microsoft's nearly 600 million daily active users.

title: Adobe and Microsoft Empower Marketers with AI Agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot
summary: Adobe and Microsoft are expanding their collaboration by introducing AI agents within Microsoft 365 Copilot to empower marketers. This includes the private preview of Adobe Marketing Agent and the development of Adobe Express Agent, allowing users to leverage Adobe's creative and marketing capabilities directly within Microsoft applications like Teams, PowerPoint, and Word. These agents aim to enhance content creation, audience targeting, actionable insights, and cross-team collaboration for marketing professionals.

title: Longtime Microsoft HR chief Kathleen Hogan shifts to corporate strategy role for CEO Satya Nadella
summary: Microsoft's longtime Chief People Officer, Kathleen Hogan, has been appointed to the new role of Executive Vice President of Strategy and Transformation, reporting directly to CEO Satya Nadella. In this capacity, Hogan will focus on the company's overarching corporate strategy and continuous transformation, particularly in the AI era. Amy Coleman will succeed Hogan as Chief People Officer.

title: Microsoft announces new HR executive, company veteran Amy Coleman
summary: Microsoft has appointed company veteran Amy Coleman as its new executive vice president and chief people officer, succeeding Kathleen Hogan. Coleman, who joined Microsoft in 1996, will report directly to CEO Satya Nadella and lead HR for the software giant, which employs 228,000 people. Hogan will transition to a newly established Office of Strategy and Transformation.

title: 4 tech giants look cheap ahead of earnings
summary: Ahead of their earnings reports, four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Microsoft—are considered undervalued. Analysts see Apple as undervalued despite recent revenue guidance adjustments, Amazon as continuing its impressive growth, Microsoft showing broad-based strength, and Facebook maintaining its competitive advantages in digital advertising despite recent controversies.

title: US tech sector now decently undervalued
summary: The US technology sector, as of March 26, is now considered decently undervalued, shifting from being modestly overvalued a quarter ago. The median tech stock is 13% undervalued on a price-to-fair-value basis, with hardware, software, and semiconductors also below fair value estimates. Wide-moat names like Microsoft, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and Tyler Technologies, along with cybersecurity firms like Palo Alto Networks and Okta, are highlighted as attractive long-term investments due to their recurring revenue models and sticky customer bases.

title: Nvidia-Backed AI Startup CoreWeave Sets IPO At $2.7B; OpenAI Will Get $350M In Stock
summary: Nvidia-backed AI startup CoreWeave is preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) aiming to raise up to $2.7 billion, which would result in a $26 billion market valuation. OpenAI is set to receive $350 million in CoreWeave stock, and Microsoft, a major customer, will account for less than 50% of future committed contract revenues after the IPO. CoreWeave also announced new compute instances featuring Nvidia's upcoming RTX PRO 6000 GPUs and a new Norway data center, expanding its European presence.

title: SCHG Vs. VUG: Both Are An Equally Great Way To Capture The Tech Rebound
summary: The article discusses how SCHG and VUG are strong ETF options for gaining exposure to the tech sector, especially given the expected growth in AI spending. While both are considered excellent for long-term investors bullish on technology, SCHG has historically shown a slight edge over VUG due to its top holdings. Both ETFs are characterized by low expense ratios and strong dividend growth, making them suitable for aligning portfolios with future tech advancements and higher valuations.

title: IT Infrastructure Services Market Size, Growth & Trends Chart by 2033
summary: The global IT infrastructure services market, valued at USD 104.35 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 236.18 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.5%. This growth is driven by digital transformation, cloud adoption, increasing cybersecurity threats, and government initiatives. North America currently dominates the market, with Asia-Pacific showing the fastest growth.

title: Equities in 2025: Embracing a Broader Landscape
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a broadening landscape of equity market opportunities for active investors in 2025, moving beyond the dominance of US mega-cap tech. The report highlights potential in small-cap companies, developed markets like Japan and Europe, and emerging markets such as India and specific sectors in China. Investors are encouraged to pursue diversified, active strategies and explore long-term themes like reshoring and increased energy demand.

title: Industrial AI in action: How AI agents and digital threads will transform the manufacturing industries
summary: This article discusses how AI agents and digital threads are set to revolutionize the manufacturing industry, transforming fragmented systems into dynamic networks. Microsoft, alongside partners, is showcasing these innovations at Hannover Messe 2025, highlighting AI agents that empower frontline workers, advance digital engineering, and prepare factory edges for AI, ultimately making AI-powered digital threads a reality for manufacturers. The advancements are aimed at boosting efficiency, accelerating innovation, and enhancing decision-making across various aspects of manufacturing operations.

title: What a study at consumer giant P&G says about AI's potential impact on teamwork
summary: A new study conducted at Procter & Gamble reveals that AI significantly enhances both individual and team performance, particularly in brainstorming new product ideas. While AI-assisted individuals outperformed unassisted human teams, AI-assisted teams generated a higher number of "exceptional" ideas. The study suggests that AI should be viewed as a complement to human labor, fostering collaboration and encouraging more holistic problem-solving rather than merely a tool for automation and headcount reduction.

title: Microsoft Expands AI Collaboration with Korea, Empowering Innovation and Growth Across Key Industries
summary: Microsoft is significantly expanding its AI collaboration with Korea, showcasing key industry innovations and accelerating AI transformation. The company unveiled new deep reasoning agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot, making their global debut in Korea, and reaffirmed its commitment to fostering innovation through partnerships with major Korean companies like KT Corporation, LG Electronics, and Hanwha QCells. Microsoft also emphasized responsible AI principles and launched an AI Skills Initiative to enhance AI literacy across various sectors in Korea.

title: Microsoft named a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Customer Relationship Management, Q1 2025
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Customer Relationship Management, Q1 2025, for its Dynamics 365 platform. The platform is lauded for its unified approach to sales, marketing, and service, driven by generative AI advancements like Copilot and AI agents. This recognition highlights Microsoft's commitment to providing an integrated, secure, and responsible AI-powered CRM solution that helps organizations meet evolving customer expectations and improve operational efficiency.

title: Accelerating Japan's growth with AI - Microsoft Stories Asia
summary: Microsoft is making a significant $2.9 billion investment in Japan to expand its cloud infrastructure and boost AI capabilities by 2025, including the introduction of Azure High Performance Computing with NVIDIA GPUs. This initiative aims to accelerate Japan's AI transformation across various industries, enhance cybersecurity, and equip millions with AI skills through new programs like "CyberSmart AI" and partnerships with academic institutions. The investment underscores Microsoft's commitment to supporting Japan's economic growth and innovation in the AI era.

title: CoreWeave prices IPO lower than expected, and its stock still sags on its debut before recovering
summary: CoreWeave Inc.'s stock experienced a volatile debut, initially falling over 5% despite pricing its IPO lower than anticipated at $40, but later recovered to close 2.5% above its initial trade price. The cloud computing infrastructure company, which raised $1.5 billion, faced investor concerns regarding its reliance on two major customers, significant debt, and the capital-intensive nature of its business model. Despite attracting Nvidia as a major backer, many investors questioned CoreWeave's long-term sustainability and the broader macroeconomic climate's impact on IPO appetite.

title: "Toshiba Revitalization Plan": Microsoft 365 Copilot deployed to 10,000 Employees
summary: Toshiba Corporation has deployed Microsoft 365 Copilot to 10,000 employees as part of its "Toshiba Revitalization Plan" to boost productivity and creativity. The initiative aims to transform business processes and increase profit potential, with trials showing a reduction of 5.6 hours of work time per person per month. Toshiba is also developing a "use case catalog" and automatic recommendations based on Microsoft 365 and Viva Insights logs to maximize Copilot's adoption and effectiveness across various departments.

title: SC Ventures & INSEAD: Introducing personalized corporate training with Azure OpenAI Service
summary: SC Ventures, in collaboration with INSEAD, developed Project Eduverse, an AI-driven platform leveraging Azure OpenAI Service to provide tailored corporate training. This platform addresses the challenges of upskilling an evolving workforce by offering secure, simulated learning experiences for critical conversations and skill refinement. Project Eduverse has been used in over 50 use cases, enhancing workforce readiness across various industries and functions.

title: AI²: How radical innovations are revolutionizing tech and health care
summary: This article discusses the transformative impact of "AI²" — artificial intelligence (AI) and artificial incretins (GLP-1s) — on the technology and healthcare sectors. It highlights AI's role as a major productivity enhancer, driven by large tech investments and the rise of LLMs, and positions GLP-1s as ushering in a new era of healthcare. The authors emphasize that both innovations are multiyear developments with vast, evolving investment potential beyond their initial applications, making them crucial for active managers to monitor.

title: Is Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (NASDAQ:PANW) The Best Stock to Buy and Hold for 2 Years?
summary: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (NASDAQ:PANW) is highlighted as a company worthy of consideration for a two-year investment, demonstrating strong sales growth and significant hedge fund interest. The article also discusses the recent dip in tech stocks, particularly AI-related companies, and expert opinion suggesting that despite current turbulence, AI stocks are expected to rally over a two to three-year horizon. It emphasizes patience for investors believing in the long-term potential of AI.

title: JLL transforms real estate with Azure AI Services, delivering a year’s worth of insights in a week
summary: JLL has partnered with Microsoft and Databricks to use Azure AI Services, enabling it to transform real estate with data-driven insights. By implementing solutions like JLL Azara, the company helps clients to aggregate diverse data, cut costs, and make informed decisions, delivering a year's worth of insights in one week. This collaboration empowers commercial real estate clients with accessible, actionable intelligence, improving workplace performance and sustainability.

title: XLK ETF Holdings List — AMEX:XLK
summary: This article lists the top holdings of the State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) as of February 25, 2026. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, Broadcom, and Micron Technology, detailing their weight percentage, number of shares, and market value within the ETF.

title: PIC makes investment decisions twice as fast using Microsoft 365 Copilot
summary: Public Investment Corporation (PIC), Africa’s largest asset management firm, significantly accelerated its investment decision-making process by integrating Microsoft 365 Copilot. This adoption has reduced the time for investment approvals from 12 to six months and drastically cut down the time spent on tasks like creating presentations and surveys. PIC achieved this through a structured change management strategy and is now looking to expand Copilot's use further across its organization.

title: DGRW ETF Holdings List — LSE:DGRW
summary: This article provides a list of the top holdings for the DGRW ETF (WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth UCITS ETF USD) as of March 2, 2026. title: Jim Cramer Says He’s ‘Concerned’ About Alphabet (GOOG) Amid AI Threat – ‘I Stopped Going to Google’
summary: Jim Cramer expresses concern about Alphabet (GOOG) due to the increasing threat of AI impacting its search business, stating he has stopped using Google. Despite these concerns, the article highlights Alphabet's strong performance in YouTube ad revenue and Cloud revenue, as well as its strategic investments in AI to maintain market dominance. The company's valuation also appears attractive compared to its sector average.

title: SPUS ETF Holdings List — AMEX:SPUS
summary: This article provides a list of the top holdings for the SPUS ETF (SP Funds S&P 500 Sharia Industry Exclusions ETF) as of March 16, 2026. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet Inc., with their respective weight percentages, share counts, and market values. The ETF focuses on sharia-compliant investments.

title: Reflecting on 50 years of IT at Microsoft: Five key lessons and insights on the future - Inside Track Blog
summary: As Microsoft celebrates its 50th anniversary, this article reflects on the transformative journey of Microsoft Digital, highlighting five key lessons learned over the decades. These lessons include digital transformation being a continuous journey, the critical importance of change management, a customer-first approach to IT, prioritizing people and talent, and AI as the future of IT. The article emphasizes how these principles have shaped Microsoft's success and will continue to drive its innovation in the coming decades.

title: MSFU ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:MSFU
summary: This article provides the holdings list for the Direxion Daily MSFT Bull 2X ETF (MSFU) as of March 18, 2026. The fund's primary holdings are U.S. Dollar at 87.80% and Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) at 12.20%.

title: We Energies says electric rates for data centers should cover infrastructure costs in Wisconsin
summary: We Energies has proposed special electricity rates for data centers in Wisconsin, requiring these energy-intensive customers to pay for the infrastructure built to support their operations. This proposal aims to prevent residential and business customers from subsidizing the massive energy demands of new data centers like those from Microsoft and Cloverleaf Infrastructure. The utility is asking state regulators to approve these rates to ensure large customers cover the full cost of their energy consumption and the associated infrastructure.

title: MSCI World verliert an Wert: So reagieren ETF-Anleger richtig
summary: The MSCI World ETF is currently experiencing significant losses due to a tech correction in the US market, a weak dollar, and political uncertainties like new tariffs. With over 70% invested in US tech giants, the index is highly susceptible to these fluctuations. The article advises long-term investors to remain invested, consider diversifying into emerging markets or Europe, and assess their risk profile to potentially balance their portfolio with bonds or commodities.

title: AI's Potential for Supply Chain
summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) offers immense potential for optimizing supply chain processes and enhancing decision-making, yet many organizations struggle with successful, scalable deployment. To overcome these challenges, chief supply chain officers (CSCOs) need to define a clear AI strategy, create a common language for operations, prioritize use cases, and measure the value of their AI investments. Incorporating advanced AI approaches like Generative AI (GenAI), Composite AI, and Agentic AI can further accelerate this transformation, requiring continuous adaptation and cross-organizational support.

title: Bitwise MSTR Option Income Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:IMST)
summary: The Bitwise MSTR Option Income Strategy ETF (IMST) aims to provide current income by investing at least 80% of its net assets in MicroStrategy Incorporated (MSTR) common stock and selling call options on MSTR. As of February 27, 2026, IMST is trading at $11.01, representing a 12.8% decrease year-to-date from its starting price of $12.62. The ETF has assets under management of $16.26 million, a dividend yield of 229.97%, and a net expense ratio of 0.98%.

title: Bookworm: This ASX name meets many of Terry Smith’s criteria
summary: This article examines Terry Smith's criteria for a quality business, focusing on five key tenets: high return on operating capital, growth potential, intangible and difficult-to-replicate assets, resilience, and lack of reliance on leverage. The author then applies these criteria to the ASX company Cochlear (ASX: COH), highlighting how it aligns with many of Smith's principles despite being considered overvalued by analysts. The piece concludes by reinforcing Smith's investment philosophy of investing in good companies, not overpaying, and holding for the long term.

title: HP imprime forte investimento no Brasil. Saiba o papel do País na evolução da companhia
summary: HP is making significant investments in Brazil, leveraging the country's operation as fundamental for its global business, which had a revenue of US$53.6 billion. The company's evolution in Brazil is driven by the adoption of AI in products, a strong focus on sustainability, and an increase in exports from local factories. Brazil plays a strategic role in HP's global supply chain and has shown double-digit growth in PC and printer sales, surpassing global averages.

title: N-able Ecoverse Platform Strengthens Cyber Resiliency for the Microsoft Cloud, Adding Adlumin Breach Prevention for Microsoft 365
summary: N-able has enhanced its Ecoverse platform by integrating Adlumin Breach Prevention for Microsoft 365, aiming to bolster cyber resiliency for businesses using Microsoft Cloud services. This new addition helps protect against identity-based attacks, account takeovers, and unauthorized access by using machine-learning to detect and respond to anomalous user behavior within Microsoft 365 environments. The solution contributes to N-able's broader strategy of providing end-to-end Microsoft 365 management, security, and recovery tools.

title: Fortune 100 Company Plans $1B Data Center in Conway
summary: The Conway City Council has approved a memorandum of understanding with an unnamed Fortune 100 company to build a $1 billion, 300,000-SF data center in the city. This project, which will create about 50 jobs, represents the largest capital investment in Conway's history, driven by the area's power availability and real estate. The agreement includes a property tax abatement and extensive infrastructure upgrades to support the facility.

title: Microsoft announces quarterly earnings release date
summary: Microsoft Corporation has announced that it will release its fiscal year 2025 third-quarter financial results after the market closes on Wednesday, April 30, 2025. The results will be available on the Microsoft Investor Relations website, with a live webcast of the earnings conference call scheduled for 2:30 p.m. Pacific Time. The company, known for its AI-powered platforms and tools, reiterated its mission to empower individuals and organizations globally.

title: N-able Launches Vulnerability Management, Microsoft 365 Breach Prevention Features
summary: N-able has introduced significant updates to its product suite, including built-in vulnerability management for its unified endpoint management solutions (N-central and N-sight) and the integration of Adlumin breach prevention for Microsoft 365 into its Ecoverse platform. These enhancements aim to provide MSPs with unified tools to identify and remediate vulnerabilities across operating systems and to proactively prevent identity-based breaches in Microsoft 365 environments. The company emphasizes that robust security is now a critical and mandatory service for MSPs managing corporate tech infrastructures.

title: FYAI: How agents will transform business and daily work | The Microsoft Cloud Blog
summary: This article discusses the transformative potential of AI agents in business and daily life, drawing parallels to historical technological advancements. Charles Lamanna, Corporate Vice President of Business and Industry Copilot at Microsoft, explains how AI agents are moving from being "add-ons" to core drivers of business, enabling new levels of efficiency and scale. The piece highlights real-world examples from companies like Estée Lauder, Dow, and Microsoft, and envisions a future where agents handle routine tasks, freeing up individuals for more meaningful activities.

title: Technology Alliances
summary: Absolute Software offers "Technology Alliances" that integrate its self-healing security solutions with leading cybersecurity and IT platforms. These alliances aim to ensure applications remain operational, protected, and secure by leveraging Absolute's Application Resilience and Persistence Technology to counter security gaps and software vulnerabilities. Partners benefit from enhanced security offerings, continuous protection against cyber threats, and seamless integration capabilities.

title: Balkrishan Kalra
summary: Balkrishan Kalra is the President and CEO of Genpact, a global professional services and digital transformation provider, having been appointed in 2024. He is recognized for his leadership in AI-powered transformation, having overseen initiatives like the Cora platform and numerous generative AI solutions. With an estimated net worth over $15 million, primarily from Genpact shareholdings, Kalra's career highlights include significant achievements in technology deployment and driving sustainable growth for the company, despite Genpact's various controversies regarding executive conduct, workplace ethics, and tax practices.

title: Ten things to know about the European Union's new product liability directive
summary: The European Union has passed a new product liability directive (PLD) which significantly expands the scope of product liability laws, impacting manufacturers of both consumer products and digital goods like software and AI. The directive introduces changes such as a shift in the burden of proof, extended liability periods, and new categories for recoverable damages, all aimed at modernizing product liability for the digital age and increasing consumer protection. Businesses operating in Europe face increased litigation risks and costs, with member states obligated to implement these changes into national law by December 2026.

title: Is Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) a NASDAQ Stock with the Highest Upside Potential?
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) is identified as a NASDAQ stock with significant upside potential, ranking first on a list compiled using analysts' upside potential and hedge fund sentiment. The article discusses Wedbush Securities' Dan Ives' concerns about tariffs and geopolitical tensions impacting tech supply chains and earnings, while highlighting Marvell's strong position in AI and cloud markets through partnerships like with Amazon Web Services. Bank of America reiterated a Buy rating on MRVL due to its AI opportunities and growth trajectory.

title: Meta AI Lawsuit: US Publishers File Amicus Brief
summary: The Association of American Publishers (AAP) has filed an amicus brief supporting a class-action lawsuit against Meta, arguing that Meta's use of copyrighted material for AI training is not "fair use." The AAP asserts that Meta systematically copied protected works for its LLaMA AI model, often from pirate sites, despite the existence of a growing market for licensing AI training materials. The brief highlights that Meta's actions undermine copyright protections and financial investments of authors and publishers.

title: iShares MSCI World ETF: Ob das eine gute Idee ist?
summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF has experienced significant price drops, leading investors to hope for a recovery. However, the article raises concerns about a concentration risk due to the high proportion of US tech stocks (over 70% US equities, with more than 10% in leading tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, Meta, Alphabet, and Tesla). It suggests that a US recession could heavily impact the ETF and advises investors to diversify by adding other ETFs, such as those tracking the MSCI ACWI, to their portfolios.

title: 60 days to launch: Coca-Cola reaches millions with immersive campaign built on Azure
summary: Coca-Cola leveraged Microsoft Azure, particularly Azure AI Foundry and Azure AI Speech, to launch an immersive holiday campaign in just 60 days. This campaign featured a custom AI Santa model that conversed in real-time with over a million people in 26 languages, successfully enhancing emotional connections and brand loyalty across 43 markets. The initiative highlighted Azure's robust capabilities for scalability, multilingual support, and secure, AI-powered marketing campaigns.

title: KT rolls out Thai-tailored large language model
summary: South Korean telecom provider KT Corp. has launched a large language model (LLM) platform specifically for the Thai market, developed in collaboration with Jasmine Technology Solution (JTS) and Korean AI startup Upstage. This marks the first time a South Korean company has built and deployed an end-to-end generative AI infrastructure abroad. The platform includes a Thai-specific LLM, an LLMOPs system, and a GPU Farm, trained on local datasets to accurately respond to queries about Thai politics, history, and culture.

title: Progress, not perfection: What we can learn from Microsoft and HSBC’s strategy shifts
summary: Microsoft and HSBC have both recently acknowledged difficulties in meeting their climate targets, but have responded differently. Microsoft recalibrated its strategy to focus on direct carbon reduction and clean energy, while HSBC delayed its operational climate targets due to uncertainties. These contrasting approaches highlight the need for flexibility in climate standards and for companies to prioritize progress over perfection in their decarbonization efforts.

title: Microsoft Data Center Approved by San Jose Planning Commission
summary: The San Jose Planning Commission has approved a proposal by Microsoft Corporation for a new data center at 1657 Alviso Way. This project involves constructing two single-story data center buildings totaling 397,205 square feet, including office space, a new electrical substation, and a reconstructed visitor center. The development, which faced previous delays, signifies a continuing trend of data center construction in the South Bay area.

title: AI Moves Faster than Security, CISOs Say Resilience is Way Forward
summary: A recent survey by Absolute Security of 500 U.S.-based CISOs found that AI is outpacing cybersecurity measures, creating new risks. While CISOs largely support AI deregulation, they acknowledge it makes their jobs harder, especially with concerns about foreign platforms like DeepSeek and "Shadow AI." This has led 83% of CISOs to prioritize cyber resilience over traditional security, with 90% having implemented a resilience strategy to ensure business continuity against inevitable threats.

title: Pillar Security Raises $9 Million in Seed Round
summary: Pillar Security, an AI security startup based in Tel Aviv, Israel, has successfully raised $9 million in seed funding. The round was led by Shield Capital with participation from Golden Ventures, Ground Up Ventures, and strategic angel investors. The company plans to use the funds to expand its research and development efforts and its go-to-market strategy for its end-to-end security platform for AI-integrated software systems.

title: 10 cloud programming languages developers need to know
summary: This article identifies ten essential cloud programming languages for developers, detailing their use cases, advantages, and limitations. It emphasizes the importance of choosing the right language based on factors like cloud client and service integration, targeted stack layer, team familiarity, and risk aversion. The languages covered include JavaScript, Node.js, Python, C, Go, .NET, Java, Kotlin, Swift, and Rust.

title: 3 Top Schwab ETFs to Buy With the S&P 500 in Correction
summary: This article recommends three Schwab ETFs (SCHG, SCHD, SCHX) for investors seeking to diversify their portfolios and reduce risk during a market correction. It highlights the features, holdings, performance, and expense ratios of each ETF, catering to different investment goals like large-cap growth, dividend income, and broad large-cap exposure. The article suggests these ETFs as solid investment options due to their low expense ratios, diversified portfolios, and historical performance.

title: How food manufacturers are embracing AI to forecast trends and navigate regulatory hurdles
summary: Food manufacturers like Kellanova and Ingredion are increasingly using AI for product development, quality assurance, and supply chain management. This technology helps them navigate regulatory hurdles, forecast consumer trends, and adapt quickly to market disruptions. AI platforms, including those from Tastewise, provide insights into ingredient substitutions and consumer preferences, enabling companies to stay competitive and efficient.

title: Microsoft strengthens in-house cyber governance, training
summary: Microsoft has significantly advanced its Secure Future Initiative (SFI) to bolster cyber governance and training, driven by past nation-state-linked cyberattacks. Key achievements include rolling out secure development toolkits to 22,000 employees, linking performance reviews to security standards, and implementing phishing-resistant multifactor authentication for 92% of productivity accounts. The SFI program, launched after previous security breaches, aims to cultivate a security-first culture and fortify enterprise-wide cybersecurity risk management.

title: Microsoft Boosts Azure Disk Efficiency With 'Performance Plus' Tier
summary: Microsoft has generally released "Performance Plus" for Azure Managed Disks, enhancing storage throughput and IOPS for disks larger than 1TB. While it offers improved performance and is a free upgrade, existing disks require migration, and Performance Plus disks are not eligible for Azure Site Recovery. Testing reveals that although Performance Plus improves efficiency, Premium V2 still demonstrates better consistency under heavy loads.

title: Why Fidelity’s Dividend ETF (FDVV) Is a Safe Haven in Today’s Market
summary: The Fidelity High Dividend ETF (FDVV) offers investors a blend of safety and income through exposure to diversified large-cap dividend-paying businesses. Its significant yield of 3.28% and relative resilience make it attractive for those seeking compounding opportunities and portfolio growth, especially in uncertain economic times. With a low expense ratio of 0.16%, FDVV provides a cost-effective way to invest in established companies that can withstand market volatility.

title: Productivity Management Software Market to reach USD 200.39 Billion by 2032, driven by demand for automation, collaboration, and cloud solutions | SNS Insider
summary: The Productivity Management Software Market is projected to grow significantly, reaching USD 200.39 billion by 2032 from USD 61.14 billion in 2023, driven by increasing adoption of cloud solutions, AI tools, and workflow optimization, with a CAGR of 14.14%. North America currently leads the market, but Asia Pacific is expected to show the fastest growth, while the Content Management & Collaboration segment holds the largest revenue share and AI & Predictive Analytics is the fastest-growing solution type. Cloud-based solutions dominate deployment, large enterprises are the largest segment, and the BFSI sector leads industry adoption.

title: Sam Altman Steps Down As Chair Of Nuclear Startup Oklo, Clearing Way For Supply Talks With AI Companies
summary: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, has resigned as chairman of the advanced nuclear reactor developer Oklo. This move aims to prevent a conflict of interest as OpenAI and Oklo are set to engage in discussions regarding an energy supply agreement. Oklo, a US-based company, is focused on developing its Aurora nuclear power plant to provide clean energy for the growing demand from data centers, including potential deals with AI companies.

title: Digital Realty Deploys Three Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Cloud On-Ramps
summary: Digital Realty has expanded its global relationship with Microsoft by deploying three new Azure ExpressRoute peering locations in Atlanta, Brussels, and Vienna. This addition brings the total number of Azure ExpressRoute cloud on-ramps hosted on Digital Realty's PlatformDIGITAL® to 15, enabling customers to establish dedicated, secure, and low-latency connections to Azure services. The collaboration aims to provide enterprise-grade connectivity for hybrid IT solutions and prepare customers for advanced private AI capabilities.

title: NTNX Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: Nutanix, Inc. (NASDAQ: NTNX) operates in the information sector as a software publisher focused on hybrid multicloud computing, offering a unified, cloud software platform for running applications and AI and managing data across different environments. The company emphasizes its Nutanix Cloud Platform (NCP) designed to support hybrid multicloud infrastructure, including deployments in core data centers, at the edge, and in public clouds with a consistent operating model. Nutanix highlights its subscription-based business model, global customer base, and focus on AI, security, and compliance, with offerings like Nutanix Kubernetes Platform (NKP) and Nutanix Enterprise AI (NAI).

title: Ziff Davis files copyright lawsuit against OpenAI in US federal court
summary: Ziff Davis, a digital media publisher, has filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against OpenAI in Delaware federal court, alleging that OpenAI unlawfully used its publications to train and operate its large language models like ChatGPT. The lawsuit claims that OpenAI "intentionally and relentlessly" reproduced Ziff Davis's content, which includes nearly two million articles annually, without authorization. OpenAI, in response, stated that its AI models are trained on publicly available data and are grounded in fair use.

title: ORKT Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: Orangekloud Technology Inc. (ORKT) is a Singapore-based company specializing in no-code and AI-driven application development, particularly through its eMOBIQ® platform, targeting SMEs and corporations. The company focuses on digitalizing business operations across various sectors like manufacturing and retail, utilizing AI to streamline app development and integration. Orangekloud has strategic partnerships and has addressed Nasdaq listing compliance, indicating its active presence in both technological innovation and capital markets.

title: An open letter to third-party suppliers
summary: JPMorgan Chase's Chief Information Security Officer, Patrick Opet, addresses third-party suppliers in an open letter, highlighting the growing cybersecurity risks associated with the "software as a service" (SaaS) model. He emphasizes that the increasing reliance on SaaS creates vulnerabilities and concentration risks within the global economic system. Opet urges software providers to prioritize security over rapid feature development, arguing for built-in security, modernized architectures, and collaborative efforts to prevent the abuse of interconnected systems.

title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Among Billionaire Steve Cohen’s Large-Cap Stock Picks With Huge Upside Potential
summary: Billionaire investor Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has identified Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) as a top large-cap stock pick with significant upside potential. CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and is urging immediate patching and implementation of specified mitigations.

Organizations using Metallic are advised to prioritize protecting M365 credentials managed within the service and implement mitigation measures.

title: Court Orders OpenAI to Retain All Output Log Data: Considerations for ChatGPT Users (via Passle)
summary: A recent court order in The New York Times' copyright infringement case against OpenAI requires OpenAI to preserve and segregate all ChatGPT output log data, overriding its deletion commitments. This decision raises significant concerns for ChatGPT users regarding data privacy, contractual obligations, and the handling of sensitive, proprietary, or personal information. Organizations are advised to assess their data processing practices with ChatGPT, review contracts, and consider potential risks due to the Halt on data deletion.
